### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-13T17:21:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/june-2024-jab-update-and-it-is-excellent.29004/post-337068>

The origin of the furin cleavage site (FCS) in SARS-CoV-2 is a critical element in understanding the forensic origins of COVID-19. One theory suggests that the FCS may be derived from feline MERS-related sequences, while another posits it may be derived from human lung-specific epithelial sodium channels (eNaC) identified in Ralph Baric's research.

This theory involves the collaboration between Ralph Baric, Peter Daszak, and Shi Zhengli, particularly through their DEFUSE proposal to DARPA, which was rejected. Despite the rejection, it is possible that the research continued independently at the Wuhan Institute of Virology without Baric's direct involvement, as Daszak and EcoHealth Alliance already had an active NIH grant studying the risk of bat coronaviruses with the WIV, including gain-of-function experiments.

Daszak's eagerness to advance the research and Shi's familiarity with Baric's techniques could have led to the introduction of the FCS using human eNaC as a source.

Another theory posits that the research was driven primarily by the People's Liberation Army, without significant involvement from Daszak, his NIH funding, or his U.S. associates. In this scenario, the PLA would likely have used a feline MERS-related FCS, leveraging the genetic material and knowledge independently acquired by Chinese researchers, indicating potential biotechnological espionage.

Understanding whether the FCS was introduced through natural mutation or artificial insertion has been vital for distinguishing between a natural zoonotic origin and a laboratory-origin scenario. Additionally, it may be crucial for the forensic analysis of COVID-19's origins. This differentiation has significant implications for biosecurity measures, scientific accountability, and assigning culpability for the COVID-19 pandemic.

Acquiring lab protocols from the WIV is essential for further understanding, as it is increasingly established that a lab origin is probable.

### magnetic — 2024-06-09T16:53:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-gain-on-low-carb-keto.29008/post-335603>

Just a quick question for those who might have experience in weight gain(body fat) while doing low carb or keto. It seems some people actually suffer from this and it's strange because it runs completely counter to the prevailing understanding that you only lose weight. Ray peat crowd likes to use this as ammunition against us. Any thoughts? Thanks

### NikkiYork — 2024-06-09T17:04:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-gain-on-low-carb-keto.29008/post-335605>

> [magnetic said:](/goto/post?id=335603)
>
> Just a quick question for those who might have experience in weight gain(body fat) while doing low carb or keto. It seems some people actually suffer from this and it's strange because it runs completely counter to the prevailing understanding that you only lose weight. Ray peat crowd likes to use this as ammunition against us. Any thoughts? Thanks

I have this. I don’t know what it is.
I have no stamina to look at Ray Peat. He doesn’t interest me.

I can actually lose weight, or maintain, when I go on an extremely low calorie diet that excludes carbs and fat. I’ve done it three times now.

It’s a 1000 calorie diet with 120g protein, almost no carbs no fat.
Since I know of Jack, I skipped the no fat part and I gained 35 pounds.

If I eat more than 1300 calories a day I gain weight and that is not great for somebody who is 5’7” tall.

I believe now that 1300 calories is still starvation and I need to fix my guts, my melanin sheets, my everything before I can worry about weight.

Recently I ate cake and ice cream, because I was like dang, now I’m fat, might as well enjoy it. Yeah, so now it’s my fault  too much carbs, but that’s just a phase. It doesn’t make me gain more weight at least.

Alas, something is not aligned in my metabolism and I am pretty sure it’s because of where I am living.

I actually think my body wants to tell me something. It might be a protective fat layer, that it wants me to have. Not sure.

It’s an interesting subject. Thanks for bringing it up.

Your body is spending more energy and resources digesting and processing and converting the carbs you eat into the proper glucose and glycogen you need as opposed to your body just converting some fats but more protein into said glucose and glycogen. ### Anthony Smith — 2024-06-28T20:04:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-gain-on-low-carb-keto.29008/post-336245>

> [DrEttinger said:](/goto/post?id=336243)
>
> For what this may be worth, here are my two cents on the topic. A person can only explain something with the combined knowledge they possess. The explanation can't be complete if the information is incomplete.
>
> The Keto diet vs. omnivorous diet vs. SAD diet vs. vegan diet builds a different microbiome. Some bacteria extract calories more effectively than others. One hundred calories of apple juice vs one hundred calories of an apple may yield different outcomes in different individuals.
>
> \- Now throw in a sympathetic dominant lifestyle vs a parasympathetic one (vagal tone).
> \- Exercise level, sleep, and light habits.
> \- Consider times of life, infancy, and pregnancy when you can actually create more fat cells if there is too much weight gain.
> \- When and where you eat, and how often you eat...
> \- Leptin, insulin, GLP-1, PYY resistance.
> \- Adrenal cortical hyper and hypofunction, anterior pituitary hypofunction.
> \- Non-alcoholic fatty liver disease due to increased levels of LPS due to leaky gut due to...
> \- Every person is unique and what works for one may not work for another.
>
> This list can go on about why it's hard to lose weight. Diet may be the center of the bell curve for weight control. People who are there aren't complaining. The edges of the bell curve are where people get frustrated because they primarily only understand the food aspect of weight loss.
>
> I have to be a detective in my practice because I don't get to the center of the bell curve patients.

Thank you Dr Ettinger. This is a good reminder that there is not a “magic bullet” when it comes to health (or most anything). We are all uniquely different and what works for me could do nothing or even be disastrous for someone else.

### JanSz — 2024-06-28T23:18:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-gain-on-low-carb-keto.29008/post-336248>

knee joint is particularly vulnerable to injury due to its complex structure and the significant stress it endures
Signs your knee joint is injured include pain, stiffness, weakness and popping or crunching sounds when you move
Common knee problems include **torn ligaments**, cartilage injuries and osteoarthritis
**Maintaining a healthy weight is important, as excess weight may lead to quicker degeneration of cartilage and an increased risk of knee injuries**
Depending on the knee condition, **weight loss**, physical therapy, knee braces, exercise, tai chi and acupuncture can help, as can nutritional interventions for joint health, like bone broth

\=================

> I learn fast. It's important to be like that in life. Not exploring other ideas is detrimental to well being. So anyway I learned what I could here and there a lot of good people here too. I thank everyone's help.

I just had a conversation with an Alaskan native couple yesterday that were looking at buying my house. They are looking at moving from their village a couple hundred miles outside barrow where it is still a hunter gatherer lifestyle. They just finished their hunting season. 2 whales a bowhead and beluga and a lot of bearded seals. They fish for trout and grayling. They gather berries when they are ripe. They can or smoke all of this during the short 3 months of summer. I asked how much berries they collect and eat over the year and they said it varies as much as 100 gallons to none if it were a dry year. This is their diet for the year. Even during a good year eating few handful berries a day is really all they are getting. The woman was in very good shape looked maybe 40 the man had a large pot belly and looked his age. Same diet, I’d imagine same lifestyle choices.
they looked better then the majority of people these days and seemed happier then most as well.

I’ve been keto/carnivore for 10 years now. Personally I feel when my cortisol is high and it’s usually 1 not eating enough fat. 2 not eating eating enough calories. 3 not getting outside enough. I also found cycling clean carbs is essential. Being 0 carb for months or years on end doesn’t work or feel good.

This is also a good example why I don’t understand the sunrise thing about fats. They literally don’t have sunrise for like 2 months but the diet is 90% fat and the Inuit were always very healthy when they lived there traditional diet.

I asked about what evidence changed your mind regarding diet. Your feelings whether justified about Jack or Einstein or irrelevant.

### JanSz — 2024-06-29T00:07:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-gain-on-low-carb-keto.29008/post-336252>

### [Suitable Fats, Unsuitable Fats: Issues in Nutrition](https://raypeat.com/articles/articles/unsuitablefats.shtml)

Ray Peat

raypeat.com

disease called **steatitis or yellow fat disease** was found to be produced in various animals that were fed **too much fish or fish oil.**

and a deficiency of vitamin E.

\=================
The cheapness of the seed oils led to their use in animal feeds, to promote growth. By the 1940s, the polyunsaturated oils, **including fish oils**, were known to cause deterioration of the brain, muscles, and gonads in a variety of animals
\=================
The commercial pressure on fat research has created **a new way of writing** research reports, that several decades earlier wouldn't have been acceptable. For example, the effects of a specific fat on a few of the components of a complex process such as clotting are often described in the title, introduction, and conclusion of an article **as if they were revealing** a way to prevent heart disease.
\==================
The suppressive effects of unsaturated fats on mitochondrial energy production have been widely investigated, since it is that effect that makes animal **fattening with PUFA so economical**. Rather than interpreting that as a **toxic effect**,
**Polyunsaturated Fats**:

-   [**Linoleic Acid**:](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_unsaturated_fatty_acids)
-   [**α-Linolenic Acid (ALA**](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_unsaturated_fatty_acids)**)**
-   [**Stearidonic Acid**](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_unsaturated_fatty_acids)
-   [**Eicosapentaenoic Acid (EPA)**](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_unsaturated_fatty_acids)
-   [**Docosahexaenoic Acid (DHA)**](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_unsaturated_fatty_acids)
-   [**Docosatetraenoic Acid**](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_unsaturated_fatty_acids)

\====================

Crime up here unfortunately is a problem because of the high rates of drug addiction, alcoholism, homelessness. I see this now as a lack of Pomc being activated.

### Huck — 2024-06-30T15:08:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-gain-on-low-carb-keto.29008/post-336299>

I just read about something the carnivore community calls Priming. The idea behind it is most people are undernourished, so when they start keto or carnivore or any diet for that matter, they are undernourished. Since ketogenic diets tend to diminish appetite, the person may stay in an undernourished state. I think the EpiPaleo diet addresses that by saying to eat until full, and eat foods that are highly nutritious.

Carnivore Priming recommends starting with 1 week of 3 meals a day plus snacks and eating until full, followed by a week of 3 meals a day, followed by a week of 2 meals a day.

I'm not advocating for carnivore, but I think they may have identified an important problem - people cutting calories back while being undernourished.

Probably a better way to say it is people who are electron undernourished have to increase electrons, or they will continue with the same problems.

This is a short segment that starts at 3:26, and you can stop it at 4:12.

Just because you go in the sun and activate the POMC pathway... does not mean you will benefit from the metabolites. The epigenetics of a fucked up mind (your software program that you work off of : beliefs) can be more powerful to some than what can be done via light, food, exercise, and sleep.

I agree, my grandmother in India lived 92 yrs mainly in bombay without any of the diet dogmas. She stayed home unlike what jack says and was the healthiest is our entire family. She almost hated going outside snd since I was born I didn’t see her go for an even a walk. But she did have a good routine, didn’t watch T.V much always liked to sit in silence meditating. Always sat near the window so got light. Loved doing house work till her last years. Was always in the balcony flipping the drying. So again got light. Believed in eating ghee with her wheat and butter on her bread while the rest of India was deep into vegetable oils she always criticised them. Was very lean and no aches or pains. She drank a cup of tea first thing in the morning couple of hours laster 1 or 2 boiled eggs for breakfast and drank a cup of milk. Ate her Indian vegetarian lunch and dinner touched no meat or fish. She loved some savoury and sweet snack once a week but always skipped dinner if she ate those or just drank a cup of milk instead.

I am still figuring out what works for my son in terms of diet and he doesn’t metabolise as much fat as he could probably for his age. And we have been low carb for years. He’s improving but he does need some more carbs that is very apparent now. So 60gms of millet has been added. Eggs whites are not tolerated by him. But he’s probably okay with couple of yolks. Can’t say for sure but we’re not going to remove them since choline is so important and he’s a growing boy. We tried removing them earlier few times and don’t see much difference.

My daughter on the other hand escaped autism because we were already on a the path to eating better and no grains were involved in her weaning. She doesn’t want any carbs at age 7 in the u.k except for fruits, that’s her weakness. And a lick of honey. She loves fatty meat mosty beef and has thrived on raw milk, organs and meats and some seafood. Seafood is her least fav. She is very intelligent probably the most in her class, can read books for hours.

Totally the opposite of my son who went through gluten and dairy intolerance’s and then slipped into severe autism at age 2. He changed our world! His sister benefitted from all this.

Mums diet was very different couple of 3 years before she was conceived. We were low carb but not very low just eating more meats and fish now along with organs and stocks. Adding ghee to everything. Eating sourdough bread and fermented rice pancakes.

My wife is now 46 and starting to feel symptoms of peri menopause but fat surely is her main concern… if she goes low on it she doesn’t do too well. She tried more protein and not much fat to lose some weight which she die but had very heavy periods. She then needed to put on some weight as she was borderline and would be considered underweight when she visited the GP. She then realised and in turn reversed her asthma in the U.K winter eating tons of raw cream with some blueberries even though that’s not ideal to add fruit it still cured her asthma and rid her of the inhaler. She keep fat as her benchmark, carbs are tricky for her if they aren’t fermented. She’s okay with fermented grains but not starches and fruit. She gets sinus headaches. Hence she to be careful about choice of carbs and quantities. She has to go for walks or she puts on weight on higher carbs as fats are also high in her diet. Walking outside is her exercise. She doesn’t do anything else. Kids school runs ensure that she get out multiple times.

She can’t wake for sunrises yet. We began eating a millet porridge recipe from India - 2 tbsp of flour each with 1 1/2 tsp of sugar and 3 tablespoons of ghee with our eggs lately instead of sourdough just rye that we have been eating a couple of years as we were looking too slim on our faces and that’s not healthy either. Our outdoor time has increased post covid and coming across jacks work. He hasn’t given me much feedback when I became a silver member that I could easily understand so I’m not anymore. Was hoping for some good advice from him. But following his advice on DHA and outdoors. Can’t move our location yet. My wife loves raw oyesters now and has mad cravings for them some days. She wishes she had tried them earlier.

We’re all eating slightly differently from each other in one home. You have to find your own balance with food is what I believe in now 100%

When it the monsoon season in Bombay we always were always eating deep fried onion fritters and this continues even now. It was not in ghee when I was growing up but sunflower oil in the last few decades which has resulted in horrendous health and in winter always added more fat to food which is also when the festival period also begins. Corn on the cob grilled on coal with butter, lemon, chilli powder and salt is very popular during monsoons. They are now moving back to ghee and traditional oils that have high temp tolerance for frying that were omitted in the last 40 -50 years. There are alot of Indian nutritionists spreading good advice about fats and traditional grains. They also tell expats to continue with the traditional grains like rice or millets and ferment them then eat with protein and ghee in the U.K. especially winters.

Especially when I am overwhelmed by the excessive Linoleic Acid exposure,
I am thinking of **oysters as belonging to a mollusk** category.
That is the only food (I think) that is not exposed to a human feeding it with seed oils.
All mollusks are good food (hopefully low in LA.

oysters are mollusks. They belong to the invertebrate phylum Mollusca, which also includes other shelled creatures like clams, mussels, and scallops.
\======
Mollusks include many different classes and species.

Cephalopods: These are the most neurologically advanced of all invertebrates.
Cuttlefish
Giant squid
Nautilus2
Octopus
Squid
Gastropods: These are the most diverse mollusks and account for 80% of the total classified species.
Abalone
Murex
Nudibranch
Snails
Slugs
Bivalves: These mollusks have a shell that is divided into two hinged parts1.
Oysters
Clams
Mussels
Scallops
Zebra mussel
Ark shell
Scaphopods: These are also known as tusk shells.
Tusk shell
Monoplacophorans: These are deep-sea mollusks with a single, cap-like shell.
Polyplacophorans: These are also known as chitons.
Aplacophorans: These are small, worm-like marine mollusks.
Caudofoveates and Solenogastres: These are small, worm-like marine mollusks.

### AntonisK — 2024-07-03T11:03:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-gain-on-low-carb-keto.29008/post-336498>

Unless you constantly measure, you cannot tell what kind of diet is right for you or anyone else for that matter (by measuring, I mean body fat & lean mass).

There's no such thing as "one size fits all", this should be obvious to all.

We can agree that there are some "foods" that are truly dangerous to all and should be avoided (trans fats, seed oils, ultra/high processed foods), other than that things are quite personalized.

First thing to consider is insulin sensitivity; anyone with insulin resistance should not consume carbs (except some low GL vegetables) to begin with. As Charles used to say "you must deserve your carbs".
Prioritizing animal protein is the first step to make sure you preserve (at least) or increase (ideally) your lean muscle mass by stimulating muscle protein synthesis (MPS). Muscle mass and strength are shown to be linked to longevity.

Good fats are the second thing to include (EVOO, omega 3 etc.) and then some seasonal vegetables.
Also, there is no need to count calories if you do things right, the plan is to make life easier and simpler (less stress), not put another pain in the neck in our - already filled with stress - life.

Knowing that the vast majority of people are overweight and/or obese - which means they have low insulin sensitivity - we can safely say that they should start by limiting carbs and increasing protein.

Light exposure and all that great and interesting facts that are discussed here should be complementary to a fixed and balanced diet, they are not going to solve the problem alone, that's by belief.

Also, I believe that keto and carnivore diets should not be forever eating plans for the majority of people, they are useful tools for the practitioner when properly implemented.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-03T16:02:35+0300

"Jack never puts this forward, and it's a shame because of how strongly the mind influences our 'state of health.'"

When dopamine and melatonin are optimized, you do not need to rely on psychobabble. When they are not, you think positive affirmations are beneficial. I have no problem with using them, but I see through them for what they mean.

Your nonvisual photoreceptor damage operates at a level far above your ability to overcome this deficit. This is where Lipton, Dispenza, JanSz, you, and Leland Stillman operate.

I have zero issues with this take if you choose to believe it. I do not. I go deeper than this level. Just do not assume you know what I think. I see you tend to do that quite a bit on here. You have tried to hijack this site by posting your email contact and FB groups to poach people. If people want to hire you I am all for it. I am just not going to allow you to do it on my own site. You and Jansz can now start your own community elsewhere.

>
> First, read Bruce Lipton's "Biology of Belief" book or watch one of his early videos on the topic (2012).
>
> The concept is different from how you think about it. Your thoughts, emotions, or perceptions can influence your biology (epigenetically) for good or bad. It's not really about one's conviction or belief about what they are doing, though the nocebo/placebo effect can have an impact.
>
> Are you anxious, fearful, worrying, apathetic, in pain, terror, living in shame, blame, or regret..? Do you view the world or people as a constant threat..? Do you think you are non-deserving, terrible, or dying..? Are you prejudiced? These thoughts, emotions, and perceptions can negatively influence your biology. Love, gratitude, acceptance, happiness, joy, laughter, optimism, fun, exhilaration, adventure, youthful mindset... can have a positive epigenetic influence.
>
> All the sun in the world on your skin and avoidance of ALAN will not make up for living in chronic anxiety, worry, fear, terror, shame, blame, and regret. Jack never puts this forward, and it's a shame because of how strongly the mind influences our 'state of health.'
>
> I recommend Ryan Holiday's book The Daily Stoic to my patients as one tool for dealing with this. Controlling what you can and accepting what you can't is a good first step.
>
> Add in responsibe light, sleep, movement, and nutrition habits, and you have your bases covered.

I have downloaded the book you recommended and will give it a read.

I definitely am anxious about everything very adamant that everything has to be optimised otherwise it is bad and I do often view things as a threat.

Thanks.

### Jims — 2024-07-03T18:18:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-gain-on-low-carb-keto.29008/post-336524>

> [MatthewT said:](/goto/post?id=336515)
>
> What do you mean when you say “you get sicker by experience in the UK” ?
>
> Do you mean to say that when you drop carbs out you end up making the issue worse?

I meant from experience when I increase specific carbs like non fermented grains and (starches from grains or veg) or fruits I get into problems in the winter. My immune system doesn’t cope well I get coughs and colds. Fatigue, brain fog and weight gain follow too. I have found my sweet spot in terms of quantity for carbs and stick to it. If I’m very active I can add an extra slice of sourdough with butter in my meal. I never stopped eating grains just switched to soured versions even in winter in the U.K. Increasing DHA resolved my back aches that tortured me for years. It’s only recently that I have begun eating some millet porridge that’s not fermented and cooked in ghee for breakfast with eggs as the weather got warmer and I was tanned this year by June. The cravings for carb increase kicked which has not happened in the past years when getting a tan in the U.K wasn’t a priority

Kids do need food to grow not just light. The light part is being looked at but food is important too.

If I was an expert already like you are I wouldn’t need to be here. I am here because I need to learn. And for that we need response.

We have done the light work and nnEMF and will continue. The ‘move’aspect has been understood as well but it cannot happen yet unless we win a lottery. My questions recently have been not answered anywhere in the forums or blogs or maybe I haven’t found them yet. I had asked if children need to be woken at sunrise when jack posted about the beta oxidation and if oysters are good to continue when having hayfever symptoms. These would be great to hear about. These kind of questions are the ‘mean time’ ones that could improve a child’s quality of life while still having to live in the U.K for the near future. Not many parents here or asking these questions at the moment that I could take examples from and implement.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-05T01:22:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-gain-on-low-carb-keto.29008/post-336638>

Details require your focus. If you don't focus you'll hear it from me. I'd suggest you read this Jims ---->

RULES TO PROTECT YOUR INNER PEACE AT THIS WEBSITE

1\. Cut off toxic people in your life. Although it is not easy to do it, for you to have peace of mind, you have to. This includes members who pay you.

2\. Distance yourself from people who only give out negative thoughts and energy. They will never help you grow into a decentralized thinker.

3\. Choose your battles wisely. Not everything requires your energy and reaction. Details matter. It requires your focus.

4\. Be careful in letting people be a part of your life. Not everyone is a friend. Most students are not.

5\. Unwind. When everything starts to feel tiring, go on a vacation to recharge yourself. You deserve it.

6\. Learn when to walk away. If it is no longer giving you peace of mind, it is not worth it. Sometimes I have to push members into retirement with a ban.

7\. Always make time for yourself. Being alone feels therapeutic at times. Silence allows you to think

8\. Have less time for social media and more of starting a new hobby. Be productive with your time. Stop wasting it on the superfluous.

9\. Be adaptable to change. Always brace yourself for endless possibilities. This subject requires your awareness.

10\. Knowing your triggers is your responsibility. Not all people will adjust to your temperament. Deal with that appropriately. By design, I have fed many trolls here for a long time. Once I realize they add nothing, I will adjust rapidly.

C

> [AntonisK said:](/goto/post?id=336498)
>
> Unless you constantly measure, you cannot tell what kind of diet is right for you or anyone else for that matter (by measuring, I mean body fat & lean mass).
>
> There's no such thing as "one size fits all", this should be obvious to all.
>
> We can agree that there are some "foods" that are truly dangerous to all and should be avoided (trans fats, seed oils, ultra/high processed foods), other than that things are quite personalized.
>
> First thing to consider is insulin sensitivity; anyone with insulin resistance should not consume carbs (except some low GL vegetables) to begin with. As Charles used to say "you must deserve your carbs".
> Prioritizing animal protein is the first step to make sure you preserve (at least) or increase (ideally) your lean muscle mass by stimulating muscle protein synthesis (MPS). Muscle mass and strength are shown to be linked to longevity.
>
> Good fats are the second thing to include (EVOO, omega 3 etc.) and then some seasonal vegetables.
> Also, there is no need to count calories if you do things right, the plan is to make life easier and simpler (less stress), not put another pain in the neck in our - already filled with stress - life.
>
> Knowing that the vast majority of people are overweight and/or obese - which means they have low insulin sensitivity - we can safely say that they should start by limiting carbs and increasing protein.
>
> Light exposure and all that great and interesting facts that are discussed here should be complementary to a fixed and balanced diet, they are not going to solve the problem alone, that's by belief.
>
> Also, I believe that keto and carnivore diets should not be forever eating plans for the majority of people, they are useful tools for the practitioner when properly implement
>

### 1oevets — 2024-07-09T08:38:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-gain-on-low-carb-keto.29008/post-336862>

> [magnetic said:](/goto/post?id=336859)
>
> I have to apologize about the number of peaters healing or being healed. I think you’re right a lot of them don't look to be succeeding in their quest to well being. After looking at the forum more that seems to be e case. Do you think it's because of the massive supplement and pharmaceutical usage?

From what I saw there was no talk of real root causes. They are very myopic on diet and hormones. I was there looking for ideas on how to help my kids ulcerative colitis. Most suggestions were based on progesterone pregnelone cyclosporidine and numerous other drugs/supplements not something I really want experiment with on my 6 year old.

They never took it to that next level and would ask why is this or that low or high.
They would just find a supplement or pharmaceutical to try to fix it. It’s not so different than most drs. I think we can agree that this is the wrong approach.

Most of the dietary ideas are very contradictory to what I’ve seen help most in me and my family. I found keto do to what a dr said would be career (firefighter) ending arthritis in my knee if I didn’t start taking drugs. I was 25 at the time. Eating keto/carnivore I was able to not only continue as a structure firefighter but I got into wildland firefighting for a few years. I’m 40 now and still climbing mountains hunting sheep and packing 150 lb moose quarters on my back. If I were to eat peaty id never be able to do the things I love. I’ve tried Saladino animal based approach but even that will eventually cause my knee to ache.

What I really find valuable with Kruse’s point of view is he really has taken a huge 1000 foot view on why humans are getting sick. Emf, Food, water, light, magnetism and has shown why or how the body uses these mechanisms and how we are breaking these laws of nature making ourselves get sick.

Whether he is 100% right about everything he saids I’m not sure. I am sure that he has a better understanding of how the human body works and how our environment affects its function than any other person I’ve come across.

### 1oevets — 2024-07-10T03:14:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-gain-on-low-carb-keto.29008/post-336906>

the ray peat diet was pretty close to what I grew up eating as a kid until I was 16 and gained some freedom. My mom cooked every meal, brought my lunch to school until 9th 10th grade. Worst thing I ate according to peats ideas would be peanut butter and jelly sandwiches for lunch and Friday night pizza with the family. My mom saw through the vegetable oil marketing and never used them and didn’t let us eat fast food or processed food. I was pretty healthy but I definitely was on the chubby side. 16 yo 5 8 170 playing jv football. I’m 5 9 now, Biggest I got eating sad was 210 after my first kid was born. Eating keto carnivore I can lean out to 170 and have some ab definition with a little work but most the time I’m 180-185.I tried Saladino animal based approach which is pretty close to peat a few years ago but quickly put 15-20 lbs on was always hungry. Aches and pains started again and generally felt like crap. Personally I found every couple weeks if I have a light to moderate clean carb day I do pretty well. Going long periods 0 carb you can feel the cortisol rises and I don’t like it. I’m interested experimenting with adding more carbs to my diet when we move to Texas and see if this approach works…..

### Brent Patrick — 2024-07-10T05:51:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-gain-on-low-carb-keto.29008/post-336907>

Carbs are highly charged they have alot more blue light photons which is why they enter cytochrome one and not cytochrome two which is for fats.
When you eat out of season mainly rely on fats you are missing out on cell signaling in cytochrome 1 you have lower redox and reduced superoxide burst.

**"In the ubiquitination series, I showed you a loss of cell signaling in cytochrome 1, means redox becomes reduced at cytochrome 1 and lowers superoxide burst and affects cardiolipin release. Cardiolipin release changes the size and shape of mitochondria indicating a redox shift has occured. This tells us ultraweak biophoton emission and oxygen consumption has changed metabolically indicating to us a redox shift in metaobolism has occured. This changes the phenotype of the organism by altering the electronic state of the tertiary and quaternary bends in semiconductive proteins.

What are the implications of losing this pulsed superoxide in a zip code of mitochondria? You lose the ability to collect and move electrons in the brain’s neurons using DHA. This means the mitochondria in these neurons cannot harvest those electrons of their excited light energy." JK**

\= Seasonal diet with plenty of seafood!

### AntonisK — 2024-06-15T13:03:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hiking-boots.29010/post-335807>

> [Gcazz said:](/goto/post?id=335642)
>
> Does anyone here have any hiking shoes they like or recommend?
> Going to Tasmania soon for a holiday and can’t wait to check out the thermal springs among other things and also doing a few hikes so was hoping someone here might have some shoe suggestions.

First, I have to agree with Anthony Smith, test your shoes in nearby familiar territory before the actual hike!
After more than 20 years of hiking, I would say you can't go wrong with Salomon & La Sportiva, they are the best shoes/boots I've ever used.
Some questions for you:
1) Do you need a waterproof shoe or not ?
2) Do you need a low-cut or boot ? if you hike light and on easy terrain, then a low-cut is a better option. If you hike with a fairly heavy backpack and/or rough terrain, then a higher shoe (boot) is mandatory.
3) Do you need a stiff sole or not ? if not carrying heavy backpack and not steep/rocky terrain, then you don't need a stiff sole.
Remember, more rigid shoes/boots generally have better ankle support but are heavier and less breathable due to reinforced material.
Salomon X Ultra 4 and La Sportiva Ultra Raptor II are both amazing, very light and comfortable with great grip.
Hope that helps!

# Thread: Laptop Question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-question.29011/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### healthy2015 — 2024-06-10T19:44:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-question.29011/post-335645>

Hi all,

I am guessing, using a laptop outside in the shade, not plugged into a wall ( battery only), with a long Ethernet cable (no WiFi) is much better than using a desktop pc in doors plugged into a grounded socket, with iris software? This is for work.

So are there better laptops to purchase that emit lower EMFs that anyone has tested?

Thank you

### NikkiYork — 2024-06-10T20:33:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-question.29011/post-335649>

Get a meter, it’s awesome! You can test everything and you’d be surprised. It’s not expensive.

### healthy2015 — 2024-06-10T20:56:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-question.29011/post-335651>

> [NikkiYork said:](/goto/post?id=335649)
>
> Get a meter, it’s awesome! You can test everything and you’d be surprised. It’s not expensive.

Thanks Nikki we have a meter already. What i didnt want to do was buy a laptop that emitted high EMF's especially if someone on the forum had tested already.

### JanSz — 2024-06-10T22:08:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-question.29011/post-335657>

> [healthy2015 said:](/goto/post?id=335651)
>
> What i didnt want to do was buy a laptop that emitted high EMF's especially if someone on the forum had tested already.

You
list laptops that emit lower electromagnetic interference

Copilot
Certainly! Based on the latest information available, here are some laptops that are known for lower electromagnetic interference (EMI):

-   [**Dell Latitude E6230**: These laptops are favored for work and are noted for their low EMI emissions](https://www.emfanalysis.com/low-emf-computing/)[1](https://www.emfanalysis.com/low-emf-computing/).
-   [**Toshiba and Acer Models**: Specific models from Toshiba and Acer have been recommended for writing projects due to their lower EMI emissions](https://www.emfanalysis.com/low-emf-computing/)[1](https://www.emfanalysis.com/low-emf-computing/).
-   [**HP 14" Slim Ryzen 3 3200U**: This laptop has a hardware “OFF” switch for Wi-Fi and an Ethernet port, which can help reduce EMI when wireless is not in use](https://forums.anandtech.com/threads/recommendations-for-a-low-emf-laptop.2568518/)[2](https://forums.anandtech.com/threads/recommendations-for-a-low-emf-laptop.2568518/).

It’s important to note that while these laptops are recognized for lower EMI emissions, no laptop is completely free of electromagnetic fields.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-06-10T22:12:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-question.29011/post-335658>

There are 3 EMF’s from any computer. Electric fields (high if the computer is plugged in/charging and not grounded), magnetic (from a fan), and RF from WiFi/bluetooth. With ANY computer/lap top/tablet/phone for low EMF:
\- use on battery or a grounded charging connection
\- turn off WiFi & Bluetooth
\- make sure the ethernet connection is also shielded & grounded (not CAT5 but CAT6A+)
If your set up is good I do not believe the specific device matters. Your outside setup is better because you are outside . . . Inside is bad because you are inside and exposed to all the other nnEMF inside, NOT from the grounded computer connected to ethernet.

### Pratima — 2024-06-10T23:44:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-question.29011/post-335663>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=335658)
>
> There are 3 EMF’s from any computer. Electric fields (high if the computer is plugged in/charging and not grounded), magnetic (from a fan), and RF from WiFi/bluetooth. With ANY computer/lap top/tablet/phone for low EMF:
> \- use on battery or a grounded charging connection
> \- turn off WiFi & Bluetooth
> \- make sure the ethernet connection is also shielded & grounded (not CAT5 but CAT6A+)
> If your set up is good I do not believe the specific device matters. Your outside setup is better because you are outside . . . Inside is bad because you are inside and exposed to all the other nnEMF inside, NOT from the grounded computer connected to ethernet.

thanks, Just checked mine and it says CAT6 which can be remedied.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-06-11T00:42:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-question.29011/post-335665>

The shielded ethernet cables that can be grounded have metal plugs not plastic. Use a body voltage (BV) meter - touch the ethernet cord, if the body voltage goes up, it is not grounded (you have an electric field on/in the network). If BV goes down, the network is grounded. Same applies to your computer, lap top, tablet, phone.

I kind of expected it, but I don’t understand it either.
I wanted to find out how large my batch of vaccines was. Maybe somebody could tell me? That should be available information, no?
Because then I could put the reported vaccine related injuries into relation and understand how toxic my batch was so far.

There are vaccine batches that do not have a single reported incident. Those must have been placebos. I mean come on, there is always a person who is allergic to anything - accept for maybe saline solution.

I want to know how deadly some batches are. I want to know how many of my batch peers get cancer and what type of cancer. I want to know everything about what was put into my blood stream.

I’m not looking for adverse effects.
The CDC offers a report that provides whatever unknown information about the different batches of vaccines.

I would hope to find out how many vials were produced with the number of mine. Would also be cool to see how many were sold and to what locations.

It would just be nice to get information as the CDC apparently has “reports”.

I want to read it, and they said, you can request that report, so I did. But it was denied, because I am a private person.
It doesn’t have information on patients in it, so I don’t understand why I cannot read the report.

ACCESS DENIED

It would be very interesting to me to know how many vials of each batch were produced and/or administered.

Do we have 50 death out of 500 doses of xyz or do we have 50 deaths out of 5,000,000 doses of xyz? It would be nice to know. I doubt that anybody would tell us, but does it hurt to ask?

### Anthony Smith — 2024-06-11T00:24:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/covid-vaccine-lot-report-denied-by-cdc.29013/post-335664>

When I did my research 2020 I found 90%+ of the adverse effects reported could be traced to 4% of the batches. These 4% batches were distributed nation wide, where as all the other batches were distributed regionally - NW, SE etc. Much, at least some, of the info you are lookin for could be extrapolated from VAERS. If there are any reports for your batch and they are all regional and/or few or no reported adverse effects then I think it safe to say it is not one of the “nasty juice” 4%. Good to know. I helped people who had to get it to keep a job etc. (I advised against it but their choice) to at least find a batch with little to no adverse reporting. We do what we can do.

Also remember most/all of the official reporting is bogus. Get hit by a car, test positive for COVID, die of injuries, Death certificate says died of COVID. I have been told that vaxed or not vaxed was most of the time not reported. The official “standard” was if you got COVID within 14 days of being vaxed it was NOT vax related. They lied, they are lying, they will continue to lie. Holland removed the people who died WITH COVID from the people who died FROM COVID and their COVID death rate dropped by 90%. Probably safe to apply that globally.

# Thread: Any UK people here? What does your diet look like?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-uk-people-here-what-does-your-diet-look-like.29015/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet

### MatthewT — 2024-06-11T17:44:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-uk-people-here-what-does-your-diet-look-like.29015/post-335690>

Just seeing if there’s any fellow UK people in here and would love to hear what you eat in a day if so.

I want to gain weight as I’m really slim and the way it seems to me is a bit of extra weight is protective against things like emf, I don’t want to get fat or build a ton of muscle just a few more lbs probably a bit of both lean muscle and fat.

If I’m to follow the advice of epi paleo and I’m not misunderstood I would be eating super low carb all the time in the UK as we don’t get very much UV, with this in mind what foods could I utilise to put on the mass?

### Anthony Smith — 2024-06-11T20:48:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-uk-people-here-what-does-your-diet-look-like.29015/post-335696>

Guinness (liquid bread)

### Jims — 2024-06-12T20:22:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-uk-people-here-what-does-your-diet-look-like.29015/post-335745>

> [MatthewT said:](/goto/post?id=335690)
>
> Just seeing if there’s any fellow UK people in here and would love to hear what you eat in a day if so.
>
> I want to gain weight as I’m really slim and the way it seems to me is a bit of extra weight is protective against things like emf, I don’t want to get fat or build a ton of muscle just a few more lbs probably a bit of both lean muscle and fat.
>
> If I’m to follow the advice of epi paleo and I’m not misunderstood I would be eating super low carb all the time in the UK as we don’t get very much UV, with this in mind what foods could I utilise to put on the mass?

Hi, I’m in the U.K too and still learning what will work food wise. We introduced finger millet flatbread and pancakes in a small quantity, eaten with ghee or coconut oil for our son who is also in need of some help with weight gain. He doesn’t do well on potatoes yet even the small ones which are supposed to have less starch. We will try again when it warms up Have also used buckwheat flour in the past but prefer the millet. We were too low carb looking back now. It has helped with his grip and focus but he is getting a lot of hayfever symptoms which creates its own challenges with ADHD, although better than last summer but still bad.

### rtd — 2024-06-12T21:08:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-uk-people-here-what-does-your-diet-look-like.29015/post-335749>

Raw milk. Start with 1 gallon a week. In my unscientific N=1 trials, raw cow milk adds mass faster then goat milk. FYI: I prefer raw goat milk

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2024-07-03T13:59:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-uk-people-here-what-does-your-diet-look-like.29015/post-336502>

I was born in the UK
What's your BMI currently?

I'm here this summer prior to getting to the tropics permanently.

My current day to maintain weight and bear in mind I'm 54 with 45% water in the body is to have a light breakfast of fruit and ham and a dinner of eg 10 Oz of lamb with roast potatoes (ghee) and ketchup
But I'm not a healthy youngster like yourself
I remember putting on weight eating chocolate. Because chocolate comes from the tropics and whilst it stifles melanin it's full of deuterium.

What exactly is your body shape ? Ectomorph ? Mesomorph ? Frame ?

My BMI is 18.6 at the moment so just out of the underweight range and I’m certainly an ectomorph.

It’s hard to find foods like you say that are low in deuterium and high in calories, there’s only so much meat I can stomach so it’s just finding the additional calories outside of that.

I’m eating in the morning at the moment beef mince with eggs cooked in ghee then turkey steaks, sardines sometimes some organic dark chocolate with some almond butter for lunch and another meat based meal for dinner whether it be salmon, prawns, more beef or turkey and oysters sometimes. I am still not eating in a big enough surplus to gain weight.

I use mostly a farm shop where it is labelled the country it is from and I buy strictly only English produce.

I won’t get very far trying to put on weight with just meat and lettuce though, that is why I ask on here, I hoped there would be other people from where I am from who have faced a similar issue and overcome it and would offer me advice, I’m not willing to mess up my health to accomplish this as I have other issues more important to me so reaching out to wiser people seemed to be the best option to me - I assume this is what this forum is intended for right?

How much exercise do you do ?
As one person mentioned if you are young and have a low BMI and eat till you are full and don't feel hungry then I wouldn't worry about it too much. Seriously

I could not put weight on as a16-18 year old at all. I was stuck at 10 stone 8 not knowing at the time that that was ideal for my height

I know that one friend of mine at university was stick thin and remains pretty much under the 25-6 range of BMI, at uni he was 18-19 to the point of graduation.
He has run the coast to coast in South island new Zealand, a couple of times. He took up triathlon in his 40s and now he's still thin. But muscular I think he doesn't eat enough fat. I stayed with him in Tasmania for 4 weeks and the meat was devoid of fat that he would buy.

I actually felt hungry

I wouldn't worry about your weight. The moment you hit thirty you'll put on weight. If you're in your 20s now then enjoy being able to eat and respire and not put weight on.

Roast local potatoes in lard or tallow and eat those. If you still cannot put weight/ muscle/ fat on then accept that your pathways are optimal

Anyone who wants to add mass: one gallon of milk a day. There is a reason young men who are really skinny use this to add mass. It is not for older people. It is not permanent.

As not a young man, when raw milk became legal in my state I started consuming it. Loved it! Too much. At one point I was crushing 3 gallons a week. Guess what? I packed on weight really fast. Why? I was consuming 10 days of average calories in 7 days. And that was if I was following the US recommendation for men having 2500 calories/day. Now I am working on losing all that weight.

### Annelieuk — 2024-07-05T18:54:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-uk-people-here-what-does-your-diet-look-like.29015/post-336675>

If you have awful health you CAN NOT recover in the U.K! I live on this rainy damp island too and it's hard even if you're not suffering with ill health. I know it's overwhelming to make these changes but I took my two small girls to Costa RIca as my eldest was showing signs of autism. After 1 year she fell into her body and became a perfectly normal little girl, of which I am certain was due to living off grid, being grounded and eating seasonally. It was NOT easy to up and leave. We did 5 years in C.R and came home 5 years ago. 2 years into being home she started to show signs of OCD. She is now 17 and gorgeous and healthy but has had to work REALLY hard on her mental health, managing EMF;s phone use, blocking blue light etc,, however she is desperate to go back to the tropics - she's half Iranian and knows when it's time to have a good bout of sun stability. However, I also KNOW that we must all move back to the equator soon for my kids and husbands health, as they just desperately NEED it. It's so much harder when you have a family but when you get your mind right about the environment side of things and the high need for light stability to heal, it just becomes an adventure. Top tip is to try and get to the Canary Islands for few weeks over the winter (ideally when there isn't a Calima) - we do this every Feb and it's been a life saver!

I can’t pack up and leave but if my wife and kids could get away for longer it is worth exploring. We are both from Mumbai in India and lived in England for more than 20 yrs. My daughter who is 7 loves her school and she’s too social to be home schooled. She’s in fact terrified of it after we began homeschooling my son. We do have a home there but it’s not off grid by any stretch. Where do you go in the canaries? Thanks for the tip! Getting another holiday in winter would be great but don’t have the finances sorted yet. Hopefully we can get away again this year

I know it's overwhelming to make these changes but I took my two small girls to Costa RIca as my eldest was showing signs of autism. After 1 year she fell into her body and became a perfectly normal little girl, of which I am certain was due to living off grid, being grounded and eating seasonally. It was NOT easy to up and leave. We did 5 years in C.R and came home 5 years ago. 2 years into being home she started to show signs of OCD. She is now 17 and gorgeous and healthy but has had to work REALLY hard on her mental health, managing EMF;s phone use, blocking blue light etc,, however she is desperate to go back to the tropics - she's half Iranian and knows when it's time to have a good bout of sun stability. However, I also KNOW that we must all move back to the equator soon for my kids and husbands health, as they just desperately NEED it. It's so much harder when you have a family but when you get your mind right about the environment side of things and the high need for light stability to heal, it just becomes an adventure. Top tip is to try and get to the Canary Islands for few weeks over the winter (ideally when there isn't a Calima) - we do this every Feb and it's been a life saver!

### Sue-UK — 2024-07-07T11:42:57+0300

Hi Matthew

If you put methylene blue in the search and Jack's name it will show you what he has said about it. Its not something to mess with if you don't know what you are doing. Its not a decision based on finances but I've chosen to stay in the UK, based on my own n=1. All my food comes from the supermarket. I eat carbs all year, just much less in winter. PM me if you want to know how I shop for food, but bear in mind its based on my experience, my n=1, and I know nothing about yours. My interest is finding ideas that are more affordable for those who don't have a lot in terms of financial resources, (and the savings I make help fund trips to the sun in the winter.). While you work towards being able to move abroad if that's what you decide and it becomes possible, squeeze the low hanging fruit until the pips squeak while you learn more and are able to apply new knowledge safely based on your context. Its all very well if its possible to move abroad, but I don't accept that if I was financially constrained or for any other reason based on my situation couldn't do that I would be doomed. If CT is a metabolic back door, there's others to find and tap in the UK, and finding them is my challenge.

### Sue-UK — 2024-07-07T12:38:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-uk-people-here-what-does-your-diet-look-like.29015/post-336791>

> [MatthewT said:](/goto/post?id=336789)
>
> potatoes were a thought they’re always in season but I’m sure I heard they’re high in deuterium that’s why I ask so I can be pointed in the right direction, the reason I imagine why this forum was created.

Deuterium is a huge rabbit hole. I see it as a nutrient, but like any nutrient, you can have too little or too much. There are nuances. Deuterium is not the enemy unless its in the wrong place eg mitochondria, or excessive in the wrong season such as winter, or when avoiding available UV by living indoors during the summer, sunscreen etc. There are also deuterium depleting pathways (such as during REM sleep), which is another reason why getting circadian rhythm sorted and prioritising good sleep is important. I'm not sure if REM sleep depletes it, or if its necessary for REM sleep to happen and its used rather than depleted, but years ago, unrelated to deuterium, when I reported I was dreaming and remembering my dreams Jack said that was a good sign. UV plus deuterium is not the same biological response as deuterium in the absence of UV whilst living in the warm lane in winter, which is why I live in the cold lane in winter, eating enough carb to get good sleep. Zero carbs screws my sleep, n=1.

### Sue-UK — 2024-07-12T12:59:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-uk-people-here-what-does-your-diet-look-like.29015/post-336995>

> [Dennis Clark said:](/goto/post?id=336976)
>
> Hi, [@Sue-UK](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9658/)...Do you know of any studies demonstrating how UV-A "pressure" induces deuterium to make UV-C light? I've seen mention of it by Jack a number of times. It may be called "photonic pressure" or "radiation pressure" by physicists. How it works in biology, though, is what I'm most interested in. Thanks!
>
> Cheers,
> Dennis

Hi Dennis

Musings

Not _how_ UVA pressure induces deuterium to make UVC, physics and equations leave me like a deer in the headlights., but it being in the emission spectrum is something I can get my head around. The problem with biology research on zoo or lab animals is that it wouldn't replicate how it happens in nature where I think the "recipe" could be unique for each mammalian species.

In his book Cross Currents, Becker said

_It appears that, over 2 billion years of evolution, living things have taken advantage of the two portions of the electromagnetic spectrum that could be depended upon to always be present: the geomagnetic field and visible light. In this view, it is no more surprising that life developed specific organs to sense the geomagnetic field and to derive timing information from it than that it developed specific organs to sense and derive information from light."

"More recently, it has been shown that the pineal is also sensitive to the daily cyclic pattern in the Earth's magnetic field. Melatonin secretion in human subjects may be changed at will by exposure to steady magnetic fields of the same strength as the geomagnetic field. **Apparently, nature determined that biological cycle activity was too important to be left to one environmental signal alone. "**

"Nature intended the pineal to simultaneously receive the same signals from the daily pattern of day-night and the same rise and fall in strength of the geomagnetic field. Obviously, when one or both signals are abnormal, the pineal does not respond in the normal fashion, and the body's biological cycles become disturbed - with important clinical consequences."_

Bears go into hibernation grounded and in the cold, deuterium loaded from berries and don't poo or pee for months. Humans have a circadian cycle for body temperature. If dying cells emit light, that is a possible source for a predator at the kill site, even if the prey is low in deuterium..... and so on....

So far, for me UVA is just one potential "pressure" and nature provides more than one, or mammals wouldn't live in places where there's no sunlight for months at a time, or if they are nocturnal. They would presumably rely more on the geomagnetic field rather than sunlight for circadian input. There's cold, there's diving to great depths, etc that could provide the deuterium "squeeze" - then there's electricity, and there are electrogenic microbiome bacteria. I wonder if its possible that if a microbiome electrogenic bacteria's electricity hits deuterium, deuterium's emission spectrum provides the UVC ? and if it could also split off a proton to provide to the host making it part of the deuterium depleting pathways?? Then as poo contains a lot of dead bacteria, its what frequencies of light they emitted when they were active and when they were dying ....

JK - _**"Dopamine is made in the eye by sunlight and** i**t can be made in the gut by your microbiome.** **People forget that bacteria release 5000 times more light than eukaryotic cells and this light is capable of making dopamine from the aromatic amino acids in the gut.** This is why serotonin and phenylalanine and tyrosine are stored in the enterochromaffin system there."_
So can the microbiome (fed by the photosynthetic food web linking it back to sunlight) produce the UVA which in turn puts pressure on deuterium leading to an emission of UVC ......

CT can increase dopamine, as can sunlight and the microbiome. Melatonin can increase by a change in the earth's magnetic field. How it works in biology type research would need to be mind bogglingly complex to imitate what nature is doing effortlessly.

But from a UK biohacking point of view I try and look for how different mammals obtain or produce UVC from deuterium in cold dark and boring situations, and for nocturnal mammals. If CT is a metabolic back door, its unlikely its the only one .....

# Thread: Low Vitamin D, 25-Hydroxy labs, despite being outside all day
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-vitamin-d-25-hydroxy-labs-despite-being-outside-all-day.29016/>

### ajohnson90 — 2024-06-11T18:57:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-vitamin-d-25-hydroxy-labs-despite-being-outside-all-day.29016/post-335692>

I’m located in SLC, UT, and am outside pretty much all day. I wake up with the sun, crack the windows and get ready for work. Drive to work with the windows down and from about 7:30-9 I am inside but then Im outside from 9-7:30. Then when I go home I have the windows open until the sun sets.

That said, only my arms, legs, head, and neck are exposed to the sun while I’m at work. I do expose my torso to sun on my days off

I went in for some bloodwork and was surprised to see that my Vit D labs were at 33. From what I understand 50 is the baseline for real health. I have also read that the conventional vitamin D lab may not be the best method of measuring vitamin D in the body as there are multiple forms and they differ between active and stored, iirc.

I’ve listened to some of Dr Kruses interviews where he talks about how EMFs can disrupt vitamin D synthesis, but it’s not something I entirely understand on a practical level.

Any insight or links to blogs I should read or podcast I should listen to?

Thanks!

### Anthony Smith — 2024-06-11T20:54:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-vitamin-d-25-hydroxy-labs-despite-being-outside-all-day.29016/post-335697>

Measure and find the circuits that are affecting you in bed and switch them off at night for a 70%+ reduction in daily nnEMF. A body voltage meter will also help with daytime EMF mitigation.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-panel-free-t3-vs-total-t3.29017/post-335699>

Hello all,

My appointment with endocrino is the 4th of July, so I'm learning from the newsletter 101 what tests to ask the doctor. I also need to convince her to request those tests from "optimizing labs" document. In Quebec, it is a wrestle game.

One of my questions is about free t3 vs total t3. I never had a free t3. I know they are different, but would like to hear from the mitochondriacs specifically.
What can I say to my doctor to get the free t3 specifically ?

I've been hypo for a very long time but doctors never disclosed that information to me until 2020. Yes, that is normality in Quebec. They say they call if abnormal... but they rarely call in any case.
I never tried levothyroxine, because I was waiting for my liver (Budd-Chiari) to heal to see if things would improve. And from Jack Kruse I understand I have always been hypo, and hospital ref ranges are skewed. My THS 4 or 2 was still hypo...A glimpse at my thyroid panel, for context. Thank you.

Oh, and my intuition, after reading yesterday [@CultKittenSunLiquor](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/28339/) "No longer autistic"/T3 post was that I have to try T3 med very soon to get my brain (back)!! But I'll be a bit more patient and read read read.

### Mike117 — 2024-06-12T13:47:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-panel-free-t3-vs-total-t3.29017/post-335723>

Hey Daphnee,

I live in Quebec too (Levis) and I know how frustrating it can be getting the proper care in our public health system.

I see a lot of patients with thyroid problems. Getting the proper lab tests here is very complicated. If your TSH is normal, even if your doctor prescribed a T3-T4 test, the lab won't check them. They work with algorithms that automatically stop them from going deeper if TSH is normal.

Then, if your TSH is abnormal, they will check free T4 but if free T4 is normal, they won't check for free T3. Your best bet is to go to a private clinic to see an endocrinologist that will send you to a private lab like Biron in Quebec. They will test everything he requested. But if you go to the public health system, they won't test what he requested. They will do what their algorithms tell them too.

### 0.66eV Eva — 2024-06-12T14:57:00+0300

Hey Mike,

Yes, doctors often say, "don't worry, we have an algorithm for that". I'm astounded each time.

May I ask you: so you managed to get your free T3 and not total T3 from Biron ? And all the other tests?

Happy to learn that there is still an option here for proper labs! I had the feeling doctors from private clinics would be ruled tightly by the same algorithms and Quebec College of Physicians...

### [Omega-3 Index Complete | OmegaQuant](https://omegaquant.com/omega-3-index-complete/)

 omegaquant.com

### Consequences of Low-Carb Diets​

-   Keto diets can provide immediate symptom relief and rapid weight loss, but may worsen health in the long run due to the reliance on **gluconeogenesis**, a less optimal metabolic pathway for producing glucose internally
-   Low-carb diets fail to improve metabolic flexibility, inducing physiological insulin resistance instead, as they do not address underlying issues with glucose metabolism. Essential body parts, including the brain, muscles during strength training, and various organs, require carbohydrates for optimal functioning, highlighting **the inefficiency of forcing the body to produce its own glucose through gluconeogenesis**
-   Relying excessively on **gluconeogenesis** for glucose production is **energetically costly and inefficient**, requiring a significant energy investment for a relatively low energy return, and is stimulated by stress hormones, potentially leading to muscle loss over time
-   Low-carb diets **can hinder thyroid function** by reducing the liver's ability to convert T4 into the active thyroid hormone T3, essential for regulating metabolism, due to a decrease in glucose availability for the liver
-   Reintroducing carbohydrates in a balanced manner can improve metabolism and overall health, countering the negative effects of long-term low-carb dieting by enhancing the body's energy production and usage

### 0.66eV Eva — 2024-06-14T17:45:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-panel-free-t3-vs-total-t3.29017/post-335780>

Thank you so much. I appreciate a lot!

I'm surprised about the high carb diet and low fat. I definitely need to read a lot more... as I was going toward a high fat, 0 carb diet. Espiecially in a low UV environment. When I eat starch, I get huge inflammation flare-ups though, even instant tooth enamel loss. My blood sugar metabolism is broken.

# Thread: Viruses as micro-organisms or something else?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/viruses-as-micro-organisms-or-something-else.29024/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Chlo_e — 2024-06-12T22:08:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/viruses-as-micro-organisms-or-something-else.29024/post-335754>

As someone who's been through traditional medical training, I never thought I'd be questioning the nature of 'viruses'. In fact, when I first heard alternative theories to viruses as microorganisms I lost my head and got into a totally unnecessary online argument. However, over the last couple of years there have been too many instances that have made me pause and reconsider this topic. What if viruses are not microorganisms at all?

I know JK's position is that viruses are obligate intracellular pathogens. I would like to offer a different perspective based on a broad range of research and observations. What if viruses are actually a bodily response to changes in native or nnEMF? What if viruses are actually frequencies and the effects we observe in people and/or animals are the reactionary cellular and biochemical effects of electromagnetic information that the body is struggling to process or function with? What if transmission can occur between people or groups, but not because of transfer of microorganisms or 'infected' cellular content, but because of the transfer of fluid containing water that carries electromagnetic information? I.e what if there is no germ theory in relation to viruses and it is all happening at an EMF level with downstream biochemical and cellular dysfunction causing clinical signs and symptoms? With the host response then being determined by individual characteristics such as nutrition, sunlight exposure, genes and other variables?

I can't find any anti-viral treatment, vaccine, medication or otherwise, that actually targets a microorganism. They all arrest or interfere with cell function in one way or another- i.e OUR OWN CELL FUNCTION.

Are we completely chasing shadows and we've shoe-horned germ theory onto viruses because we couldn't explain them in any other way? And of course we humans cannot deal with uncertainty and so we are often willing to accept explanations for things that may not actually have a good or solid explanation as yet. I'm posting this in curious good-faith to see what other people think, rather than trying to be overtly contrarian.

### Chlo_e — 2024-06-27T12:43:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/viruses-as-micro-organisms-or-something-else.29024/post-336182>

> [Jingpaw said:](/goto/post?id=336173)
>
> As my brother Alec Zeck once said :
>
> “Here’s how freaking simple it is:
> Virologists add snot from a sick person (that is to contain “viruses”) to viral transport medium (VTM), and then add this mixture to a foreign cell line, alongside antibiotics, nutrients for the cells, and sometimes other stuff.
> The cells break down, experiencing what’s called the cytopathic effect (CPE). This is not a piece of evidence — .
> We did the same steps without any sample of snot (meaning no possible source of any “virus”) and achieved the same results . This means we achieved the same results in which virologists claim the presence and pathogenicity of a virus without any possible “virus” present.”

Yes, thank you for outlining this. I agree that the methodology used to 'isolate' viruses does not show anything resembling what is then interpreted as a virus. I am incredulous that we have built entire professions and systems out of such a flawed concept - although I suspect it has been engineered that way rather than accidental

### Jack Kruse — 2024-06-27T17:33:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/viruses-as-micro-organisms-or-something-else.29024/post-336191>

I think my Viral marketing post tells you exactly how I see viruses in an evolutionary framework. This thread on X covers just how important decentralized ideas are for humans. [https://x.com/DrJackKruse/status/1806293547991114131](https://x.com/DrJackKruse/status/1806293547991114131)

### Chlo_e — 2024-06-27T18:51:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/viruses-as-micro-organisms-or-something-else.29024/post-336196>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=336191)
>
> I think my Viral marketing post tells you exactly how I see viruses in an evolutionary framework. This thread on X covers just how important decentralized ideas are for humans. [https://x.com/DrJackKruse/status/1806293547991114131](https://x.com/DrJackKruse/status/1806293547991114131)

Your thoughts on this are interesting. I wonder whether- if the word virus were to be substituted with energy, water memory, or electromagnetic information, we may still have a cogent rationale that doesn't involve micro-organisms. It would probably be a moot point if we were all living by Nature's laws, but it seems we have strayed so far from Nature that we must now unpick everything to find our way back. Like trying to undo a very large, complicated knot with our toes!

### Chlo_e — 2024-06-29T13:31:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/viruses-as-micro-organisms-or-something-else.29024/post-336256>

> [Jingpaw said:](/goto/post?id=336173)
>
> As my brother Alec Zeck once said :
>
> “Here’s how freaking simple it is:
> Virologists add snot from a sick person (that is to contain “viruses”) to viral transport medium (VTM), and then add this mixture to a foreign cell line, alongside antibiotics, nutrients for the cells, and sometimes other stuff.
> The cells break down, experiencing what’s called the cytopathic effect (CPE). This is not a piece of evidence — .
> We did the same steps without any sample of snot (meaning no possible source of any “virus”) and achieved the same results . This means we achieved the same results in which virologists claim the presence and pathogenicity of a virus without any possible “virus” present.”

I've just connected your comment with the podcast host- the same Alec Zeck? He is doing very good work!

# Thread: Hi from Greece!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-greece.29031/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AntonisK — 2024-06-15T09:49:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-greece.29031/post-335804>

Hi everyone,

I am a personal trainer & dietitian from Athens/Greece. I've been in this amazing journey of improving body composition and health for over 30 years now and I absolutely love this!
Being adjective and suspicious as a person about almost every "mainstream" advice and directives, I always try to read between the lines and have an open mind about learning.
Having learned from some of the best in the field and having the drive to learn more, I "accidentally" fell upon Dr. Jack Kruses' name from a podcast of someone I follow and respect.
After listening to the two-part podcast with Rick Rubin & Andrew Huberman, my mind had expoled and my jaw dropped with what I heard. What a great perspective, a whole new window of knowledge has opened and much more reading and studying for me!
In many ways, Dr. Kruses' approach "explains" and "verifies" much of what I've learned in all these years; things that I thought were somehow radical and controversial and only believed because I highly trusted the man behind (just for the shake of conversation and respect the man was Charles Poliquin).
So, here I am and looking forward to expand my knowledge with all you amazing people!

### JanSz — 2024-06-15T15:29:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-greece.29031/post-335809>

> [AntonisK said:](/goto/post?id=335804)
>
> things that I thought were somehow radical and controversial and only believed because I highly trusted the man behind (just for the shake of conversation and respect the man was **Charles Poliquin**).
> So, here I am and looking forward to expand my knowledge with all you amazing people!

I still highly respect Charles.
Because of his advice I was able to escape GERD and esophagus cancer for about 25 years.
His advice was based on incorrect premise but worked.

Over here I came across another great man, [@DrEttinger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/22307/)
It turns out that I was low on (potassium RBC).
Hard to investigate that direction since my (potassium serum) was always right.

\==========

### AntonisK — 2024-06-15T18:03:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-greece.29031/post-335815>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=335809)
