### rtd — 2025-10-29T21:17:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-361829>

Rough day from not enough sleep. I woke up around 0030 and could not fall back to sleep. No caffeine either. The change in day light amounts feels like its messing with me. Two more hours to go.

### rtd — 2025-10-30T23:15:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-361913>

As Yogi Berra once said, "Deja vu all over again". Same poor sleep pattern last night.

I found a tick embedded into my arm; no bulls eye ring around the bite area yet it hurts. The head maybe stuck in. Did a body sweep and didn't feel any other ticks. It was a small tick.

Finishing up the last of the oyster haul. Will make a stock from the shells with a mirepoix.

I swear my weight jumped over 8 pounds in the last few days. I attribute it to water weight from lack of sleep.

This morning I caved and had an espresso. I regret it. My caffeine intake has been near zero for over a month.

Am trying to foster and maintain a positive attitude yet I am very tired and have a lower tolerance.

Good news is I have fresh oysters to go home. Have to focus on the positives; reframing.

And before I return home, a quick trip to the grocery store to purchase lamb loins for me and the dog. There is a local farm I need to visit for lamb. I am not sure of the lamb seasons. Also another trip to the library as another book on hold arrived- an oyster cook book. My brain is already fabricating a reason to get a burrito from a place near the grocery store. I just may. And then eat oysters.

### rtd — 2025-11-02T14:02:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-362015>

Sunday morning confession: I got the burrito on Friday. I went to Chipotle and there was a full sports team in uniform, in line. So I left and went to a local place and got a shrimp fajita burrito. No rice or beans. Just shrimp with onions and peppers. Which is better option IMO.

Just did morning sunrise with my dog and am going back to bed to try to sleep more. I want to feel rested, I want energy.

Right now I wish my body had an off/on button. So I could shutdown and recharge.

### rtd — 2025-11-03T22:36:41+0200

### rtd — 2025-11-04T22:46:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-362165>

This AM I spent one hour in the sun. I believe this "shift" I am experiencing is light based, going to a poorer seasonal light environment. CT and red light therapy. I am craving rice and beans on the regular, being good by abstaining. I am eating local gourds from a farm down the road. Rutabagas are yummy. Made a hash with them.

My weight has jumped 15 lbs. Not good. Always room for improvement. Light cycles are real.

Going back to protein/fat for the winter. I bought a salmon fillet and two pounds of ground bison. The ground bison is not local, although there maybe a bison rancher in the Amherst, MA area. Its been awhile since I have been there, Amherst- my collegiate stomping grounds.

Looking forward to walking with my dog today. My hip flexors and/or hamstrings feel tight. Crazy high wind levels.

### rtd — 2025-11-06T23:51:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-362265>

To say my sleep has been poor this week is an understatement. I am very tired, jumpy, anxious. My resting pulse is flirting near 100 BPM; this morning it was low 70s and my BP at one point was 150/90. I meditated and took another BP reading: 127/80. If I shut my eyes and breathe, pulse lowers to the mid 80s. In September my BP and pulse were lower; at times I felt faint.

I am too much in my head from not sleeping. At this point I am open to taking a sleeping medication although I live by myself and don't want to take it and be alone. A previous housemate was on sleep medication and this person was active on it, cooking, cleaning and even drove once. Scary.

This too shall pass. I know other people have bigger problems than I. Writing this down is helpful to my journey. I know I can get better. I was doing it for months. Autumn and winter hit differently. Sometimes we need someone to lean on. I know I can stay at a friends house on their couch if need be. As an adult, I am ashamed to feel the way I do. Being up the past few nights, all I have are my thoughts and my dog. My dog is good companion yet she needs to be taken care of too. I am her care giver, her human.

I've been subpar at work this week, and part of last month when I was feeling ill. This a stressor too. I read the headlines and see layoffs rising, government shutdown, etc. Life and timing. I have some savings I could tap into if I take medical leave, if that is possible. The cost of living is high. I dunno. Babbling now.

Protocols. Follow them. Provide the best inputs to my body I can. Stay positive. Embrace the suck.

### rtd — 2025-11-07T15:34:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-362282>

I went to bed around 1845. Slept off and on through the night. Blood pressure was higher than normal. I think I had a panic attack at one point. Cumulatively I got more sleep last night than the past few days. I woke up with the sunrise and felt more human.

### rtd — 2025-11-07T21:55:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-362297>

Two more hours to go until my weekend starts. A better day today, productive. No appetite though. Had half a can of tuna fish. Also I did not drink as much water as normal. Maybe make a water schedule. Hydration is key. I do have a tired/sleep headache though. That is why I am thinking dehydration (plus skin is getting drier). Maybe a Saturday Drip Bar session for a Meyer's cocktail IV as a hydration hack ($150).

I can feel my sinus passages drain now and then and can successfully blow my nose. Prior to the septum surgery, that was not happening. I am thankful for that. In fact, I learned a co-worker has a deviated septum and has poor sleep. I referred the surgeon I used.

I weighed myself this morning, 212 lbs. Two mornings ago, 218 or 219 lbs. Aug 1st, 195 lbs. I had a mid November goal to be 180 lbs. Lets see how close I can get to that by New Years.

Reading several books on oysters. Planning a trip back to farm to talk with Jen, see how I can assist. Thinking maybe this Tuesday (Veteran's day).

Finishing up "Adrenal Fatigue: 21st Century...".

Keeping a Chroma SkyPortal 2.1 on in my main room for my dog and for when I get home. (I like this light, maybe I'll post a picture of it). Although she sleeps mostly in a different room, she has several window places in the living room to perch and look outside. Last night she did not want to walk! When I showed her leash she ran and hid under furniture. Granted, it was very high wind and she is so low to the ground. I changed out of my work clothes and sat near her. She came out of hiding for play and belly rubs. She had bison and carrots for dinner; I had water (Topo Chico 18oz). Then a quick, no leash, outside for her to go and we went to sleep. She woke me up three times to use the bathroom and I am ok with that. That is why the before bedtime long walk is key, she'll sleep through the night after a long walk.

The house temperature is mid 60s F. The bathroom is colder, I have the heat on in their at 60. Who likes sitting on a cold toilet? Summertime maybe. I am going to order new bed pillows, [cariloha bamboo pillow](http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pillows-sleeping.31811/post-361906). The red light above my kitchen sink went out. Its not even a month old, low hours too. Anyways- just clearing out my thoughts. What else, hmmm.

Was thinking how nice it would be to have a small wood stove or propane in the garage. That is where my exercise bike is located. (Not ideal, is what it is. I need something at my residence in the winter yet if I do have pregnenolone steal happening, I question if I should exercise. I probably can do low HRV zone bike pedaling. In better weather I can wheel it outside).

If I owned, definitely would have something like that. Maybe this weekend I will look at new apartments; currently I have no signed lease. There really is no good place EMF wise here. I think I stated before that I believe living here I can improve my health to a point, better than now yet I don't know how high.

Gotta push harder to find an endocrinologist to explore HRT. A place near me does the pellets; I do not want that technique. Perhaps they have other therapy options not listed online. Or perhaps this is my body telling me I have to relocate. A move cannot be lateral and certainly not a step back. My state most likely will turn to be more socialistic because that's the demographics. Choices, choices....

For the heck of it, just checked my resting pulse: upper 70s. I think tonight is going to be better sleep. One blood pressure reading last night, 172/98, pulse in the 90s. In September my evening blood pressure was 90-120/55-80, pulse in the 60s, sometimes mid 50s when drifting a sleep.

### rtd — 2025-11-08T04:56:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-362317>

Pre bedtime blood pressure measurement: 123/70, pulse 81. Had a nice walk this evening.

### rtd — 2025-11-08T14:51:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-362327>

Didnt sleep much as I wanted. Fell asleep 2-3am, woke up around 4am feeling odd. Went through some health checklists and determined my blood sugar was probably low based on symptoms. No glucose monitor to check. I didn't eat much past two days. First I drank a glass of water and waited, nodded off for a bit.

Awoke with same symptoms. Put a little honey under my tongue and waited. Symptoms went away. I did skip dinner as I wasn't hungry and was pushing through to wait until post sunrise to eat breakfast, to which I did. I was up and outside for the sunrise. Eggs with mushrooms, and chourico. Estimating I fell short of my 70g protein goal by 30g. (Note to self, buy more eggs).

I recall reading night time low blood sugar is an adrenal fatigue symptom. Will have to work out my evening dinner time, starting by eating dinner for number one. Even if it is an earlier dinner, have to make sure it's enough protein and fat.

I did have a few glorious farmer snot rockets outside while walking the dog. It's a new dirty pleasure. The instant relief, the ease of nasal breathing. It's up there when you have to pee really bad and then are able to go. I don't think that will ever stop being a good feeling.

Looking at notes, the last good, full, night of sleep I had was Oct 11th. So what happened? I was sick for a week. Started flu like symptoms- aches, chills, fatigue. I took unplanned time off from work that week. And looking back a year, I got sick around that same timeframe. Interesting.

### Mr.AJ — 2025-11-08T15:53:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-362331>

Reading your last couple of updates, I'd be almost certain you've got an ALAN/nnEMF problem in your sleep environment AND you are not getting enough sunlight, particularly AM. Even if days are only 7-8 hours of sun ATM I think being out there for all of it is probably necessary. Grounded. T shirt, shorts, grounding shoes if the ground is frozen.

I think when you get beta oxidation back online from proper AM clock signals you stop seeing that low blood sugar at night because if you can beta oxidate you deplete liver glycogen slower than if you can not. nnEMF destroys this though.

### rtd — 2025-11-09T02:59:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-362379>

This morning it rained until around 1100, then sunny/partially cloudy. Was outside on my deck in boxers unit 15:30. Got dressed, walked the doggo. My skin is red in some areas. Also napped a bit in grass.

Ate a big, early dinner of lamb loins. Cooked mushrooms for tomorrow. I feel ok despite lack of sleep.

Evening blood pressure: 114/67, pulse 89 (post household chores, laundry going as I type)

Tuesday I see Jen the oyster farmer. I ordered 8 dozen. Gifting half while I have the means. Work uncertainty, paying into the karma cup.

I did look online at properties, both rental and for sale in my area. (I have no lease). One property for sale would be ideal for my style of living and I can afford it. If I choose to stay and buy, I don't think I'd journal here anymore the way I'm doing now. Or maybe I would And pivot. Still would read the forum

### rtd — 2025-11-09T19:04:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-journal.29033/post-362424>

Woke up at sunrise, outside for an hour grounded. BAB was a pork tenderloin. Didnt take a blood pressure measurement. I listened to the May 2012 webinar (Hormone Replacement). Learning is iterative.

Today's agenda:
•Meal prep
•Finish laundry
•Read more Brain Gut series blogs
•Setup a local (no Network connection) Enoch AI (shout out to [@DLO](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19314/) )
(I will document the setup and can share to those who inquire. I have a Nvidia Jetson Nano Orin with 8 GB with a cheapo HP laptop, Windows 11 OS, using Oracle Virtual Box with a Linux environment)
•Chat with my parents
•Inventory books

[@Mr.AJ](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/31332/) : My ALAN is locked down. I am in a poor nnEMF environment regularly. Other than seasonal changes and me getting sick, trying to identify causal changes. It's possible my apartment environment has changed. Will have to measure. I have yet to exercise my consultation time that came with the purchase of [@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/) BVM kit...

New pillow comes Monday.

# Thread: Pain Management Conolidine for my dying husband now for his spine fractures
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pain-management-conolidine-for-my-dying-husband-now-for-his-spine-fractures.29034/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Martha Ray — 2024-06-16T23:26:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pain-management-conolidine-for-my-dying-husband-now-for-his-spine-fractures.29034/post-335827>

I had not gotten enough information on Conolidine (GDR Labes) for my husband from my in May when Hospice drugs were not working for brake through. Hospice was not productive and my husband and other family disallowed the Red Clover Tea and Broccoli Sprouts I had begun at first observance of possibility of pancreatic mass even though if I was reading scans correctly looked like 1 cm decrease in size of tumor.
He passed under home hospice care on June 4, 2024 the morning of the day I was released from the hospital with a back injury.
Now I am coping trying with my own pain to avoid opioids and OTC along with other natural alternatives for back pain.
X-ray show multiple fractures in my spine they told me unknown if old or new..They refused to do an MRI. Very poor service from a Sentara Hospital. The more natural remedies sunlight and ice are not yet able to compete with **_Dual Advil_** or low dose **_Hydrocodone_**. I do not want to continue to take those but I am in intense pain.
God and Time are at work for my emotional heartache.
I am praying for optimal help to recover physical health. Any ideas appreciated.

Martha Ray

# Thread: Difficulty tanning the top part of my head
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/difficulty-tanning-the-top-part-of-my-head.29035/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mr.AJ — 2024-06-17T03:30:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/difficulty-tanning-the-top-part-of-my-head.29035/post-335831>

Hi everyone,

First time posting here so if this is the wrong area to post this I apologize.

Have been following Jack's work for around 2 years now. Generally aim for as much sun throughout the day as possible. I've built up quite a solid tan this year, but unfortunately I've been unable to build a tan in the places that my hair has fallen out on the top of my head. I often feel toasted/slightly sun burnt in the area because I'm having trouble building melanin there. I was wondering if anyone else had gone through this problem and had any solutions they could recommend.

Current routine while working outside is sunrise -> 11:30 am outside in the sun w/ shorts on. 11:30-12:30 make lunch, eat outside. 12:30 - until I need a break, outside in peak UV (occasionally will cover the top of my head with a shirt if it starts to feel like it's getting burnt). Shade outside until 5-6pm then outside until sunset. Light out by 9pm. Been hitting a good 10-12 hours of sun throughout the spring/summer time. Diet is mostly steak/eggs/local goat dairy/sardines/mackerel/oysters. Then sweet potatoes/sourdough bread for lunch. Fruit and deserts occasionally, always eaten outside.

All light bulbs in the house are red/UV-a or incandescent and are rarely on past dark Iris on computers/red light setting on phone. I've removed most of the negative ALAN.

nnEMF is still a serious issue where I live and probably not something I can solve without moving (in the works)

Attached two photos of the top of my head.

This seems like something red light maybe be able to help with but I thought just seeing tons of AM/PM sun would solve the problem but unfortunately is has not.

Any advice would be appreciated.

I'm not sure if there is a best practice here, but I'm thinking of either:
\-Shielding the alternator (not expecting material thickness to redirect the field lines enough though)
\-Shielding the footwell

\-Replacing the alternator with another source of energy stored in the trunk to supplement the battery

# Thread: Ovarian follicles, fertility, inhibin, and biophoton creation = transgenerational risks to offspring
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ovarian-follicles-fertility-inhibin-and-biophoton-creation-transgenerational-risks-to-offspring.29042/>
**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-06-19T00:11:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ovarian-follicles-fertility-inhibin-and-biophoton-creation-transgenerational-risks-to-offspring.29042/post-335884>

_**Ovarian follicles, fertility, inhibin, and biophoton creation = transgenerational risks to offspring**_

Ovarian follicles are small, fluid-filled sacs in your ovaries that each contain an unfertilized egg. You’re born with a set number of eggs and ovarian follicles — usually anywhere from 1 to 2 million — and the total declines over time.

By the time you enter puberty, about 25 percent of these follicles remain, around 300,000. At menopause, fewer than 1,000 ovarian follicles are typically left.

Your follicles themselves go through various stages of development. First, primordial follicles will [form in a fetus’s ovaries](https://www.whattoexpect.com/pregnancy/fetal-development/fetal-sex-organs-reproductive-system/). They remain that way until puberty, at which point they begin to develop — just like the rest of you — and become primary follicles.

Right around the time of ovulation, some of these follicles begin to swell. One will become dominant, and the others that started growing — called antral follicles — will recede.

The dominant follicle then breaks open and releases an egg from your ovary, which travels through your fallopian tube toward the uterus. This egg will hang out for about 24 hours, creating an opportunity for [fertilization to occur](https://www.whattoexpect.com/getting-pregnant/fertility/how-fertilization-happens.aspx). The cycle continues each month until you go through menopause. Menopause happens when inhibin in the blood rises.

The antral follicle test is a transvaginal ultrasound that measures how many antral follicles you have. An ultrasound technician or doctor uses a probe inserted into the vagina to examine and count the number of antral follicles on the ovaries. It only takes a few minutes, similar to a pelvic exam.

Antral follicles are very small — between 2 to 9 millimeters in diameter — but you can still see them on an ultrasound as hypoechogenetic regions on the screen. The number of antral follicles visible can give your doctor a ballpark sense of how many total eggs (including the many follicles still too small to see) you have remaining in your ovaries.

While it’s often done on the third day of your menstrual cycle at the same time as other fertility tests, in reality it can be done at any time.

How many antral follicles are normal? It depends on your age and heteroplasmy level in your ovary. If you’re older, you’ll have fewer antral follicles as someone who’s younger. On average, women in their mid 20s to early 30s have around 12 to 30 antral follicles, while women aged 35 to 40 may have between 8 and 15 and women aged 41 to 46 may have somewhere between four and 10. If you have fewer than five antral follicles at ANY age, your doctor may advise certain fertility treatments, like trying in vitro fertilization (IVF) or use donor eggs.

What about women with PCOS? Follicle number does not mean fertility. PCOS is proof of this. Women with PCOS have more antral follicles than women without PCOS. It’s not unusual for a woman with PCOS to have more than 30 antral follicles total. Women with PCOS produce above-average levels of the hormone testosterone, which hinders ovulation. The follicles struggle to release eggs, so they hang out instead of going through the ovulation process. Women with PCOS lack solar redox because testosterone is reduced by sunlight.

Sunlight is stored in mammals at the electronic levels in cells. The light stored gives you the life you get. This is true for you and your perspective baby.

Light needs electrons to work = photoelectric effect.

When we lose electrons for any reason back ot our environment our biomolecules become oxidized and our cells liberate light as a result. This light is called ultraweak biophotons. The oxidation of biomolecules occurs by **_hydrogen abstraction_** by superoxide anion and hydroxyl radicals or by the cycloaddition of singlet oxygen initiate a cascade of oxidative reactions that lead to the formation of electronically excited species such as triplet excited carbonyl, excited pigments and singlet oxygen. The abstraction of hydrogen is defined by the **removal of a hydrogen atom or group from a molecule by a free radical**. Hydrogen/deuterium atom abstraction is often confused with deprotonation, which is the removal of a hydrogen atom (i.e., a proton) by a base in an acid-base (proton transfer) reaction.

When deuterium is abstracted out of our biomolecules by light, cells begin to need to use more UV light to complete this process. The light used in deuterium abstraction needs to be more powerful due to the kinetic isotope effect of the extra neutron in deuterium. As a result of this cost of mass, our cells need more VUV-UVC-UVB-UVA light to abstracted the heavier isotope of hydrogen because of the extra neutron. This means as we age, increase our heteroplasmy rate, or accumulate more deuterium in our tissues for any reason, you are losing the most powerful part of light in our cells and as a result things that rely on UV light become defective.

Generally, the way oocyte selection occurs in mammals is by hydrogen abstraction. The oocytes with the lowest atomic mass are ejected first in a woman's life. Her best egg is usually the one with the lowest deuterium content. The eggs with the most deuterium and exited last. This means the older a pregnancy becomes the more light is needed in the transgenerational process. In this way you can see now why high maternal age and transgenerational epigenetic diseases occur. It is also why more chromosomal abnormalities occur as mammals age. It begins to explain how childhood cancers actually occur when you understand the biophysics. It also explains why migration problems might happen in morphogenesis in the fetus. If the UV light is expended abstracting deuterium in eggs overloaded with deuterium there is less light left at the elctronic level to drive the cell cycle past the mitosis level. This is the key reason why many childhood cancers begine. It also explains why this is a huge problem in infertility, cancer, and in morphogenesis (autism).

One of the first steps in a fertility workup is to measure markers that indicate [how many eggs you have](https://www.whattoexpect.com/getting-pregnant/ovulation/ovarian-follicles). One of those markers is anti-mullerian hormone (AMH). What does AMH tell you about your fertility? AMH is a hormone released from eggs and the higher the number the more eggs you likely can make. The mistake most owmen are making today who delay childbirth is not to harvest and store their eggs earlier in life. In a tech world this decision should be made before one even has a relationship especially if you know your career is going to delay family. In an electromagnetic driven world this is even important to consider.

If you do get pregnant late in life or you have high heteroplasmy rate you might want to consider an Inhibin A test.
The inhibin A test is done to measure the amount of this [hormone](https://healthy.kaiserpermanente.org/health-wellness/health-encyclopedia/he.sth149942#sth149942-sec) in a pregnant woman's blood to see if the baby may have chromosomal abnormalities like [Down syndrome](https://healthy.kaiserpermanente.org/health-wellness/health-encyclopedia/he.std120885#std120885-sec). Inhibin A is made by the [placenta](https://healthy.kaiserpermanente.org/health-wellness/health-encyclopedia/he.stp1633#stp1633-sec) during pregnancy.

The level of inhibin A in the blood is used in a maternal serum quadruple screening test. Generally done between 15 and 22 weeks, this test checks the levels of four substances in a pregnant woman's blood. The quad screen checks alpha-fetoprotein (AFP), human chorionic gonadotropin (hCG), a type of estrogen (unconjugated estriol, or uE3), and the hormone inhibin A. The levels of these substances—along with a woman's age and other factors—help the doctor estimate the chance that the baby may have certain problems or birth defects.

Inhibin can also be used in the fertility perimenopause menopause transition. In fact, in the early in the menopause transition, when cycle irregularity is first observed, the initial event is a decline in circulating inhibin B levels in the early follicular phase. In the late perimenopause, levels of estradiol and inhibin A also fall, inhibin B levels remain low and FSH is markedly elevated. Inhibin B is a peptide hormone that is produced primarily by growing antral follicles that are responsive to FSH. As the follicle continues to grow and become dominant, inhibin B secretion declines. As the growing pool of antral follicles decreases during aging, levels of inhibin B are diminished.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-06-19T00:32:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ovarian-follicles-fertility-inhibin-and-biophoton-creation-transgenerational-risks-to-offspring.29042/post-335885>

Inhibin B is a peptide hormone that is produced primarily by growing antral follicles that are responsive to FSH. As the follicle continues to grow and become dominant, inhibin B secretion declines. As the growing pool of antral follicles decreases during aging, levels of inhibin B are diminished. Studies in women have demonstrated that decreased inhibin B secretion is associated with elevated FSH levels and decreased oocyte quality and fertility potential. Thus, inhibin B has been used as a predictor of ovarian reserve. However, the late appearance of inhibin B in the menopausal transition has led to the suggestion that it may be a better indicator of ovarian activity than of ovarian reserve, due to its direct link with growing follicles.

In children who are born Inhibin B is used differently.

Inhibin B is a heterodimeric glycoprotein and similar to AMH, is a product of Sertoli and ovarian Granulosa cells under the stimulus of FSH. Both _in vitro_ and _in vivo_ studies have demonstrated the important role of inhibin in the negative feedback regulation of FSH \[59,60\].

In male infants, inhibin B concentrations are measurable in the postnatal period and rise during the first week of life. Unlike AMH which decreases with pubertal onset, inhibin B increases progressively as boys approach the onset of puberty, increasing during pubertal stages G1 to G3 and declining slightly thereafter. Less is known about the role of inhibin B in girls during childhood and puberty, however like their male counterparts they follow a similar pattern of secretion, albeit at significantly reduced concentrations. In contrast to AMH in females, inhibin B concentrations vary across the menstrual cycle, with higher levels found in the follicular phase, peaking after the FSH surge, followed by a decline during the luteal phase. Clinical studies have suggested inhibin B as a useful marker to help distinguish between CDGP and hypogonadotropic hypogonadism (HH) in both males and females, where the absence of stimulating FSH in HH results in low inhibin B levels.

Constitutional delay of growth and puberty (CDGP) is classified as the most frequent cause of delayed puberty (DP).

### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-20T17:17:31+0300

# Thread: ajohnson90 Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ajohnson90-optimal-journal.29043/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ajohnson90 — 2024-06-19T01:13:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ajohnson90-optimal-journal.29043/post-335887>

Hello fellow Mitochondriacs,

I wanted to start a new journal to be a little more precise and comprehensive.
10 years ago at the age of 25 I started experiencing debilitating chronic health issues. Initially my sleep quality went way downhill, I started having night sweats, sleeping 12 hours a day, and waking up feeling like I pounded 40 beers and wrestled a bear - I was exhasuted, groggy, my eyes and mouth felt like they were burning and I was dehydrated, with yellow foamy urine.
A year passed and I woke up one day with intense brain fog that hasn't relented.
Over the next few years I started to develop anxiety, depression, panic attack, paranoia, mood swings, weight gain, cold hands/feet, TMJ issues, scalloped tongue, bruxism, vision issues, heart palpitations, POTS, orthostatic hypotension, dyspnea, gut issues like bloating, discomfort, and irregular stool quality, fatigue, exhaustion, weakness, and a "burning" sensation in my muscles akin to working out, but when doing basic tasks like changing clothes or carrying groceries.

I was subsequently diagnosed with mild obstructive sleep apnea, chronic fatigue syndrome and fibromyalgia.

The sleep apnea diagnosis is interesting because the first symptom to appear was sleep issues, but it's also very mild. I have an AHI of 5 and now that I've finally adjusted to my CPAP I experience less than 1 event per hour with no real dip in oxygenation.

I tried so much stuff to address my symptoms. Everything that is popular now, I was doing over a decade ago, and nothing helped at all.

I spent alot of time outside as a kid and into my 20s, I remember feeling an intuitive call to eat breakfast outside every morning in high school- no one I knew was talking about the benefits of the sun back in the late 2000s, but it felt like the right thing to do. I started taking cold showers around the same time.

Fast forward to the illnesses, I continued these practices and discovered Jack in 2017, making his protocols a priority in my life. Still I found no discernible benefit.

There have been two times in my life since the health issues started that they actually went away.

The first time was in 2019 when I had my wisdom teeth removed, I speculated that there was a structural issue causing the sleep issues and a few days after getting my wisdom teeth removed, I woke up feeling great and sprang out of bed like a happy, healthy human being. And my urine was clear! This only lasted for a month though and my issues came back.

In 2020 I moved from 35N (OKC, OK) to 40N (SLC, UT) and my issues worsened- the CFS and Fibro really came into the picture around then.

in April 2022 I participated in Erwan Le Corre's BreathHoldWork Meditation course. Ive spent alot of time studying respiration and practicing breathwork, so this was just an interest of mine but I also knew if I could learn to hold my breath for a long time I could quell the mental healkth issues. Later in the day, on the last day of the 4 week course, I felt a release in my body, as if a boa constrictor had been wrapped around me the last decade and it just let go. That night I slept like a baby and all of my issues went away. Every single one of them, No more brain fog, no more fatigue, pain, mental issues, gut issues etc. I felt 100% healthy and energized. I even lost 40lbs over the next few months

For some reason this only lasted for about 4 months and my sleep issues slowly came back, followed by the rest of my issues. It's worse than ever but I have to figure out what's going on or I won't make it.

That's my story so far, and I need to figure out how to get back to that place of health and clarity, or I'm not going to make it.

I'll post my relevant labs and health info below and answer any questions and responding to any advice you guys have.

### Revelation — 2025-04-12T17:16:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ajohnson90-optimal-journal.29043/post-350517>

# Thread: Mitochondria makes water that acts as a highly reflective mirror for visible light in cells.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondria-makes-water-that-acts-as-a-highly-reflective-mirror-for-visible-light-in-cells.29045/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-06-19T21:47:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondria-makes-water-that-acts-as-a-highly-reflective-mirror-for-visible-light-in-cells.29045/post-335905>

By careful choice of the type and thickness of the dielectric layers in matter, one can design an optical coating with specified reflectivity at different wavelengths of light. Dielectric mirrors are also used to produce ultra-high reflectivity mirrors: values of 99.999% or better over a narrow range of wavelengths can be produced using special techniques. Alternatively, they can be made to reflect a broad spectrum of light, such as the entire visible range for water. That is what Nature did when she forced a bacteria and archea to merge and become a mitochondria. She did it to create a dielectric mirror for visible light to build complexity in eukaryotes.

All cells emit ultraweak UV biophotons via mitochondrial metabolism. So this means the light created from cells needs to be made useful in some way. Creating a dielectric mirror in mitchondrial water produced at cytochrome c oxidase is one of the things that does this.

Dielectric mirrors are very common in optics experiments in physics. What do these mirrors do? Examples of their applications include laser cavity end mirrors, hot and cold mirrors, thin-film beamsplitters, high damage threshold mirrors, and the coatings on modern mirrorshades and some binoculars roof prism systems.

Water is a dielectric material. ... The surface of water can also act like a mirror and change the path of light.

**CITES**

1.

2\. [https://www.sott.net/article/328936-How-electrons-affect-our-lives-Dr-Jack-Kruse](https://www.sott.net/article/328936-How-electrons-affect-our-lives-Dr-Jack-Kruse)

### Jack Kruse — 2024-06-19T21:53:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondria-makes-water-that-acts-as-a-highly-reflective-mirror-for-visible-light-in-cells.29045/post-335906>

Why is a high reflective mirror needed in cells?

As a charge or an electric dipole in water approaches an interface with proteins or lipids, the electric field lines of the charge extend into the protein with less damping than in water. This means dielectric mirrors concentrate light's effect in proteins and lipids inside of cells. As the field energy in the protein rises, the charge is repelled from the protein-water interface as a consequence of the laws of physics.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-06-19T21:59:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondria-makes-water-that-acts-as-a-highly-reflective-mirror-for-visible-light-in-cells.29045/post-335907>

When your redox potential drops you cells have more frustrated hydrogen bonds? What does that mean in terms of light and frequency?

As water molecules get near a large protein molecule in a cell they can't form all four of the hydrogen bonds that they normally form with other water molecules in cells. Each frustrated hydrogen bond costs an energy of about 1 kcal/mol or EH = 0.04336 eV per molecule. In other words, a loss of redox not only costs you energy, a loss of negative charge, and increases the numbers of frustrated hydrogen in cells and this decreases the ability of the cell to achieve quantum coherence in life.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-06-19T22:10:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondria-makes-water-that-acts-as-a-highly-reflective-mirror-for-visible-light-in-cells.29045/post-335908>

Water has a low refractive index but a very high dielectric constant due to the different physical properties that these characteristics are associated with.

1.  Refractive Index:
    \- Refractive index is a measure of how much light is bent, or refracted, when it passes from one medium to another. In the case of water, it has a relatively low refractive index compared to many other substances. This means that light does not bend as much when passing through water as it does through some other materials.
    \- Water's low refractive index is due to the fact that its molecules are relatively simple and uniform in structure, which results in less bending of light as it passes through water.

2\. Dielectric Constant:
\- The dielectric constant, also known as relative permittivity, is a measure of a material's ability to store electrical energy in an electric field. Materials with high dielectric constants are good insulators and can store large amounts of electrical energy.
\- Water has a very high dielectric constant compared to many other substances. This is because water molecules have a significant electric dipole moment, which allows them to orient themselves in response to an electric field and store electrical energy effectively.
\- The high dielectric constant of water is important for many biological and chemical processes where water acts as a solvent, facilitating the dissolution and interaction of charged molecules.

In summary, the low refractive index of water is due to its simple molecular structure, which causes less bending of light, while its high dielectric constant is a result of its ability to store electrical energy efficiently because of the electric dipole moments present in water molecules.

I guess EMF exposure is almost impossible to remove in a modern city and I get out in the countryside (without a phone in my pocket!!) whenever I can and walk barefoot where possible. And MRI is a shorter term exposure than the almost continuous lower grade fields from AC and wireless tech-killery, albeit pretty intense.

Hope you can talk more about specifically the magnetism side of things in another podcast. I have never heard ANYONE discuss the potential downsides of MRI technology, even if they refuse to travel so as to avoid 5G scanners, although I have figured out a way round that.

### rk257 — 2024-12-10T14:01:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondria-makes-water-that-acts-as-a-highly-reflective-mirror-for-visible-light-in-cells.29045/post-343622>

> [Tim R said:](/goto/post?id=340868)
>
> I've listened to a lot but still do not understand why exposure to strong magnetic fields from e.g. an MRI are not as much of a problem as exposure to non native EMF?

All non-Schuman frequencies likely degrade cellular function, yes. However, _chronic_ exposure is the issue. How many biological functions are performed in a half-hour MRI? How many during a year of intense WiFi exposure? The MRI might give a severe jolt to the (relatively) few processes that occur during the scan, but the WiFi EMF will degrade the efficiency of _every_ process that occurs during that year.

> Hope you can talk more about specifically the magnetism side of things in another podcast. I have never heard ANYONE discuss the potential downsides of MRI technology, even if they refuse to travel so as to avoid 5G scanners, although I have figured out a way round that.

From copilot

**Toxoplasma gondii** is a common single-celled parasite that can infect various tissues in the body. Recently, researchers discovered that it can also infect the prostate. Here’s what we know:

1.  **Infection in Mice:**
    -   In experiments with mice, **Toxoplasma gondii disseminated to the prostate within two weeks after infection.**
    -   Once in the prostate, it remained in the form of latent tissue cysts for at least 60 days and could persist for the host’s lifetime.
    -   [The presence of these parasitic cysts led to **chronic inflammation** in the prostate](https://medicine.iu.edu/blogs/research-updates/common-parasite-can-infect-and-promote-disease-prostate)[1](https://medicine.iu.edu/blogs/research-updates/common-parasite-can-infect-and-promote-disease-prostate).
2.  **Human Implications:**
    -   If Toxoplasma gondii triggers chronic inflammation in the human prostate, it becomes significant due to the parasite’s prevalence in the human population.
    -   [Up to 22% of Americans are infected with Toxoplasma gondii, which is **transmitted through oocysts from infected cats or ingestion of raw/undercooked meat**](https://medicine.iu.edu/blogs/research-updates/common-parasite-can-infect-and-promote-disease-prostate)**[1](https://medicine.iu.edu/blogs/research-updates/common-parasite-can-infect-and-promote-disease-prostate).**
    -   Researchers found similarities between the pathology triggered by this parasite in mouse prostates and features observed in humans suffering from BPH.
    -   [BPH affects nearly half of men by age 50 and is associated with lower urinary tract symptoms](https://medicine.iu.edu/blogs/research-updates/common-parasite-can-infect-and-promote-disease-prostate)[1](https://medicine.iu.edu/blogs/research-updates/common-parasite-can-infect-and-promote-disease-prostate).

Biophotons are ultra-weak light emissions produced by living organisms, thought to arise from metabolic processes like oxidative reactions in mitochondria. Centralized scientists and AI bots hypothesized them to play a role in cellular communication, potentially influencing gene expression or signaling pathways. FIAF, also known as angiopoietin-like protein 4 (ANGPTL4), is a protein produced by tissues like fat and the gut, and it’s a key player in lipid metabolism and microbiome construction. Neither understand how UPEs control FIAF. If the UPE is coherent then FIAF inhibits lipoprotein lipase, affecting how fats are stored or broken down, and its expression ramps up during fasting. The microbiome, meanwhile, is the community of gut microbes that can shift in response to diet, fasting, or host metabolism, influencing energy balance and health. If the UPEs in mitochondria is not coherent, the UPE changes and mental illness becomes more probable. You cannot burn fat so it changes neural signaling.

Centralized scientists and technocrats who build AI systems will say no direct study says “biophotons control FIAF to affect the microbiome,” but we can hypothesize based on related mechanisms. This is patently false. Why?

FIAF has known absorption and emission spectra to light frequencies. Because of that, a study is superfluous because each chemical in the world has an optical fingerprint. Just because a scientist has not published the work is immaterial to this BASIC biophysical fact in spectroscopy. This was what I brought Ray Peat over ten years ago, and he was impotent enough to answer my critiques of his work. This podcast covers these details.

### thejessallen — 2024-06-22T08:03:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jess%E2%80%99-full-moon-energy-journal.29053/post-335990>

For 7 years I was deeply involved in living a yoga lifestyle. A classical yoga lifestyle. Which means all natural fiber, no spandex in sight. Its purpose is an individual inward journey to experience liberation and complete spirituality.

Not like western yoga focused on flexibility or fun

Classical yoga becomes fun through conscious choice to see it that way then experience it that way,

Although often fucking painful when a new level of practice begins or when getting back to it after time away.

In classical yoga full moon nights are significant and one night where staying up and enjoying the energy is built into the culture and lore.

The scientific term is “the yogic sciences”

to my delight (and to the delight of my 83 year mom Mama Gigi) - Jack’s content and the quantum biology content align with a lot of the ancient lore of yoga - the yogic sciences

I am finishing up my year long, functional Medicine health coaching certification program. I did not know Jack or quantum biology before I signed up. It’s shocking how the functional medicine curriculum has. Nothing in it about light water and magnetism. It’s left me in it interesting mental quagmire as to what I will, do next.

For sure step 1 - move to El Salvador

I live in Tennessee now at a yoga ashram with my two teens. My 15 year old (Dibble will move to El Salvador with me) and my 18 year old (Sebastian) will stay in TN and attend college. he loves Tennessee. I do too. It is so beautiful here. It breaks my heart to think about what “parasites (jack renamed “the elites” to parasites) are doing to America and how most Americans are doing nothing to stop it. My kids and I made an exploratory trip to El Salvador in May. And signed a two-year lease in the San Blas community. A 5 min walk to the beach - regular sunrise at the beach is a dream about to manifest ️

The annual Bitcoin conference is in Nashville Tennessee this year. I figured I would be living at the Yoga ashram that is about two hours away from Nashville. I have a ticket to go at the end of July. I love Bitcoin. I earned my first satoshis at the end of 2016. Lost too much of it trading shitcoins for a few years. Became a Bitcoin maxi in 2021 and now HODLing

The full moon energy… before Jack, kids and I felt better when getting to bed by 10 or earlier. Once I understood the benefits of circadian biology. Consistent earlier bedtime is the norm.

But full moon nights, no matter what day they fall on, this night we stay up.

I believe there is a scientific data showing people are more awake. On full moon nights. nature didn’t make block out shades - so I’m embracing the energy of the full moon night.

Writing in my optimal journal for the first time.

I love to journal. I’ve been journaling for over a decade. Journaling for joy is how I call it and having this opportunity to journal in the energy of this black swan mitochondriac tribe adds fuel to my fire of joy.

Blessed I am to be here

Grateful for Jack and everyone who has helped him so he can help us

ALARMING to the centralized thinkers and expected for the decentralized clinicians.

MORE TRUTH FOR YOU.

A new study from South Korea finds a “potential link” between COVID-19 injections and BRAIN DAMAGE.

▪️23% higher risk of Alzheimer's.

▪️138% higher risk of mild cognitive impairment.

There is a complete media blackout on this massive story.

Continue reading for all the details on this study that the media is hiding from you.

Time and time again, things that were called a ”crazy conspiracy theory” has been proven true.

For example, a recent peer-reviewed study found that 74% of sudden deaths were likely because of the covid injections!

But now, a new study has emerged, looking at something most of us haven’t considered.

This study from South Korea examined a massive sample of 558,017 people (of which 38,687 were unvaccinated) and found very alarming results.

People who got the COVID injections were 23% more likely to have Alzheimer’s disease.

Not only that, the study found that those who got the COVID injections also had a whopping 138% higher risk of Mild Cognitive Impairment.

That is not an insignificant number.

The truth is running rampant.

The authors of the study conclude the following:

”Preliminary evidence suggests a potential link between COVID-19 vaccination, particularly mRNA vaccines, and increased incidences of AD \[Alzheimer’s disease\] and MCI \[Mild Cognitive Impairment\]. This underscores the need for further research to elucidate the relationship between vaccine-induced immune responses and neurodegenerative processes, advocating for continuous monitoring and investigation into the vaccines’ long-term neurological impacts.”

The truth is out there if you search for it. The problem is that the truth is a problem for the experimental group.

A group of renowned researchers has just published a bombshell study that proves a direct correlation between Covid mRNA shots and surging numbers of sudden deaths seen around the world. The study was conducted by Christof Kuhbandner, PhD, Professor of Psychology at the University of Regensburg in Bavaria, Germany, and mathematician Prof. Matthias Reitzner at Osnabrück University, a public research university located in the city of Osnabrück in Lower Saxony. In their study, the top scientists identified a direct correlation between surging excess mortality in Germany and Covid mRNA injections. The esteemed authors of the study are now raising the alarm about the devastating side effects of the shots and calling for governments to launch full investigations into their explosive findings. The pre-print paper for the study is titled “Differential Increases in Excess Mortality in the German Federal States During the COVID-19 Pandemic.” The German academic researchers initially sought to analyze the influence of COVID-19 on mortality in the sixteen German federal states. However, they found during the analysis that the Covid “vaccines” themselves are causing the surge in sudden deaths, not the virus.

The truth is out there if you search for it. The problem is that the truth is a problem for the experimental group.

How do you like the truth now?

Any advice would be very welcome, Thanks Jess, Annelie

TLDR - I enjoy staying in local places to feel the vibe.

I suppose it depends on what you wanna do after… are you thinking about living here soon? Or just visiting for the different events

I love to stay in local Airbnbs and get a sense of the town and people.

I stayed in Puerto de La Libertad - I did not like that at all. It was right in the middle of nnEMF hell and had so many buildings there wasn’t any way to see the sun. I felt the drain. No me gusta

We did stay in San Salvador for a week. I know the big city is also full of nnEMF but I could go to the top of the building every day and see the sunrise and watch the sunset. That was phenomenal. I do like the fun of the city and wanted that experience but knew I wouldn’t live there.

We stayed in Atami - that was really nice. There are saltwater ocean pools. Having a car is nice there. As it’s away from a lot of things.

If you’re a Bitcoiner. Staying in El Zonte - is surreal because you will be surrounded by bitcoin business signs everywhere. And for me that was just awesome. We didn’t stay there. But visited several times.

It’s also near the Bitcoin farmers market. So be sure to attend that and let me know when you go. Would love to say hello in person.

That was a really long answer and I hope it’s helpful ;-)

Have fun!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recurring-bakers-cist.29059/post-336039>

In the recent year or so I have started having issues with water accumulating in my right knee. Diagnosed with a bakers cist. I did have meniscus issues in that knee back in 2001 and water was accumulating as well. Just recently I'm starting to feel a bit of a Bakers cist in my other knee as well. It almost seems like my immune system is attacking my joints. Last year I did start eating tomatoes after a 10 year pause but have stopped that 6 months ago. Now the knee is having a hard time healing and ridding of the excess water accumulatio. Any suggestions on how to heal from this?

### TaylorJamesTJ — 2024-06-24T03:02:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lyme-coinfections-journey-since-2016.29061/post-336073>

Hello everyone,

I live in Las Vegas. I have a Solar Company which ties me down here otherwise I would leave instantly. I like guitar, psychedelics, nature, motorcycles, etc.

Back in 2017 I was diagnosed with Lyme, Bartonella, Rocky Mountain Spotted Fever, Mycoplasma, Babesia, etc. Couldn't work for 1.5 years. Wasn't sure if I was going to die. Was seriously suicidal for 8 straight months.

These days, my Lyme is pretty much gone. I wrote a book about the journey and I hope to help people more and more in the future. I still have some Bartonella and viruses. But mainly now it is histamine type of symptoms. A lot of it comes from stress which is what I am working on.

I am looking forward to getting to the next level of health and be able to fully exercise (lift weights) again.

There's a ton of info on here, so going to try and find any simple guides to start making the most significant moves right now for where I am at.

Jack can you explain what is going on with ME/CFS? Fibromyalgia? I saw in the comments on your forum that you say its a tough fix but its possible. I'm sure it has to do with water chemistry and dysfunction of the autonomic nervous system and the gut. Because it causes such temperature dysregulation, is Cold Thermogenesis and sunlight something still to consider to regenerate the DC current and restore the flow of electrons? Thanks Jack!

ANSWER: Nature had the foresight to make decisions in hindsight using sunlight. Why? Because light has more non linear properties than anything in the cosmos. The government found out how to make LIGHT A BIOWEAPON in the 1950s. It should be both fascinating and humbling to scientists that the massless, timeless, and phenomenal manifestation of light are the irrefutable requirement for all physical structure on Earth and in the cosmos. It turns out these abilities are why ME is mis-understood. The only constant in a universe of relentless flux because of how light works in us.

The problem with these "discoveries" is that they become systematized and programmed by paradigms. The public needs to realize that "systems and programs" are for computers.... and they are not designed for living things like us. We are designed to be wirelessly connected to the sun and fully connected to Earth to work properly in nature to remain well.

We simply need to connect with nature and feel the zeitgebers..........and Mother Nature will take care of us.

My answer is complicated and illuminating: If you read the last few blogs in my OSF series of blogs at [www.jackkruse.com](https://forum.jackkruse.com/www.jackkruse.com) you will see the reason a system goes south is because there is not enough electrons present within the cell membranes or the mitochondria of that system. Fibromyalgia is a loss of electrons in the muscles and collagen fascia of the entire body that dehydrates us (mitochondria) and lowers our piezoelectric current. This lowers the dielectric constant in cell water and it is become magnified when things like blue light and nnEMF dehydrate our cells. It usually starts in one cell type but it can spread fast if your base line redox is low. It also will alter proton recycling in the mitochondrial matrix. So if you are "young healthy 20 something" who works out in a blue lit gym, or a blue lit Harvard lab, you by definition, have a lowered redox potential because you DESTROYED POMC signaling via alpha-MSH. You also live at a high latitude and in a city with high population density when you were struck down. You likely have no idea how those two things dehydrate cells to set the stage for ME/FM/CFS. Dehydration of cells due to mitochondrial damage is the key initial starting point for ME. It can lead to a local loss in physiologic control over certain muscles too, just by a lack of red light from the skin above the muscles. The goal is not to avoid foods, molds, or toxins, with this issue, but to fix the structure of the tissues and gut by replacing the electrons lost to build the capacitor in cell water back. This turns your energy engine, the mitochondria, back on to protect your cells once again. Here is how I figured it all out.

Read and share it if you find it illuminating.

### TaylorJamesTJ — 2024-10-16T23:03:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lyme-coinfections-journey-since-2016.29061/post-341107>

Thanks for all the info Jack, interesting stuff. I've known about Plum Island and what I have found interesting is back in 2017 when I would talk about Lyme and/or Plum Island, people looked at me weird. Now everybody has heard of Lyme disease before and people consider the possibility of Plum Island.

**swanny810** - I've taken so many tinctures and done so many treatments it's crazy. Also, now that I think about it, in all my time of connecting the last 7 years, I haven't met a single black person with Lyme disease either.

Update on what I've been doing:
\*I haven't missed a single sunrise for the past 10 days (I take dogs for a 45 minute walk, in my neighborhood I can't always see the sun but look in it's direction whenever it is visible). I have been sleeping better. When I walk the dogs in the morning, I walk barefoot. Before the walk, I drink a smoothie with Kefir, prebiotics, protein, eggs, and eat oysters for DHA.
\*I eat Dinner by 4:00pm and don't eat anything after that.
\*Have tried 3 different grounding shoes and they ahve all tore my feet up, another pair is coming in today, hopefully they work so I don't have to rough my feet up on walks lol
\*I swapped out all my bulbs in my house to red bulbs or low blue light bulbs (Boncharge).
\*All lights off whenever possible after sun goes down
\*Unplugged smart devices like Google speakers, my TV, etc
\*Bluetooth off on both of my phones. Turned 5G off.
\*Cold plunge 3x per week (was everyday but have been limiting it)
\*I got UVA/UVB reptile lights and black lights and have them pointed at me while doing my meditation in the morning with infrared panel.

\*Just did a VO2 max test and I tested in the highest category (superior) somehow. My result was 50.2
\*Nature as much as I can and work outside when I can
\*Vitamin D level was at 53. Waiting on Leptin, Ferritin, Reverse T3 results.
\*I've listened to webinars on this site back to Sept 2023, multiple podcasts.
\*I have read Epi-Paleo RX, Mitochondriac Manifesto, Life on the Edge, The Fourth Phase of Water, Red Light Therapy, "Power, Sex, Suicide"
\*Going to read Mitochondria and the Future of Medicine, The Body Electric, The Invisible Rainbow, The Light Doctor,

Just failed a VCS Surviving Mold Test (right eye) so maybe have had a recent mold exposure recently.

Working on separating from my business so I can visit El Salvador or live part time by a Caldera or similar in Mexico to let Nature finally heal me of this 8 year journey with Lyme + Coinfections. Eventually need to get off all these supplements, medications, and hormones that I am on.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-06-24T06:24:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lessons-learned-during-the-failures-ive-endured.29064/post-336088>

**MARRIAGE ADVICE FROM THE FAILURE FILE**

1\. Marriage is not for quitters
2\. You will feel proud of yourself looking back knowing that you honored your vows
3\. If you do marriage right, it will bring out the best in you and in your spouse
4\. Sex is important but not the only thing in marriage
5\. When younger, make love as much as you can, enjoy it. A time is coming when all you have are memories
6\. Friends will come and go, you will know so many people as a married person, but keep your spouse your priority
7\. Don't stay together for children, eventually they will leave you two. What will you have to hold?
8\. Growing old doesn't mean you cannot make your spouse feel special, keep making his/her heart beat, Use your mind
9\. Do not harden your heart, otherwise marriage will be too great a burden
10\. Do not hold grudges, otherwise stress will kill you
11\. Eventhough you two are married, don't forget to build yourself, and work on your goals as an individual. Don't lose you
12\. Not every advice is good advice. Discern
13\. Love your spouse the best way you can, not as a reward to your spouse for treating you right, but because you have personally chosen to
14\. It is not for you to say you are a good husband/wife but for your spouse to say I have a good husband/wife
15\. Don't work too hard chasing after money for your family that you lose your family
16\. Praying for your spouse is your personal responsibility that you cannot delegate to another person
17\. Start each day with warm words and the tone of the day will be good
18\. Don't be scared of aging, be scared of aging without a purpose or a legacy in mind
19\. As you work, think about retirement. Save, invest, plan. You will not always have the energy you have
20\. Fathers, don't disconnect from your children as they grown up. If you do, you will want them near you in your old age but they will struggle to bond with you
21\. Mothers, don't hurt your children with words. Mummy wounds are difficult to heal from
22\. Remember what you sow in your younger years of marriage, you will reap in your older years
23\. Marriage is possible if the two work on it
24\. You may pretend to the outside world you two are happy but behind closed doors, you two know the true state of your union. Stop living a public lie, have true love
25\. Every marriage has dark spots but the wise couple keep the shades and windows open to let the sun in
26\. If you feel like quitting; step back, breathe, be objective and most likely you will realize you don't have to quit. Don't always trust feelings, wait them out
27\. One day you will give an account to God for how you treated your spouse.
28\. What a blessing it is to have lived having loved and been loved...

Remember love is beautiful.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-06-27T14:36:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lessons-learned-during-the-failures-ive-endured.29064/post-336186>

The song that goes with this thread's ideas is below. What happens when you lose faith in any relationship? .

Losing faith in oneself can occur when an individual starts to doubt their abilities, judgment, or purpose. It's as if they begin to question their own identity, values, and beliefs, leading to feelings of uncertainty, self-doubt, and potential despair. This can happen for various reasons, such as:

1.  **Setbacks or failures**: Faced with a series of setbacks or failures, an individual may feel like they're not good enough or capable enough.
2.  **Self-criticism**: Harsh self-criticism can erode confidence and lead to self-doubt.
3.  **Comparison to others**: Constantly comparing oneself to others can create unrealistic expectations and lead to feelings of inadequacy.
4.  **Trauma or adversity**: Surviving traumatic experiences or enduring prolonged adversity can shake one's sense of identity and confidence.
5.  **Internalized negative messages**: Internalizing negative messages from others (e.g., parental criticism, societal pressure) can lead to self-doubt.

When someone loses faith in themselves, they may experience:

1.  **Lack of motivation**: They might feel unmotivated to pursue goals or take risks.
2.  **Self-doubt**: Uncertainty about their abilities, decisions, or choices.
3.  **Fear of failure**: Fear of making mistakes or failing at something.
4.  **Low self-esteem**: Feeling unworthy or unimportant.
5.  **Anxiety or depression**: The emotional toll of self-doubt can lead to anxiety or depression.

Rebuilding faith in oneself requires a combination of self-reflection, self-compassion, and intentional actions. Here are some strategies to help:

1.  **Practice self-care**: Focus on physical and emotional well-being by engaging in activities that bring joy and relaxation.
2.  **Reflect on past successes**: Acknowledge and celebrate past achievements to build confidence.
3.  **Reframe negative thoughts**: Challenge negative self-talk by reframing thoughts in a more positive, realistic light.
4.  **Seek support**: Surround yourself with supportive people who encourage and believe in you.
5.  **Embrace imperfection**: Recognize that everyone makes mistakes; it's okay not to be perfect.
6.  **Develop a growth mindset**: View challenges as opportunities for growth and learning.
7.  **Celebrate small wins**: Acknowledge and celebrate small victories to build momentum.

Remember, rebuilding faith in oneself takes time, patience, and kindness towards oneself. By acknowledging the complexities of human emotions and experiences, we can work towards rediscovering our strengths, resilience, and purpose

### Jack Kruse — 2024-06-24T18:46:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hepatitis-b-vaccine-given-to-newborns.29066/post-336109>

Even more incredible, Hepatitis B deaths climbed after a HepB vaccine (made with human blood) was first introduced in 1981, continued to climb after a recombinant DNA technology version (still used today) was introduced in 1986, and climbed again after universal use in babies began in 1991. When deaths go down, they always credit the vaccine, when deaths go up, they make excuses (and even when the death rate goes down after introduction of a vaccine, they almost always fail to account for the rate of decline in the target pathogen prior to the introduction of a vaccine targeting that pathogen)

Caveat emptor new parents. Wise choice is to stay out of hospital for birthing at all.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-12-10T03:53:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hepatitis-b-vaccine-given-to-newborns.29066/post-343616>

When is enough enough for centralized medicine?

The CDC just dropped its 2025 Childhood vaccine schedule. Here's what a perfectly healthy baby receives from 0-12 months:
1x Resperatory Syncyctial Virus (RSV)
3x Hepatitis B (HepB)
3x Rotavirus
3x Diphtheria, Tetanus & Pertussis (DTaP)
3x Haemophilus influenzae type b (Hib)
4x Pneumococcal conjugate (PCV15)
3x Polio (IPV)
1x COVID-19 jab
1x Influenza
1x Measles, Mumps & Rubella (MMR)
1x Varicella
2x Hepatitis A (HepA)
26 doses made by habitually criminal companies that aren't liable for injury/death and profit from perpetual disease injected into a perfectly healthy baby.

### madliv — 2024-12-11T18:38:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hepatitis-b-vaccine-given-to-newborns.29066/post-343710>

When I was pregnant (back in 2016-17) I was literally harassed to get the flu and dtpa jab. The doctors and nurses kept repeating the same pamphlet like zombies... they used the same words and dogma which if you are minimally not zombie would pick up as a massive red flag. Back then I was semi unconscious about certain things but I refused and asked for better arguments to take them... they didn't exist. I even downloaded the inserts and saw that there was zero of those words the doctors and nurses repeated. Even more, it was like no testing of safety reported.

Fast forward and I had a baby, next thing I am told by my family (forever grateful for that) that "you do not question those things and that you simply do it even if you feel terrible for the baby"... that was just what I needed to start researching, see the real evidence and not giving anything to my baby... my husband was horrified yet who can fight with a mama bear (haha)... he got to convince himself by facts... he had an older kid from a previous relationship... the health between one and the other was a contrast to say the least.
Some of my friends have rejected when I have attempted to deliver some information but once a person from an aware local group on telegram of the recent poisons accepted the information and to this day tells me how happy and healthy his child is growing feeling grateful he got access to a different outlook.

### Marshall_Sponder — 2024-12-11T19:23:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hepatitis-b-vaccine-given-to-newborns.29066/post-343719>

It's impressive that you ended up making the right decisions for your child and yourself without all this community support or decentralized medical care and advice that you now know about and have - it gives me more faith in humanity that innately we can, at times, make and hold to the right decisions for ourselves even when we don't exactly know why.

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2024-12-11T23:04:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hepatitis-b-vaccine-given-to-newborns.29066/post-343746>

In comparison with the 1970s

I got the single measles vaccine, single rubella, polio sugar lump 1976, single tetanus
Single Diphtheria
And the smallpox one in January 1975 ( I was in isolation for 2-3 days )( optional) Smallpox wasn't officially eradicated at that point
Chickenpox I caught in 1978 2 weeks off school
In 1983 I got the bcg

Didn't have another except tetanus jabs, until 2001 when I was persuaded by a nurse that I should get a flu vaccine because it would stave off the flu. I felt like rubbish for 6-8 weeks and said never again.

I caught the mumps at age 13 Dad brought it home from a patient and inoculated both my mother and me.
In comparison the strongest kid in my class year was not vaccinated at all.

### madliv — 2024-12-12T18:34:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hepatitis-b-vaccine-given-to-newborns.29066/post-343792>

> [Marshall\_Sponder said:](/goto/post?id=343719)

> It's impressive that you ended up making the right decisions for your child and yourself without all this community support or decentralized medical care and advice that you now know about and have - it gives me more faith in humanity that innately we can, at times, make and hold to the right decisions for ourselves even when we don't exactly know why.

Thank you Marshall, I appreciate it! My mother always used to tell me I would never be happy because I question things too much hahahah I don't agree with the statement though... I have been "arguing" with teachers since pre-school!
I think when I started my research I bumped into Suzanna Humphries and Del Bigtree information among others but the first clear no no were the inserts!! not sure if they have them as open as they used to but it was as simple as read the insert to see that what the doctors said wasn't anywhere but in their marketing sheet...
Back then, my husband asked all his friends with children (as if I cared what other people do or don't do) and he thought I was insane but when you become a mom the mama instinct goes absolutely wild... many moms repressed it because a doctor or society tells them to... it is an absolute brainwash... in Spain the jabs are not mandatory in schools (although I do homeschooling) because the compliance is very high, I am guessing 99%

# Thread: WHY BEING IN THE SUN & have a low D3 level & STILL BEING SICK TELLS ME YOU GOT A VITAMIN A/nnEMF PROBLEM?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-being-in-the-sun-have-a-low-d3-level-still-being-sick-tells-me-you-got-a-vitamin-a-nnemf-problem.29068/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-06-24T19:48:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-being-in-the-sun-have-a-low-d3-level-still-being-sick-tells-me-you-got-a-vitamin-a-nnemf-problem.29068/post-336117>

**WHY BEING IN THE SUN & still having a low D3 level & STILL BEING SICK TELLS ME YOU GOT A VITAMIN A/nnEMF PROBLEM?**

You know Vitamin D is yoked to Vitamin A right. I've only said it 1000 times in blogs and podcasts. So when you live in the sun and Vitamin D does not rise here is why-------> [https://x.com/whygetfat/status/1805271712755499224](https://x.com/whygetfat/status/1805271712755499224)

Now that the story is complete, you can see that any kind of blue light or non-native EMF basically uncouple vitamin A from melanopsin. That released retinol becomes…the best way to describe it is a nuclear weapon for all photo receptors. So you'll be interested in this. "You may not know it, B12 is a photoreceptor in humans. OK? And guess what happens when you have melanopsin and retinol damage? That's the reason why vegans have such demolished B12s and shrinks their brains and destroys their non visual photoreceptors everywhere including MELANIN.

WHY?

It happens because when you put them in a blue-light environment, they can't recycle their vitamin B12 through the liver. The other thing that people don't know: if you take every single vitamin known on this planet, the only one that emits light, guess what it is? Vitamin A. When Vitamin A is liberating light inside it needs melanin to be present to absorb it. If it is not present you get toxic levels of ROS that destroy the matter in cells and destroy all signaling. And that's the reason why retinol is ALWAYS bound to every opsin in the human body. It is why and how we protect AMO physics in cells. So that means rhodopsin, neuropsin.....cholesterol....melanin.....catalase....cytochrome proteins.....transgender issues.....bad sleep.....and all the circadian genes.

Magnetoreception discussed in Decentralized medicine #5 is basically taken out massively-----> [https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-5-106652542](https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-5-106652542)

[**Vegans don't eat foods of animal origin, such as**](https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=6fa7d1b51df764e2JmltdHM9MTcxOTI3MzYwMCZpZ3VpZD0xMzk3N2YyYi1mODI4LTZmODItM2E4Mi02YjgzZjliODZlNzMmaW5zaWQ9NjA0MQ&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=3&fclid=13977f2b-f828-6f82-3a82-6b83f9b86e73&psq=what+vegans+dont+eat&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuaGVhbHRobGluZS5jb20vbnV0cml0aW9uL3RoaW5ncy12ZWdhbnMtZG9udC1lYXQ&ntb=1)[1](https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=9bc5729830199301JmltdHM9MTcxOTI3MzYwMCZpZ3VpZD0xMzk3N2YyYi1mODI4LTZmODItM2E4Mi02YjgzZjliODZlNzMmaW5zaWQ9NjA0Mg&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=3&fclid=13977f2b-f828-6f82-3a82-6b83f9b86e73&psq=what+vegans+dont+eat&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuaGVhbHRobGluZS5jb20vbnV0cml0aW9uL3RoaW5ncy12ZWdhbnMtZG9udC1lYXQ&ntb=1)[2](https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=feaa2e18e5058829JmltdHM9MTcxOTI3MzYwMCZpZ3VpZD0xMzk3N2YyYi1mODI4LTZmODItM2E4Mi02YjgzZjliODZlNzMmaW5zaWQ9NjA0Mw&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=3&fclid=13977f2b-f828-6f82-3a82-6b83f9b86e73&psq=what+vegans+dont+eat&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZHJrYXJlbnNsZWUuY29tL3doYXQtdmVnYW5zLWRvbnQtZWF0Lw&ntb=1):
