### Laudy Cincotta — 2024-07-03T17:28:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/visiting-el-salvador.29094/post-336514>

> [Pratima said:](/goto/post?id=336490)
>
> any accommodation recommendations for costa de sol which has easy walk to beach and relax and near food/restaurants?

What is your timeline? I am not sure about short term rentals, as we live here full time and have done so for 14 months.

Rohen is correct there are many air bnbs here so you need to find what works for you.

You actually need to look at Costa del Sol or a map of it to understand it.

It is at least 20km if not more of a stretch of beach on the Pacific side running the length of the coast down to a dead end point that they call 'La Puntilla'. The one road in/out is called Bulevar Costa del Sol.

Most of the beach side of this coast line are private properties with private beach front access.

Having said that there are some public access points to the beach so you would need to work out where they are in proximity to where you want to stay. I have seen some but we are beach front so its not on my radar as i have access to the beach.

The other side of Bulevar Costa del Sol runs along an estuary and again, you would need to find where the public beach access points are if you go to that side of the coast.

Its a rather low population density area compared to La Libertad so it is very different.

There a number of street vendors scattered along the Bulevar selling pupusas and various meals, fruits/vegetables, tortillas etc but we don't eat there, we cook from scratch and prefer that, so it really is researching and finding what works for you. Rohen did a great job of researching the area so it can be done, he might have more suggestions about where to eat.

There is a Betos and a La Pampa (both not very good in my opinion) and an Ajacutla which we go to for the seafood aspect which is the only place we eat here. Just so you can put it into perspective, we need to drive 10 minutes to get to it.

Don't get me wrong, there are things down here, there are 3-4 pharmacies, a small supermarket (which in my opinion is uesless) is 10 minutes away, and a lack of tourists/surfers but Costa del Sol proper is not that populated, thats why we like it and have an organised lifestyle worked around making sure we have what we need and it works for us.

So it's not what you want or are used to that is the question, you can't just up and walk to shops and restaurants, its too sparse for that and i would recommend you have a car because it's hot, dusty and you need to travel distances to get what you want.

I will say if you do want to come down this way still, do your research and the place that you should look at San Marcellino which is about a 10 minute drive away, still in Costa del Sol on the same stretch of beach at the beginning of the Bulevar heading in.

Over there its centered around a few streets that have some shops, restaurants, pharmacy and a local fast food chicken outlet too and many more shops in comparison.

To me that sounds more like what you are asking about, so finding an air bnb up there sounds like what you want and that's the area i would point you, to but bear in mind, more people, more businesses means more emf's/noise etc, so you need to work out what the trade off is for you being near restaurants and in fact being here.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-03T18:04:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/visiting-el-salvador.29094/post-336521>

If you stay in Costa del Sol you will need a car as most things are not close. If you want your kid to be involved that place likely won't be top of your list. The beach areas in La Libertad are teaming with kids his age.

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2024-07-03T21:26:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/visiting-el-salvador.29094/post-336549>

I ate the local fish that was caught by the fishermen and I would make a trip into San Salvador for a big shop. I prefer to cook for myself but if you want to eat out Laudy is right. There was a pupusa seller literally two minutes away and a butcher also. But I was near that awful transmitter.
I had planned to be at that place for the full time I was in El Salvador but I had to change that because of the transmitter

You do however get spectacular sunsets. But sunrises are not as spectacular

You do need a car there.

What a wonderful community this is that is sharing so generously. Within 1-2 days of posting, I got a place to say at MT in Atami for a week, a private spanish tutor and an El Salvadorian who will drop a rental car in front of me. I have lots of hints where my son can find connection. Does look like the Bitcoin beach is a good place to find community for him. All fingers crossed that his severe depression, anxiety, negativity and out-of-control anger will subside. He was not like that. Hopefully that means he can reverse course and hopefully he can see positive change for himself. The point is that he has to find hope again, and the only way is for him to experience good things again.

# Thread: Mitochondrial haplotype testing interpretation
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondrial-haplotype-testing-interpretation.29095/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Emily Burgoon D.O. — 2024-07-02T04:19:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondrial-haplotype-testing-interpretation.29095/post-336410>

HI,
I've spent some time searching here, if I missed it I apologize.
Is there a breakdown anywhere that describes interpretation and differences in implementing treatment for the various possible results?

### Anthony Smith — 2024-07-02T07:31:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondrial-haplotype-testing-interpretation.29095/post-336419>

Use the advanced search feature . . . Posted by Jack Kruse

### JanSz — 2024-07-02T17:24:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondrial-haplotype-testing-interpretation.29095/post-336441>

[https://www.familytreedna.com/pdfs/?path=%2Fpdf-docs%2FInterpreting-Genetic-Genealogy-Results\_web\_optimized.pdf](https://www.familytreedna.com/pdfs/?path=%2Fpdf-docs%2FInterpreting-Genetic-Genealogy-Results_web_optimized.pdf)

I Have the Results of My Genetic Genealogy Test, Now What?
\=======================================

1.  [**Haplogroups:** There are existing mtDNA haplogroups, similar to Y-DNA haplogroups, which can help trace ancestral origins to a particular branch of the mitochondrial genetic family tree](https://www.genealogyexplained.com/dna-testing/mitochondrial-dna-testing/)[1](https://www.genealogyexplained.com/dna-testing/mitochondrial-dna-testing/).
2.  [**Interpretation of Results:** The results of an mtDNA test provides a person’s haplotype](https://www.familytreedna.com/pdfs/?path=%2Fpdf-docs%2FInterpreting-Genetic-Genealogy-Results_web_optimized.pdf)[2](https://www.familytreedna.com/pdfs/?path=%2Fpdf-docs%2FInterpreting-Genetic-Genealogy-Results_web_optimized.pdf). [Family Tree DNA performs additional testing with every mtDNA test to determine an individual’s haplogroup](https://www.familytreedna.com/pdfs/?path=%2Fpdf-docs%2FInterpreting-Genetic-Genealogy-Results_web_optimized.pdf)[2](https://www.familytreedna.com/pdfs/?path=%2Fpdf-docs%2FInterpreting-Genetic-Genealogy-Results_web_optimized.pdf). [The differences are listed as mutations that can be compared to the thousands of other mtDNA mutation listings that are stored in the Family Tree DNA database and in publicly-available databases](https://www.familytreedna.com/pdfs/?path=%2Fpdf-docs%2FInterpreting-Genetic-Genealogy-Results_web_optimized.pdf)[2](https://www.familytreedna.com/pdfs/?path=%2Fpdf-docs%2FInterpreting-Genetic-Genealogy-Results_web_optimized.pdf).

### Daniela Lawler — 2024-07-20T19:45:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondrial-haplotype-testing-interpretation.29095/post-337391>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/few-questions-plz-go-easy-on-me.29099/post-336456>

1) my ancestors would have been eggs/meat/potato/raw dairy/sourdough folk. I seem to feel best doing the same. I have local sources for all the above. Is there a reason I shouldn't follow their path? I love all those things, a lot. Some obviously will be out of season. Raw dairy is from the conventional dairy system for instance. Concerned about lack of dha.

2) making tea from leaves around the farm. Stinging nettle, raspberry leaf, dandelion ect. Must be helping my circadian rhythm, right? Same light environment.

3) deuterium depleted water at 44n. I'm experimenting with partially freezing unfiltered rainwater. Then discarding the ice. Thoughts? Worth it?

4) kiniki brand clothing, anyone know how to source just the fabric?

5) if dark hair absorbs a ton of emf, should I shave my mullet!? The horror.

6) are fermented foods sought after because they are more 'alive' thus more light?

7) has anyone ever heard Jack talk about poison ivy? Conventional wisdom is that the oil from the plant causes the itchy rash. I don't know what to believe anymore.

8) wood fired sauna, is the sweating or the IR light more valuable?

Thank you all.

### JanSz — 2024-07-02T19:02:45+0300

> [tomstaplez said:](/goto/post?id=336456)
>
> 3) deuterium depleted water at 44n. I'm experimenting with partially freezing unfiltered rainwater. Then discarding the ice. Thoughts? Worth it?

Deuterium creates heavy water.
In container heavy water is down, light water is up.
When you are freezing water, ice forms on tap.
Freezing water and removing the ice increases deuterium content in the remaining water.

Threw out the water.
Keep ice for consumption.
**Overall, it makes mostly no difference either way.**
//////////////////////
I use this water. Many times, I tested this water and results of using it.

We cannot keep sending our brightest into centralized science and be shocked when they return; they are pushing the narratives of BigHarma on the public. This is how the game is set up with incentives. People must understand history to understand how to embrace decentralized thinking and make it their lexicon. Centralized fiat incentives determine outcomes in all aspects of human existence.

Here is a lesson for you on academia, and I hope you take it well.

1\. [https://x.com/DrJackKruse/status/1808526673077231982](https://x.com/DrJackKruse/status/1808526673077231982)

3\. Tom, a student of mine, said, "Robert Maxwell poisoned the well for profit in a true centralized fashion. It’s incredible what peeling back the onion reveals." My response: [https://x.com/DrJackKruse/status/1808531274425942205](https://x.com/DrJackKruse/status/1808531274425942205)

Distill the lessons: I believe people must understand history to understand how to embrace decentralized thinking and make it their lexicon. Centralized fiat incentives determine outcomes in all aspects of human existence. The scientific method isn't worth the paper it's printed on these days because none of is true. It is a digital ledger of PhD nonsense of what corporations have bought and paid for and has been allowed in by experts hired by Federal agencies captured by the government to print what they see fit. To print the narrative, they want the sheep to follow. The world follows the USA example. Those days are ending

Taking a sabbatical from lying is the best course of action for a centralized scientist or clinician to employ now. Throw your wooden shoes into the clock gears of the centralized science machine to break it and begin anew with decentralized principles at the cornerstone.

What has my time in centralized medicine taught me?

That my pen is mightier than my scalpel because my pen is filled with ink that writes decentralized ideas down that make others realize that they have been lied to for decades. The reversal of the Chevron Deference is going to devastate the regulatory agencies in healthcare very very soon.

# Thread: Biological mechanism of agitated anger and 0-life-zest?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biological-mechanism-of-agitated-anger-and-0-life-zest.29108/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### drsongihan — 2024-07-05T01:01:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biological-mechanism-of-agitated-anger-and-0-life-zest.29108/post-336632>

At some point the human becomes an adult and that adult is responsible for his/her own life, but till then, if we are talking about a 10 yo, 13 yo, 14 yo boy, the parent is responsible for helping and guiding that child. Can somebody point me towards a blog, or paper or podcast on the topic of uncontrolled anger, sensitivity about any little b.s. and 0 life zest? Officially it's called agitated depression (in the older definition) or DMDD (by the non-sensical DSM category by the American Psychiatric Association) or bipolar depression with mixed state. I read Jack's two long and detailed blogs on bipolar depression (needed to read several times, its fascinating and dense) and I get that the circadian and molecular clocks are broken, but I would like to better understand the bio-molecular process of anger when it happens and what receptors are involved so that on our long journey of healing, I know what to focus on and how to navigate the situation. I understood that avoiding nnEMF and daily AM/PM sunlight is key, and we will embark on our healing journey at the 12th latitude in a few days. But I have no illusion that this will take a while and constant work is needed. The problem is that it's very hard to get compliance and corporation out of an out-of-control-angry teenager who thinks I have gone mad and everything is b.s. He bought into this, sort of, but then when he rapidly cycles into a mixed state of anger again, it's mental chaos. I need to stay steady and calm, and the way my brain works is that if I understand better what is happening at the very molecular level, then I can react better and steer the ship better.

P.S. Lithium is my go-to medicine and it does work. I am very unsure about Lamotrigine that he also takes (a sodium channel blocker). Without it, it does seem like he slips into depression and sleepiness. But when I re-start Lamotrigine, it seems he first gets more agitated/angry and it eventually stabilizes. I could not get rid of Lamotrigine because of this see-saw effect and because I have no idea whether it is a good stop-gap measure or fundamentally counter productive? Jack and another expert told me about the Calcium channel, but Lamotrigine slows down the sodium Chanel. So I am confused.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-05T01:38:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biological-mechanism-of-agitated-anger-and-0-life-zest.29108/post-336639>

Unhinged anger is due a lack of melanin in the mitochondria of the amygdala. The vagus nerve for primates became a phylogenetic structure that supported their ability to recognize emotions to promoting safety and survival in a chaotic environment. Kids and young adults who are unmyelinated until 28 lack this ability. This is why parents need to be parents and not friends to young silly talking monkeys who have serious nnEMF problems. Their brains are unprotected and yours is. This is why environment mitigation helps.

The Vagus Rx for kids also makes a difference. [https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/water-exercise-rx-jack-kruse/](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/water-exercise-rx-jack-kruse/)

There is no chance that I can have him see sunrise and have enough exposure to sun in Chicago, and the water RX and breathing exercises without intense sunlight and without nnEMF mitigation would be futile in my current environment, especially as he is not complying. Had I started this program when he was 10 I could have done this with less resistance and easier. Now he is physically stronger and taller than me, angrier and more out of control, and it requires more mental and physical strength to make him comply. Can't wait to come down. I just pray that he does not have a manic episode the day of flying and that I can somehow travel without an incidence.

### drsongihan — 2024-07-05T08:50:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biological-mechanism-of-agitated-anger-and-0-life-zest.29108/post-336647>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=336646)
>
> Hot garbage. Very dangerous drug whose literature is a mixed bag. SSRI use in kids with behavior issues is a recipe for disaster
>
>
> ### [Anger with murderous impulse induced by lamotrigine - PubMed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/24736088/)
>
> Use of LTG for epilepsy in intellectually disabled patients was reported to be associated with onset or exacerbation of aggressive or violent behavior. The two cases would suggest that LTG may cause anger so severe as to be accompanied with murderous impulse when administered to patients with...
>
>  pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

Incredible. Psychiatrist after psychiatrist would sell me this as the "best" mood stabilizer, and made it sound like they knew what Lamotrigine is doing. Lamotrigine was the last medicine standing because of that, but I already had observed that it has a duality of effect and was suspicious. The paper you sent at least shows two case studies, and is hence much more insightful. This Nature paper from May 27 2024 ([https://www.nature.com/articles/s41398-024-02944-6](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41398-024-02944-6)) basically outs that psychiatrists know literally nothing about Lamotrigine, and yet they prescribe it right and left as the safest option. It's a joke: a randomized trial with 31 healthy people to figure out the effect of Lamotrigine, without any attempt at a mechanistic understanding published in 2024 in Nature Translational Psychiatry. Why don't we do randomized trial to improve the engine of an airplane or to uncover the impact of lime on the tastiness of food, but since its randomized, we need to scramble the food choices -- pancake, breakfast egg, fish, desert .....

The psychiatrist would first give SSRI, then SNRIs -- both disasters for bipolar depression. One even insisted on stimulants -- dangerous. Every single one of these made things worse. Nobody tells you (except for 1 doctor) that SSRIs/SNRIs flip people with bipolar AND that the withdrawal symptoms are horrible for anybody. People hence take antidepressants for years because as soon as they try to get off it, they feel worse. The pharmaceutical company hooked them up for life! Zoloft, Celexa, Lexapro ... these are all horrific drugs that have dual and triple actions, they down regulate the production of key neurotransmitters, while also activating sigma-1, LSD and other receptors, again signaling to our body to down regulate these neurochemicals. Now, while the one doctor was better than anybody else just by knowing that each SSRI has a different combinatoric dual actions, I was then left wondering why do we have LSD receptors that Zoloft apparently activates if LSD is a synthetic drug? Does our body make something like LSD the same way it makes opioids??!!!

The next category is antipsychotics and mood stabilizers. Most of them cause weight gain, in people who already have issues with metabolism, this puts them on a road to destruction. Relative to that, Lamotrigine is sold as the solid "wonder" drug since it does not cause weight gain and does not suck the life out of you. Lamotrigine seems to mess with the conduction / polarization of mitochondrial membranes, somehow, and in some people it acts like a damper or filter, in others it dumbs and numbs people down, and in yet other cases it causes a short.

Only one doctor--the best one because he told me from day-1 that we don't know enough--put things together that there is bipolar in play and put my son on Lithium, the only medicine that actually helped. I unilaterally stopped everything else, but was holding off quitting Lamotrigine. Once in ES I plan on slowly titrating off.

### drsongihan — 2024-07-15T03:47:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biological-mechanism-of-agitated-anger-and-0-life-zest.29108/post-337179>

My 14yo son and I are now 1 week in El Salvador. The beginning was rough. Thanks to Jack McKenna, MT and India for helping me with so much positivity and good information.

The beginning was rough NOT because ES is rough or I had a hard time seeing the beauty of this country or did not have something I need, but because my son went on a "strike" and refused to embrace the new environment. This is despite him agreeing to this travel initially. He did not come out of his room for 3 days, and did not eat for 72 hours, but drank plenty. Had anger outbreaks, refused to see the sun or go to the beach.
Eventually he came out, first like 2-3 hours, shut down again for a whole day, then next 3-4 hours, shut down again for almost a whole day, came out for 1-2 hours, etc, like up and down, but with the wave clearly dampening.

Today he spent ALL DAY in the sun or outside from 8.30am till 6.30pm with only a 1-2 hour break. I can see his mood stabilizing, his voice and energy stabilizing, and he even started to make jokes. Crazy.

Just yesterday evening I was worried because he had again not eaten for 48 hours, slept for something like 30 hours, and still could barely eat 1/2 of his burger. I noticed that his reaction to sunlight, even in Chicago, initially was that 1-2 hours of exposure made him sleep like crazy. In El Salvador, he first fell into a 4 day sleep "comma". His reaction to sunlight initially was like being jumped way too strong power, but it seems like today, he could finally hold it for 8 hours without too much incidence.

I registered for a consult with Jack, so will ask him lots of concrete question, but in this forum I was wondering whether any of you are familiar with what this pattern of sleeping excessively and not eating for 48 to 72 hours mean? My interpretation is that the little bit of sunlight started to generate melanin and he was sleeping to generate more that he desperately needs, and perhaps therefore his Circadian clock was in "sleep" mode for days?

Not specifically no, it's more a way of thinking/approach to arrive at the answers rather than a specific answer.

If it's of any use, I've listened to talks in the past regarding the circadian link with bipolar. For example, some psychiatrists have noticed that blue-light blocking glasses can stop mania in it's tracks in patients who try it and some wear them for hours or days to get a handle on the manic episode. Conversely, the shorter days have the opposite effects with depressive episodes. I think it was Thomas Wehr who had a patient with rapid cycling and he exposed him to 12 hours light/dark cycles for a year and the patient went into remission- although this patient was very unwell and institutionalised and there was more leeway with these treatments as it was several decades ago. There is also the solar flux phenomenon where mania incidence increases in correlation with the coming of Spring and the longer days.

I know Jack has blogs on bipolar- I'm sure you've read them. If I were to work backwards based on clinical observations and Jack's blogs- I would be thinking- blue light and circadian rhythm biology is a big factor. I think the cryptochromes are key (possibly the opsins) but the blue light magnet cells are so sensitive to frequencies, they are meant for blue light and the earth's natural vibrations - not the junk EMF we have introduced. What is more, I think their positioning in the eye makes them vulnerable to electrochemical insults that modern environments are full of. If these cells are damaged or destroyed, perhaps other areas of the brain or nervous system change their dopamine signalling, because insufficient blue light = tyrosine will not become dopamine, so if the apparatus is damaged, the sunlight isn't having the same effects.

Travel is known to throw off circadian rhythm- there is research on air travel and the increased risk of depression and psychosis and other psychiatric manifestations. Jeg lag is common. But if the clocks are already damaged..

I really do think the above books will help you and your boy. Nature is the key, copy nature, everything has vibration and a vibrational footprint- even objects we consider inanimate, life force is a real concept and it needs replenishing. Remove all the negatives from the environment- tech is a huge one. Also any tech with facial recognition technology (a lot of the newer smartphones)- get rid of them- they are pulsing IR into the delicate lens of the eye every 10-12 seconds and it's damaging the proteins and water structure because we're not meant to receive IR in that way. The covid jabs are also driving photoreceptor/magnetoreceptor damage, but the recovery principles remain the same.

I hope you keep searching and I think what you're doing to help your son is wonderful. Maybe also look into sourcing primary water/deep aquifer spring water for him if you can find some in the area you've moved to. Good luck!

Blue light toxicity. I get the same way. Strong sunlight and all I want to do is sleep. Jack has told me on a number of occasions about how badly blue light toxic I am.
Initially when I went to the tropics I started off in Panama, but after lunch I would be stuck to the hammock and almost fall asleep in there. It improved after about 5 -6 weeks and I would go and be in the pool all afternoon until about 5pm and then would head back to make supper.

As for the mechanism that I'm not completely sure about. However I can't go without eating

A point was made in the latest podcast that ATP production is turned off under UV light
Blue light elevates glucose levels
It may be that the Glucagon gets to work under the red light of the sun and if you're in that state why do you need to eat ? Less insulin is being released.
I certainly found after a few months at the tropics that by night time I didn't need too much to eat. Especially towards the end of the day.

But I'd fill up in the morning and usually go through a whole day till the evening when I was in El Salvador in the fourth month

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-05T17:22:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-redox-potential-stolen-land-confiscation-usa-style.29110/post-336661>

This policy is about land confiscation that will lead to bad food which will be used to control the population by lowering mtDNA thermodyanmics while blocking the sun. It is all about population control and obedience by land confiscation. This is imperilism 2.0 brought by the WEF/Royal Family/Papacy/Tyrants in the Uniparty. People forget what happened in the 1972 election after the 1968 election.

Food inflation became a real issue to Nixon's re-election. Remember the 1973 Oil shock? So what did they do? At the time the US dept of agriculture had around 75K employees while Farmers numbered over 6 million Americans. Nixon introduced farm subsidies to grow wheat and corn and this lowered the food quality while reducing prices.

Nixon made good on his promises to lower food costs.

He fostered carbs to replace animals fats. He then used the legislative branch to codify the centrlaized idea in the USDA dietary guidelines in 1974 with another idiot politician George McGovern. The code was Titled Dietary Goals for the United States, but also known as the "McGovern Report." This is how government was used to lower prices by lowering quality while stealing farm land by lowering the profits of farmers and forcing them off their land. Nixon and McGovern sold us the centralized idea suggested that Americans eat less fat, less cholesterol, less refined and processed sugars, and more complex carbohydrates and fiber.

This is the same repackaged idea the WEF is using right now in the EU to steal EU farmers land while destroying their monetary values in land. Many people I support today (RFK Jr) think net net Nixon was good on the environment because of these policies but you need to see how they were used in totalilty against the government.

I disagree with Bobby on this because of science. Nixon was told in 1968 to pull off this audacious plan he would need to help corporations help. Their scientists told his administration to pull this off you need to clean up the water and environment to retool farms to make shit food to control the population via food/photosynthesis.

So what did Nixon do?

The 1960s and 1970s was a time of broad popular concern about the health of the environment. People forget Nixon was the VP of Eisenhower for 8 years. He was also very connected to LBJ by corporations both supported in the land business in Texas and California in the 50s-60s. Nixon had ties to Reagan who was the governor of California who agreed not to run for President in 1968 once LBJ quit. LBJ was dogged by Vietnam so Nixon came in and he decided to focus in on the domestic economy and he went to work on America's bread basket via land management and environmental changes.

This was expressed most prominently in the first Earth Day demonstration in 1970 during his administration. These idea were good in intent and nefarious in subsequent actions because they led to the creation of a national framework of environmental policies during that changed the way SCS put conservation on the ground. The National Environmental Policy Act (P.L. 91-190), signed into law in 1970 by President Richard Nixon, required federal agencies to evaluate and report on the environmental impacts of their activities. Water quality and non-point source pollution became important areas of concern with passage the Federal Water Pollution Control Amendments (P.L. 92-500) in 1972 and the Clean Water Act (P.L. 95-217) in 1977.

The protection of wetlands emerged as critical issue with SCS participation in the Water Bank program, which provided incentives to landowners to protect wetland habitat. This gave the government control over this asset and they could sell it out via agency regulation.

**How did this happen? The 1984 Chevron Deference decision.**

This gave Federal agencies the power to circumvent the constitution and leave final decision making up to agencies. This is how the land & water was stolen by the government. Many in the government, including the Kennedy clan helped these policies. During the 1970s of Nixon/Ford/Carter/Reagan, SCS also gained greater authority to monitor and assess the nation’s natural resource base. Congress authorized the National Resources Inventory (NRI) in the Rural Development Act of 1972 (P.L. 92-419) to better understand the implications of land use changes for soil erosion. The Soil and Water Resources Conservation Act of 1977 (P.L. 95-192) extended this authority and required USDA to regularly report to Congress on the condition of the soil and water resources on non-federal lands as part of a process for developing more effective conservation policies and laws.

All of these laws set the stage for corporate take overs once Federal Agencies were granted power under Chevron Deference. This is how America chronic disease epidemic germinated. Pun intended.

The farm crisis of the 1980s created an opening for the implementation of innovative conservation policies developed as part of the Resource Conservation Act (RCA) process. THIS IS WHEN CHEVRON DEFERENCE HITS THE USA. You must know your history to see how land and food were stolen. Hi, I am from the government and I am hear to help. LOL Remember how it started: “Land must be nurtured; not plundered and wasted.” – Hugh Hammond Bennett. the NRCS’ first chief. Those who do not understand history are doomed to repeat it. What happens next on the time line? The Food Security Act of 1985 (P.L. 99-198), with its Sodbuster, Swampbuster, and Highly Erodible Lands provisions, made conservation a prerequisite for participation in USDA programs.

It also established the Conservation Reserve Program (CRP) to provide rental payments to farmers for putting cropland into grass or trees. Another important development during this period was the widespread adoption of conservation tillage practices, which has led to a significant reduction in soil erosion.

**Why did they do this?
**
**Because growing wheat and corn destroys the soil while creating shit food to dumb America down to accept their bullshit centralized policies. Nixon not only began the Fiat money shocks he ushered in the chronic disease epidemic by helping Big Food and Big Agra steal American farmers land.** SCS working with its partners played a significant role working to administer these programs and develop the necessary tools and technology to make these conservation innovations possible = Chevron Deference now could be fully used to complete the transfer of wealth and time to the Federal government and away from the We The People. Nixon expanded government and the US Dept of Agriculture over the next 20 yrs grew to 450K employees while American Farmers fell to 3 million where it is today. You are fucking clueless if you do not know the hostory of centralized American land policy and land confiscation and why it was done.

What did i say to Bobby Kennedy during the Tetragrammaton podcast? What is the difference between Fact and Fiction? Only Fiction has to make sense. The truth behind what we are facing is that the bigger the government gets the worse it is for people. Stop letting people lie to you.

# Thread: Laptop screen balancing device

### Kendall Toerner — 2024-07-06T02:53:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-screen-balancing-device.29112/post-336720>

I'm an inventor and designer and have been wanting to shift my work toward meaningful electronics. As a result I am developing a device for balancing the blue light of screens with red (NIR). It mounts to the top of your laptop or computer monitor.

Am I allowed to post it / share here? Even suggestions and thoughts would be great. Using the profits to help promote educating the masses on light and environment.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-07-08T01:29:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-screen-balancing-device.29112/post-336812>

### [http://www.phasmlight.com](http://www.phasmlight.com) -​

### Technical Specs​

-   Size: 2.6″h x 2.6″w x 1.6″d
-   Weight: 3.2oz
-   Battery: Built-in rechargeable battery
-   Charge and continuous power uses a USB-C cable
-   Battery Recharge Time: from empty about 2 hours
-   Battery Life: about 1-28 hours depending on settings (see Directions Tab for battery life estimates based on settings)
-   Infrared frequency: 850nm
-   Ultraviolet frequency: 395-410nm
-   Distance: IR reaches over 75 feet depending on camera settings (we’re still testing this based off of initial test results with our POV cam)

### Anthony Smith — 2024-07-08T01:37:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-screen-balancing-device.29112/post-336813>

> [Kendall Toerner said:](/goto/post?id=336721)
>
> [View attachment 28519](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/28519/)

Hi Kendall, how far along in development are you? Could you make it with NIR & UV as well as the visible red? Having used the one I have shown which is designed for video/photo work, I would prefer your low profile.

Thanks for that. Great portable solution. When i'm unable to work outside due to weather - I run red led strip lights ~ 660nm and in the process of adding NIR ~ 850nm + some purple UV. I would set this up as background lighting.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-07-08T02:03:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-screen-balancing-device.29112/post-336816>

When I can’t be outside (it is 106ºF outside right now) In my main set up right behind my screen I have a 100Watt UV light, 2x 500Watt 850nm lights and a full spectrum aquarium light above my head. Be sure to measure for electric fields if the lights are not grounded.

### Kendall Toerner — 2024-07-08T16:51:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-screen-balancing-device.29112/post-336828>

> Interesting interesting. How do you know it’s giving off the right light balance?

I'm measuring the output at the user's average face distance from the screen to use the proper LED/lens combo

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=336809)
>
> Great idea. Have you checked the balance with a spectrometer? Also what wave length is the UV?

Haven't finalized yet but was thinking:

UV
280 nm and 340 nm

Visible
660 nm

IR
830 nm
850 nm

I want the user to be able to turn any of them off or on if desired

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=336813)
>
> Hi Kendall, how far along in development are you? Could you make it with NIR & UV as well as the visible red? Having used the one I have shown which is designed for video/photo work, I would prefer your low profile.

For sure I was thinking just invisible frequencies at first but if the visible can be turned on or off I think it's a nice addition.

Based on the lenses I'm testing with I should be able to get it a little lower profile than shown.

Currently have a working prototype but looking for a manufacturing vendor who can give a good price on the machined body and silicone mount.

Main question at this point is if I can get away with using just the USB C port as power (15 W) since the purpose is just to balance the blue light from the computer screen with IR and add in the lack of UV for aromatic absorption mainly for dopamine in an indoor context

### petrichor — 2024-09-03T21:44:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-screen-balancing-device.29112/post-339213>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=336816)

> When I can’t be outside (it is 106ºF outside right now) In my main set up right behind my screen I have a 100Watt UV light, 2x 500Watt 850nm lights and a full spectrum aquarium light above my head. Be sure to measure for electric fields if the lights are not grounded.

What's the reason for the aquarium light? Also, do you have any concerns about looking directly at the phasm light for extended periods while at the computer?

### Anthony Smith — 2024-09-04T06:03:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-screen-balancing-device.29112/post-339221>

> [petrichor said:](/goto/post?id=339213)
>
> What's the reason for the aquarium light? Also, do you have any concerns about looking directly at the phasm light for extended periods while at the computer?

Flowers, fish and reptiles need full spectrum light to live . . . So do we. There are lots of lights for critters, not so many for humans. Make like the sphinx outside and a “critter” inside. Outside of course is the best.

### Mat.I — 2024-09-04T06:48:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-screen-balancing-device.29112/post-339223>

Also, worth noting that full spectrum especially for LED's may not always be true full spectrum unless they add NIR. Thing to watch out for are the bulbs marketed as full spectrum, but are only "full-visible" spectrum. Anything LED unless specifically engineered with a continuous spectrum will have a very messy spectra. The best light source which is very rich in IR (with a continuous spectrum) is an incandescent bulb. I'm in AUS and can still get these online.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-09-04T08:08:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-screen-balancing-device.29112/post-339224>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=339223)
>
> Also, worth noting that full spectrum especially for LED's may not always be true full spectrum unless they add NIR. Thing to watch out for are the bulbs marketed as full spectrum, but are only "full-visible" spectrum. Anything LED unless specifically engineered with a continuous spectrum will have a very messy spectra. The best light source which is very rich in IR (with a continuous spectrum) is an incandescent bulb. I'm in AUS and can still get these online.

Check out the lights for plants, fish and especially reptiles . . . they usually include NIR and UV.

### Mat.I — 2024-09-04T09:50:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-screen-balancing-device.29112/post-339227>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=339224)
>
> Check out the lights for plants, fish and especially reptiles . . . they usually include NIR and UV.

Hi Anthony,
They may include it in some, however is a very messy spectra that drops heavily and does not peak in the NIR range. E.g. incandescent peak in the NIR range and would consist of ~ 90% infrared with continuous spectrum with a peak ~ 1 micron or 1000nm etc. Research the spectrum of incandescent and compare with others. I found some examples of advertised full spectrum grow lights below. Also a room that is well illuminated by incandescent bulbs could deliver a reasonable dose of red/NIR which is consistent with exposure levels referenced by PBM experts such as Hamblin and others in the PBM space.

Incandescent are the way to go by far (in the past i was blinded by the majority of full spectrum LED marketing bullshit), however eyes are wide open now. Working on a project at the moment.. Just need to see if I get my delivery/import of incandescent bulbs without being blocked...

### petrichor — 2024-09-04T19:24:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-screen-balancing-device.29112/post-339253>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=339221)
>
> Flowers, fish and reptiles need full spectrum light to live . . . So do we. There are lots of lights for critters, not so many for humans. Make like the sphinx outside and a “critter” inside. Outside of course is the best.

Oh I am aware of that, I was just curious why specifically an aquarium light. There are many full spectrum options.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-09-04T21:34:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-screen-balancing-device.29112/post-339259>

> [petrichor said:](/goto/post?id=339253)
>
> Oh I am aware of that, I was just curious why specifically an aquarium light. There are many full spectrum options.

My wife loves fish and plants so we have several aquarium lights and grow lights . . .
In my experience anything “medical”, full spectrum etc for humans are more expensive and only full “visible” spectrum.
Lights for fish, plants and especially reptiles include more UV and NIR. Just a cheeper way of getting there - because incandescent lights can be hard to get (almost impossible in CA).
42% of natural daylight is IR, 5% UVA, 1% UVB (depending on time of day and location).
This is why when inside, especially when in front of a screen I often have an aquarium full spectrum light, a UV light and a NIR (850nm) light close by. Lots of light, little to no heat.

### eve001 — 2024-09-06T15:03:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-screen-balancing-device.29112/post-339326>

Interesting and complex. Love the elegant design.

And I recall these interviews on light with light expert Dr. Alexander Wunsch at Bio360. (German)

Could one place a red light above the computer screen to mitigate the blue light peak? the host asks.
And Dr. Wunsch answers that the source location/angle of the bad blue light and of the red lights above are so different that our sensitive organisms/eyes can notice it and that is not an accurate substitute for healthy light with the frequencies in their natural position respective of eachother.

Dr. Wunsch does seem very meticulous and seems to have a great sense of precision.
He's the one from the aromatic aminoacid slides Dr. kruse further elaborates on.

Is his objection too extreme? It makes me think of nudism.
Clothes interfere with the daylight on some parts of our skin but not elsewhere Is that confusing our non-visual photo-receptors?

If it is, that would not makes us all go naked all the time.
So if there's a place for clothing there could be a place for unopposed blue LED compensating light solutions.
We'd need precize studies to see what the impact is, or trust our senses/guts.

I spent lots of money on LEDS without blue and green and now wonder if that was a good investment.

To design ways to easily create outdoors offices could also be a great path of innovation. = bring PC out, create outdoors working spaces solutions low in nnEMF, but with the minimally necessary electricity, with grounding, daylight, protection of wind/rain/cold/nnEMF.
Or to look for ways to bring the daylight in, in new ways of building.
Mirrors? Do mirrors change light quality?

### petrichor — 2024-09-06T21:08:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-screen-balancing-device.29112/post-339350>

>
> Dr. Wunsch does seem very meticulous and seems to have a great sense of precision.

I wonder what is worse though, having it present but imperfect, or not having it at all? I would think the latter...

### Richard Watson — 2024-07-06T08:52:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pillilps-vintage-tanning-and-ir-light.29113/post-336726>

I bought this light on marketplace for $20, Ive tried to find info on what the IR spectrum is on line with no luck, I know its made in the 70's and was made in Holland and is called Ultraphilips de Luxe. Anyone help me out here...

### Richard Watson — 2024-08-17T00:24:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pillilps-vintage-tanning-and-ir-light.29113/post-338521>

Ive still not managed to learn whats the system that IR light is. I asked Phillips via email but never heard back.
The IR on this unit has been amazing. Three years ago I slipped on wet grass and ripped off three back tendons off the hip, I had them reattached two weeks later. The last year the hip was becoming quite sore on walking, but using this light twice a day for half hour has improved the hip to about 95%. This light throws out a lot more heat than LED's.
Being old I worried about how long this light will last and therefor keen I seeing if I can find something similar.
Even if you dont know anything about this unit, can anyone maybe have a suggestion where to hunt up info.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-08-17T01:21:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pillilps-vintage-tanning-and-ir-light.29113/post-338523>

Can’t find any specs on it. I did notice it has IR and UV.
You say it gets hot on the IR setting. If it gets very hot it is not NIR but far infrared 1000-3000nm
850nm NIR will get warm, so if it gets hot then it is a higher frequency.

### Pratima — 2024-08-17T08:54:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pillilps-vintage-tanning-and-ir-light.29113/post-338536>

Hi Richard. The details do not specify the range, but it does say that it is FIR (far infrared), not NIR (near infrared). I have a frozen shoulder currently and purchased this [https://infraredlights.co.nz/collec...d-infrared-red-light-therapy-300-with-stand-1](https://infraredlights.co.nz/collections/introducing-the-new-lightforce-classic-range/products/copy-of-lightforce-pro-led-infrared-red-light-therapy-300-with-stand-1)

### Richard Watson — 2024-08-17T09:18:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pillilps-vintage-tanning-and-ir-light.29113/post-338537>

I'm in Canterbury Pratima.
I have a LED 670nm light that I use most days for my eyes.
wonder if I would have still achieved the amazing results with NIR

### Anthony Smith — 2024-08-17T19:09:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pillilps-vintage-tanning-and-ir-light.29113/post-338550>

> [Richard Watson said:](/goto/post?id=338537)
>
> I'm in Canterbury Pratima.
> I have a LED 670nm light that I use most days for my eyes.
> wonder if I would have still achieved the amazing results with NIR

660nm light is good for skin issues, scars etc. it goes in 2-3mm. 850nm NIR light goes in 70mm+/-, it does not get hot so does not cause inflammation, in fact helps reduce it. I use mine 2 - 3x per day for 15 mins because I read that is optimal to stimulate the mitochondria and longer times can reverse this. I am not a medical person, there may be new data on all this I am not aware of but this works for me. Most infrared security lights are 850nm.

# Thread: How to protect eyes against wind and bugs without using sunglasses?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-protect-eyes-against-wind-and-bugs-without-using-sunglasses.29114/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Olivert — 2024-07-06T10:37:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-protect-eyes-against-wind-and-bugs-without-using-sunglasses.29114/post-336728>

I guess answer to many of you might be obvious but I am uncertain on how to go about this.

When I am biking out I need to use some glasses that protect my eyes against wind and bugs but don’t block sunlight (for reasons obvious to everyone here).

Now, to me one obvious solution that occurred to me here is to just use blue light blocking glasses. One concern there is that red/orange ones do obscure vision a bit and so it is not the safest option.

Alternative could be using daytime blue light blockers. Here however is where I grow bit uncertain whether it is a good idea because: do I really want to block natural blue light from the sun when I am exercising outside?

Or is there any other type of glasses or protection I could use that wouldn’t block any spectrum of light from sun? Or am I missing something here and are there different ideas for eye protection against wind and bugs while still getting full light spectrum from the sun?

Thanks for any ideas here as I am perplexed whether to crash on a bike or ruin my longterm health with sunglasses

# Thread: Steve's optimal journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steves-optimal-journal.29115/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Steve Z — 2024-07-06T22:47:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steves-optimal-journal.29115/post-336764>

History on maternal side:

Grandma: No major health issues most of her life. Died from lung cancer at 78, after around 60 years of smoking. Wore glasses. Mostly an indoor dweller. Was overweight but not obese. Had a brain issue with surgery around age 60. Lived in California until mid 1960’s, then moved to Oregon.

Mother: Also a smoker. Said she was allergic to seafood, got sick on it once I recall as a young child. Mostly an indoor dweller but enjoyed working in the yard. Wore glasses. Was overweight but not obese. She developed rheumatoid arthritis in her late 30’s. Mom passed away at 49 due to iatrogenesis (she got aplastic anemia years after taking methotrexate which built to a toxic level in her system). Her RA was never under control, it just ate away at her slowly, and was hard for me to witness. My mom grew up in California, then left in the early 1970’s to move to Oregon.

My history: born with blue eyes and blonde hair. Still have blue eyes but hair got darker (brown) by 5th grade. I grew up saying I was ‘light sensitive’, and would have a hard time looking at say a white car on a sunny summer day. I burned fairly easy. My vision was good as a kid but by middle school I was very near sighted. Wore contacts and occasionally glasses from roughly 8th grade until my 30’s. I did lasik at 32, not knowing any better. I have lived at the 44th latitude essentially all of my life. I’m 54 now and since age 25 have worked in a dispatch center – shiftwork, overtime, working indoors, in front of a lot of computer screens. By age 49, after promoting and spending the prior 6 years on night shift I felt broken. I was broken. However, my labs told my standard doctor things were fine. I had the energy of an 80 year old man, never felt rested even after a long sleep, had long term depression, brain fog, and never felt good.

Starting around 2018, I started listening to a lot of podcasts looking for answers and came across Dr. Kruse via Ben Greenfield. There was something about what Dr. Kruse was saying. He sounded crazy but I just felt he knew what I needed – call it entanglement - so I kept listening. And reading a lot. I was in the $5/month club from 2020-2022, then became a gold club member in late 2022. I have easily spent 1000 hours listening to Dr. Kruse podcasts, reading his blogs, and listening to the private recordings.

Since 2018-ish I have made major progress but am still not optimal – likely tied to redox. I have exited a very toxic marriage, have no signs of depression for 4+ years, bought and religiously use blue blockers at night, spend regular time outside in the sun, don’t miss a sunrise (even if just on a break at work), have embraced the cold, bought a magnetico, turn the breaker to my bedroom off at night. I started taking sun trips during winter last year (first year it was financially possible) and plan to increase that going forward. I've been on dayshift since 2018, but often come in to work at 3am or work until 10pm in the winter.

In 2023 I bought a junker house trying to re-establish myself financially. It was more of a junker than I realized, so I spent a lot less time outside, more blue light stress, and had more stress in general than planned this last year. It is in the finishing phase now, however my wellness declined during this period, plus I gained some weight. The experience was a learning opportunity for where my boundaries are health wise to at least not loose ground.

I know I still have major blue light toxicity from my job. There is a lot of nnEMF near my work per a tri-meter. Population density isn’t too bad where I live but not great either (around 250K people in the metro area).

My epi-retinal membrane detached 2 years ago. My eye doctor hypothesized it was from weight lifting. I did not have the surgery to remove it, because they remove your vitreous fluid and you later get cataracts. Seemed like a no-brainer to say hell no to that. I no longer feel anxiety (was quite bad initially) and while vision in that eye has improved there are blurry areas in my visual field still. I believe my eye doctor did OCT scans as part of his evaluation.

I am not on any medications and don't have any diagnosis, and want to improve. I know I need more sun, am getting that as I can, plus am planning sun trips this winter. I need a long vacation in the tropics or playa del carmen area. Would love to find mito minded people near by (Oregon).

Labs in late 2022 showed elevated LDL. The doctor talked about statins (which is a hell no from me). I haven't seen them since and they are no longer with the practice. Haven't done labs since.

### Steve Z — 2024-12-09T17:40:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steves-optimal-journal.29115/post-343583>

For some reason my right knee has gone from feeling slightly off, to occasionally painful to fairly painful over the last 6 months. I have done a good amount of CT during this time, plus get regular sun and red light exposure, and yet the pain/problem has just slowly increased. My knee was not obviously swollen.

There was no injury or event to start this process, and no history of problems with the knee. The knee did not get worse if I walked numerous miles on it. Walking was not painful in general but getting up and starting to move took a moment. My walking stride was a bit off as I tried to compensate.

I hadn't thought much about it until it became fairly painful. That's when it dawned on me how odd it seemed - no trigger event and no history of issues. Doing regular CT it seemed inflammation would be reasonably in chest, and yet this problem persisted and got worse. The worry in the back of my head tied back to my mom having RA.

I spent a week in the Florida panhandle last month and hoped the relaxation would help. It did but not very much. Then I tried DMSO and RevGenetics TetraCircumin plus spent about a weeks' worth of nights massaging the area. And now the pain is still there but only 20% of what it was.

My life stress has been way too high for about 12-18 months, so maybe that's is a root cause. That issue will be resolved in another month or two.

Has anyone else had something similar happen?

### Bodycoach — 2024-12-11T13:36:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steves-optimal-journal.29115/post-343692>

How did you apply the DMSO?

### Steve Z — 2024-12-15T06:36:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steves-optimal-journal.29115/post-343917>

> [Bodycoach said:](/goto/post?id=343692)
>
> How did you apply the DMSO?

Topically around the knee. The container says its 90% DMSO in an aloe vera gel.

### Steve Z — 2024-12-25T19:48:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steves-optimal-journal.29115/post-344344>

Update on the knee issue. After paying a lot more attention to this, and going to a chiropractor I know but haven't seen since 2017, it turns out my hips were rotated and locked into place. I began to realize the pain wasn't really in my knee joint, it was the connective tissue running on either side of my knee cap that was problematic.

The adjustment helped a lot. Right after I was able to walk normally. As the day progressed my body kept trying to go back to where it had been. Lots of stretching. Today is was back where it had been. More stretching, massage and foam roller work. Looks like this will take some work to resolve. But at least I seem to have a clear picture of the cause.

### Steve Z — 2025-03-29T20:27:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steves-optimal-journal.29115/post-349593>

My hip/back/leg issue continues to wax and wane the last 3-4 months. The cycle seems to be: after a long stressful work week I'm tight again (too much sitting, long hours, stress), and after a long weekend of stretching and getting walks I'm better. Back and forth. Forth and back. I hope with spring here and my house rehab project winding down I can get back to basics. More time in morning sun, more afternoon & evening in sun, more walking/moderate exercise, etc.

In several recent podcasts Dr Kruse has talked about stress having only 1 pathway in the brain. When tight, my body creaks and pops a lot when stretching - way more than ever before. I wonder if I over-stressed myself this last year or so. Does stress dehydrate the body?

<edit> I no longer think DMSO had any benefit for this issue, outside of the placebo effect.

### Steve Z — 2025-10-27T10:56:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steves-optimal-journal.29115/post-361669>

A new chapter on the journey to optimal -

Something changed recently.

In my original post in this thread, I said I started making significant changes around 2018/2019, following Dr. Kruse's principals. I probably should have moved to the tropics for 6 months to a year, but it wasn't in the cards for me then (EDIT: I was not willing to leave my tween & teen kids). I stayed in my 45-degree north latitude and changed as much of my life as I could - seeing the sunrise, dark nights when I could, blocking blue at night when I couldn't, grounding, eating seafood, cold exposure, stress reduction, etc.. When I started, if I did everything right, I might get to the point at the end of the weekend that I would not feel horrible. I kept working the program and slowly improved but never got close to optimal.

For years, if I saw blue light at night without blockers on, I would feel a little ill. This was after 25 years in a job requiring long hours staring at a computer screen. I did cold exposure but didn't acclimate well to it. Dr. Kruse has said that smokers have a hard time cold adapting. I grew up in a cloud of secondhand smoke and theorize I had negative transgenerational epigenetic factors from my grandma and mom. Plus I imbibed some myself.

In late spring this year, I finished a house renovation and was able to slow down. I ran across something on the Forum about salt intake so boosted my salt intake, even putting it in my occasional morning coffee and noticed I didn't excessively urinate for the next several hours. I felt more energy. I spent many mornings sitting on my front porch getting direct morning sun for an hour or more. Lots of afternoons walks in the sun. Doing cold plunging outside in a grounded tub. Other than salt, I was doing the same things, just more of them than I was able to for the prior 2 years.

In the last month or so, I can suddenly tolerate unfiltered blue light exposure in the evening without feeling ill or queasy. Maybe my blue light toxicity has diminished enough to pass over some threshold? I didn't leave my blockers off as a result but there has been a definite shift for the better in this area. In that same time frame my cold adaptation has notably improved and it seems clear my hormone panel is improving (testing is upcoming). In the last 2 months, my forward strides are substantial, after years of being static. Those years of being stagnant were due in part to the house renovation. That took a lot of time, stress and energy, pulled me indoors more and seemed to be a long slow slideways walk, health-wise, but I have my own place again.

Is this a normal progression some people go through? Get somewhat better - stagnate - then suddenly jump ahead substantially. Or do you think it's just a mirror of my life and how much I was able to do the right things?

### Steve Z — 2026-01-05T03:19:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steves-optimal-journal.29115/post-364765>

Sharing some recent thinking -

Using first principal thinking, it appears I entered life with higher heteroplasmy. My mom and grandmother both smoked (indoors, the norm of that time). My mother had some sort of psychological break or blowup as a teen, developed an autoimmune condition (RA) in her late 30's and passed away a little shy of 50. My mom and dad tried conceiving for years, adopted my sister and about a year later they were surprised to learn my mom was pregnant. Taken together, those factors paint a picture.

Adding to that, I had early circadian disruption from jobs in middle and high school. Post college I worked indoors staring at screens for long hours doing shiftwork. With light blue eyes I am more susceptible to blue light, and always felt my eyes burn from screens and would turn the brightness way down.

### Steve Z — 2026-01-05T03:29:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steves-optimal-journal.29115/post-364768>

Update on me -

I have been doing CT at colder temps than ever before - water temps in the high 30's or low 40's.
I spent many mornings last summer reading in the morning sun for 1+ hours and was outside a lot.

Additionally, some lab results came back -

Vitamin D was 40. Not where Dr. Jack recommends but better than prior tests. And that's from blood taken in December.
BUN/Creatinine ratio is still high (around 19).
Testosterone was decent, higher than any prior test but not where I want it.

These numbers provide some clarity on where I am, and on what I need to work on.

# Thread: WHY DOES LACK OF LIGHT ON SKIN CAUSE GUT PROBLEMS?  ABSCOPAL EFFECT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-does-lack-of-light-on-skin-cause-gut-problems-abscopal-effect.29116/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 23
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-07T01:41:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-does-lack-of-light-on-skin-cause-gut-problems-abscopal-effect.29116/post-336770>

Your skin get irradiated by full spectum sunlight and the Vitamin D and NO signal maintain gut integrity. Microcirculatory alterations and mitochondrial dysfunction are considered to be the main pathophysiological mechanisms in organ dysfunction during critical illness like septic shock. Gut dysbiosis is gut shock without the global effects. This tells me NO loss is behind all diseases associated with dysbiosis When NO is decreased you also need to assume oxygenation is not optimized in your enterocytes. It should follow, that reduced oxygenation may lead to a disruption of gastric and intestinal barrier function. This allows direct translocation of bacteria and bacterial toxins into portal. venous and local lymphatic circulation. This does not allow for processing in the gut associated lymphatic system which needs to spend time with antigens in order to learn what and how the body will respond. This implies timing is critical to learning. It means magnetochemistry is involved involving altered ROS/RNS. NO is one of of those free radicals.

This second hit leads to a secondary, more refractory shock and additional multiorgan dysfunction and usually affects the brain because it gets 20% of cardiac output, but the portal circulation is innervated by the vagus nerve a cranial parasympathetic nerve. Thus, the goal of irradiating the eyes and skin is done to be the best redox strategy is to improve the redox power in the splanchnic blood supply ti improve mucosal oxygenation. This in turn, strengthens the electrical strength in the tight junctions of the iintestinal barrier improving integrity, by way of optimizing mitochondrial function.

How is the barrier electrified?

Why does the intestinal barrier have a a Vitamin /d receoptor if it never sees the sun?

The answer is Vitamin D comes from a non visual photoreceptor called cholesterol.

When human skin is exposed to sunlight where tons of cholesterol is located, it is the solar ultraviolet B photons with energies between 290 and 315 nm that are responsible for causing the photolysis of 7-dehydrocholesterol to previtamin D3. This photochemical process occurs in the plasma membrane of keratocytes; as a result, the thermodynamically unstable cis, cis isomer of previtamin D3 is rapidly transformed by a rearrangement of double bonds to form [vitamin D](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/medicine-and-dentistry/vitamin-d)3.

Approximately 50% of previtamin D3 is converted to vitamin D3 within 2 hours. The delay is important for the gut. See time plays another role in biology of mammals. As vitamin D3 is formed in the membrane, its open flexible structure is likely jettisoned from the plasma membrane into the [extracellular space](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/medicine-and-dentistry/extracellular-space). Once vitamin D3 enters the [extracellular fluid](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/medicine-and-dentistry/extracellular-fluid) space, it is attracted to the [vitamin D binding protein](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/medicine-and-dentistry/vitamin-d-binding-protein) (DBP) in the circulation, and thus enters the dermal capillary bed. Sunlight sulfates it to make it water soluable to get into tthe blood which is made of 93% water. There is meets with DBP to get to your gut to electrify it. This is how the abscopal effect of light on the skin improves gut integrity.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-07T02:12:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-does-lack-of-light-on-skin-cause-gut-problems-abscopal-effect.29116/post-336771>

Nature has more magics it uses in sunlight besides its power to electrify membranes. Melanin, Vitamin D, cholesterol, heparin, and nitric oxide all respond to the UV part of the spectrum when cells are irradiated by sunlight or biophotons powered by UV biophotons. Few people know that the UV, and blue green part of the spectrum work in unison to very locally vasodilate blood vessels in the skin and the gut. I showed people the the melanopsin 2014 paper 1000's of times that showed blue light is capable of dialating blood vessels but not one person ask me about the AMO physics of the interaction. This area interested me as a neurosurgeon because I was looking for a mechanism to explain the queer things that happen in brain bleeds called subarachnoid hemorrhages. People who get cerebral aneurysmal bleeding always develop leaky gut and have gut integrity issues. It is also behind why we no longer needed Vitamin C as a silly talking primate. I have always felt that a lack of light on the skin was somehow operating in unison is behind most of the cerebrovascular pathologies & gut integrity issues that neurosurgeons treat.

It took me ten years to figure it all out, but altered light signaling from the eye & skin is why AVMs and aneurysms form and rupture in the brain. It is also why the gut loses its mucosal integrity. Both are linled to a loss of electrified power from a lack of mitochondrial power in cells in those tissues. It is also why some people develop SAH in the brain with minor trauma and why other develop ulcers from bacterial invasion of dysbiosis.

UV light induces and abscopal effect in the skin on the brain and gut to cause these problems. That was my eureka insight some 20 years ago.

They are all symptoms of low redox power in an organ. Nitric oxide (NO) is a well-known “gasotransmitter” in the literature because a Nobel Prize was given for its discovery in 1992. It is a gas that acts focally and locally when it is released from tissues and alters what mtDNA can do right there and now. It does not induce global effects. This tells you something about TIMING too. It acts RAPIDLY, not CHRONICALLY. Vitamin D actions act CHRONICALLY and not focally.

This tells you that NO signaling is likely behind many light induced chronic diseases. Electriified membranes absorb gas and turn it into many other biomolecules. Life figured this out 3.8 billion years ago as the first picture below shows. The second one shows you how NO blocks ATP creation and induces free radicals to make endogenous light inside of cells from oxygen. This is where magnetochemistry begins.

See the Decentralized medicine #5 blog for more.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-07T02:24:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-does-lack-of-light-on-skin-cause-gut-problems-abscopal-effect.29116/post-336772>

NO is a gaseous signaling molecule that has a unique ability to induce a relaxation of the smooth muscles in the arterial walls of the splanchnic circulation and this brings more oxygen to the gut surface. The decision to electrify or use magnetochemistry is made by the NO level in the gut. Recalll from my "Kruse for Dummies" lecture what I said about unique signals and how they work. The presence of NO lowers ATP and raises ROS. NO is a photoswitch for metabolism. ROS/RNS creates more light in the gut ATP electrifies the gut barrier. They are how information is transferred in biological systems by Claude Shannon's theory of information. Every one else is playing checkers looking at biochemical pathways while I am playing 4D chess because I understand how light operates in physical chemistry.

Without full spectum sunlight on the eye or skin, endothelial dysfunction and peripheral arterial damage should be EXPECTED by the decentralized clinician. So should gut dysbiosis. Rarely is it even considered. No one in medical curriculums looks at the data in BigHarma literature to see this data much less understand the clinical significance. A lack of NO production at our integument or eye surfaces link PAD directly to cardiovascular dysfunction. It also links glaucoma, cataracts, baldness, infertility eczema, roscea, and acne as well. The local effect in the effected tissue become generalized in the entire organ as the lack of NO production spreads among colonies of mitochodria in the tissue like a cancer. It progresses as the mal irradiation or malillumination gets worse under ALAN or nnEMF influence.

**ROS**

The common forms of intracellular ROS include superoxide anion radical (O2 •-), hydroxyl radical (•OH), hydrogen peroxide (H2O2), and lipid hydroperoxides (LOOH)

([Hayyan et al., 2016](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8898899/#B76); [Chakrabarty and Chandel 2021](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8898899/#B32)).

In the GI tract, the main sources of ROS and RNS are NADPH oxidase enzymes \[NOX/dual oxidase (DUOX)\], the mETC and NOSs.

Among them, O2 •- is the main oxidant molecule produced by adding an electron to molecular oxygen (O2), which is itself an atomic radical due to its electron configuration which gives it a **_paramagnetic signal_**.

In the presence of NO, a crucial mediator of GI physiology, O2 •− rapidly reacts with NO, thereby forming the highly reactive peroxynitrite (ONOO−)

in electron chain transport 1–2% of oxygen consumed during this process is converted to O2 •− when electrons leak out from the mETC and are aberrantly transferred to molecular O2 (West _et al._, [2011](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5446568/#bph13428-bib-0163)). Superoxide generated by mETC reacts with NO radicals formed by the interaction of NO with mitochondrial redox‐metal centers. The reactive ONOO‐ product can cause mitochondrial integrity disruption due to irreversible nitration of proteins, inactivation of enzymes and DNA damage.

In the mitochondrial matrix, O2 •− can react with SOD2, leading to the formation of diffusible H2O2 that regulates cellular functions by modulating the activity of redox‐sensitive transcription factors (Nrf2, HIF‐1 and NF‐κB), altering kinase activity (MAPK and IκB kinase‐β) and inhibiting phosphatases (PTEN and PTP1B)

HIF-! is the one that links directly to the clock genes. Cell hypoxia is controlled by hypoxia inducible factor (HIF-1). Light illumination on the skin controls the gut clocks. Poor sunlight, darkness, geoengineering will have a major effect on cellular hypoxia via lowered sunlight on the gut and its molecular clocks.

Do you know the link of HIF 1 to the sun? HIF-1 belongs to the same protein family as the light-inducible circadian core protein Period 2 (PER2) That is a link most do not realize. It is right here in this paper ---->(Liu et al., 2012).

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-07T04:57:22+0300

This is why peripheral arterial disease is always/often associated with light induced diseases via the abscopal effect. The link is the aberrant use fo the electromagentic spectrum to communicate to create NO to act as the photoswitch between electric and magneto chemistry.

RNS, include nitric oxide (NO), peroxynitrite, and nitroxylanion, is also an important class of oxidative bio-signal molecules. Nitric oxide synthases (NOS) are a group of enzymes that produce NO from L-arginine (L-Arg), O2 and NADPH. Skeletal muscle contains three NOS subtypes, which are neuronal NOS (nNOS), inducible NOS (iNOS) and endothelial NOS (eNOS)

Modern lighting and the AC power grid affect all RONS production but really destroy NO production. RF/microwave cell radiation impairs production of NO from arginine by eNOS. This, in turn, induces oxygen variations in the cytosol and matrix by altering blood pressure and partial pressures of oxygen in a cell to induce endothelial dysfunction in the target organ.

Mitochondria are intimately involved in importing nitrogen into tissues to create the substrates that eventually become NO when sunlight is present. Nitrogen substrates are not created from the direct synthesis by eNOS.

Nature provided the clue to me why humans got rid of Vitamin C for glutathione in this AMO physics dance. When Vitamin C is missing in subcutaneous tissues, glutathione become more reactive with locally produced NO. This mimics a radical pair or triad effect we see in avian compass navigation. Here was more evidence for me that magnetochemistry in humans being used and timing was more important than I'd ever imagined.

When glutathione and nitric oxide are powered by terrestrial sunlight this allowed humans to produce S-nitrosoglutathione (GSNO). I think this is why human primates lost the majority of their integumentary hair to absorb more solar light to power their new territory in the frontal regions and it added a ton of charge density to the gut. This is why is shortened and got smaller. The better the skin absorbed solar photons the more melanin from the hair follicle migrated to the interior and the more NO was made in the skin and gut.

This allowed the skin & gut to become a MASSIVE charge capacitor for the brain and heart by allowing the skin to become a depot station to store massive amounts of nitric oxide. This is why human immune T cells are so common in the skin and why leptin was placed in SQ fat. Other primates do not have these phenotypes even thought their genomes are close to identical.

This told me the primate body plan was changed using magnetochemistry and not genetic therapy all indiced by the timing of photoswitching in the skin. Solar light completely induced this evolutionary change in our primate cousins. Everything I believed was thrown out the window from medicial school that day 20 yrs ago. We never need genes to change this.

We used timing to change the metabolic pathways in the skin using hair loss and removal of Vitamin C from the radical triad mechanism to do it. As a consequence of this dance using more light on the skin, keratinocytes were able to sense more visible light combinations with purple, blue, and green light to easily photocatalyze the release of NO from glutathione. Not only does this cause a relaxation of the blood vessels, but it also frees up glutathione to react with hydrogen sulfide gas to produce sulfate to make every other chemical in the skin water soluable to get access to body parts to have global effects. This is how light develops its abscopal effects.

No one has figured out how this all works in humans but this is how I have seen it for 20 plus years. To date no one has published a thing using my ideas. But I can explain why subtraction of Vitamin C in humans links to hair loss. It also explains why balding people all have bad surface topology. None of the photoswitches work. This explains most of the integumentary and ocular diseases we see today. It also explains obesity, too. It also explains autoimmunity.
