### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-07T05:11:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-does-lack-of-light-on-skin-cause-gut-problems-abscopal-effect.29116/post-336784>

### NADPH oxidases​

The membrane‐bound multimeric NOX and DUOX complexes are the only known enzymes generating ROS not as byproduct but rather as their primary function (Lambeth and Neish, [2014](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5446568/#bph13428-bib-0014)). The mammalian oxidase family comprises five NOX members (NOX1–5) and two dual oxidases (DUOX1–2). With the exception of NOX5 that is not expressed in rodents, both humans and mice express all oxidase isoforms. NOX and DUOX homologs differ in their structures, mechanisms of activation and tissue expression patterns. The main oxidases expressed along the GI tract are NOX1 and DUOX2 found in the epithelium (NOX1 in the ileum, cecum and colon; DUOX2 in all segments of the gut), NOX2 expressed by professional phagocytes and DCs, and NOX4 present in the epithelium, fibroblasts and smooth muscle cells (Bedard and Krause, 2007; Mandal _et al._, [2010](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5446568/#bph13428-bib-0110))

Skeletal muscle has several sources of ROS, multiple organelles, including peroxisomes, endoplasmic reticulum, and mitochondria, are well known to produce O2 •- ([Dickinson and Chang 2011](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8898899/#B45)). Many cytosolic enzymes such as NADPH oxidases, and monoamine oxidases also generate O2 •- locally ([Powers and Jackson 2008](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8898899/#B146)).

**_As the main source of ATP in the mammalian cells, the mitochondrial electron transport chain (ETC) can produce O2 •- from at least 11 different sites on both sides of the inner mitochondrial membrane_** ([Shadel and Horvath 2015](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8898899/#B167)). Under physiological conditions, the complexes I and III of ECT are currently believed to be the major production sites of O2 •- ([Brand 2010](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8898899/#B16)). Due to O2 •- is too strongly charged to easily cross the inner mitochondrial membrane, it may act a locally role in mitochondria ([Valko et al., 2007](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8898899/#B185); [Henriquez-Olguin et al., 2020](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8898899/#B78)).

O2 •- produced by organelles or cytoplasmic enzymes is rapidly and spontaneously converted to H2O2, and this process is accelerated by superoxide dismutase (SOD) isoforms ([Powers and Jackson 2008](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8898899/#B146))

Because of its relatively lower reactivity, H2O2 is recognized as the primary redox signaling molecule in the redox regulation of biological activities ([Henriquez-Olguin et al., 2020](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8898899/#B78); [Veal and Day 2011](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8898899/#B186); [Sies 2014](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8898899/#B170)). Although the intracellular concentration of H2O2 is maintained in the low level range (1–100 nM), the overall cellular concentration is much higher than that of O2 •-, at 10–3 nM ([Sies and Jones 2020](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8898899/#B169)). (Another TIME clue)

At the same time, its concentration in normal cells is in a strict dynamic equilibrium state. Metabolic activity or various stressors, such as chemokines, growth factors, and physical stressors, stimulate the generation of H2O2, while its removal is driven by an effective reduction system ([Sies and Jones 2020](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8898899/#B169); [Parvez et al., 2018](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8898899/#B141)); that is, peroxyredoxin (Prxs) and glutathione peroxidases (GPx) catalyze the conversion of H2O2 to H2O ([Rhee and Kil 2017](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8898899/#B154); [Brigelius-Flohé and Flohé 2020](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8898899/#B20)).

As a signaling molecule, H2O2 directly oxidizes specific sulfur-containing amino acids (cysteine and methionine = read my blogs on both), which are crucial for protein function, activity, stability, subcellular localization, and interactions, thus regulating various physiological processes in cells and organs, such as cell activation, proliferation, differentiation, migration, fusion and angiogenesis ([Tan and Suda 2018](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8898899/#B176); [Zhang et al., 2019](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8898899/#B202)).

It is worth noting that mitochondrial nicotinamide nucleotide transhydrogenases (NNT) are also involved in clearing cellular H2O2, which is achieved by shifting the reducing equivalents from NADH to NADPH ([Hanschmann et al., 2013](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8898899/#B74); [Mailloux 2018](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8898899/#B115)). H2O2 can easily cross the cell membrane and react with Fe2+ to produce •OH, which is the neutral form of hydroxyl ion (OH-) ([Henriquez-Olguin et al., 2020](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8898899/#B78)) ([Figure 1](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8898899/figure/F1/)). Although its half-life (10–9 s) _in vivo_ is very short, •OH is the strongest oxidant in ROS due to its high reactivity and the lack of specific scavenging enzymes ([Pastor et al., 2000](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8898899/#B142)).

### Jack Kruse — 2025-07-30T20:14:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-does-lack-of-light-on-skin-cause-gut-problems-abscopal-effect.29116/post-357218>

Today's lesson on heat sinks and quenching radicals is linked directly to the GOE.

UV light on the skin photolyzes nitrite (NO₂⁻) and nitrate (NO₃⁻) in the epidermis into NO, a potent vasodilator. This increases venous oxygen saturation and lowers blood pressure by relaxing vascular smooth muscle. Research (e.g., Liu et al., 2014) confirms UV-induced NO boosts cutaneous blood flow by ~60% within minutes.

NO regulates stem cell proliferation and differentiation in niches (e.g., bone marrow, skin). Low NO levels impair stem cell function, while UV-induced NO enhance photorepair mechanisms of 380nm light via mTOR, linking to tissue health and immune modulation.

Corrin rings in vitamin B12 bind NO, quenching its bioactivity to limit diffusion and ensure localized effects (e.g., vasodilation, immune signaling). This binding modulates NO’s global impact, preventing excessive vasodilation or ATP depletion. Improving blood flow while stopping ATP production defines the action of a 'heat sink" This idea came directly from the GOE when oxygen was causing a HOLOCAUST on earth.

The GOE (~2.4 billion years ago) introduced oxygen, creating toxicity risks. NO’s role in reducing ATP production (by inhibiting mitochondrial respiration) evolved as a protective mechanism against oxygen overload, a legacy preserved in modern blood and tissue physiology. This aligns with my decentralized photo-bioelectric thesis that disrupted CCO (e.g., by nnEMF) mimics GOE stress, amplifying chronic disease running rampant on Earth today.

Of course. That picture of mitochondrial nanotubes defines ER failure.

Tie the lessons together Mat of the last 20 blogs.........CAN YOU DO IT?

Start with this paper of Martin Picard on nanotubes. Then add my wisdom to what he has missed.

[https://www.cell.com/trends/cell-biology/pdf/S0962-8924(17)30146-0.pdf](https://www.cell.com/trends/cell-biology/pdf/S0962-8924\(17\)30146-0.pdf)

**Light/dark and Charge Control:** Have you ever asked yourself the of question how light and dark affecting nanotube formation. The manner in which it happens strongly suggests a photobioelectric response. A lack of daytime light (e.g., UV-A, red/NIR) or darkness at night disrupts circadian mitochondrial function, increasing ROS and charge leakage (e.g., proton gradient collapse). Nanotubes likely fuse mitochondria to increase surface area, acting as a larger capacitor to stabilize bioelectric charge. This is supported by Liu et al., who note nanotubes enhance membrane potential (ΔΨm) under stress. This is why GDF15 isused in mitoception.

**ER Failure and Lipid Loss:** in cell membranes loaded with DHA: Prolonged nanotube activity, driven by defective mitochondria (e.g., from blue light), overtax the ER, which synthesizes lipids (e.g., DHA) for mitochondrial membranes. Loss of melatonin (nighttime antioxidant made 95% in mitochondria), melanin (photoprotection), and DHA (membrane fluidity) impairs ER function, leading to protein misfolding and stress, linking to diseases like autism, neurodegeneration, and EDS (Perkins et al., 2017, Journal of Cell Science).

**Oxygen Toxicity and Capacitance:** Nanotubes reflect mitochondrial MRO2 use and are reflection of the cell acting to reduce oxygen toxicity by buffering ROS, building a larger capacitive network to control current. This aligns with the GOE lessons of evolution, early life adapted to rising oxygen via light-sensitive structures (e.g., aromatic rings in prokaryotes, but upgraded tomicrotubules in eukaryotes), a precursor to mitochondrial nanotubes. Light is driving this all, not electrical current. Electrical currents are down stream light and dark effects. Time to wake up Martin. Ask better questions.

Look at the photorepair slide in those blogs of the last series............I dare you to.

Biophotons are ultra-weak light emissions produced by living organisms, thought to arise from metabolic processes like oxidative reactions in mitochondria. Centralized scientists and AI bots hypothesized them to play a role in cellular communication, potentially influencing gene expression or signaling pathways. FIAF, also known as angiopoietin-like protein 4 (ANGPTL4), is a protein produced by tissues like fat and the gut, and it’s a key player in lipid metabolism and microbiome construction. Neither understand how UPEs control FIAF. If the UPE is coherent then FIAF inhibits lipoprotein lipase, affecting how fats are stored or broken down, and its expression ramps up during fasting. The microbiome, meanwhile, is the community of gut microbes that can shift in response to diet, fasting, or host metabolism, influencing energy balance and health. If the UPEs in mitochondria is not coherent, the UPE changes and mental illness becomes more probable. You cannot burn fat so it changes neural signaling.

Centralized scientists and technocrats who build AI systems will say no direct study says “biophotons control FIAF to affect the microbiome,” but we can hypothesize based on related mechanisms. This is patently false. Why?

FIAF has known absorption and emission spectra to light frequencies. Because of that, a study is superfluous because each chemical in the world has an optical fingerprint. Just because a scientist has not published the work is immaterial to this BASIC biophysical fact in spectroscopy. This was what I brought Ray Peat over ten years ago, and he was impotent enough to answer my critiques of his work. This podcast covers these details.

### Support — 2024-07-09T15:25:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-don%E2%80%99t-receive-any-emails-from-any-jack-kruse-mails.29118/post-336864>

> [MikeJustin said:](/goto/post?id=336826)
>
> Hi guys,
>
> Since I’m a member I don’t get any mails from any jack kruse mails on my email account.
>
> I whitelisted support@jackkruse and info@kruseatdestin.
>
> I only get the mails from the forum and patreon.
>
> Any of you have this problem and does any of you have an idea how to fix this?

Hello!

Unfortunately, this is a spam filter issue on the receiving server. For some reason, your provider is not letting our emails through. Our system tracks the sending and shows that they are sent out of our server successful. We aren't not getting any ping back showing errors in delivery which means that once it's hitting your filter on the incoming email server it's getting blocked for some reason.

I have sent you a double opt-in message, subject line "Please confirm your subscription". Check your junk email for this if it's not in your in-box. If this does not work, you will need to contact your provider for assistance and to unblock our address.

Team Kruse

### Redox and resonance are tightly coupled in Nature.​

When we see waves coming toward us ashore we are actually seeing the energy of the sun powering wave action. Wind causes the see to pulsate so that a certain rhythm of waves occurs. This is exactly how the sun and Earth act to create the Schumann pulse as the solar wind crashes into the magnetosphere and is distributed to the ionosphere and Earth below in Aurora and lightning.
When living things are screened \[blocked\] from this geomagnetic field, they don't develop correctly. This tells us that redox and resonance are deeply coupled to morphogenesis in way we still are yet to understand. This diminishes the fidelity of the signal. This changes the waves that begin in our thalamus to create the life we get.

Today, not all life vibrates at exactly the Schumann resonance because man made waves oscillate at many different frequencies in all the zip codes on Earth modern man has created. Researcher now suggests that while early life was synced at around 7.83 Hz, the cellular activity in animals slowly drifted to other frequencies as the environment changed, with different frequencies being used for different types of activity in the brains of living things. For example, specific frequencies in human brain waves have been linked to specific mental states such as alertness, dreaming, and deep sleep. It is also linked to HRV. The Schumann resonance is closest to frequencies found in humans' deep relaxed state, suggesting primordial life could have been in a state similar to deep relaxation.

# Thread: Skin health and sun
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-health-and-sun.29128/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ChelseaBear — 2024-07-09T23:37:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-health-and-sun.29128/post-336894>

Working away on my tan here in the 50th parallel. I have been eating well for many years, good protein like local pastured beef and chicken (I raise pastured chicken), wild fish, seasonal veggies. I eat lots of eggs and I make pate with heavy cream and butter and liver from our chickens. Mostly local foods, very low carbs. I drink water with mineral drops. I spend lots of time outside, bare feet on the soil or sand. But my skin (I'm 39) which is GLOWING and clear, is often quite dry or dull on my legs. I wonder if the sun might be damaging it? What are your tips for supporting a healthy skin to receive sunshine? Thanks!!

### coho3131 — 2024-07-10T01:42:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-health-and-sun.29128/post-336902>

If it was me, I would try eating more fat if you have dry skin or hair. I like to add extra pork fat or suet to our soups and stews.

My skin is less dry after getting full body sun all year round, versus wearing shorts and t-shirts.

Maybe there is a private place where you can get sun on your whole body.

I'm not sure you need the mineral drops if you are drinking well water or bottled spring water.

# Thread: Optimal health question regarding deep wound and sleep
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-health-question-regarding-deep-wound-and-sleep.29129/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### drsongihan — 2024-07-10T19:51:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-health-question-regarding-deep-wound-and-sleep.29129/post-336924>

My primary goal in my journey is to help my teen with bipolar depression (catatonic depression, no will to live, anger).
I am myself healthy as far as I can see, but I have two questions related to my own health.

1) I tend to compromise sleep because I am so eager wanting to be awake to learn and do and use my time. I have no problem sleeping whenever I decide to sleep and in almost any environment. I only have rarely insomnia, probably when I drink 2 coffee in the evening. I do know if I sleep too little, my muscles hurt, but recently I figured out that I can eliminate that when I take a cold bath (thanks to the Kruse wisdom) or sunbathe.

Still, I assume that this is fundamentally a bad thing since I might be shortening my lifespan? How do I balance the need to sleep enough vs use my time awake to do things (learn, research, read, talk to friends, help). How little sleep is enough? How do I know I sleep enough?

2) The only other place where I notice that my health is not optimal is that when I have a wound (it could be due to bug bite or anything) it tends to make a deep crater, become dark and generate an ugly scab that is really hard to ignore. It's mostly on my legs and arms, i.e. the areas that are exposed to sun. Doesthis mean anything? Is a deeper, crater-like, wound a foreboding of something or is there a mechanism that can explain this phenomenon?

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-07-10T20:40:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-health-question-regarding-deep-wound-and-sleep.29129/post-336927>

> [drsongihan said:](/goto/post?id=336924)
>
> How do I balance the need to sleep enough vs use my time awake to do things (learn, research, read, talk to friends, help)

Use only the solar cycle (not a clock) to determine bedtime and wake time. Go to sleep when you are tired and get up when you are not.

> [drsongihan said:](/goto/post?id=336924)
>
> How little sleep is enough?

Depends on your personal context (your environment, your redox potential, your age, etc.)

> [drsongihan said:](/goto/post?id=336924)
>
> How do I know I sleep enough?

How do you feel when you wake in the morning?

> [drsongihan said:](/goto/post?id=336924)
>
> The only other place where I notice that my health is not optimal is that when I have a wound

Wound healing is circadian. If wound healing is off, sounds to me like your rhythm is off

And if something is biting you and leaving a crater, I would go get that checked.

### caroline — 2024-07-10T22:42:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-health-question-regarding-deep-wound-and-sleep.29129/post-336930>

Have a look at the lion diet ....and Mikhaila Peterson's story

### drsongihan — 2024-07-11T05:50:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-health-question-regarding-deep-wound-and-sleep.29129/post-336943>

> [Joseph LeBlanc said:](/goto/post?id=336927)
>
> Use only the solar cycle (not a clock) to determine bedtime and wake time. Go to sleep when you are tired and get up when you are not.
>
>
> Depends on your personal context (your environment, your redox potential, your age, etc.)
>
>
> How do you feel when you wake in the morning?
>
>
> Wound healing is circadian. If wound healing is off, sounds to me like your rhythm is off
>
> And if something is biting you and leaving a crater, I would go get that checked.

I agree! The solar cycle as the alarm is a good compass, and I will keep trying. However, at 40 to 50 latitude, the summer nights are short and the winter nights are long, so it just does not mesh with practical life so easily unless somebody does not need any centralized money to make their life work. For everyone at 40 latitude and up moving to lower latitude is not a practical solution for planet earth either? It only works in this circle because we all are still the minority, and in my circle I would be the "crazy" one, which plays in our favor that moving is an option. Anyway,
as soon as I turn on artificial light (despite using blue blocking glasses) in the evening we are disrupting the solar cycle as our compass. I guess we will have to try our best, but the logical consequence is that we should sleep longer in winter and shorter in summer if we live in high latitude?

How I "feel" has two aspects. It's more complicated because we are complicated mammals with a Ferrari engine up there, as Jack would say.

One is the rudimentary physical wellness. The other is the eagerness and motivation of wanting to learn, understand, discover, change the world .... and is also as much human nature as tending to our rudimentary physical wellness, of course as long as we are healthy at our core. If we are sick, the latter anyway goes out the window. I guess we have to always watch and balance, and in my case, I abused the rudimentary physical wellness because I have no ailment but it could easily catch up with me in the future, so better watch out. One thing I noticed is that being out in the sun longer does seem to give me more energy (initially it made me more tired).

Re wound, all my wound tend to be deeper, not just when a bug bites. They do heal not too slow, but in ways that tend to make a big scab, and then leave a scar or at the minimum a dark spot. The doctor will tell me "don't scratch" but I know that this is not the issue. Rather, it's how my body react to wounds. Do you have an article that shows how wound healing is connected to the Circadian rythm?

### drsongihan — 2024-07-11T16:14:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-health-question-regarding-deep-wound-and-sleep.29129/post-336956>

I just read some of the videos that Janz posted, and realize that wound healing is directly a nitric oxide problem, and perhaps a sign that I did not have sunshine into some areas. What is interesting is that my wounds are rather in areas that sun hits (leg, arm) and not in other areas. But my feeling is that it's a balance thing. Whatever skin is more exposed must see sunlight!

### doctorwu85 — 2024-07-12T17:20:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-health-question-regarding-deep-wound-and-sleep.29129/post-337001>

> [drsongihan said:](/goto/post?id=336924)
>
> 1) I tend to compromise sleep because I am so eager wanting to be awake to learn and do and use my time. I have no problem sleeping whenever I decide to sleep and in almost any environment. I only have rarely insomnia, probably when I drink 2 coffee in the evening.

I haven't seen much discussion on this, but you should try quitting caffeine entirely. Very powerful and addictive chemical that, at least if you are as sensitive to it as I am, is going to wreck your ability to align your circadian clock with the light and dark cycles. Lots of high-functioning people have the same problem with wanting to use their time at the expense of sleep, but I found that once I gave up caffeine I was much more willing to go to bed early and found it easier to wake up naturally in the morning.

Caffeine is one thing I have failed to give up. I have drank coffee with heavy cream religiously basically every morning for as long as I can remember.

But, I do often wonder if I could be even more optimal without the coffee ritual. I highly suspect that I could be, but I fail every time I attempt to abstain from it. Most I remember going is 2 days without it in recent memory. It always pulls me back in. Caffeine is certainly a hell of a drug

“And not a word about light” seems a general theme in the science literature. I am amazed how we got brainwashed into never thinking about sunlight. I listened to the interview of Dr Nathan Bryan by Perlmutter. What Bryan found is not wrong. However its astonishing to me that Perlmutter asks him “so how do we make more NO” and he talks about green leafy vegetable, and all sorts of things, but cannot bring over his lips “UVB” without which NO is not made. He utters the word sunlight once and that makes me think it’s on purpose. He wants to sell his NO releasing medicine ultimately not how we make our own NO. It’s a general theme in all science articles. Still, I appreciate Bryan’s work because he at least made the connection to doing less: no fluoride tooth paste. No mouthwash. Chewing slow. Eating green leafy vegs. We can consume his study, just need to salt it with sun.

### doctorwu85 — 2024-07-13T05:21:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-health-question-regarding-deep-wound-and-sleep.29129/post-337037>

> [Joseph LeBlanc said:](/goto/post?id=337013)
>
> Caffeine is one thing I have failed to give up. I have drank coffee with heavy cream religiously basically every morning for as long as I can remember.

I still enjoy a decaf (with heavy cream as well) from time to time.

> [Joseph LeBlanc said:](/goto/post?id=337013)
>
> My energy levels and sleep seem to be okay from what I can tell. Unfortunately, I don't have any baseline data on myself though. Just kinda go off of relative feelings.
>
> But, I do often wonder if I could be even more optimal without the coffee ritual. I highly suspect that I could be, but I fail every time I attempt to abstain from it. Most I remember going is 2 days without it in recent memory. It always pulls me back in. Caffeine is certainly a hell of a drug

It's definitely more addictive than it's given credit for. When I quit drinking coffee daily it took me a couple of months to completely rid myself of the withdrawal symptoms. The first couple days I would fall asleep before 9pm, whereas normally I would be up past midnight. Then headaches, irritability, cravings, etc.

Apparently there is a genetic component to caffeine metabolism, with both fast and slow variants. I am definitely in the "slow" bucket, so it used to mess up my sleep even with a single cup in the morning. Might be worth an experiment to go without it at some point, but if you're a fast metabolizer, I suspect a morning cup has much less of a negative effect on your internal clock.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-07-13T17:01:44+0300

If caffeine metabolism is at all related to alcohol metabolism, then I might actually be a slow metabolizer. Alcohol has major, lasting effects on me. Just drinking even a single drink can lead to effects that follow me well into the next day.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-07-13T19:07:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-health-question-regarding-deep-wound-and-sleep.29129/post-337083>

What I have noticed over the years reading/watching “experts” is there are truth seekers and then there are those pushing a position - especially when it comes to health, food and faith. I look for the truth seekers.

In the physical world, an important point when looking for info on how something will affect YOU - be it caffeine, alcohol, palio, carnivore, nnEMF (from “DE” to RF) remember . . . It depends (on YOU). Yes there are general principles that will apply to most - but specifically for YOU . . . It depends. So many variable's both in you and your environment.

I have done caffeine and alcohol for a season (I like the flavors) and gone without for a season, I’ve been a vegetarian (2 years), I’ve eaten carnivore - I don’t notice any difference. Fasting (3 days, 10 days, now mostly intermittent) - works for me. Light, water, magnetism - works for me. Prayer/meditation - works for me. I have also noticed that the most ignorant people on any given subject tend to be those who are an “expert” in another.

# Thread: Out in the open
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/out-in-the-open.29130/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3

### Uswebers1 — 2024-07-11T04:46:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/out-in-the-open.29130/post-336937>

Hi,

This is my story and I’m sticking to it. I am 6’2” at the moment BMI 27.0
I am 2/3 to 71 times around the sun.
I grew up on L.I., NY, Jones Beach was part of my childhood. At 18 I left and spent the next 4 years in the USN between California and Vietnam on an Aircraft Carrier, and beaches inbetween. After my time spent I worked outside installations, in Silicon Valley, California, for about 6 years. Moved to inside work for about asa Machinist, but with the internet as and fast, cheep overseas turnout, machine shops were laying off and closing the doors. Getting tired of getting laid off i got a job delivering mail for Uncle Sam. My dream job returned in aways wanting a job i could work out side. Starting at 52 years of age I’ve just about hit 20 years.
i am mostly Norwegian and English, my mtDNA is H. I have had dirty blonde hair, now gray/blonde. My eyes are green. I have had a BMI of around 30.0 for most of this time. I went strict dietary Vegan for 8 years, eating vegan cookies and cakes along with some good veggies, until 2013. I think this is what set me up for future failure. Then after a couple of Hawaiian Luaus i went SAD diet. In 2018 i got keto fever with the keto junk and some good food. Then i cane across Dr Paul Saladino just as he started his Carnivore, and followed him into fruit and honey. I didn’t take the Jab. I like science, I’m not all that smart, so when I heard it was this new kind of Jab, i was very interested. Well after i found out what it was i said no you’re not messing with my DNA. Plus i was listen to Alex Jones.
Well anyway lI walk 14 miles (30,000steps) house to house 5 days a week in a Mediterranean type climate, with shorts all year long. Plus i stopped using Sunscreen a out a year ago. My glasses are UV block reading glasses.
So i started years ago can’t remember, maybe around 50 having BPH problems. Getting close to a pill to pee but managing through some magnesium at night and trying to figure out what foods to eliminate. So 20 years later my PSA is 9.?, i did the biopsy a couple of years ago and got really nausea during the experience. It was clear. So they have been bugging for another. I read up and finally talked them i to an MRI so if it was positive they would know were to poke. Well after all this medical malpractice and not wanted to be injected with radioactive die. I just leave them alone and ignore them. In the mean time I was studying up on cancer and anti parasitic drugs and teied bouts of 2 of them and it seems my urine volume got larger and my flow has really never been better in the revent past. Not having any inability to pee at night. Si naybe their is sime slow growing cancer involved as these Anti-parasitics help in cancer cell Apoptosis supposedly. Then I found thru my Food youTube addiction Dr. Jack Kruse, and i knew immediately that this guy was a genius, for putting it all together down to the light-electron-water-magnetism eureka moment. It all makes so much logical sense, even with my weakness in these combined sciences. So i think even though Iive and probably will remain here in this environmentally toxic jungle, I believe my health will improve. Already I don’t put my phone in my heart pocket anymore. i was already using sime amber lights at night, bu Dr Jack have been evolving, or is it deevolving my life style.
You k ow how when someone finds gold and they know they shouldn’t tell anyone, but because of their great excitement they can’t help but tell everyone? Now most everyone thinks I'm crazy. But I do find some intelligent folks
Thank you Dr Jack
Thank you Dr Jack
Thank you Dr Jack

### Uswebers1 — 2024-07-11T06:39:35+0300

### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-05T17:16:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-intro-to-dr-jack.29131/post-338099>

Stars cannot shine without environmental darkness.

Light has no relative power without understanding darkness.

This is why you must embrace the suck the life.
Life has a tension between comfort and discomfort. Discomfort affords us the perspective to ask better questions about our current assumptions. In my opinion, discomfort emerges from our minds when our choices are suppressing our true identity.
A great way to poison a human is to remove any hardship or struggle from his life. One of the most essential things for any leader is never letting anyone else define who you are and what you choose. Being unconventional does not mean the idea of a mito-hack cannot work. Unconventional means the prescription has not made it into conventional textbooks. This is what quantum biology is and the recent melanopsin data show us; you better pay deep attention to Black Swan mitochondriacs as your environment is made unfavorable by the trends of modern human networking.
Darwin gave us the false meme that life is about the survival of the fittest and that competition is critical to success. It is not. Why do I say this? It is about the survival of the wisest. Unless you know the epigenetic code, which is our software program that runs the genes, which are the hardware in cells, the gene of DNA has no fundamental meaning. That has made Darwin and Watson Crick's perspective meaningless for us and for medicine. This paradigm of "genomic belief" has been utterly toxic for success with illness.

Letting go of toxic environments you put yourself in can be one of the hardest things to do in life, but it is one of the most wise things one can do to change their life. Life is much less a competitive struggle for survival than a triumph of cooperation and creativity. Build your career and network around this concept and you'll win. The most important thing in understanding life's cipher is hearing what isn't being said aloud. It is analogous to what we've found in genetics. Ignoring the genes and paying attention to the junk is where the rainbow really lies. Nature never gives up her secrets easily. We must learn to ignore the noise genes create in professional science and focus on the junk DNA.

The same is true with talk. Words we use are not as critical in human communication as we believe. Just as it is the silent part of the genome that runs our DNA, in language, it is the things not said in words, but present between words that are critical. They hold the key to successful understanding in life. The art of reading between the lines is the quest of the wise mitochondriac. Always pay attention to the pauses, the nonverbal chatter, and the movement that occurs between words to understand what is really going on.

Learning is like the fuel that moves the electrons of your body toward a destination of success. Learning requires an ear that listens and an intellect that is aware and has the patience to endure mistakes. Errors are the growing pains of wisdom. The invariable mark of wisdom in you is when you see the possible in the improbable and the miraculous in the common. Wisdom is not built by time and does not comes not from age, but from education, learning, and thinking.

The irony of modern centralized science is this: Physicists/astronomers believe that if they want to understand the beginning and end of the universe they should aim understand the life cycle of the stars to unlock the mystery of the evolution of the universe. They believe if they can work out how stars change and evolve over time they believe they could begin to understand how the universes changes over time. The irony? Stars emit light and it is the light that changes the universe. None of the scientist in biology have ever thought once about how light causes the evolution of cells in living beings. Amazing contraction to me. The lesson to be learned and tattoo'd into your brain?

Stars cannot shine without environmental darkness.

Light has no relative power without understanding darkness.

If you get it wrong,

### CHANGES IN LIGHT PREDICT EPIGENETIC PROGRAMMING and that explains where your problems and diseases come from. It is not your genome that define you.​

# Thread: Hormones and thermodynamics.  Anything losing energy gets larger.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hormones-and-thermodynamics-anything-losing-energy-gets-larger.29135/>
**Subforum:** Heal Your Hormones
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-11T16:58:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hormones-and-thermodynamics-anything-losing-energy-gets-larger.29135/post-336957>

Hormones and thermodynamics. Anything losing energy gets larger.

The Skoptzy (or Skoptsy, meaning the castrated), also called the White Doves, were a Christian sect whose male members, to attain their ideal of sanctity, subjected themselves to castration. Their origin in the 18th century, their spread through a large part of Russia and into Romania and Bessarabia.

Tandler and Grosz obtained an x-ray of the skull in a 20-yr-old Skoptzy man, who had been castrated at age 10 yr and observed that the sella turcica was grossly enlarged. The longer the destruction of the gonads was present the larger the pituitary became. We see the same effect in people with hormone destuction who have pituitary tumors. All pituitary tumors are associated with energy loss and fossa enlargement given enough time has elapsed. Koch obtained x-rays of the skull in 10 Skoptzy men and reported that the pituitary glands were normal in size in 3, enlarged in 4, and“ particularly” enlarged in 3. In the latter group, there was also erosion of the dorsum sellae of the pituitary.

There is a deep decentralized lesson here for decentralized clinicians.

### [Long-Term Consequences of Castration in Men: Lessons from the Skoptzy and the Eunuchs of the Chinese and Ottoman Courts](https://academic.oup.com/jcem/article/84/12/4324/2864451)

Castration of men and males of other species was almost certainly the first experiment in endocrinology (if not in zoology), and the literature on the subj

 academic.oup.com

# Thread: Too Much of a Good Thing Could = Overcompensation for Some Bad Things/Deficits?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/too-much-of-a-good-thing-could-overcompensation-for-some-bad-things-deficits.29137/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Olivert — 2024-07-11T17:53:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/too-much-of-a-good-thing-could-overcompensation-for-some-bad-things-deficits.29137/post-336964>

I have been wondering about my unusual labs where some good things like Zinc or B12 come way over reference range or TG that are below referenced range.
Zinc 23.11 umol/l
Vitamin B12 1139 pmol/l (but highest I had was 1800)
TG 0.31 mmol/l at relatively high carb diet (approx. 300-400g/day)
Also hsCRP is below 0.1 so it is considered 0 on my lab results - this despite overtraning and being constantly injured in the past until I learned better from Dr. Jack.

Those things would be considered good, but I wonder if they are not counteracting or coming at the expense or result of overcompensation for some other deficits and shortcomings. For example my white blood cell count is very low, I have neutropenia but I rarely get sick - my WBC is 1.9 but was as low as 1.5 and highest I ever had was still just 2.5. Also my iron and ferritin and sex hormones are extremely low. As you might have guessed centralized medicine had no idea what is going on and could not help provide any guidance in this. I was doing lots of research but clearly not sufficiently enough as I was unable to connect the dots.

So what I was wondering is that could for some reason some of my labs be so "good" or "high" or "low" precisely because it is overcompensation for others being so "bad"? I do not suffer any severe symptoms from anything, but I am curious just what is going on and what could one extrapolate into larger picture view from these "strange" or unusual interactions and results?

# Thread: My new persona - middle Tennessee mom
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-new-persona-middle-tennessee-mom.29142/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### thejessallen — 2024-07-13T04:14:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-new-persona-middle-tennessee-mom.29142/post-337035>

Hola fellow mitochondriacs

I’m a mom of an 18 year old Sebastian

1\. Is this a safe place to dox myself?

2\. Yesterday I consciously adopted a new persona - middle Tennessee mito mom

I tell people the true wellbeing starts with a sunrise and sunset daily - they say “when I fix my \_\_\_\_\_ issue I will have time for sunrise and sunset, right now. I’m in survival mode.”

People are open and almost all are excited to learn, one possible solution to their survival issues is.. daily sunrises and sunsets…

Yesterday, I attended my son Sebastian’s college orientation day at middle Tennessee state university MTSU.

I got the t-shirt and some sunset.

I love how the colors match the flag El Salvador.

# Thread: Blessing in Disguise?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blessing-in-disguise.29146/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### rtd — 2024-07-13T17:15:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blessing-in-disguise.29146/post-337067>

I rent a floor in a multifamily house. Earlier this week my pantry shelving (5 shelves) crashed down. Whoever put up the shelving did not secure the mounting hardware into any studs, sheetrock anchors were used.

After a big cleanup from a few exploded jars and cans, I decided to reduce inventory via donation and garbage. Many products were not aligned with the epi-paleo approach. I did preserve rice, lentils, etc. strictly as a hedge against emergency situations (heaven for bid).

The shelves were replaced with a 5 tier adjustable wire rack. My previous abode I used a method with wire racks in a cool basement to organize items by expiration date using a First In First Out (FIFO) approach. The "fall" allowed me to get back to this method, albeit at a more fluctuating temperature and humidity.

Sometimes a problem (shelving failure) is the solution (cleanse and regroup).

I would put “some” in the Grounding/Earthing community in this category.
Earthing - connecting with Nature outside - is VERY healing. Your bodies DC electrical system in direct contact with the Earth’s DC electric system. Grounding makes you part of the circuit. Connecting with Nature - IN - Nature - VERY good.

So “some” would say - while still inside - you can get the same (natural) effect by connecting to your homes AC powered electrical system ground (the very system that is causing the problems) OR you can throw a wire out the window and ground outside. **All you need is our grounding sheet, pillow, pad etc.**

Think about it, now you have a 50ft(?) wire (antenna) attached to your big toe making you part if a circuit - your homes 60Hz (50Hz) AC field circuit. This field now has a path of least resistance - through you - to ground AND ALL the ground effects from the grid, your transformer you share with your neighbor’s pool pump etc has direct contact with YOU. Also this wire/antenna is attracting RF signals. If metal bed frames and springs are bad, how can a metallic pad and wire connected to you be good?

“Grounding” inside your AC powered home and not outside in DC powered Nature (away from all AC electric fields) - is the same as getting an artificial light treatment implanted into your body and not going outside in the sun.

Turn off the power in and around your sleep area, DISCONNECT from the AC circuits/systems that cause the problems. Earth on the Earth - under the sun.

# Thread: WHY THE USA HAS TO REJECT BASEL 3 AND START OVER :  CHEVRON IMPLICATIONS
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-the-usa-has-to-reject-basel-3-and-start-over-chevron-implications.29150/>
**Subforum:** The Kruse Longevity Center
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-13T22:14:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-the-usa-has-to-reject-basel-3-and-start-over-chevron-implications.29150/post-337105>

The banking cartel is looking to to push everyone into feudalism with their Basel 3 regulations and nothing in our Constitution requires us to follow it. Unfortunately the regulators of the currency at the Fed do have a say because of how Chevron Doctrine worked prior to its reversal.

We need to take away banking regulators power and give them back to Congress so the SCOTUS can be the ultimate arbiter of what is legal.

The capital regulation plan, also known as the Basel III Endgame proposal, will require banks to maintain nearly [30% more capital](https://fsforum.com/news/required-gsib-capital-increase-would-far-exceed-government-estimates), resulting in increased borrowing costs for lower- and middle-income Americans. If you listened Federal regulators (Powell) have not properly considered how this proposal will impact Americans across the country, especially those in underserved communities. This means those people will need Bitcoin more than one can imagine.
Even a few sats will matter of the Fed implements Basel 3.

Basel 3 will reduce bank lending and this will help private credit issuers. Those are the people who prop up the banking cartel.

Faced with additional capital requirements, banks have two broad choices — raise additional capital to maintain existing footprint or shrink current footprint. Evidence from the initial Basel III implementation reveals that banks will choose the latter. One of the consequences will be a further shift of lending to unregulated firms that are free from oversight and capital requirements, increasing the risk of financial instability. Basel 3 tells us that we are getting closer to financial calamity and Bitcoin world.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-13T22:16:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-the-usa-has-to-reject-basel-3-and-start-over-chevron-implications.29150/post-337106>

Private credit funds are [pools](https://www.bairdwealth.com/globalassets/pdfs/help/private-credit-funds-disclosure.pdf) of actively managed capital that invest primarily in loans to private companies — a type of nonbank financial intermediation, or NBFI also called shadow banking. So, while investors could start a bank with the goal of lending to businesses, they can also choose to avoid the expansive bank regulatory framework by, instead, pooling their funds and hiring what in banking would be called loan officers. This regulation-light approach is allowed because lenders to these funds, unlike depositors, give up their right to withdraw money early and (Scout’s honor!) they will not seek government assistance if the loans go bad. This is a horrible idea.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-13T22:21:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-the-usa-has-to-reject-basel-3-and-start-over-chevron-implications.29150/post-337109>

There is nothing new about nonbanks offering banking-type products. Also not new is that one can typically point to bank regulation as a key driver for such innovation. For example, money market mutual funds were a [market response](https://www.sec.gov/comments/4-619/4619-34.pdf) to the caps on interest payments by banks on deposits (former Regulation Q). They are now a $5.7 trillion market [prone to runs](https://academic.oup.com/rfs/article-abstract/34/11/5402/6289999?redirectedFrom=fulltext) and [requiring assistance](https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/mmlf.htm) from the Federal Reserve during stressed market conditions to stem financial contagion.

The U.S. banking system now provides [just 33 percent of total credit](https://www.bis.org/statistics/totcredit.htm) to the U.S. non-financial sector today. The lower this number goes the less Bitcoin you need the higher it goes the more you'll need.

If Basel 3 goes in as law that is a key signal.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-13T22:24:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-the-usa-has-to-reject-basel-3-and-start-over-chevron-implications.29150/post-337110>

I learned about this the hardware during hurricane Katrina in 2005. There is capital requirements that do exist for [insurance companies](https://fedfin.com/karen-petrou-the-apples-to-martians-comparison-of-pe-capital-to-that-demanded-of-banks/), but they do not define capital as narrowly or [rigorously](https://content.naic.org/industry/ucaa/chart-foreign-statutory-capital-surplus) as banking regulators do and this makes them more risky and subject to loss. This is why I pulled all my money from annuities that they offered in the early 2000s during the .com scam.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-13T22:26:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-the-usa-has-to-reject-basel-3-and-start-over-chevron-implications.29150/post-337111>

Hedge funds and the markets-related activities of insurance companies are completely the wild west and many people have their capital tied up in these vehicles. They should be considered toxic if Basel 3 is adopted.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-13T22:44:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-the-usa-has-to-reject-basel-3-and-start-over-chevron-implications.29150/post-337114>

Corporations have relationships with banks for a slew of credit and other products and services. So why would a bank let a customer take this business elsewhere?

Simple answer: once the bank factors in the cost of figuring out the additional capital required for these credit transactions, the cost of that additional capital, the costs imposed from the back and forth with bank supervisors examining the bank’s transactions, and then any additional capital and other constraints if the bank is subject to [stress tests](https://www.federalreserve.gov/supervisionreg/stress-tests-capital-planning.htm), then the price at which the bank would be able to provide the credit will be easily undercut by the private credit funds.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-13T22:46:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-the-usa-has-to-reject-basel-3-and-start-over-chevron-implications.29150/post-337115>

Private credit also locks up investor funds. Investors in private credit funds typically cannot withdraw their money on demand. This means if you participate in this pool you are taking massive risks in a Basel 3 world. There are long lock-in periods and even more complex structures. If you invest in these pools you can read about [zombie funds](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/features/2023-09-24/private-equity-zombie-firms-leave-pension-funds-with-hard-choices#xj4y7vzkg) for these credit funds.

Some nonbank lenders can [impose withdrawal limits during stressed market condition](https://www.wsj.com/articles/investors-yank-money-from-commercial-property-funds-pressuring-real-estate-values-11670293325)s — if a bank were to do it, it potentially could be treated as a failed bank. In theory, this reduces the risk of a failure in a private credit fund creating contagion and wider financial instability.

Unfortunately, this view misses the point that when [systemic risk comes calling](https://www.berkshirehathaway.com/letters/2016ltr.pdf#page=5), lock-in periods will not matter. Basel 3 creates systemic risks in the entire sector.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-13T22:51:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-the-usa-has-to-reject-basel-3-and-start-over-chevron-implications.29150/post-337116>

Burdensome regulations drive U.S. banks out of the business of providing credit to communities. This vacuum will be filled by poorly capitalized private credit funds (and other nonbank lenders) that will provide credit when times are good but likely disappear when times are tough. You need to avoid these areas if Basel 3 comes in.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-13T22:54:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-the-usa-has-to-reject-basel-3-and-start-over-chevron-implications.29150/post-337117>

Everyone saw how counterparty risk took down many of the banks who did business with crypto businesses 2020-2023. The same risks will happen to non banking credit providers. In this way, Basel 3 is the mechanism for land & asset confiscation for the banking cartel. You need to be aware of this and how the Fed talks about this going forward.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-13T22:58:21+0300

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-13T23:08:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-the-usa-has-to-reject-basel-3-and-start-over-chevron-implications.29150/post-337120>

The GFC of 2008 was largely the result of imprudent bank lending & borrowing in real estate, & the legislation and regulation that followed largely focused only on the banks and, specifically, the amount of capital they hold. This created the void that non banking credit providers now fill. They have few capital controls so their risk profile for you is off the charts. Communities will be wiped free if they are financed this way.

My concern is retirement and pension funds some of you may have cash in. Pension funds are increasingly using hedge funds to boost their yields for the public and this makes them very risky. Moreover, insurance companies are increasingly owned by private equity looking for additional sources of funds. All of us pay the price when large, heavily leveraged firms go belly up — which is why we should think twice about creating a regulatory framework that drives business away from the brightly lit world of highly regulated banks and into the shadows of private credit. **_Personally, I want Basel 3 because I think it leads to fiat explosion faster._**

But for some of you living on a fiat standard and owning assets in the USA you may not like this.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-13T23:14:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-the-usa-has-to-reject-basel-3-and-start-over-chevron-implications.29150/post-337121>

What is being sold to the Fed by Basel? That Basel 3 is needed because the financial risk of the GFC is still uncovered in banks.

Basel III is **an internationally agreed set of measures developed by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision in response to the financial crisis of 2007-09**. It is not a binding agreement for Congress unless the Fed mandates it. Congress should never do this and in my opinion it is the biggest reason why the recent Chevron decision is BIG.

Basel is trying to sell this to Powell as a needed measure to strengthen the regulation, supervision and risk management of banks. Putin has cut his ties with the Rothschild banking cartel in the last 5 years. The BRICS seem to be headed this way as well.

The smart move is for the USA to back fiat with Bitcoin and tell Basel 3 to fuck off, but given the DNC and Biden reliance on Senator Warren I seriously doubt this happens.

Basel 3 is composed of three parts, or pillars.
Pillar 1 addresses capital and liquidity adequacy and provides minimum requirements.
Pillar 2 outlines supervisory monitoring and review standards.
Pillar 3 promotes market discipline through prescribed public disclosures.

As of July 2024 it is over 1000 pages. I have read most of it.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-13T23:17:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-the-usa-has-to-reject-basel-3-and-start-over-chevron-implications.29150/post-337122>

Public comments were open until Dec 1, 2023. And they were brutal. The end result of that was a complete lack of initial concessions and multiple dissents by Fed and FDIC board members indicate there are components of Basel III endgame. This just popped up again in the Powell Testimony in front of Congress last week.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-13T23:19:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-the-usa-has-to-reject-basel-3-and-start-over-chevron-implications.29150/post-337123>

The regulatory agencies expected to finalize the proposals with enough time to take effect on July 1, 2025 and have a three-year phase in of capital ratio impact until June 30, 2028. But the recent SCOTUS overrule of Chevron is likely going to make Congress much less reliant on Powell, the Fed, and the FDIC.

The next Congress might have a lot more say than the current one does. This means Warren could be a bigger problem going forward.

### bennefit-coaching — 2024-07-14T12:52:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/benne-from-berlin.29152/post-337142>

Hi guys! My name is Benne (Benedict), I live in Berlin and I'm looking forward to connect with you guys  I'm about to leave the city in the next days to the countryside of Germany (and closer to the equator in September) after a talk to Jack about my psoriasis. I have some questions that will also somehow introduce myself:

\- The first option wher to go (and heal) on my mind is to go to El Salvador.
\- Who is already there?
\- Where is a good area to heal and also to connect with you guys? A vulcano area with some lakes or even the ocean?
\- How can I find a place to rent? Is there anybody who has a place to rent?
I like to move a lot (hikes, workouts, surfing, movement culture, movnat, yoga), I need some (cable) internet to do my online coaching business. I need fresh organic food. I'm invested in bitcoin already and want to go more into it in the next days.
My spanish skills are very limited yet. Is that a big problem?
I'm looking forward to hear from you
Benne

I finally bought the cheapest of all emf meters, a handheld AM radio as Jeromy Johnson recommends. Despite my in-line DE meters reading under 15mV on all my electrical wiring it doesn't measure what actual devices themselves are emitting why the AM radio as I've learned is also essential.

### Saichi — 2024-08-01T17:59:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mac-users.29153/post-337930>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=337913)
>
> Is the issue still present when you disconnect all cables with exception of the wired keyboard? If not, what others cables do you have connected e.g. ethernet?

I just checked disconnecting my ethernet and thunderbolt to external sound card, no audible decrease in EMI most coming from the laptop itself. About half the amount on the wired keyboard versus laptop keyboard though.

### Mat.I — 2024-08-02T07:09:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mac-users.29153/post-337979>

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=337930)
>
> I just checked disconnecting my ethernet and thunderbolt to external sound card, no audible decrease in EMI most coming from the laptop itself. About half the amount on the wired keyboard versus laptop keyboard though.

It would likely stem from the circuit board in the keyboard and/or laptop. If it is happening with out a key press then, based on how the circuit board would be configured it may be constantly check for status (even key status). This would likely only be low mag and electrical intensity however it could be in a frequency band that could have detrimental impacts on the user etc.. Everything I'm hearing about Mac's are no good whatsoever in terms of EMF. A mechanical key board could be a solution (for the key board issue). Re: oscilloscope as it is DC you would need something that can pick up the currents / frequency. Maybe a hall effect type sensor / probe / clamp connected to the oscilloscope could work (depending on sensitivity you may also need to consider pre-signal amps). You can get certain meters that have frequency band functions, however this is just a range. Is there an electronics place near you maybe get them to measure it for you? I assume you have also gone through a process of elimination to determine that the PC/Keyboard is the cause and not other emissions around you.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-10-14T05:49:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mac-users.29153/post-340964>

Wireless uses Bluetooth 2.4 GHz (same as your microwave). A wired connection, depending on the set up CAN pick up an electric field from the computer. It is all about whether the system is properly grounded or not. Measure and find out.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decentralized-thinking-and-political-affiliation.29157/post-337171>

I am used to my world order being upside down and confusing wherever I look and go these days. Just today, I learned that pasteurization of milk has nothing to do with killing some germ in milk, but germs that can come along from cow's behind, and if done right, that raw milk is much better to digest. The idea that raw milk is easier to digest than pasteurized milk is opposite of what we learned, like it seems with everything.

Another part that I cannot reconcile is decentralized thinking and political affiliation and the idea who is "kin". I listened to Russel Brand's Brandemic Comedy special and it was not only hilarious, but very much truth spilling. He talked about, with humor, how crazy hypocritical it is that the whole mainstream media was talking about and showing Wuhan's wet market, riling up xenophobia and running the narrative that the crazy raw bat-eating Chinese have caused the coronavirus gone viral, but kept a secret that Wuhan had a gain-of-function program on bat's coronavirus, studying how to make the virus more deadly, but even when such news was trickling out that the narrative turned into "evil Chinese government running such dangerous program", but not a word about the U.S. role (Fauci etc). I thought Russel Brand's show was brilliant and brought home the message of truth, but in a humanistic and kind way, while being hilarious.

Yet, his channel is full of pro-Trump b.s. I mean, what has Trump anything to do with decentralized thinking? Sure, he is an out-of-the-box thinking individual, but driven by greed, self absorbed narcissism, unkind, fuels hatred, making ordinary people feel "he gets them" while also emptying their pockets. He is as far from decentralized thinking, curiosity driven and humanistic vision as it gets. But why is somebody like Russell Brand at the same time pro-Trump?

I guess there is no such thing as political affiliation for me anymore. Each person, each situation and each message has to be vetted one by one, separately, by my own judgement whether it's truth-seeking or not. Sigh. This is a lot of work, but worth the journey.

Fascinating. I knew about misfolding caused by broken inflammatory response. I knew that cysteines are potent anti oxidant. But the concept that the cysteines are there to protect proteins from the potentially harmful effect of UV excitation of aromatic amino acids is not a connection I made.

One of the most prominent protein that misfolds and forms tangles in the broodest category of neurodegenerative diseases is tau. Tau tangle deposits are diagnostic of Alzheimer's, CTE, progress supra nuclear palsy and even Down syndrom. There is no a cure nor diagnosis from the medical community. A lot of sensational non-sense, yes, a lot of scandals, yes, but no diagnosis. Literally, definitive diagnosis comes post mortem.

It was found in the past decade that tau tangles spread by a prion-like mechanism. In other words, not only the prion protein, but also tau tangles act like a prion, spreading from cell to cell.

The native protein tau has two natural cysteines, at site 291 and 322. These disulfide bonds are never formed, neither in healthy tau when associated with microtubules, nor in pathological tau when they stack into amyloid fibrils that have prion like competency. I was always wondering why the hell would tau have two native cysteines if it does not use it? Well, it looks like it is a protection switch that I need to look into.

Amazing.

Reading Tensegrity 5 motivated me yesterday to fill up my fridge with fish.
This morning left my 'sanctuary' after a fishy breakfast feeling sharp and happy. Went for a sunny AM walk at 8:00-9:00.
In the fields - ecological farmers area - started to feel lots of brain fog.
Safe and Sound meter registered high RF. Very few insects - alarmingly few.

What is it we feel?

### Anthony Smith — 2024-08-16T21:05:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ehs-sufferers.29159/post-338508>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=338489)
>
> ### [Effects of Radiofrequency Electromagnetic Radiation on Neurotransmitters in the Brain - PMC](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8415840/)
>
> With the rapid development of electronic information in the past 30 years, technical achievements based on electromagnetism have been widely used in various fields pertaining to human production and life. Consequently, electromagnetic radiation ...
>
>  www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

Thanks for posting this, proof nnEMF has all kinds of effects on brains/biology.

Note about the power level/scale they are using - milli-Watts/centimeter2 (mW/cm2) not µW/m2 . . .
1 mW/cm2 = 10,000,000 µW/m2 (Safe & Sound Pro2 uses µW/m2))

Most of the tests were done at very high power levels, levels I have never seen. I rarely see a reading above 0.3 mW/cm2 (3,000,000 µW/m2) But do do see this often -
When first turning on a cell phone or when just sitting at the airport.
I often get 0.15 mW/cm2 (1,500,000 µW.m2) from a cell phone call 1-2 inches from the phone. Distance from nearest tower will affect this, the further the tower the stronger the phone signal will be.

But a couple of these tests were done at 0.02 mW/cm2 (=200,000 µW/m2) a reading I see all the time. (Very close to this from Bluetooth headsets.) I wish they had tested lower (real world) levels for several hours a day for weeks or months, but that would probably jeopardize their grant money!

Keep in mind the Building Biology Institute (BBI) say RF below 0.1 µW/m2 is of no concern and above 1,000 µW/m2 (0.000,1 mW/cm2) is of extreme concern.

>

Hi Anthony,
Yes, power densities are quite high in most the studies with exception to the lower 0.02 mW/cm2 ones. This is a long standing issue about how regulators determine safe public exposure levels as the main focus is based on thermal effects (tissue heating) SAR etc. However, there are quite a number of studies that also look at the non-thermal effects, but this gets brushed under the carpet. Another way to look at it would be as a dosage e.g. power density over days, weeks, months, years etc as it would also have a cumulative effect, hence the chronic health issues. This effect could be partially offset with adequate sun light (subject to environmental conditions), which is why this may be useful for gen pop of indoor living etc for the worst case ( to account for no light controls).

I assume you are already across the information through the Bio initiative.org. Links below -

### [BioInitiative Report 2012: Table of Contents](https://bioinitiative.org/table-of-contents/)

The BioInitiative Working Group report on electromagnetic fields (EMF) and health effects from power lines, cell phones, and wi-fi.

bioinitiative.org

### [Conclusions from the BioInitiative Report 2012](https://bioinitiative.org/conclusions/)

Several conclusions were reached from the BioInitiative Report 2012, including health effects caused by exposures to cell towers, wi-fi, EMF, RFR and more.

bioinitiative.org

### [Henry Lai's Research Summaries - BioInitiative Report 2012](https://bioinitiative.org/research-summaries/)

A collection of data regarding RFR scientific literature and free radical damage from RFR & ELF published between 1990-2012.

bioinitiative.org

### [RF Color Charts Summarizing Several Studies - BioInitiative Report 2012](https://bioinitiative.org/rf-color-charts/)

The RF Color Charts simply summarize several different studies reporting health effects relevant for cell towers, wireless devices and more.

bioinitiative.org

Quite a bit of research available at lower level exposures (including human studies).

Also, just in case you haven't come across this -

ETAIN project however is primarily based on SAR & TWA. Using this method/data sets - [https://www.nature.com/articles/s41370-021-00287-8](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41370-021-00287-8)

Link to RF "dose" calculator - [https://www.etainproject.eu/dose-calculator](https://www.etainproject.eu/dose-calculator)

### Mat.I — 2024-08-19T02:32:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ehs-sufferers.29159/post-338609>

## 11. SUMMARY, CONCLUSIONS AND PERSPECTIVES​

The findings documented in this review are as follows:

1\. The calcium hypothesis of Alzheimer’s disease (AD) argues that increased intracellular calcium (\[Ca2+\]i) is the central cause of AD.

2\. The primary mechanism of action of EMFs is the activation of VGCCs, with such activation producing rapid, in some cases almost instantaneous, increases in \[Ca2+\]i.

3\. Excessive \[Ca2+\]i causes each of the following specific and nonspecific important AD effects: Elevated levels of Aβ; elevated levels of APP; hyperphosphorylated tau protein and consequent neurofibrillary tangles; lowered memory function produced in part by lowered synaptic activity, including the disappearance of dendritic spines; increased apoptotic and autophagic cell death.

4\. AD may be caused, in part, by a vicious cycle with excessive \[Ca2+\]i causing excessive Aβ, which produces, in turn, excessive \[Ca2+\]i.

5\. EMF exposures in animal models of AD or neurodegeneration produce still additional changes that have roles in producing the changes described immediately above: lowered miR107, increased BACE1, elevated LC3-II, oxidative stress, deformed synaptic spines and bouton formation.

6\. Genetic and pharmacological studies show that VGCC activity itself has important role in causing AD. VGCC activity may be important not only for producing excessive \[Ca2+\]i but also because of direct VGCC protein-protein interactions impacting synaptic function.

7\. The two main pathways of action activated by \[Ca2+\]i producing pathophysiological effects, excessive calcium signaling and the peroxynitrite/free radical/oxidative stress/NF-kappaB/inflammation pathway each have essential roles in AD causation.

8\. 12 different occupational exposure studies have each shown that people in occupations causing them to be exposed to substantial EMF exposures have a higher AD prevalence.

9\. Very young people exposed to many hours per day of EMF exposures from Wi-Fi or cell phone usage develop digital dementia.

10\. There has been a decrease in the age of onset of AD in humans over the past 20 years or so, which has been suggested to be caused by increases in human EMF exposures.

11\. Studies reviewed by Tolgskaya and Gordon \[[60](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9189734/#r60)\] showed that rodents develop over a period of months widespread neurodegeneration from low intensity microwave frequency EMF exposures which impact many regions of the brain including the cerebral cortex and hippocampus, regions that are heavily impacted in human AD patients. This EMF caused rodent neurodegeneration resembles human neurodegeneration in having high levels of brain cell death, changes in the structure brain synapses and massive loss of synaptic connection.

12\. The El-Swefy _et al._ \[[61](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9189734/#r61)\] study showed that very low intensity mobile phone base station radiation produced massive widespread neurodegeneration in 4 weeks. 11 measured changes and 4 observed changes in this EMF-caused neurodegenerative process were each greatly lowered by the VGCC blocker amlodipine. Among the changes documented were loss of approximately 34% of the brain cells, oxidative stress, high levels of apoptosis and inflammation. Excessive \[Ca2+\]i acts _via_ excessive calcium signaling and the peroxynitrite pathway of ac- tion to produce each of the 11 measured changes neurodegenerative changes.

13\. While many of the studies on EMFs causing neurodegeneration in rodents did not measure any specific correlates of AD, four studies on EMFs causing AD in rats did measure such specific correlates. Two of these were discussed in detail \[[86](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9189734/#r86), [87](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9189734/#r87)\].

14\. A series of nanosecond EMF pulses given to two-month old rats produced universal of near universal very early onset AD \[[78](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9189734/#r78)\].

15\. When these same pulses were started at 2 months of age and continued each subsequent day, universal or near universal AD was seen eight months after the first day of pulsation – that is in 8 month old rats \[[79](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9189734/#r79)\]. Some signs of AD are seen after only four months after the beginning of irradiation. Because rats typically live to be 36 months old, these EMF pulses are causing universal or near universal very very very early onset AD. The El-Swefy _et al._ \[[61](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9189734/#r61)\] findings are even worse – there irradiation started in 4 to 5 month old rats and massive neurodegeneration was evident after only 28 days of irradiation.

16\. If humans were to succumb to very, very, very early onset AD at a similar fraction of the human life span as these rats do from EMF exposure \[[79](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9189734/#r79)\], then we might expect humans to come down with EMF-caused AD at roughly 21 years of age.

17\. The rat studies of EMF causation of AD \[[78](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9189734/#r78), [79](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9189734/#r79)\] clearly show that EMFs lower the latency period of AD and epidemiological studies suggest this is also true in humans.

18\. The very early AD onset found in a study \[[78](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9189734/#r78)\] and the very very very early onset AD or other neurodegeneration found in a few studies \[[61](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9189734/#r61), [79](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9189734/#r79)\] should be viewed as remarkable in another context. Up until 30 years ago, early onset AD in humans was caused mainly by relatively rare mutations that each have powerful causal roles in AD \[[23](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9189734/#r23)\]. However, we see here universal or near universal very or very, very, very early onset AD being caused solely by EMF pulses.

These eighteen findings provide a specific type of evidence arguing that EMFs cause AD, possibly including very very very early onset AD in humans. These must be interpreted in conjunction with two other important findings:

Electronically generated EMFs are coherent, being emitted at a specific frequency, in a specific direction with a specific polarity and phase and for that reason, they generate vastly higher electrical forces and time varying magnetic forces than natural incoherent EMFs \[[22](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9189734/#r22)\]. Those time varying magnetic forces are very highly penetrating and can act both directly and indirectly to place strong forces on the electrical charges on the VGCC voltage sensor to activate the channels \[[22](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9189734/#r22)\]. They can, therefore, produce very highly penetrating effects deep in the human body even when they are present at millimeter wave frequencies where electric fields are largely absorbed in the outer 1 mm or so of the body. Five studies cited in another study \[[22](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9189734/#r22)\] showed that non-pulsed MM-waves produced EEG changes in the human brain, with four of them also showing other neurological effects in the human brain, confirming the presence of penetrating effects.

Pulsations, both modulating pulses and pure nanosecond pulses produce much higher effects than non-pulsed EMFs of the same average intensity. Moreover, the entire telecommunications industry is in a big push towards greater and greater pulsation in order to carry larger and larger amounts of information. This includes, of course, 2G leading to 3G leading to 4G leading to 5G and, in addition, smart meters, smart cities and even smart highways, _etc._ This push for smarter, ever more highly pulsed devices may well be leading us to the ultimate disaster: universal or near universal very very very onset AD in human populations. Both animal and human studies, discussed above, show that EMFs can not only greatly increase the incidence of AD but can also decrease the latency period; the human latency of AD may decrease from about 25 years to perhaps 5 or 10 years. This means that it is possible that exposures we already have, 5G and possibly also 4G and smart meters, may have already caused the ultimate disaster, but we do not know it yet because we are still in the latency period.

The confluence of these changes of our understanding of EMFs put us at great risk of producing this ultimate disaster in the light of the 19 different findings regarding EMFs and AD listed above: the coherence of electronically generated EMFs, which produce vastly greater electrical and time varying magnetic forces than do natural incoherent EMFs \[[22](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9189734/#r22)\]; the high level sensitivity of the voltage sensor controlling the VGCCs and other voltage gated ion channels to those forces; the importance of pulsation; and the push to faster and faster modulating pulsation in order to carry ever more information together put us at unprecedented risk.

As per Arthur Firstenberg's work - it is noted that neither low or high power microwave RF are safe on the basis that the greater the power = more heat however less power results in more leakage of the blood-brain-barrier and the more calcium that leaks out of cells.

As Jack said this is due to _Quantum foam. Causes more perturbation of the atoms in a cell = less favourable thermodynamics. Covered in the Ricci podcast.

Potassium needs to stay bound tightly within cells in order for intracellular water to remain structured to maintain semiconduction. When potassium is released water loses its structure and you rely on the ancient evolutionary system of the ATPase, lowering your margin of safety in disease avoidance._

I am writing this thread to reach out to anyone who might be able to help me in my desire to move to El Salvador. To anyone who is presently living in El Salvador or is enroute in the next few weeks, I would like to schedule a visit to the country to have a look see at the environment, the people, and the possible land opportunities. My finances are such that I do need to find a small place to hang my hat on to rest, car rental, and where to stay economically. Health wise, I need to be near the salt water to ground and walk about, and don't mind driving into the hills to sleep at night. It would be great to connect with someone who is already in El Salvador and has made the transition or making the transition from the U.S. to the tropics. My email is ([healthyassurance@gmail.com](mailto:healthyassurance@gmail.com)), and from there I can provide a phone number to call when possible. I thank you kindly for your input regarding this S.O.S, message in a bottle. Michael Moreau
