### John Patrick — 2024-07-16T17:27:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-michael-moreau.29160/post-337220>

Thanks to you Jack the Administrator, I can look at other options a bit more but have to budget a trip as best I can. Also, the car I was considering bringing to El Salvador when I make the big more, is a 2002 Honda CRV I bought for 5 grand in 2018, so Kelley Blue Book is about 1,200, and the tax bringing it in country someone wrote in chat is like 60%. That is rather insignificant from my point of reference, if they allow that age of car into the country. I had my mechanic do a complete on it, so it is roadworthy. When I go the call 4 years ago that my brother was not going to live more than two weeks, from a doctor in Blake Hospital, I drove cross country from Washington to Florida, in it and slept in it at truck stops, so I am used to that form of making ends meet. and must get to the tropics to see if this challenge with the cramping feet is, a returning of what some call Post Polio Sequalae. Walking is becoming really difficult. Your mention of SV-40 in the Ruben-Kruse-Kennedy photo (Assembly 2023) video - got my attention big time. Having been paralyzed with so-called Polio at age 5, in 1955, I spent months recuperating in Port Jefferson Hospital, then was released barely, on a wing and a prayer. Only then to be inoculated with 5 subsequent Salk Vaccines, and one Sabin Sugar Cube. I thought natural immunity was good enough as nature intended. If so why would the lab coat boys need to sextuple down. Doing the best I can with this script from genocide.

### pfedorow — 2024-07-16T22:30:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-michael-moreau.29160/post-337224>

> [John Patrick said:](/goto/post?id=337220)
>
> Thanks to you Jack the Administrator, I can look at other options a bit more but have to budget a trip as best I can. Also, the car I was considering bringing to El Salvador when I make the big more, is a 2002 Honda CRV I bought for 5 grand in 2018, so Kelley Blue Book is about 1,200, and the tax bringing it in country someone wrote in chat is like 60%. That is rather insignificant from my point of reference, if they allow that age of car into the country. I had my mechanic do a complete on it, so it is roadworthy. When I go the call 4 years ago that my brother was not going to live more than two weeks, from a doctor in Blake Hospital, I drove cross country from Washington to Florida, in it and slept in it at truck stops, so I am used to that form of making ends meet. and must get to the tropics to see if this challenge with the cramping feet is, a returning of what some call Post Polio Sequalae. Walking is becoming really difficult. Your mention of SV-40 in the Ruben-Kruse-Kennedy photo (Assembly 2023) video - got my attention big time. Having been paralyzed with so-called Polio at age 5, in 1955, I spent months recuperating in Port Jefferson Hospital, then was released barely, on a wing and a prayer. Only then to be inoculated with 5 subsequent Salk Vaccines, and one Sabin Sugar Cube. I thought natural immunity was good enough as nature intended. If so why would the lab coat boys need to sextuple down. Doing the best I can with this script from genocide.

Believe you can only import like cars as old as 10-12 years and its expensive. Better to sell and buy here

### drsongihan — 2024-07-16T22:35:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-michael-moreau.29160/post-337225>

> [John Patrick said:](/goto/post?id=337219)
>
> Dr. Songihan, thank you for your reply ever so kindly. How might I contact Maria Theresa, (MT), does she have an email address, or do I call her on a phone. I will look up Atami. You mention (need), WhatsApp for contacting people, so I should get that app and Maria will be listed in that app in some way, shape, or fashion. I am very new to the app tech world, as you can tell. Now, what about Waze. Is that another sort of app on the phone, or something else I need to learn about. So, it seems a phone is critical to getting about anywhere these days. I presume you are in El Salvador now, and what do folks do about food if they rent a room. Hot plate set up, kitchenette, or just eat whatever at a grocery store or farmers market? Water sources might be bottle from the store or tap in the rented room? Thank you for your information. It gives me a point to put on the map.

I think I can post here
Maria Theresa: +503 7682 7715
You can message her per WhatsApp. She has a room with air conditioning. It’s 5 min walking to a beach with magnetic sand. They have shared kitchen and shared washer. The kitchen has everything so you don’t need to buy anything, but its community one. You can buy grocery with your car and they also have a once a week organic food delivery that you can sign up for. If you stay with Maria Theresa she has clean water available in bottles and in big water dispensers. She offers eggs gratis but the rest you would buy and put into their fridge.
It’s 20 min walk to 5 min walk to the Bitcoin Farmers market on Sundays 9-12!
Oscar told me he has another car for 20 a day. You need to use WhatsApp.
Oscar: +503 7118 5291
Think of WhatsApp like texting and Waze like Google map but works better.

Dr. Songihan, wow, what a treasure trove of info to get me started in going forward. You are so kind to send this important info my way via the thread of Dr. Jack. Talk about a "lifeline", well you provided a wonderful way to introduce me to El Salvador.

Hello pfedorow: when you mention better to sell in U.S. where I live now, and "Buy here", meaning El Salvador, can you describe your experience in "buying" a car (if you have) in El Sal, how old the cars are, what condition, average price, just a few pointers. Meaning, if I buy a car, where do I go, a big city, a local garage off the beaten path, I have no idea what the landscape or terrain looks like yet, so I am thinking that everywhere a person goes, there are old houses, chickens running around, a gas station every 10 miles away, small grocery stores, or is it like modern city life?

### pfedorow — 2024-07-17T18:02:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-michael-moreau.29160/post-337261>

> [John Patrick said:](/goto/post?id=337248)
>
> Hello pfedorow: when you mention better to sell in U.S. where I live now, and "Buy here", meaning El Salvador, can you describe your experience in "buying" a car (if you have) in El Sal, how old the cars are, what condition, average price, just a few pointers. Meaning, if I buy a car, where do I go, a big city, a local garage off the beaten path, I have no idea what the landscape or terrain looks like yet, so I am thinking that everywhere a person goes, there are old houses, chickens running around, a gas station every 10 miles away, small grocery stores, or is it like modern city life?

I don't own one yet here, but I believe many members got Jeremy Franco to help out. Also, there is a company called [Bitcoin cars](https://www.instagram.com/bitcoincars_elsalvador) that helped a few people i know get a car.

There are lots of accident recovery vehicles here, so it is great to get a mechanic and a report on the vehicle before you purchase it.

It is pretty modern, but there are lots of time warps from the 1970s here. It is growing fast and changing even faster. There are lots of little tiendas to buy small things, but you probably want to do grocery runs to get what you need.

Anthonys post reminds me: bring an Ethernet cable and whatever connector from Ethernet to your computer and phone. Even 10 years ago we did not have WiFi everywhere but now it’s an exception to not have it. MT ‘s place has WiFi turned off but you can easily connect via hardwire to the internet.

### Laudy Cincotta — 2024-07-18T02:59:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-michael-moreau.29160/post-337281>

> [John Patrick said:](/goto/post?id=337198)
>
> Hey this is Michael Moreau, and I attended the Jack Kruse Q & A regarding financial issues about the globe, on July 14th, yesterday, 2024. I am writing this thread to reach out to anyone who might be able to help me in my desire to move to El Salvador. To anyone who is presently living in El Salvador or is enroute in the next few weeks, I would like to schedule a visit to the country to have a look see at the environment, the people, and the possible land opportunities. My finances are such that I do need to find a small place to hang my hat on to rest, car rental, and where to stay economically. Health wise, I need to be near the salt water to ground and walk about, and don't mind driving into the hills to sleep at night. It would be great to connect with someone who is already in El Salvador and has made the transition or making the transition from the U.S. to the tropics. My email is ([healthyassurance@gmail.com](mailto:healthyassurance@gmail.com)), and from there I can provide a phone number to call when possible. I thank you kindly for your input regarding this S.O.S, message in a bottle. Michael Moreau

MIchael the advice from the others is great, take it and run with it.

My advice is not to worry and jump in and figure it out when you get here, there is plenty of help and DO NOT be afraid.

If i were in your shoes i would start at Maria Theresas because this is a scouting trip and majority of the community are in and around Atami where she is, so you have many people to help out, answer questions and point you in the right direction.

Use MT's as a base and drive around wherever you want to look at from there. Organise day trips and have fun.

She could organise a cheap rental from what i see. I leaned on Jeremy Franco and would always recommend him, i use his mechanic and they sourced me a car, until then i rented from the mechanic Jeremias. Lovely guy.

Remember when you get here that this is a developing nation so its not what you might be used to.
Infrastructure is improving all the time. The major highways are now amazing and still being extended. Lots of potholes in side streets but you just dodge them!

Your comment 'I have no idea what the landscape or terrain looks like yet, so I am thinking that everywhere a person goes, there are old houses, chickens running around, a gas station every 10 miles away, small grocery stores, or is it like modern city life?'

Expect old run down houses with lots of new development happening so a mix. Its very poor and the houses are very basic for many.

City more modern, lots of shopping malls if thats what you require and they will be familiar. Lots of little stores to buy produce daily, bigger shopping trips into the city to stock up.

Yes to chickens running around, as well as horses, cows, pigs, ducks etc in my area, i am not sure as much in Atami but you will definitely see them! I love it still and don't think i would get sick of it except the stray dogs drive me demented but in time i am sure that will change too.

Lots of houses with razor wire - remnants from the years of civil war and gang violence.

Gas stations popping up everywhere as are more local shopping spots.

Wonderful and very helpful local Salvadorans, beautiful people with very little, very kind, it's been a very humbling experience and a privilege to be living amongst them.

You will make friends no matter where you chose to live, locals are wonderful and you have a car so can drive to meet community.

I live 1.5 hours away and can go in sometimes 3 times a week to the city or La Libertad and its not a problem, i love where i live but its not for everyone, point being find a place that you love, that suits your needs, that fits into your budget and then build your community. It will happen quicker and easier than you are thinking.

Keep asking questions and everyone will help, just book your trip and the rest will fall into place.

Good luck, you are going to be fine, you have already made the hardest decision which is to relocate, the rest will happen as it needs to.

Laudy, thank you for your enthusiastic support and directive information as it is easy to get used to your descriptions of practical life in the country. When I went to Playa Del Carmen (Yucatan), two and a half years ago to check it out for my health, I stayed at a youth Hostel. Unbeknownst to me, the place turned into a disco-inferno, and they proceeded to party till 4 or 5 in the am. So, after the warm golden sunset dimed at 8 or so pm, just as I was falling asleep, the rocking of the house began. Needless to say, I had to deal with that for two weeks and really got exhausted. I will not repeat that option. I am actually looking toward the reality of seeing the little critters you mentioned as landscape wildlife that mother nature created for us to enjoy. Alltami and MTs seems to be a good choice to get some perspective and "venture" out on day hops. However, your area is also a consideration. Here's why, your area might just have a small chunk of land, and I mean small chunk, that my son and I can build a small "tiny house" on like my friend here in the states builds for the Veterans. I am looking into the future a bit I know, but a mitochondriac does that when the idea presents itself. As an outsider, I understand that flying into El Salvador, a person books a flight from the U.S, to San Salvador, then must get to your side of the country by either of three ways. Rent a car... take a long bus ride... or get a driver like Uber to drive to El Salvador. How much does that driver to "El Sal" usually cost, and If I am going through the city proper of San Salvador, why not arrange to stop at the place where they issue a NIT card, in case I wish to purchase a car, property, or a business. When I was in the Yucatan, and spoke very little Spanish, it was easy to get "taken" for various things like even renting a car, then at the markets. It appears that you are describing people who are more "at ease" with gringos from other nations and rather friendly on many levels. So, is language an issue? Jack on the call mentioned that app for translating to Spanish which makes life very easy. I will take your advice and "chill" with the overthinking and just "do". Making the arrangements, booking the ticket, getting the place to stay, are first and foremost to launch my program. What area of El Sal is considered your neighborhood, so I can look and see how far it is from the Altima beach scene. Thank you, Michael.

### drsongihan — 2024-07-19T03:56:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-michael-moreau.29160/post-337311>

> Laudy, thank you for your enthusiastic support and directive information as it is easy to get used to your descriptions of practical life in the country. When I went to Playa Del Carmen (Yucatan), two and a half years ago to check it out for my health, I stayed at a youth Hostel. Here's why, your area might just have a small chunk of land, and I mean small chunk, that my son and I can build a small "tiny house" on like my friend here in the states builds for the Veterans. I am looking into the future a bit I know, but a mitochondriac does that when the idea presents itself. As an outsider, I understand that flying into El Salvador, a person books a flight from the U.S, to San Salvador, then must get to your side of the country by either of three ways. Rent a car... take a long bus ride... or get a driver like Uber to drive to El Salvador. How much does that driver to "El Sal" usually cost, and If I am going through the city proper of San Salvador, why not arrange to stop at the place where they issue a NIT card, in case I wish to purchase a car, property, or a business. When I was in the Yucatan, and spoke very little Spanish, it was easy to get "taken" for various things like even renting a car, then at the markets. It appears that you are describing people who are more "at ease" with gringos from other nations and rather friendly on many levels. So, is language an issue? Jack on the call mentioned that app for translating to Spanish which makes life very easy. I will take your advice and "chill" with the overthinking and just "do". I second Laudy. I would say you cannot answer these questions from afar. You just need to jump. And then see for yourself.

There are cheap and expensive options to get residency but all of that takes a bit of time, and there is a procedure that requires background check and a lawyer. All very simple, but nothing you can do from far. 10 days in a country I have never thought about coming, I feel like I got really strong connections thanks to the Krusers.

But it’s a ground game and contact sport. You cannot theorize any of that from afar. Book your flight and come to stay with MT. Text her you want to stay say a week or a month. In fact I recommend a month. It’s 60 or 85 a day or 850 or 1000 a month. So you can do the math that the per month option is cheaper. In fact for a start it does not get much cheaper since it also comes with walking distance to the beach, fully equipped kitchen and a strong community. You don’t need to worry about night life or WiFi. After 7 pm it sleepy time since people are up with the sunrise around 5.30 am.

1\. Book a flight.
2\. Contact MT.
3\. Ask her to pick you up. It’s $50 and it does not get much cheaper. Or contact Oscar and have him meet you at the airport with the rental. If you do, arrive during daytime. It’s not easy to drive into Atami residential in the dark the first time. There is ok street lighting.
4\. Bring Cash and bitcoin
5\. Bring Ethernet, bring a phone with Waze and WhatsApp and a plan that allows you to use your US phone for free in ES. ATT or Telekom.
6\. If there is “stuff” you like, such as freshly ground coffee, cotton clothes or leather flip flop, whatever is important to you for daily life you have to bring it. I brought a coffee grinder, coffee press, high quality mountain salt, a wine opener, a sharp knife and an oyster knife. You can tell where my priorities lie.  there is no Amazon or Target here, so you cannot just pop into a shop and buy conveniences.

Visiting Costa Del Sol, buying a land … none of that works from afar.

So as Jack would say, buy the damn ticket and show us you did, and make sure it’s like in a few days. No need to wait much longer.

> There are cheap and expensive options to get residency but all of that takes a bit of time, and there is a procedure that requires background check and a lawyer. All very simple, but nothing you can do from far. 10 days in a country I have never thought about coming, I feel like I got really strong connections thanks to the Krusers.
>
> But it’s a ground game and contact sport. You cannot theorize any of that from afar. Book your flight and come to stay with MT. Text her you want to stay say a week or a month. In fact I recommend a month. It’s 60 or 85 a day or 850 or 1000 a month. So you can do the math that the per month option is cheaper. In fact for a start it does not get much cheaper since it also comes with walking distance to the beach, fully equipped kitchen and a strong community. You don’t need to worry about night life or WiFi. After 7 pm it sleepy time since people are up with the sunrise around 5.30 am.
>
> 1\. Book a flight.
> 2\. Contact MT.
> 3\. Ask her to pick you up. It’s $50 and it does not get much cheaper. Or contact Oscar and have him meet you at the airport with the rental. If you do, arrive during daytime. It’s not easy to drive into Atami residential in the dark the first time. There is ok street lighting.
> 4\. Bring Cash and bitcoin
> 5\. Bring Ethernet, bring a phone with Waze and WhatsApp and a plan that allows you to use your US phone for free in ES. ATT or Telekom.
> 6\. If there is “stuff” you like, such as freshly ground coffee, cotton clothes or leather flip flop, whatever is important to you for daily life you have to bring it. I brought a coffee grinder, coffee press, high quality mountain salt, a wine opener, a sharp knife and an oyster knife. You can tell where my priorities lie.  there is no Amazon or Target here, so you cannot just pop into a shop and buy conveniences.
>
> Visiting Costa Del Sol, buying a land … none of that works from afar.
>
> So as Jack would say, buy the damn ticket and show us you did, and make sure it’s like in a few days. No need to wait much longer.

3\. Can get to and from airport for 35 -50.
5\. can get an el Salvador Sim for under 5 , depending what you need package is around 12 for the month
6\. Maybe the leather flip flops but all of the rest you can get here. Vidris and the mall has a lot of that stuff and some coffee shops have all the tools you need. Decent salt at the green corner.if your really picky some delivery services from the USA that will bring your stuff with a charge per lb usually

### John Patrick — 2024-07-20T22:56:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-michael-moreau.29160/post-337398>

I am thanking everyone for providing information to me in this Forum, thank you... I am planning to get to El Salvador and stay for at least 4 weeks or in days, 28. I can only be out of the USA due to Social Security Disability regulations for under 30 days, otherwise they review my details and can cut off the tap. So, this whole move to thing is really touchy, and I will have to come see, then plan, then make the shift, and become a part of a new life. My timeline is to consider a move first to situate my brother in August, then visit El Sal in August or September, then make the move when I can see a small place, find a small rental, or something to live in. A small slice of land I can build a "tiny house" on to relocated to. But I am thinking too far out-front. Long range, get my son involved as a journeyman carpenter in the union, to come and build a few of these small units according to specs in El Sal, then be the person who rents them to others, offering EMF and Wi-Fi free dwellings, 6-inch construction for ease of heat and AC. My friend has already built 20 of these "tiny houses" for Veterans in the U.S. and is considering many more with the help of the state. Right now, it's a dream in the making, but now I know why I had to help him get a bunch of Veterans assembled from different churches to offer to help him clear the land. Had no real idea why I had to do it, but it is clear as can be. all these years later.

### Laudy Cincotta — 2024-07-21T05:04:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-michael-moreau.29160/post-337407>

> [John Patrick said:](/goto/post?id=337292)
>
> Laudy, thank you for your enthusiastic support and directive information as it is easy to get used to your descriptions of practical life in the country. When I went to Playa Del Carmen (Yucatan), two and a half years ago to check it out for my health, I stayed at a youth Hostel. Unbeknownst to me, the place turned into a disco-inferno, and they proceeded to party till 4 or 5 in the am. So, after the warm golden sunset dimed at 8 or so pm, just as I was falling asleep, the rocking of the house began. Needless to say, I had to deal with that for two weeks and really got exhausted. I will not repeat that option. I am actually looking toward the reality of seeing the little critters you mentioned as landscape wildlife that mother nature created for us to enjoy. Alltami and MTs seems to be a good choice to get some perspective and "venture" out on day hops. However, your area is also a consideration. Here's why, your area might just have a small chunk of land, and I mean small chunk, that my son and I can build a small "tiny house" on like my friend here in the states builds for the Veterans. I am looking into the future a bit I know, but a mitochondriac does that when the idea presents itself. As an outsider, I understand that flying into El Salvador, a person books a flight from the U.S, to San Salvador, then must get to your side of the country by either of three ways. Rent a car... take a long bus ride... or get a driver like Uber to drive to El Salvador. How much does that driver to "El Sal" usually cost, and If I am going through the city proper of San Salvador, why not arrange to stop at the place where they issue a NIT card, in case I wish to purchase a car, property, or a business. When I was in the Yucatan, and spoke very little Spanish, it was easy to get "taken" for various things like even renting a car, then at the markets. It appears that you are describing people who are more "at ease" with gringos from other nations and rather friendly on many levels. So, is language an issue? Jack on the call mentioned that app for translating to Spanish which makes life very easy. I will take your advice and "chill" with the overthinking and just "do". Making the arrangements, booking the ticket, getting the place to stay, are first and foremost to launch my program. What area of El Sal is considered your neighborhood, so I can look and see how far it is from the Altima beach scene. Thank you, Michael.

Hi Michael,

I totally understand your PDC experience, it is a phenomenon also experienced in parts of El Salvador and throughout Central America from what I have been told. You will find out when you stay in places to explore them and hopefully rule that out.

I totally understand your questions and had many myself.

Regarding the language, when living here you start to learn a little bit more by trying to communicate with locals and i use 'google translate' as my translator to help which is very useful, however, a lot of people here will try to communicate, even if its sign language, and ususally someone steps in that has a few words of english and you tend to sort it out.

Also download 'Waze' for driving, it will serve you well.

I organised and relied on Jeremy Franco to pick us up/drop us off.

My advice is maybe that is something you could do.

Speak with Jeremy before you get here and organise having his service for the day. Get a decent flight time into the country, get Jeremy to pick you up from the airport and take you to a government office to get your NIT immediately then he could drop you off at your accomodation at MT's.

It will save you time and a lot of the stress of doing it all yourself which i wouldn't advise until you start to figure things out.

My area is called Costa del Sol in La Paz region. I am 25 minutes from the airport and 1.5 hours from Atami and 1 - 1.5 hours from San Salvador depending on where i am going. I dont have a problem with the driving and find it easy to do.

There is a lot of area between Costa del Sol and Atami, as Jack said to you look at some places where the locals live without a large group of expats or tourists and it should be far more affordable.

Also there are areas a short drive from the beach suburbs that could be a compromise as well where you drive to the beach of a morning, i am sure you will find the spot that works for you.

All the best!

### Gene Kalmens — 2024-07-21T07:07:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-michael-moreau.29160/post-337408>

There is an additional info resource - a TG (telegram) group Bitcoiners in El Salvador. Telegram is another app popular as a group communication tool. The invite link is [https://t.me/bitcoinersinelsalvador](https://t.me/bitcoinersinelsalvador).

There are almost 2,000 members who discuss ALL practical aspects of life in and relocation to El Salvador. You will find businesses who offer useful services. For example, you can get a used car from a guy with a handle @BTCRonny. Francesco with a handle @moneydelics is the admin of this group.

Last year, I was able to experience first hand how kind and helpful the local bitcoiners are to the visitors and expats from the so called 1st world countries.

MT introduced me to a wise El Salvadorian men, Joaquin, who was a godsend to me. A wise and decentralized human who knows what the right thing to do is because he has his feet on the ground, literally, he has a beautiful outdoor oasis he built and follows his intuition and his nose as his guide. I was going to learn Spanish from him but I instead just learned life lessons from him.

Joaquin told me that the health care in the state run hospital is free. There is a small clinic in El Tunco for “snake bites” that is for free and the larger hospital is near Playa San Diego, right around the roundabout between Attami and going up towards San Salvador. That clinic is for free and you will be cared for. If you go to a private practice what you pay out of pocket is less than what would be your deductible in the US.

According to Joaquin the healthcare system is good there and I believe him!

# Thread: WHY DID WE DELETE VITAMIN C GENES AND WHAT DOES UV GUT HEALTH HAVE TO DO WITH IT?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-did-we-delete-vitamin-c-genes-and-what-does-uv-gut-health-have-to-do-with-it.29163/>

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-16T23:17:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-did-we-delete-vitamin-c-genes-and-what-does-uv-gut-health-have-to-do-with-it.29163/post-337227>

The next big question in DMs and emails I got from this pod was how did we delete Vitamin C genes and how was it related to UV light and hair loss?

ANSWER: How do you get past the nuclear membrane once you electromagnetically make Vitamin D in the skin? Sunlight energy is also used to sulfate cholesterol FIRST to get the Vitamin D train rolling in the skin to the blood to get to nDNA. When it is sulfated, it becomes water soluble in blood. When it isn't sulfated, your liver responds to protect the nucleus in cells, and it upregulates LDL production to act like a sponge to protect the AMO physics inside of cells. So, when your centralized dermatologist tells you to stay out of the sun and take the secosteroid Vitamin D from a plant or animal, it is also packaged in seed oil because it is normally a fat-soluble compound. Few realize that the simple act of taking D3 pills and living indoors behind glass walls is raising your LDL cholesterol constantly. BigHarma knew it, which is why BigHarma taught every doctor since they found the evidence in the 1950s military documents that they could profit massively by training doctors ignorant of light's effects to put their patients on statins. They knew to blame it on biochemistry gone awry and baited the PhDs with the papers on the mevalonate pathway Q cycle. The mevalonate pathway is a sequence of cellular reactions leading to farnesyl pyrophosphate, the common substrate for the synthesis of cholesterol, dolichol, dolichyl phosphate, and CoQ10, and for protein prenylation (a post-translational modification necessary for the targeting and function of many proteins). When you review the history of the evidence, you know they are playing you like a fiddle. You are their mouse, and they are the cat. Few see the game plan and less of you get it. Cholesterol and Vitamin D3 are nearly identical in chemical structure. Most people need to learn this. Sunlight naturally sulfates these things (pic below). The only difference in both bio-molecules is a single, double bond in the second ring of the cholesterol backbone. Remember, light is BURIED at the electronic level in all things, including cholesterol and Vitamin D. Your pills are sold to you soaked in seed oils because it is a fat-soluble vitamin. But do we transport it as such? NOPE. The act of seed oil addition is done on purpose by design because BigHarma knows what deuterium vs hydrogen can do. They've known about the effects of deuterium in drug delivery because of the physical chemistry trials they have done. They never disclosed this to their curriculums to medical students or pharmacy students by design. This is why they want centralized pharmacies like WalMart, CVS, and Walgreens versus compounding pharmacies. When the drugs are ready-made, all one needs to do is count the atrophy of one's chemistry skills. A compounding pharmacist is an alchemist of physical chemistry. They are more apt to realize what Pfizer and Moderna are up to in New Jersey factories. The AMO physics of these two biomolecules are nearly identical, with one exception. HYDROGEN. This gives Vitamin D3 one less hydrogen atom than the closed ring of cholesterol has. If you spent any time reading my Tensegrity 6 blog about my original hacks of the periodic table I did 20 years ago, it should make you think a lot more carefully about atomic details. One hydrogen is the only difference at the atomic scale between them, but from the quantum thermodynamic perspective, you learn that there is a shit ton of energy buried in hydrogen's bond at the electronic and vibrational level. These are the bonds that food gurus and biochemists do not see, much less understand, so they ignore it at your peril. Here is why public health goes awry: because of a lack of focus on what really matters in a cell. Here, you will see the wisdom of my lessons in the Tensegrity 6 blog at play. Look it all: the charge density sunlight adds to the system that a pill cannot. See, never divorce biophysics from biochemistry. If you do, you lose. The photonic energy in sunlight is used to oxidize hydrogen sulfide to make sulfate. This process sulfates everything at our surfaces and just below the surface, and this makes many things water soluble and lowers charge density. Sunscreen was innovated by BigHarma to block sulfation by design. When you block sulfation, you create a need for more drugs because you lower the tissues in energy, and this exacerbates the timing mismatch in mtDNA. This fuels more mitochondrial mutations and creates disease phenotypes, which fuels their centralized business models. A cursory review of what sulfation does to photovoltaics tells you how Nature uses sulfation to get past a highly charged membrane. Sulfation from solar exposure makes the fat-soluble vitamin WATER soluble to travel in the blood with VDB. It discharges the charge density to ruin the capacitance of Vitamin D. This is how you enter the nDNA environment like a ghost using the physical chemistry and photochemistry of the nonvisual photoreceptor system in your skin. Nature uses more magic than sunlight. Melanin, Vitamin D, cholesterol, heparin, and nitric oxide all respond to the UV part of the spectrum when it is in sunlight. Few people know that the UV and blue-green parts of the spectrum work in unison to very locally vasodilate blood vessels in the skin. I showed people the melanopsin 2014 paper 1000 times, which showed that blue light is capable of dilating blood vessels, but not one person asked me about the AMO physics of the interaction. This area interested me as a neurosurgeon because I was looking for a mechanism to explain the queer things that happen in brain bleeds called subarachnoid hemorrhages. We see them in trauma and in aneurysm ruptures. I have always felt that a lack of light operating in unison is behind most of the cerebrovascular pathologies that neurosurgeons treat. It took me ten years to figure it out, but altered light signaling is why AVMs and aneurysms rupture. It is also why some people develop SAH with minor trauma. They are all symptoms of low redox power in the brain. Nitric oxide (NO) is a well-known “gasotransmitter” in the literature because a Nobel Prize was given for its discovery in 1992. It is a gas that acts focally and locally when it is released. It does not have global effects. This tells you something about TIMING, too. It acts RAPIDLY, not CHRONICALLY. Vitamin D actions act CHRONICALLY. This tells you that NO signaling is likely behind many light-induced chronic diseases. Electrified membranes absorb gas and turn it into many other biomolecules. Life figured this out 3.8 billion years ago. NO is a gaseous signaling molecule that has a unique ability to induce a relaxation of the artery wall and a resulting drop in blood pressure. Recall from my "Kruse for Dummies" lecture what I said about unique signals. They are how information is transferred in biological systems by Claude Shannon's theory of information. Everyone else is playing checkers, looking at biochemical pathways, while I am playing 4D chess because I understand how light operates in physical chemistry. Without full spectrum sunlight, endothelial dysfunction and peripheral arterial damage should be EXPECTED by the decentralized clinician. It is not. No one in medical curriculums looks at the data in BigHarma literature to see this data and much less understand the clinical significance. A lack of NO production at our integument and eye surfaces ALWAYS links PAD by way of intimal thickening = directly to cardiovascular dysfunction. The local effect becomes generalized in the entire organ as the lack of NO production gets worse under ALAN or nnEMF influence. This is why PAD is always linked to cardiovascular disease. The link is the aberrant use of the electromagnetic spectrum to communicate and create NO. Modern light RF and cell radiation impair the production of NO from arginine by eNOS. This, in turn, induces high blood pressure by causing endothelial dysfunction. Mitochondria are intimately involved in importing nitrogen into tissues to create the substrates that eventually become NO when sunlight is present. Nitrogen substrates are not created by direct eNOS synthesis. Nature provided a clue to me as to why humans got rid of Vitamin C genetically for glutathione in this AMO physics dance. When Vitamin C is missing in subcutaneous tissues, glutathione becomes more reactive with locally produced NO. This mimics a radical pair or triad effect we see in avian compass navigation. Here is more evidence of magnetochemistry in humans being used. When glutathione and nitric oxide are powered by terrestrial sunlight, this allows humans to produce S-nitroso glutathione (GSNO). I think this is why human primates lost the majority of their integumentary hair and absorbed more melanin from the hair follicle to the interior. This allowed the skin to become a better charge capacitor for the brain and heart by allowing the skin to become a depot station to store massive amounts of nitric oxide. This tells me magnetochemistry timing induced this evolutionary change. We never need genes to change this. DECENTRALIZED MEDICINE 101 is why we lost our hair and why we no longer have Vitamin C genes.

# Thread: WHY DID SILLY TALKING MONKEYS GET RID OF VITAMIN C AND THEIR HAIR?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-did-silly-talking-monkeys-get-rid-of-vitamin-c-and-their-hair.29164/>

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-16T23:20:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-did-silly-talking-monkeys-get-rid-of-vitamin-c-and-their-hair.29164/post-337228>

The next big question in DMs and emails I got from this pod was how did we delete Vitamin C genes and how was it related to UV light and hair loss?

ANSWER: How do you get past the nuclear membrane once you electromagnetically make Vitamin D in the skin? Sunlight energy is also used to sulfate cholesterol FIRST to get the Vitamin D train rolling in the skin to the blood to get to nDNA. When it is sulfated, it becomes water soluble in blood. When it isn't sulfated, your liver responds to protect the nucleus in cells, and it upregulates LDL production to act like a sponge to protect the AMO physics inside of cells.

So, when your centralized dermatologist tells you to stay out of the sun and take the secosteroid Vitamin D from a plant or animal, it is also packaged in seed oil because it is normally a fat-soluble compound.

Few realize that the simple act of taking D3 pills and living indoors behind glass walls is raising your LDL cholesterol constantly. BigHarma knew it, which is why BigHarma taught every doctor since they found the evidence in the 1950s military documents that they could profit massively by training doctors ignorant of light's effects to put their patients on statins. They knew to blame it on biochemistry gone awry and baited the PhDs with the papers on the mevalonate pathway Q cycle. The mevalonate pathway is a sequence of cellular reactions leading to farnesyl pyrophosphate, the common substrate for the synthesis of cholesterol, dolichol, dolichyl phosphate, and CoQ10, and for protein prenylation (a post-translational modification necessary for the targeting and function of many proteins).

When you review the history of the evidence, you know they are playing you like a fiddle. You are their mouse, and they are the cat. Few see the game plan and less of you get it. Cholesterol and Vitamin D3 are nearly identical in chemical structure. Most people need to learn this. Sunlight naturally sulfates these things (pic below). The only difference in both bio-molecules is a single, double bond in the second ring of the cholesterol backbone.

Remember, light is BURIED at the electronic level in all things, including cholesterol and Vitamin D. Your pills are sold to you soaked in seed oils because it is a fat-soluble vitamin. But do we transport it as such? NOPE. The act of seed oil addition is done on purpose by design because BigHarma knows what deuterium vs hydrogen can do. They've known about the effects of deuterium in drug delivery because of the physical chemistry trials they have done. They never disclosed this to their curriculums to medical students or pharmacy students by design.

This is why they want centralized pharmacies like WalMart, CVS, and Walgreens versus compounding pharmacies. When the drugs are ready-made, all one needs to do is count the atrophy of one's chemistry skills. A compounding pharmacist is an alchemist of physical chemistry. They are more apt to realize what Pfizer and Moderna are up to in New Jersey factories.

The AMO physics of these two biomolecules are nearly identical, with one exception. HYDROGEN. This gives Vitamin D3 one less hydrogen atom than the closed ring of cholesterol has. If you spent any time reading my Tensegrity 6 blog about my original hacks of the periodic table I did 20 years ago, it should make you think a lot more carefully about atomic details. One hydrogen is the only difference at the atomic scale between them, but from the quantum thermodynamic perspective, you learn that there is a shit ton of energy buried in hydrogen's bond at the electronic and vibrational level. These are the bonds that food gurus and biochemists do not see, much less understand, so they ignore it at your peril.

Here is why public health goes awry: because of a lack of focus on what really matters in a cell. Here, you will see the wisdom of my lessons in the Tensegrity 6 blog at play. Look it all: the charge density sunlight adds to the system that a pill cannot. See, never divorce biophysics from biochemistry. If you do, you lose. The photonic energy in sunlight is used to oxidize hydrogen sulfide to make sulfate. This process sulfates everything at our surfaces and just below the surface, and this makes many things water soluble and lowers charge density. Sunscreen was innovated by BigHarma to block sulfation by design.

When you block sulfation, you create a need for more drugs because you lower the tissues in energy, and this exacerbates the timing mismatch in mtDNA. This fuels more mitochondrial mutations and creates disease phenotypes, which fuels their centralized business models. A cursory review of what sulfation does to photovoltaics tells you how Nature uses sulfation to get past a highly charged membrane.

Sulfation from solar exposure makes the fat-soluble vitamin WATER soluble to travel in the blood with VDB. It discharges the charge density to ruin the capacitance of Vitamin D. This is how you enter the nDNA environment like a ghost using the physical chemistry and photochemistry of the nonvisual photoreceptor system in your skin. Nature uses more magic than sunlight.

Melanin, Vitamin D, cholesterol, heparin, and nitric oxide all respond to the UV part of the spectrum when it is in sunlight. Few people know that the UV and blue-green parts of the spectrum work in unison to very locally vasodilate blood vessels in the skin. I showed people the melanopsin 2014 paper 1000 times, which showed that blue light is capable of dilating blood vessels, but not one person asked me about the AMO physics of the interaction. This area interested me as a neurosurgeon because I was looking for a mechanism to explain the queer things that happen in brain bleeds called subarachnoid hemorrhages. We see them in trauma and in aneurysm ruptures.

I have always felt that a lack of light operating in unison is behind most of the cerebrovascular pathologies that neurosurgeons treat. It took me ten years to figure it out, but altered light signaling is why AVMs and aneurysms rupture. It is also why some people develop SAH with minor trauma. They are all symptoms of low redox power in the brain.

Nitric oxide (NO) is a well-known “gasotransmitter” RELEASED IN TISSUES BY UVA LIGHT. This is printed in the literature because a Nobel Prize was given for its discovery in 1992. It is a gas that acts focally and locally when it is released. It does not have global effects. This tells you something about TIMING, too. It acts RAPIDLY, not CHRONICALLY. Vitamin D actions act CHRONICALLY. This tells you that NO signaling is likely behind many light-induced chronic diseases. Electrified membranes absorb gas and turn it into many other biomolecules. Life figured this out 3.8 billion years ago. NO is a gaseous signaling molecule that has a unique ability to induce a relaxation of the artery wall and a resulting drop in blood pressure. Recall from my "Kruse for Dummies" lecture what I said about unique signals. They are how information is transferred in biological systems by Claude Shannon's theory of information.

Everyone else is playing checkers, looking at biochemical pathways, while I am playing 4D chess because I understand how light operates in physical chemistry. Without full spectrum sunlight, endothelial dysfunction and peripheral arterial damage should be EXPECTED by the decentralized clinician. It is not. No one in medical curriculums looks at the data in BigHarma literature to see this data and much less understand the clinical significance.

A lack of NO production at our integument and eye surfaces ALWAYS links PAD by way of intimal thickening = directly to cardiovascular dysfunction. The local effect becomes generalized in the entire organ as the lack of NO production gets worse under ALAN or nnEMF influence. This is why PAD is always linked to cardiovascular disease. The link is the aberrant use of the electromagnetic spectrum to communicate and create NO. Modern light RF and cell radiation impair the production of NO from arginine by eNOS.

This, in turn, induces high blood pressure by causing endothelial dysfunction.

Mitochondria are intimately involved in importing nitrogen into tissues to create the substrates that eventually become NO when sunlight is present. Nitrogen substrates are not created by direct eNOS synthesis. Nature provided a clue to me as to why humans got rid of Vitamin C genetically for glutathione in this AMO physics dance.

**When Vitamin C is missing in subcutaneous tissues, glutathione becomes more reactive with locally produced NO from SUNLIGHT.** This mimics a radical pair or triad effect we see in avian compass navigation.

Here is more evidence of magnetochemistry in humans being used. When glutathione and nitric oxide are powered by terrestrial sunlight, this allows humans to produce S-nitroso glutathione (GSNO).

I think this is why human primates lost the majority of their integumentary hair and absorbed more melanin from the hair follicle to the interior. This allowed the skin to become a better charge capacitor for the brain and heart by allowing the skin to become a depot station to store massive amounts of nitric oxide. This is why human immune T cells are so common in the skin and why leptin was placed in SQ fat. Other primates do not have these phenotypes even though their genomes are close to identical. This tells me magnetochemistry timing induced this evolutionary change. We never need genes to change this.

DECENTRALIZED MEDICINE 101 is why we lost our hair and why we no longer have Vitamin C genes.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-07-29T20:35:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shortness-of-breath-tightness-pressure-in-chest-area.29175/post-337728>

I can certainly believe it.

I've been to Trinidad 3 times in my lifetime. Every time I've gone I felt like a brand new person. I had a certain vitality unlike anything I've ever experienced. I slept like a rockstar also. Actually, the first time I went, I stayed in a remote location on the island without AC. Once I adapted to sleeping hot, it was strangely some of the best sleep I recall ever having in my life.

Every time I left Trinidad and returned to the US, my sleep went back to shit and very noticeable brain fog set back in.

Never could explain this phenomenon until now. My hunch was always that it was related to my phone, bc on these trips I did not use a phone. I would always have a phone, but I kept it powered off for the duration of the trip. At the time, I had no clue the effect could come from the phone signals themselves. I just assumed being docile and staring at a phone was the culprit.

Now that I have the knowledge I've learned here, this is easy to explain now. Trinidad doesn't have near the levels of nnEMF as we have in the states. And also, in the location I stayed at in Trinidad, the levels of blue light at night specifically were non existent. I could go outside and see about 10 times the number of stars that were visible back at my Louisiana home. The night sky was incredible without all the light pollution.

For reference, I went on these 3 trips between 2010 and 2015.

### Mat.I — 2024-07-30T07:52:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shortness-of-breath-tightness-pressure-in-chest-area.29175/post-337773>

> [Joseph LeBlanc said:](/goto/post?id=337641)
>
> Haven't taken any measurements so unfortunately, I have no baseline to work from
>
>
> The only major change when I began noticing the symptoms was summer break began (I am a school teacher). So, I am getting a lot less nnEMF and a lot more sun throughout the days. Sleep/wake routines have not changed
>
>

>
>
> I feel like sleep could be better. I definitely sleep better than when I lived in my apartment 2 years ago. My intuition tells me my sleep is not fully optimized yet though.
>
>
> In general, I don't have much anxiety. I would say the symptoms do make me anxious though.
>
>
> I currently do not consistently exercise. My lifestyle is a pretty lethargic one of late. I used to be very regimented with weightlifting and HIIT routines. I do a random HIIT workout maybe an average of once every 2 weeks.
>
>
> When I do the HIIT routine, my stamina is awful. Not near what it used to be. For reference, I have been doing these sparse HIIT routines outdoors and barefoot.
>
>
> Physically, energy seems fine. I do seem to be brain foggy often though
>
>
> I'm only at home these days. I return to work beginning of Aug

You likely already know this however, sometimes it is easier giving people advice than taking it your self.

#1 - would be to measure your environment to establish a baseline. Given how close the nearest cell tower is, this would be very important to determine what RF levels you are dealing with. In addition to this you need to be checking/measuring EF (including DE), touch currents where applicable & Mag Fields whilst simulating the normal working conditions vs sleep conditions e.g. breakers isolated etc.. You need to know exactly what you are being exposed to everyday/night.

#2 - all of the above should be measured in accordance with the BB standards/guidelines (all attached) with decent equipment.

#3 - re: cell towers - in AUS we can search for nearby cell towers to determine what frequencies they are using and distance from the target location. Is this available where you are? If so worth looking into also..

#4 - I have looked at the area you live in on google Earth - it looks quite dense and has an airport (Baton Rouge Metro Airport). This would not be good due to all the radar/comms that would be used. Are you close to the airport? As [@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/) as advised you are likely in a Mud Pit..

#5 - Consider how you are spending the majority of your of your days and think about what advice you would give to others on this.

#6 - look at getting to a better place. Any places further out of the city that are ok?

#7 - You advised that the symptoms started on your break - maybe this is a sign that where you live is no good.

You really need to measure your environment.
Also, hardwiring internet does not have to be "pretty" or "complicated". Just run ethernet cables from your router LAN port (with WIFI disabled) to where you need to use it (you can make it semi neat e.g. using tape or stick on square cable ducts etc. and run at construction intersections for neatness e.g. skirtings, cornices etc or use the roof cavity or underfloor space) Or just use as needed and coil up when done. You can buy the cable already terminated fitted with the necessary jacks. You can get decent Cat 7's that are very well shielded with the ability to ground them also.

No worries. If you don't have the time to research this area it may be best to have a professional EMF building biologist do an assessment for you, however the cost of the assessment may be close to the cost of the equipment.. I know in AUS most standard assessments range from $800 to $1500 (dependant on size area and level of assessment required) this cost could cover decent grade equipment that would last a very long time providing you take care of them. However, if diving into more specialised assessments it would be worth paying the consult rather than buying the equipment for a one off assessment. My advice - research as much as possible, start with the basics and learn how to measure your environment as this will be very valuable skill to have, especially in the world we are living in today..

### BabyBecca — 2024-07-18T04:17:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introduction.29176/post-337282>

Hi everyone! I live in Louisiana and work as a nurse practitioner. I discovered Dr. Kruse about three months ago (where have I been??) after someone sent me a link to a podcast that was discussing red light therapy. During this podcast the host mentioned Dr. Kruse name in regards to discussing "light", and since then I can't stop reading and learning all things Dr. Kruse. Seeking answers for personal health struggles for the past 20 years, has sent me down many paths including allopathic and functional medicine pathways, and still no answers. Hearing Dr. Kruse talk about "no sunglasses" and the importance of sunlight IN the eye resonated with me BIG time! I have spent over 30 years in healthcare and this was the FIRST time I had heard this, and had certainly never considered it lol. Let the ENLIGHTMENT continue.

# Thread: El Salvador Landlord Requiring WiFi Stays On For Security System!!!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-landlord-requiring-wifi-stays-on-for-security-system.29178/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### thejessallen — 2024-07-18T22:41:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-landlord-requiring-wifi-stays-on-for-security-system.29178/post-337301>

Hola! 4 days ago I moved to El Salvador

The landlord has security cameras around the property.

I’ve been turning WiFi off at night which supposedly disrupts the security system

He’s asking / sort of requiring - the WiFi stay on at night - which is not going to happen

He’s suggesting EMF blankets

The security camera box is in a small room off the master. Im putting to use my new measuring tools and there is not a lot of radiation coming from the box to reach my sleeping area. But doubtful a kill switch to the room would work as most likely it’s on the same cicuit

My suggestion is to hard wire camera system to Ethernet

Curious if anyone else had has same experience and or insights on how to approach this situation!

Gracias

### Anthony Smith — 2024-07-18T23:36:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-landlord-requiring-wifi-stays-on-for-security-system.29178/post-337302>

I do not know how your system works BUT it is a thought that the security box is just a receiver (or primarily a receiver) and the cameras are transmitting to the receiver. Measure and find out. The WiFi camera I just measured was very low power, Average 1µW/m2 and peaks were about 100µW/m2 which compared to a WiFi is nothing. IF the WiFi needs to be on, put a shield between it and your bedroom.

# Thread: ANIMAL DATA CAN NEVER BE APPLIED TO HUMANS: ALL NUTRITION DATA FLAWED
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/animal-data-can-never-be-applied-to-humans-all-nutrition-data-flawed.29182/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-19T14:52:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/animal-data-can-never-be-applied-to-humans-all-nutrition-data-flawed.29182/post-337326>

**_ANIMAL DATA CAN NEVER BE APPLIED TO HUMANS: THIS IS WHY ALL NUTRITION DATA FLAWED_**

Centralized science has no proper model. They use lab animals and they are useless to predict what will happen in us.

Why do centralized scientist need to scrap most of their beliefs around food and diet? The key reason is that human only trials will avoid the controversial issues concerning "experimental scaling". That is, can results of animal experiments be extrapolated to humans with accuracy? **Extrapolation is not possible at this time.
**
As an example, researcher Dr. Mary Ellen O'Connor from the Dept. of Psychology, University of Tulsa in the 1960s observed that the drug thalidomide was proven clinically safe using laboratory rats, although the effect on humans was tragic.
As another example, physician Sally Faith Dorfman from the Center for Disease Control, Atlanta noted that aspirin is lethal to laboratory rats. So, extrapolation can fail either way. The reason is simple: How we use magnetochemistry to control the flow of energy is radically different than any other living creature on Earth.

# Thread: "Measurement Problem" with Heart Rate Monitors for Exercise
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/measurement-problem-with-heart-rate-monitors-for-exercise.29183/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Olivert — 2024-07-19T16:39:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/measurement-problem-with-heart-rate-monitors-for-exercise.29183/post-337331>

Reading _Electromagnetism and Life_ by Robert Becker and Andrew Marino I could not overlook how EMF, especially nnEMF can affect our physiological responses. Which as avid exerciser obssed with measuring my performance brings me to a question: Does it even make sense to measure your heart rate with bluetooth based heart rate monitors like Polar H10 since they emit huge amounts of nnEMF that can affect heart rate and even performance itself by measuring it?

Based on reading about all the effects nnEMF has on our physiology I can only imagine that having such a blast of nnEMF directly on my chest while exercising can have impact on my physiology and hence on the readings but also possibly on adaptations, recovery and performance. I measured nnEMF of Polar H10 and it is in extreme levels.

What I would be interested is whether there have been any studies on this or how would one go about experimenting with acute or longterm effects of using bluetooth based HR monitors during exercise? I can only imagine that even if such studies were made they would be cancelled as there is no one to profit from it, only losing lots of profits if it shows any effects.

For record what I have been experimenting as N=1 in this regard is to do my exercise as I normally do and then just at the end briefly put on Polar H10 strap to measure my HR whether it is identical to my normal measurements when I wear it during the whole exercise session. This is hard to do and control for all factors, but I think I can see some difference but I might be just making it up. But on other hand data suggest that such strong EMF has to have some impact on our physiology and if you add stress from exercise to it...

### Mat.I — 2024-08-08T03:43:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/measurement-problem-with-heart-rate-monitors-for-exercise.29183/post-338177>

> [Olivert said:](/goto/post?id=337331)
>
> Reading _Electromagnetism and Life_ by Robert Becker and Andrew Marino I could not overlook how EMF, especially nnEMF can affect our physiological responses. Which as avid exerciser obssed with measuring my performance brings me to a question: Does it even make sense to measure your heart rate with bluetooth based heart rate monitors like Polar H10 since they emit huge amounts of nnEMF that can affect heart rate and even performance itself by measuring it?
>
> Based on reading about all the effects nnEMF has on our physiology I can only imagine that having such a blast of nnEMF directly on my chest while exercising can have impact on my physiology and hence on the readings but also possibly on adaptations, recovery and performance. I measured nnEMF of Polar H10 and it is in extreme levels.
>
> What I would be interested is whether there have been any studies on this or how would one go about experimenting with acute or longterm effects of using bluetooth based HR monitors during exercise? I can only imagine that even if such studies were made they would be cancelled as there is no one to profit from it, only losing lots of profits if it shows any effects.
>
> For record what I have been experimenting as N=1 in this regard is to do my exercise as I normally do and then just at the end briefly put on Polar H10 strap to measure my HR whether it is identical to my normal measurements when I wear it during the whole exercise session. This is hard to do and control for all factors, but I think I can see some difference but I might be just making it up. But on other hand data suggest that such strong EMF has to have some impact on our physiology and if you add stress from exercise to it...

I wouldn't use a chest strap with bluetooth. Wrist based is not very accurate, however on the basic garmin's you can disable all connectivity functions and it just uses the optical sensor (on the wrist). Generally, they may read ~10bpm higher than actual HR. I personally, wouldn't bother using any wearables to measure any of this. Just tune in and listen to the feedback your body is giving whilst exercising and don't get too obsessed with exercising in the first place.

# Thread: Mysterious labs despite healthy lifestyle
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mysterious-labs-despite-healthy-lifestyle.29184/>

**Posts:** 23
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sunman — 2024-07-19T17:20:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mysterious-labs-despite-healthy-lifestyle.29184/post-337333>

Hi everyone.
My name is Edi and I live in Delaware.
I lead a lifestyle as closely as possible to what Jack prescribes in his blogs as I've read them all twice. My health improved tremendously and stayed that way for about 9 years. I am a pilot, I used to eat popcorn daily on my flying trips, after the workday is over but also eat a lot of canned sardines and canned oysters as this is the only things available on short layovers. I drink Fiji water during my work day and RO filtered water with a drop of Flannagans Crystal Energy in it on overnights that I bring from home. I usually fast through dinner time until after flying is over. My only morning intake is a smoothie made from Maca root and coconut oil plus Mercola multivitamins and krill oil. About a year ago I started using ghee instead of coconut oil but had constant diarrhea since doing this but still persisted for six months and finally changed back to coconut oil last month. At night my smoothies consist of the Seven Treasure mushroom powder and msm + magnesium glycinate in the smoothie plus a Ancient earth minerals and vitamins k2+d3 and Cod liver oil. I watch the sunrise daily whenever possible comes out to about half the time and sunset as well. I spend 1-3 hours out in strong UV sunlight daily in warm months. I do occasional cold therapy. I have the EMR-TEK red light saunas in my home and use them in winter time. I sleep on a Magnetico sleep pad and under a emf reducing bed canopy while on grounded bedsheets. no wifi in my house.
About two years ago on my way home from a foreign country I started having pains in my leg thigh muscle but didn't think much of it. about a year later I had big pains in my stomach muscles on the right side (right side of the "six pack") and the pain seemed to migrate over time to the shoulders and then left side of stomach. This was in the aftermath of me using chlorine dioxide for a few months. the pains lasted for a few months and it was worst at night. Then it subsided for a few months and last winter my right knee swelled up above the joint and I got a bakers cycst thereafter with water accumulation. this persisted for about six months and now its migrating again first to my right arm joint then left arm join and left knee. Currently my right arm join seems fine but left arm joint and both knees are swollen up and hurt at night. My blood test shows all normal except: Antinuclear Ab by Multiplex is POSITIVE, Sedimentation Rate-Westergren is 29 (very high, 0-15 scale) and C-Reactive Protein, Quant is 36 (very high, 0-10 scale). see attached.
Last year I started eating tomatoes again after being off them for 9 years but have stopped now again. I ate a lot of tomatoes throughout last years summer and fall but have stopped doing so since then.
I have a lot of mosquitoes in my backyard and think that I might have contracted Lymes disease somehow but unsure. If it is Lyme then it may be late stage Lyme in which case what may be my treatment options.
Not sure if a 30 minute consult with Jack would help here as well. Trying to post in the ASK JACK forum but it wont allow me due to insufficient privileges despite being a Silver Member.
I would really appreciate any insight into why my body is undergoing this disease and what the possible solutions may be. See my labs results attached hereto.
God bless.
Edi.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-19T20:03:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/july-19th-2024-cyberattack.29185/post-337338>

I don't think you guys fully grasp how big this is.

Around a billion computers are bricked worldwide, mostly corporate ones. This isn't just an online service going down for a few hours.

Every affected computer needs to be rebooted in fail mode and have a driver manually removed.

Most corporate computers given to employees don't let users do this themselves. Even if they could, imagine every single double-digit IQ wagie trying to handle a moderately complex task when many don't even know what a file is anymore.

I can't stress enough the scale of this happening.

My son was stranded in ATL Airport coming to El Salvador during this event.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-19T20:03:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/july-19th-2024-cyberattack.29185/post-337339>

### Michalis — 2024-07-19T22:47:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/july-19th-2024-cyberattack.29185/post-337351>

Not sure if it was by omission or bad planning or whatever. I am not 100% sure if it was a cyber attack / supply chain attack. I think we will find out soon so this is work in progress. I am well aware about the WEF cyberattack warnings etc etc. Right now I am going to keep an open mind about it.

To Jack's point It is big because you can see the scale of the fuckup if it is used maliciously. Who knows, they might have been testing the waters. As I said I keep an open mind. What happened was benign as far as I am concerned because it can be easily recovered by a tech support guy that gets their hand on the device and can follow 4-5 steps to remove the file.

On anothet note you can do things that can shut down everything. You can literally brick systems and wont be recovered unless they are rebuilt.

Generally I see a lot of incompetence in the field. A lot of cockups happening everywhere and all the time. There are a lot of people who are just not doing their job properly. They don't care. I see this every day and it makes my life harder at work. It also spikes my blood pressure which is not only a blue light issue. Literally people's attitudes making my blood boil. The world would have been a better place if people did their jobs better.

So just a heads up from an SME. Those enterprises that have Intel AMT enabled, (vpro) can remotely connect to the bricked systems and fix them. You can literally mount a USB stick remotely and rebuild the system if you got this enabled. You can even go to the BIOS or windows recovery remotely and over the internet. Intel AMT acts like a server ILO but for end user devices and can be configured with or without user consent depending the deployment scenario. So the back door has been always there. But is an enterprise grade one you can actually use and can be useful at times. I do push for using it as it can save the clients a lot of money and if configured properly it is generally secure. But they don't usually listen
There is another side to the coin too. It has also been used in the past to hijack systems. So just a heads up, this is what I am worried about a lot.

# Thread: DEFINE WHAT WE MEAN BY IONIZING RADIATION?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/define-what-we-mean-by-ionizing-radiation.29186/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-19T20:25:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/define-what-we-mean-by-ionizing-radiation.29186/post-337343>

The second restriction requires a clear distinction between ionizing radiation and NIR, and actually there is none. Both forms of radiation are electromagnetic waves, and they seem to differ only in terms of frequency. Generally, ionizing radiation is at the high (in frequency) end of the spectrum, while NIR occupies the lower part.
What frequency marks the dividing line between ionizing and non-ionizing radiation? A standard definition for ionizing radiation was found; however, it is arbitrary and does not identify an abrupt physical threshold. According to that definition, all electromagnetic radiation with energy of 12.4 eV (electron volts) or greater per photon is ionizing. All with lesser energy per photon is not ionizing.

The formula for the energy per photon of an electromagnetic wave is: E = hv
where h = 6.626 x 10^**\-34** joules-sec and v is frequency. So, 12.4 eV is equivalent to about 2,998,000 GHz or a wave length of about 3,000 A (angstroms), which is in the ultraviolet band. One angstrom is 10- meters.

As already noted, the 12.4 eV definition is arbitrary. Readily available data of elements it shows that many elements can be ionized with lower energy photons. Further, some can be ionized twice with photon energies still less than 12. eV. Research also shows that some compounds can be ionized with photon energies below those required to ionize any of their constituent elements.
One familiar example is photo-ionization, which is the basis for photography and how cells operate, and occurs in the visible light band. So, the distinction between ionizing and non-ionizing radiation is not precise by design, at least in terms of frequency and wave length.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-09-04T00:11:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/define-what-we-mean-by-ionizing-radiation.29186/post-339215>

How EMF can damage: It does not have to be via ionizing radiation. Any radiation that cause a loss of electrons is damaging to cells. If you examine the enzyme, ATP synthase, from a top down view it looks exactly like an electrical motor. It passes currents of protons through a water wire, into a perfectly cut channel to fit H+ and no other isotope of hydrogen.

Magnetic fields can generated by nnEMF change the transparency of the water channel to protons. As a result, proton currents are reduced and you have a way to diminish ATP production. Magnetic fields have a physical action on water because it is a magnetic dipole, so you can change the transparency of the most critical enzyme in the human body, modulate the amount of ATP, increase, essentially, the escape of electrons from complex one, thereby explaining practically all of the observations related to EMF.

The energy of the magnetic field itself is irrelevant in a sense. The idea that you have to reach ionization potential in order to create damage is totally wrong. You create damage because you're leaking electrons from the oxidative phosphorylation chain.

But perhaps more significantly, by altering metabolism and the production of ATP, you are confusing the programming of metabolism that has been developed over the last two billion years. Our species can evolve and is successful in great part because we were successful at generating large amounts of ATP. If you touch that, you're going to have disturbances of metabolism that will occur, over a chronic exposure, that will result in increases in the rate of #diabetes. And that is probably true for all types of electromagnetic radiation, be it power frequency, be it AM, FM, television signals. All of these have fields that can affect these charges. So the view that these electromagnetic fields are innocuous to biological systems is simply, plainly wrong.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-09-04T16:42:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/define-what-we-mean-by-ionizing-radiation.29186/post-339243>

Oxygen is an essential element for the respiration and energy metabolism of eukaryotic organisms, but due to its highly reactive nature, it is also a stress factor. Many organisms possess a sophisticated system of antioxidant defenses to protect themselves from oxygen toxicity. In animals, reactive oxygen species (ROS) receptors are exclusively embedded in membranes, primarily the inner mitochondrial membrane, and target them more efficiently than the cytosolic targets. It has been suggested that mitochondria have arisen to protect the cytosolic and nuclear compartment from an excess of oxygen. We discuss the two major questions of evolutionary interest in this hypothesis: "Why does evolution not lead to a less dangerous ROS form in the cytosol and nucleus?" This and related questions on the origin and the subsequent evolution of mitochondria are the most intriguing events in eukaryotic evolution.

The toxicity of oxygen has traditionally been linked to the formation of reactive compounds with a free oxygen electron, which are termed reactive oxygen species (ROS) in the broadest sense. The internal and external appearance of true mitochondria makes them stick out in the eukaryotic cell. In humans, a specialized family of proteins, the dimaglobins, is distributed to counteract the respiratory effects of such leaks in the mammalian lung. The bimodal functioning of mitochondria points to an evolutionary compromise between the use of oxygen for mitochondrial power generation and the avoidance of intracellular and intra-mitochondrial damage from the biological activities of the ROS. The shift between a primarily oxidative and a primarily lactate producing metabolism with oxygen tension has been termed in Kotte's Renovabiles. With the union of mitochondria-operating eukaryotic cells, one of the most important symbioses in our view began.

## 1\. Overview of OxPhos​

For the average mammalian cell, O2 is obviously used in the formation of H2O. The final step in this process is the transfer of electrons from cytochrome c to O2. In the absence of O2, very little ATP is formed by cells via oxidative phosphorylation coupled to the only pathway for the final disposal of electrons liberated by oxidizing substrates, and that is the formation of H2O from O2. Oxidative phosphorylation results in the establishment of an electrochemical potential that can be used for the transfer of protons across the inner mitochondrial membrane to the inter-membrane space. The first complex of the electron-transport chain, complex I (NADH:ubiquinone oxido-reductase), oxidizes reduced NAD, forming NAD and NADH and is coupled to the transfer of protons across the inner mitochondrial membrane to the inter-membrane space.

Complex II, succinate dehydrogenase, mediates the other input to the electron-transport chain, the addition of electrons derived from reduced succinate (fumarate + 2e- + 2H+ roughly proper to fumarate + H2O), forming fumarate + 2H+. Inner mitochondrial membrane to the intermembrane compared which is catalyzed by several enzymes, succinate dehydrogenase is embedded in the inner mitochondrial membrane. The intermediates of the citric acid cycle that lead to the production of CO2 and the intermediates of oxidative phosphorylation that lead to the production of H2O provide a link between catabolism and the major processes that govern the cell, oxidative phosphorylation, and the tricarboxylic acid cycle.

### Mat.I — 2025-07-31T06:03:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/define-what-we-mean-by-ionizing-radiation.29186/post-357257>

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=357248)
>
> Mat but would that mean when I take a body voltage reading with black lead staked to yard and my hand gripping rod on red lead it's only measuring the voltage differential and the not the true voltage burden on my body? I was never on the ground floor when I lived in Brooklyn so I never even attempted to do BV readings there.

Assuming you are not grounded & measuring in AC it will only show you the E field that has coupled to your body via capacitive coupling and display it in a voltage gradient (pressure differential from you to the reference point). It cannot tell you the strength of the field (only what has coupled to your body relative to the reference point). E.g. the potential. Note: just because you have a ground reference point is does not always mean it is clean or absolutely accurate (especially with higher ambient fields), I have posted about this before as it could be said that technically this is no true ground as it is all relative to your environment. Hence, why this is also problematic when only using the BV measurement and not checking the actual ambient fields. The true field that you are immersed in - is what you have measured with you E field meter.

# Thread: Blue light blockers for kids
