### Daniela Lawler — 2024-07-20T19:31:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-blockers-for-kids.29187/post-337388>

Has anyone a tried and tested brand they recommend for kids? I’m in the USA,

### sarafarley — 2024-07-20T20:35:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-blockers-for-kids.29187/post-337392>

Midwest Red Light Therapy just released orange-toned blue blockers for kids. I've not seen them, but I have tested their other orange lenses and they block wavelengths below 530.

### [Kids Blue Light Blockers – Orange Lens (Nighttime) 2-10 years old – Midwest Red Light Therapy](https://midwestredlighttherapy.com/product/kids-blue-light-blockers-orange-lens-nighttime-2-10-years-old/)

 midwestredlighttherapy.com

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-21T01:48:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-blockers-for-kids.29187/post-337404>

[www.luciaeyes.com](http://www.luciaeyes.com) best for kids.

Code is LIGHT20

### Daniela Lawler — 2024-07-23T19:35:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-blockers-for-kids.29187/post-337517>

thanks Uncle jack....you have to select the lens - 45% day 60% day 100% night - Im assuming thats the amount blue light you want to block? I am assuming I go for the 60% day ? would I need to purchase a separate pair for night?

# Thread: Wondering what people are using for CT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wondering-what-people-are-using-for-ct.29191/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sheas — 2024-07-21T18:01:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wondering-what-people-are-using-for-ct.29191/post-337432>

I had a DIY set up for the past 3 years and it finally died. Looking into investing in a real cold plunge tub under 10k for outside. What is everyone here using? Or hope to use one day?

### doctorwu85 — 2024-07-21T19:32:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wondering-what-people-are-using-for-ct.29191/post-337437>

Probably not what you're looking for, but I put my setup [**in this post**](http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/js-optimal-journal.28619/post-336861)

It's an indoor setup with an aquarium chiller that we made some modifications to, feeding into our normal bathtub. It can get the water to 54-55 degrees pretty reliably. As we're starting to want to go longer/colder, we're talking about having more ice on hand to add at the beginning and during the plunge, but getting the water to 55 with zero labor has saved a lot of time and mess.

### sheas — 2024-07-21T20:02:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wondering-what-people-are-using-for-ct.29191/post-337438>

> [doctorwu85 said:](/goto/post?id=337437)
>
> Probably not what you're looking for, but I put my setup [**in this post**](http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/js-optimal-journal.28619/post-336861)
>
> It's an indoor setup with an aquarium chiller that we made some modifications to, feeding into our normal bathtub. It can get the water to 54-55 degrees pretty reliably. As we're starting to want to go longer/colder, we're talking about having more ice on hand to add at the beginning and during the plunge, but getting the water to 55 with zero labor has saved a lot of time and mess.

Thanks I will check that out. I sealed off a chest freezer with a temp gauge to keep it at 48 and just unplug it when I want to get in or I can also keep it unplugged for a week or so once it is cold. It was a cheap and easy set up on my back porch. But now I am learning about nnEMF and thinking this set up might not be so great. Need to get a meter and figure out how to use cables instead of wifi....But that is a different thread

### Mat.I — 2024-07-22T10:09:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wondering-what-people-are-using-for-ct.29191/post-337470>

I'm using a 150gal rubbermaid stocktank outside. As for ice I use the large silicone molds, however in winter, I rarely add much ice if any.

# Thread: Hope for fractured spine - how to interpret report ?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-for-fractured-spine-how-to-interpret-report.29192/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Martha Ray — 2024-07-22T02:13:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-for-fractured-spine-how-to-interpret-report.29192/post-337455>

I fractured my spine three days before my husband died in early June of a quick 2 month bout of Pancreatic Cancer.
Tough Times.

I have never had back trouble before and showed fine on bone density tests in the past...(which I know now tests are not reliable).

**Bone health has not been on my radar and I have little idea how to live with this and what to consider medically.**
The emergency room I went to did an x-ray and said I had multiple fractures in my spine but they did not know how old they were and they would not do the MRI I wanted but told me I had a soft tissue injury. I was in the hospital 2 more days because I was screaming in pain every time I moved. Had awful issues with them with communication, meds and over my holistic way of thinking. My medical report was insultingly disparaging with inaccuracies. They sent me home with home health care for PT and Occupational Therapy.
I was discharged the afternoon on the day my husband died.
His sister and his oldest daughter held his hands that morning for his last breath and I was thankful he was not alone.

Since I've been so distracted with pain, legalities and multiple challenges and arrangements for my husband.
Thankfully my twin brother came to town and stayed 6 weeks helping me with many details and logistical help. But I've not gotten much help from the two doctors I've seen. One set me up for an MRI referred me to a spine specialist who I can not get in to see until Sept. He said not to do any more PT. And my allopathic GP/MD ordered the DEXA scan and he has asked me to take **400 mg of Selenium** which I'm pretty sure he meant **400 mcg. I have never heard of Se for Bone healing.**

I'm now reading and taking notes from Jack Kruse's articles:
**The Osteoporosis Rx** - [https://jackkruse.com/the-osteoporosis-rx/](https://jackkruse.com/the-osteoporosis-rx/)
**Osteoporosis Part 1** \- [https://jackkruse.com/osteoporosis-part-one/](https://jackkruse.com/osteoporosis-part-one/)
**Osteoporosis 2**: The Vitamin K2 Story - [https://jackkruse.com/osteoporosis-two-the-vitamin-k2-story/](https://jackkruse.com/osteoporosis-two-the-vitamin-k2-story/)
and
**Leptin Chapter One** - [https://jackkruse.com/chapter-one-on-leptin/](https://jackkruse.com/chapter-one-on-leptin/)

**I have no idea how to interpret my MRI report.** Or how to apply some practical wisdom for my condition.

Living alone, am not sure what I can and cannot do physically for my best care and healing. I am learning a little by little and am hopeful for more help.

I do have faith that God is working my challenges for good.
Claiming I Peter 5:7 ... Casting, Throwing, (Body Slamming) my anxieties on Him for He is caring for me.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-22T04:45:20+0300

Husband dies of mtDNA problem and now you have a mtDNA problem in your bone. Seems obvious where the problem is. Your environment. It did both of you in. Hypothyroidism is linked to bad bone too.

### Michelle H — 2025-08-13T19:17:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-for-fractured-spine-how-to-interpret-report.29192/post-358137>

Recently diagnosed with multiple fractures and compressions in the whole thoracic area. Intense pain 24/7. The pain moves around different spots in my midriff area, sometimes affecting my breathing. Was told that is from the nerves/compression.

Spine doctor looks at me like my life is over and nothing will help. Referred me to a doctor who may do a kyphoplasty.

I'm mad, I have intentionally lived a very healthy active lifestyle for the last 35 years, was lifting weights, hiking, taking bone supplements, getting plenty of sun, no processed foods, lots of protein. My labs are always good. I'm only 67 and still working. I felt so strong before this. No clue that my back was falling apart.

I was fine until I reluctantly agreed to try a statin - ten days later I had an allergic reaction, angioedema, severe swelling all around my midriff area. I'm mad I said yes to that. It seemed like my whole body went crazy after that.

I'll take any suggestions. Wondering how Martha Ray is doing.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-10-23T17:49:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-for-fractured-spine-how-to-interpret-report.29192/post-361486>

False. The better answer should have been included in your informed consent. 20-30% of people break the bones adjacent to the kyphoplasty because the increased strength of the repaired vertebrae makes the adjacent ones brittle on a relative basis, hence why they brake under normal loads. I tell every kypho person I see we will see each other again for more.

# Thread: The Mushroom Problem
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-mushroom-problem.29194/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jeremiasbtome — 2024-07-22T11:12:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-mushroom-problem.29194/post-337471>

I noticed there seems to be more Mushrooms growing in my park very close to me and i think some near lawns by houses

I remember jack said 5g can grow funghi

Does this mean my enviroment is getting worse?

Its in Ireland, and it is currently summer

### Anthony Smith — 2024-07-22T23:10:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-mushroom-problem.29194/post-337484>

Mushrooms are the “fruit” of mycelium in the soil. Mushrooms are a sign of healthy soil.

Mycelium and mold differ in their methods of reproduction:

#### Mycelium: Primarily through spore production​

Mycelium reproduces primarily through spore production. These spores are often produced in fruiting bodies, such as mushrooms, and are dispersed to new areas where they can germinate and establish new mycelial networks.

#### Mold: Releases spores that become airborne​

Mold releases spores into the air, which can be easily dispersed to new locations. These spores can travel through the air and settle on various surfaces, leading to mold colonization under suitable environmental conditions.

### jeremiasbtome — 2024-07-23T22:41:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-mushroom-problem.29194/post-337520>

>
> Mushrooms are the “fruit” of mycelium in the soil. Mushrooms are a sign of healthy soil.

>
> #### Mycelium: Primarily through spore production​
>
> Mycelium reproduces primarily through spore production. These spores are often produced in fruiting bodies, such as mushrooms, and are dispersed to new areas where they can germinate and establish new mycelial networks.
>
> #### Mold: Releases spores that become airborne​
>
> Mold releases spores into the air, which can be easily dispersed to new locations. These spores can travel through the air and settle on various surfaces, leading to mold colonization under suitable environmental conditions.

what about jack saying in his old house he had funghi on a tree that if it grew it would tell him 5g was near

### Anthony Smith — 2024-07-24T04:01:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-mushroom-problem.29194/post-337527>

EMF will make mold grow faster but you can have mold/fungi/mushrooms with no nnEMF also. We have had mushrooms, fungi, mold etc long before 5G (or any ”G” for that matter). As I said mycelium in the soil is normally a sign of healthy soil. I would go with a RF meter before mushrooms.

# Thread: Dogs and Vitamin D

### jeremiasbtome — 2024-07-22T22:56:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dogs-and-vitamin-d.29196/post-337483>

Is it true that dogs can only get Vitamin D from diet?

Or can they get it from the sun also?

Whats the truth?

### pfedorow — 2024-07-31T20:30:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dogs-and-vitamin-d.29196/post-337887>

> [jeremiasbtome said:](/goto/post?id=337483)
>
> Is it true that dogs can only get Vitamin D from diet?
>
> Or can they get it from the sun also?
>
> Whats the truth?

The book Forever Dog says that, but I don't believe it. They have a whole chapter about nonsense about supplements for your dog and say the same thing mainstream science says about humans and vitamin d: that we must supplement because food and natural light will never get enough. The main points I got from the book are that dogs are more sensitive to their environment than humans and need sun to thrive and live with lots of love and attention.Dogs need fat, protein, and no kibble, which probably is not great for your dog.

I don't think any modern dogs they test live an outdoor existence. Plenty of light receptors on dogs, but do they get tanked living in a modern world? My beach dog friends seem really melanated and energized. Always in the shade or in the ocean when they get too much.

### [Why Does My Dog Sunbathe? | Sit Means Sit - The Woodlands, Spring & Conroe](https://sitmeanssit.com/dog-training-mu/north-houston-dog-training/dog-health/why-does-my-dog-sunbathe/)

Why Does My Dog Sunbathe?. There are a couple of reasons why your pooch might enjoy basking in the sun. It’s not only a great source of Vitamin D – but it also just feels good.

 sitmeanssit.com

### sheas — 2024-09-25T21:38:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dogs-and-vitamin-d.29196/post-340023>

I have been trying to find this info as well. Vitamin D production in dogs and horses. My friend who is an animal naturopath told me dogs only get vitamin D by licking their fur and most are deficient so of course she recommends supplementing. So I ask why are most deficient? Not enough UV on their fur? Can they produce Vitamin D from the receptors in their eyes? Or does it have to touch their fur/skin? Or their food? Any quantum anything is hard to find for animals so I try to read blogs that mention anything about furry animals and wonder if this information can transfer to all animals.

And then horses, I wonder how much UV light is blocked by wearing fly masks and what kind of chaos that is causing in their body. So many have metabolic issues, colic, eye and skin issues.

I wish we had a quantum vet in this group. How much of all of this information transfers to furry animals.

One of my dogs is 18 years old and healthy (except she now has selective hearing lol). She has only been fed a raw diet and I don't give her any meds we don't ever go see a vet.

### drsongihan — 2024-09-26T18:00:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dogs-and-vitamin-d.29196/post-340060>

This is so interesting and makes a lot of sense. I was anyway wondering what's up with this whole indoctrination that "dogs must eat kibble to be healthy" nonsense, how does that make sense that dogs can only survive if they eat all day artificial stuff, and if they eat human food then its bad, how? So many dogs are sick, have cancer and what not and feed the pockets of vets and the dog food industry. Well my dog eats human food scraps, no supplements, she sits out on the patio all day and chases squirrel, I stopped going to the vet 2 years ago and she is squeaky healthy. I started to give her food in the morning, instead of evening, and outside to mirror the Leptin prescription. The crazy thing is that I cannot bring her to the lake in the summer because dogs need a "pass" to enter even the doggie beach, and you cannot get a pass without showing all the vaccines--dogs even have more vaccine requirements than humans. This November ballot includes "Unfence Gilson Beach" that I hope wins. The Great Lake of Michigan is a natural resource that belongs to the people. I cannot even understand how it is constitutional to fence up a lake, demand $$ for entry and that the dog is vaccinated to enjoy the Lake. If you think about it, centralized regulation for dogs is equally bad or worse than for people. Luckily the "season" is over since early September, and now we can freely enter the Lake beaches until early June next year.

I use to be involved with Shutzhund (dog sport) and breed working line GSD's (in the past) with great success in producing very solid nerved good temperament lines. I also used early neuro stim techniques (e.g. getting them use to some loud noises, tactile stimulation, light thermal stimulation etc), these techniques are well documented in the literature. Kibble is by far the worst garbage you can give to a dog. A full raw diet - that simulates what they would get out in the wild (nose to tail, inclusive of contents) is by far the best. You can get a bit creative and add certain things in and people have termed this as the "BARF" diet with certain percentage targets e.g. muscle meat, fat content, organs, bones, a bit of veg to simulate undigested gut contents that may be presence after a hunt etc.. The results are directly evident in body comp, performance, behaviour, stools, development, dental/gum health and coat.

What kind a raw meat do you give?
Can it be any meat from any store? Or do you get it deep frozen and do you unfreeze it naturally then?
How would you introduce it to a dog new to it?
Looking for a change for my dog (11y, doodle, adopted with abandonment anxiety, and microwaved like me) He's overweight and said to be allergic to beef and some more things. He got tired of chicken. He got tired of horse.
We have also an 18y senior dog here that always ate w/ the family.
And a recently adopted 11 chiuaua. These two didn't get microwaved. They have some health issues.
But they eat whatever we eat and no problems.
Mine has chronic ear infections, presumably secondary bc of allergy induced itching.
Of course doodles/poodles have difficult hairy ears also. Mine came home from trimmers always w/ tiny wounds and itching. Challenging choice for 1st dog.
Saw 10 or 12 vets. They ruin your bank account. Apoquel, local antibiotics, sometimes injected antibiotics.
I mentionned EHS and 5G and leaky gut. But they're not interested. Their business is thriving.
Most vets full of RF security camera's etc.. In case junkies come steal their meds and money.
I tried one vet who does bioresonance - no idea still how/if that works - but that guy was nuts.

There is a pet food store near me that sells only raw or raw freeze dried food. The brand I get has rabbit, ground rabbit bone, rabbit giblets, and some vegetables, cod liver oil, and sodium bicarbonate. I get raw freeze dried pork that has similar ingredients but with pork just to give them another meat option that has more fat in it. Rabbit alone doesn't have enough fat. I sprinkle the freeze dried pork on top. I did one of those glacier peak analysis so I try to keep them with meat that is not on their list of sensitivities. And I try to find local companies too. You can order through the mail from different places depending on where you live in the world. But ground bone and organ meats are important to add not just the muscle meat. if you can get rabbit, beef, duck, lamb, turkey are meats I stick with for my dogs. You can also give them chicken feet or duck feet as their bone source. It comes frozen and I let it thaw in fridge. Mine comes in individual patties so it is convenient.

My 18 yr old is a border collie Aussie mix and last year started itching really intensely. I couldn't figure it out because I knew her diet was good. Ended up being EHS, and not enough sun. And also started when we switched wifi companies to the fiber optics one! So when I spent more time outside at home, so did she since she follows me like a shadow. And her itchiness totally went away.

Can they make D somhow themselves without sun on skin, unlike humans?

I am looking for a wagon to take my dog hiking in the woods/unpaved paths.
He's overweight, chronically itchy ears, since he joined me in my (now former) 5G town.
Probably microwaved like me.

He's 11 and now 15kg with overweight.
Anyone ideas on a dog sporty buggy for moderate nature walks?
We're not going to the alps but forests and earth paths would be cool.
Weather here is often rainy.

Any ideas? Experience?

In urban areas RF changes every 10-50 meters and I feel it in my head. My dogs needs to sniff and go slow so walking him here is stressful and he's walk-deprived.
I want to take him to a bigger natural area where we can stay for several hours in low RF area.

He for now cannot always walk for long time = hence buggy.

# Thread: Frogs Melanate to Adapt to Chernobyl Radiation
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frogs-melanate-to-adapt-to-chernobyl-radiation.29197/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JeffRouseMD — 2024-07-23T01:06:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frogs-melanate-to-adapt-to-chernobyl-radiation.29197/post-337486>

Not surprising but a good example of melanin and evolution at work.

### [Chernobyl black frogs reveal evolution in action](https://theconversation.com/chernobyl-black-frogs-reveal-evolution-in-action-191034)

Research on Chernobyl frogs has shown that the ionising radiation caused by the accident triggered a process of natural selection among these animals.

 theconversation.com

and the scientific paper

[https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1111/eva.13476](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1111/eva.13476)

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-25T16:34:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/within-a-species-centralized-science-is-uncovering-a-deep-truth-with-massive-implications.29202/post-337560>

MODERN LIFE AND LIGHT GIVES YOU A VIOLET PHOTON DEFICIENCY.... A violet light deficiency gives you bad health through myopia generation, immune system disfunction and Vitamin D depletion, high blood pressure, cardiovascular disease, cancer, depression, chronic fatigue and more... As you see in the graphs below, most windows (offices, hospitals, cars) block almost all violet light and then in the second picture you see that various types of artificial indoor lighting (LED, fluorescent and incandescent) supply little to no violet light. For the cherry on top, the graphs also show how most intraocular lenses and contact lenses block violet light so if you use this technology and live/work mostly indoors you are drastically speeding up the time it takes for major disease to visit you. Open your windows, take multiple sun breaks throughout the day across the entire year, wear glasses not contacts, ditch the sunglasses and sunscreens.

### eve001 — 2024-07-28T13:36:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/within-a-species-centralized-science-is-uncovering-a-deep-truth-with-massive-implications.29202/post-337657>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=337560)
>
> MODERN LIFE AND LIGHT GIVES YOU A VIOLET PHOTON DEFICIENCY.... A violet light deficiency gives you bad health through myopia generation, immune system disfunction and Vitamin D depletion, high blood pressure, cardiovascular disease, cancer, depression, chronic fatigue and more... As you see in the graphs below, most windows (offices, hospitals, cars) block almost all violet light and then in the second picture you see that various types of artificial indoor lighting (LED, fluorescent and incandescent) supply little to no violet light. For the cherry on top, the graphs also show how most intraocular lenses and contact lenses block violet light so if you use this technology and live/work mostly indoors you are drastically speeding up the time it takes for major disease to visit you. Open your windows, take multiple sun breaks throughout the day across the entire year, wear glasses not contacts, ditch the sunglasses and sunscreens.

Cool. I will check in Dinshah Ghadiali's book on light and color what violet meant to him!

# Thread: Buying a property in El Salvador
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/buying-a-property-in-el-salvador.29207/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Carlos Stro — 2024-07-24T18:42:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/buying-a-property-in-el-salvador.29207/post-337541>

Hi Jack! We talked by phone this June while I was in El Salvador. I was with some friends including a famous Spanish soccer player. His wife and his brother were recently there and bought a property in the same place as you. I am close to buy one in the same place. I saw the loft in a video but I want to ask for your opinion. I have a map. Do you think it’s better to purchase another consult with you for this matter?

### tiffanyfinnegan — 2024-11-13T19:38:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/buying-a-property-in-el-salvador.29207/post-342304>

Question, can I buy there if a US Citizen or do I need to go through some stuff first? New here and still need to investigate but thought maybe you know off hand since you’re underway already? And off hand, best place to look for homes to get an idea of pricing, etc? Thank you!!

### Kirsten F — 2024-11-13T19:50:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/buying-a-property-in-el-salvador.29207/post-342306>

> [tiffanyfinnegan said:](/goto/post?id=342304)
>
> Question, can I buy there if a US Citizen or do I need to go through some stuff first? New here and still need to investigate but thought maybe you know off hand since you’re underway already? And off hand, best place to look for homes to get an idea of pricing, etc? Thank you!!

Hello, have you considered renting? After living in various places in Panama and now El Salvador, we have found renting is a great way to experience a place, learn more about the market, and determine if you are in the right area… it leaves you much more free to pivot. Easy to do as a foreigner.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-11-13T20:05:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/buying-a-property-in-el-salvador.29207/post-342307>

I would not talk about this stuff here.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-11-13T20:06:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/buying-a-property-in-el-salvador.29207/post-342308>

>
> Hello, have you considered renting? After living in various places in Panama and now El Salvador, we have found renting is a great way to experience a place, learn more about the market, and determine if you are in the right area… it leaves you much more free to pivot. Easy to do as a foreigner.

Renting makes no sense now.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-11-13T20:06:31+0200

# Thread: Optimal Living in the Tropics
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-living-in-the-tropics.29210/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### IanKites — 2024-07-25T09:48:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-living-in-the-tropics.29210/post-337554>

Hello everyone,

I searched the forums for this topic with no luck so hope I'm not re-hashing.

Q. Living in the tropics, getting good sun, but was under the impression that high fat was good through Cytochrome 2 as best for energy conversion. Now I read that the leakiest Cytochrome no.1 is better with Carbs as ROS is created for synthesizing melanin where 2 does not create the ROS. Can I get some feedback and thoughts on this please?

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-07-27T22:36:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-living-in-the-tropics.29210/post-337642>

In general, the more UV you get thru eyes and on skin, the more carbs you should consume

### Ryan Carter — 2024-07-28T01:00:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-living-in-the-tropics.29210/post-337646>

> [Joseph LeBlanc said:](/goto/post?id=337642)
>
> In general, the more UV you get thru eyes and on skin, the more carbs you should consume

\* Could consume.... Just because theirs light doesn't mean you have the mitochondria to handle the pulse at CC1

Even then, the carbs are no way near to the SAD or bodybuilding meat heads consume.
I think 120-140 grams per day - from natural sources is decent and realistic.

I live in Nicaragua - I consume very few carbs. I think carbs should be purely a supplement.
You also find there's seasonal variation in carbs (fruit and plants) IE Avocados, watermelon all have a season tied to the wet season - eating them out of season - results in them tasting like crap. The same is for mangos too.
Same with the cows being born - tied to the rainy season to provide the grasses for maximum probability of abundance and growth.

### IanKites — 2024-07-29T06:17:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-living-in-the-tropics.29210/post-337701>

I'm surrounded by a population of sun protected, skin whitening ghosts to not look like a poor farmers who are now obese who have grown up eating large quantities of Rice and rice noodles and still do. Those that do go outside for their work are super lean and super strong right into their senior years.
All big carb eaters. 10 degrees nth of the equator.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-30T00:37:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-living-in-the-tropics.29210/post-337752>

The answer is in the blogs how about you read them. [https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-6-107464807](https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-6-107464807)

### IanKites — 2024-08-13T09:16:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-living-in-the-tropics.29210/post-338363>

Will do, thanks for pinpointing it.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-13T18:20:31+0300

# Thread: Songi's optimal journal with 14yo son
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 145
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### drsongihan — 2024-07-25T16:58:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-337561>

I decided to start a new journal because circumstances have changed. Moving to ES with son got stunted by very personal circumstances beyond my control.
The new plan is to maximize my time in the Yucatan Peninsula in Mexico, send my kid to a private school in the northern suburb of Chicago that allows for hybrid learning so I can pull him out weeks at a time to stay in Mexico, and if that does not work, homeschooling and maximizing time in Mexico will be my escalation option.
The theme will be that we there are a thousand paths for our optimal journey, even when considering n=1, and for those who become wiser earlier, you can find optimal in many places in the world. Clearly, some locations are harder than others, and optimal in downtown Chicago or London is probably extremely hard, though downtown Chicago is still sunnier than London and at a lower latitude with 41 N as opposed to 51 N in London. Yikes. Btw, I talked to someone with SAD who told me she can optimize life in the suburb of Chicago no problem, but London or Paris -- no freaking way. She had to cut her sabbatical short to come back from Paris to Chicago. Her daughter, on the other hand, could not make it work in Chicago, so she moved to the Bay Area, which is sunny year-round and 37 N.

Anyhow, for me, selling house and converting to bitcoin is still a critical piece in my puzzle that I am pursuing right now since that will give me the freedom to buy a place somewhere in the tropics in the future. Even though I am well off by any "normal" standard (not multimillionaire rich, but just very comfortable well off) it is not enough to own a home where my work is AND to own an optimal house in the tropics--this is my goal. Why do I need to own a home where my work is? Because when you own a home, you can better mitigate nnEMF, have a yard to ground yourself, build or optimize a patio for outdoor living, have a cold plunge etc. Rental in my area is 80% Apartments, which is a no-go for me and the houses that people rent are almost always near a metro, near a train, or near a loud and busy street. You wonder why, yikes. Now, why do I need to own a home in the tropics? Same reason. Having gone from hotel to hotel, airbnb to airbnb to ES I can tell you that it is hard to find an affordable place that also is optimal. In a rental or airbnb, Wifi and the breaker is not in your control and grounding is not easy. MT's home is therefore an exception where she has Wifi turned off, walking to beach and ground right there. To me, it's important that the place that I am staying is at the ground level, steps away from grass, patio, sand etc. I have only been to one Airbnb where you are at ground, wifi can be turned off and place is affordable, but not more than MT's home and not walking distance to beach (a Airbnb in Zaragoza. I am happy to share that information with anybody who is interested).

Now we see why Dr. Jack is right that optimal health is tied to optimal money. That will be my goal.

Since I have to "hack" things in a less optimal environment, at least for a while, I have a few questions. If you see anything that is missing, please chime in.

1) Optimize nnEMF using Anthony's EMF safe and measurement in the house, so that should be okay? Optimize during the day will not be perfect, but at least at home and at night.

2) Optimize the sunlight I got by opening window and turning a Patio to outdoor living using fans for the summer and gas heater for the winter. Get mosquito traps since that it an issue sometimes. I will have energy bills like crazy but it's a small price to pay.

3) Bathtub with cold water both indoors and outdoors, either buy a big ice machine or have one of those be regulated electrically. Any tips which product to get will be welcome. I will compell my son (and myself) to have cold plunge in the morning after waking up and another one in the evening before going to bed.

4) Sleep as cold as possible. This means turn off heater in Winter to the extent possible and have air conditioning on in Summer.

5) Replace all light bulbs with red light or incandescent light.

6) IR/near-IR panel for early morning and evening, especially where my son is. Here, I do have a question, with all the mitigation above listed in place and even when opening the window in the morning the light in the morning will not be enough in Chicago. Sunrise in the winter is 7.30am. Should I use an IR/near-IR panel as "wakeup" light without UV, or should I add a UV lamp to the morning light? EMR Tech is one option, but there are cheaper options.

One option is to get all-spectrum plant growing lights. They are truly 360 - 800 nm.
Another option is IR/near IR panels for a few hundred $$ plus UVA/UVB from a reptile store
And of course the fancy option is EMR Tech

7) Blue blocking glass when looking at any monitor. I ordered Daylight saving computer, so that should help. And replacing my iPad with a e-ink Kindle should also help. Obviously, reading analogue books is best, but that is not always possible. If I need to look at the monitor in the evening, turn on red light.

8) Eat before sunset. That is not difficult in the summer. This will be nearly impossible in the winter since sunset is 4.30pm. I will try my best, and if that is difficult, eat under Redlight panel.

9) I have not understood under what circumstances (besides being out in the sun as much as possible, but if there is no sun) having a UV lamp is helpful. I presume it's emulating the mid-day sun. Hence, UV light would only be good during day if there is a snow storm and what not and we need to be indoors, we can turn the UV lamp on, but I should not have UV lamp on early in the morning when waking up and in the evening at dinner time or before going to bed since that's not Nature's light.

P.S. Do NOT comment on how we cannot biohack the sunlight. I know that full well, but sometimes circumstances are out of our control, so after doing everything possible, I am asking what to do in situations where we have to be indoors due to extreme weather in higher latitude.

10) Fly to Yucatan (3.5 hours from Chicago) and stay weeks at a time. However, this would not help for a long weekend (say 3-4 days) because the harm from flying might reverse any benefit that we got from Yucatan? The benefit is worth the flying when we can stay 1 week + ideally longer?

Or can we mitigate the damage of flying by taking CoQ10, drinking a lot, methylene blue, full-coverage plastic (polyester) clothing to mitigate blue light.

11) Sleep on a Magnetico Bed (we already have it) should help in a bad environment?

12) Drink deuterium depleted water (only my son) should help since he is in need of more support?

13) Eat protein and saturated fat (butter, steak, mackerel, salmon) rich food, as close as possible to Ketogenic Diet, or at least Paleo Diet. Avoid Sugar and Carb as much as possible. If my son is not hungry, he can just not eat. It's the best option anyway.

Here, I have another question. I noticed that his "not eating" for 24 hours to 72 hours at a time got more extreme. I think it is a sign that he is "blue light toxic". The question is then why is the blue light toxicity only now showing up in a better environment? I have a theory and I want somebody to fact check me.

\- Blue light toxic + no sunlight + high latitude (i.e. bad all round, and the environment he was in): my son was not eating much or craving sugar. I learned from Dr Jack's podcast with the dentist that sugar is body's natural way to fight ROS. So my son was fighting his blue light toxicity with sugar/carb.

\- Blue light toxic + sunlight + lower latitude (our current environment): my son is eating even less, and not even sugar/carb. Now, the sunlight is "feeding" him, but he is still blue light toxic. Otherwise, his body's natural hunger should come back?

What changes would have to occur where he craves normal food again or gets hungry again for the good stuff (fish, meat etc). Would that be when his blue light toxicity is mitigated?

I think that's ALL that I have so far learned. Did I miss something?

And yes, my goal is to grow my money and move to the tropics.

### pfedorow — 2024-07-25T19:22:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-337569>

10) listening to the two last webinars and reading the qe 47 -48 blogs, your son needs stability with light and not just sunlight. Traveling back and forth would probably be horrible. Have you networked in el Salvador to do research here and leave chicago or looked for job postings?

Have you taking your son to paras todo . they are great events with kids playing together and very group oriented

### drsongihan — 2024-07-25T20:18:37+0300

Thanks Peter. I agree, and I set everything on staying. But the nature of challenge is unfortunately not something I can solve when his dad is challenging me suddenly big times. He seemed supportive and in agreement. In hindsight, I think he thought I was bluffing. But when my house was on the market within 2 days of me sharing my plan and he saw that I am dead-serious, and that I would have to leave him with folks here months at times, his tune changed 180. Yes, the light stability is a big problem, but I did NONE of the things before that I list above so at least I can improve my son's health. I am also getting 23 and me done, but I am pretty sure that both my son and I are uncoupled haplotype, so cold could do some magic. The last bit of biohack I am asking for is indeed the light when I need to be indoors. So I am thinking of turning on the red/near-IR panel always in the morning at 6.30 - 7.30 AM no matter winter or summer, and again turn it on at 4.30 - 6.30 pm, no matter winter or summer to keep it constant, besides going out. Building a deck for outdoor living and a cold plunge. It's not perfect, but I gotta do what I gotta do. The UV light and when to use it if I must is one puzzle I cannot figure out, but I am guessing during the day. The time change is not a problem. Chicago and Yucatan Peninsula are on the same time zone, and El Salvador and Chicago is only 1 hour different. My guess is that whenever I can stay at least a week, ideally more, I should come down. The difference is that there is a direct flight from ORD to Cancun, but there is no direct flight to San Salvador. I also know people in Yucatan. But then there is catch-22. Mexico's immigration officers are horrendous, and I don't know whether they do that to show the world that they are bullies or whether they are just incompetent, but from deplaning to exiting custom takes in Cancun Mexico something like 3 hours in bright blue light, while LAX, ORD or San Salvador is all 20 min and out. I am already disliking the Mexican authorities. Yikes.

### drsongihan — 2024-07-25T20:24:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-337578>

Here is one more question on my Optimal Journal/Journey.

I started to implement every change for my son also on me of course. The one place I cannot change is my office. It's a bright office flooded with natural sunlight and with view BUT I cannot open this fancy schmancy window. The architects who designed this building should be made responsible! It should be against the law. I am thinking natural sunlight through a closed window is not much better than LED blue light because the windows these days are super-duper energy efficient, meaning that it will filter UVA, UVB, Red and near-IR = Blue light is the only light left.

But I have to work on many days in person, and I believe in in-person interactions for communication. I ordered all of my people out for lunch in the courtyard at Noon. But we still spend 7-8 hours in this office.

I guess this would be a situation, if I must stay in my office, to use IR, near-IR and UVA/UVB to lit my office? Alternatively, I can use a plant-growing light, but my worry is that plant growing light is still heavy on blue light, which I already have. So its better to ADD the missing spectrum if I am going to biohack?

Wonderful and thanks. So it becomes intractable as far as I can tell unless I break the law and they start searching my house. I don’t see that happening—by uncle Sam’s standard I am a tiny fish not worth much .

### Mat.I — 2024-07-26T12:00:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-337593>

> [drsongihan said:](/goto/post?id=337561)
>
> I decided to start a new journal because circumstances have changed. Moving to ES with son got stunted by very personal circumstances beyond my control.
> The new plan is to maximize my time in the Yucatan Peninsula in Mexico, send my kid to a private school in the northern suburb of Chicago that allows for hybrid learning so I can pull him out weeks at a time to stay in Mexico, and if that does not work, homeschooling and maximizing time in Mexico will be my escalation option.
> The theme will be that we there are a thousand paths for our optimal journey, even when considering n=1, and for those who become wiser earlier, you can find optimal in many places in the world. Clearly, some locations are harder than others, and optimal in downtown Chicago or London is probably extremely hard, though downtown Chicago is still sunnier than London and at a lower latitude with 41 N as opposed to 51 N in London. Yikes. Btw, I talked to someone with SAD who told me she can optimize life in the suburb of Chicago no problem, but London or Paris -- no freaking way. She had to cut her sabbatical short to come back from Paris to Chicago. Her daughter, on the other hand, could not make it work in Chicago, so she moved to the Bay Area, which is sunny year-round and 37 N.
>
> Anyhow, for me, selling house and converting to bitcoin is still a critical piece in my puzzle that I am pursuing right now since that will give me the freedom to buy a place somewhere in the tropics in the future. Even though I am well off by any "normal" standard (not multimillionaire rich, but just very comfortable well off) it is not enough to own a home where my work is AND to own an optimal house in the tropics--this is my goal. Why do I need to own a home where my work is? Because when you own a home, you can better mitigate nnEMF, have a yard to ground yourself, build or optimize a patio for outdoor living, have a cold plunge etc. Rental in my area is 80% Apartments, which is a no-go for me and the houses that people rent are almost always near a metro, near a train, or near a loud and busy street. You wonder why, yikes. Now, why do I need to own a home in the tropics? Same reason. Having gone from hotel to hotel, airbnb to airbnb to ES I can tell you that it is hard to find an affordable place that also is optimal. In a rental or airbnb, Wifi and the breaker is not in your control and grounding is not easy. MT's home is therefore an exception where she has Wifi turned off, walking to beach and ground right there. To me, it's important that the place that I am staying is at the ground level, steps away from grass, patio, sand etc. I have only been to one Airbnb where you are at ground, wifi can be turned off and place is affordable, but not more than MT's home and not walking distance to beach (a Airbnb in Zaragoza. I am happy to share that information with anybody who is interested).
>
> Now we see why Dr. Jack is right that optimal health is tied to optimal money. That will be my goal.
>
> Since I have to "hack" things in a less optimal environment, at least for a while, I have a few questions. If you see anything that is missing, please chime in.
>
> 1) Optimize nnEMF using Anthony's EMF safe and measurement in the house, so that should be okay? Optimize during the day will not be perfect, but at least at home and at night.
>
> 2) Optimize the sunlight I got by opening window and turning a Patio to outdoor living using fans for the summer and gas heater for the winter. Get mosquito traps since that it an issue sometimes. I will have energy bills like crazy but it's a small price to pay.
>
> 3) Bathtub with cold water both indoors and outdoors, either buy a big ice machine or have one of those be regulated electrically. Any tips which product to get will be welcome. I will compell my son (and myself) to have cold plunge in the morning after waking up and another one in the evening before going to bed.
>
> 4) Sleep as cold as possible. This means turn off heater in Winter to the extent possible and have air conditioning on in Summer.
>
> 5) Replace all light bulbs with red light or incandescent light.
>
> 6) IR/near-IR panel for early morning and evening, especially where my son is. Here, I do have a question, with all the mitigation above listed in place and even when opening the window in the morning the light in the morning will not be enough in Chicago. Sunrise in the winter is 7.30am. Should I use an IR/near-IR panel as "wakeup" light without UV, or should I add a UV lamp to the morning light? EMR Tech is one option, but there are cheaper options.
>
> One option is to get all-spectrum plant growing lights. They are truly 360 - 800 nm.
> Another option is IR/near IR panels for a few hundred $$ plus UVA/UVB from a reptile store
> And of course the fancy option is EMR Tech
>
> 7) Blue blocking glass when looking at any monitor. I ordered Daylight saving computer, so that should help. And replacing my iPad with a e-ink Kindle should also help. Obviously, reading analogue books is best, but that is not always possible. If I need to look at the monitor in the evening, turn on red light.
>
> 8) Eat before sunset. That is not difficult in the summer. This will be nearly impossible in the winter since sunset is 4.30pm. I will try my best, and if that is difficult, eat under Redlight panel.
>
> 9) I have not understood under what circumstances (besides being out in the sun as much as possible, but if there is no sun) having a UV lamp is helpful. I presume it's emulating the mid-day sun. Hence, UV light would only be good during day if there is a snow storm and what not and we need to be indoors, we can turn the UV lamp on, but I should not have UV lamp on early in the morning when waking up and in the evening at dinner time or before going to bed since that's not Nature's light.
>
> P.S. Do NOT comment on how we cannot biohack the sunlight. I know that full well, but sometimes circumstances are out of our control, so after doing everything possible, I am asking what to do in situations where we have to be indoors due to extreme weather in higher latitude.
>
> 10) Fly to Yucatan (3.5 hours from Chicago) and stay weeks at a time. However, this would not help for a long weekend (say 3-4 days) because the harm from flying might reverse any benefit that we got from Yucatan? The benefit is worth the flying when we can stay 1 week + ideally longer?
>
> Or can we mitigate the damage of flying by taking CoQ10, drinking a lot, methylene blue, full-coverage plastic (polyester) clothing to mitigate blue light.
>
> 11) Sleep on a Magnetico Bed (we already have it) should help in a bad environment?
>
> 12) Drink deuterium depleted water (only my son) should help since he is in need of more support?
>
> 13) Eat protein and saturated fat (butter, steak, mackerel, salmon) rich food, as close as possible to Ketogenic Diet, or at least Paleo Diet. Avoid Sugar and Carb as much as possible. If my son is not hungry, he can just not eat. It's the best option anyway.
>
> Here, I have another question. I noticed that his "not eating" for 24 hours to 72 hours at a time got more extreme. I think it is a sign that he is "blue light toxic". The question is then why is the blue light toxicity only now showing up in a better environment? I have a theory and I want somebody to fact check me.
>
> \- Blue light toxic + no sunlight + high latitude (i.e. bad all round, and the environment he was in): my son was not eating much or craving sugar. I learned from Dr Jack's podcast with the dentist that sugar is body's natural way to fight ROS. So my son was fighting his blue light toxicity with sugar/carb.
>
> \- Blue light toxic + sunlight + lower latitude (our current environment): my son is eating even less, and not even sugar/carb. Now, the sunlight is "feeding" him, but he is still blue light toxic. Otherwise, his body's natural hunger should come back?
>
> What changes would have to occur where he craves normal food again or gets hungry again for the good stuff (fish, meat etc). Would that be when his blue light toxicity is mitigated?
>
> I think that's ALL that I have so far learned. Did I miss something?
>
> And yes, my goal is to grow my money and move to the tropics.

Just a few questions -
When you say your son is not eating, is he dropping weight or at a stage of being underweight result of this?
Energy levels ok?
What was the centralised diagnosis?

### drsongihan — 2024-07-26T12:55:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-337597>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=337593)
>
> Just a few questions -
> When you say your son is not eating, is he dropping weight or at a stage of being underweight result of this?
> Energy levels ok?
> What was the centralised diagnosis?

Centralized diagnosis is that the psychiatrist had no idea. First they started with Depression (MDD), and then when he became more agitated, they added generalized anxiety (GAD) and then when he started to loose focus, they added ADHD to the mix. As you may or may not know, for anybody with tendency of bipolar disorder, the wrongest medicine you can give is antidepressants and stimulants.
The centralized and decentralized diagnosis is bipolar depression or a broken circadian and molecular clock.

He is a healthy looking, average weight, still fit-looking, young men who is neither gaining nor dropping weight. He does not eat when he is in blue light by circumstances (eg. airplane light) in an environment where we are trying to optimize light otherwise. In other words, if he is TOO messed up, he will eat. When he is more out in the sunlight and we try to optimize, but there is still too little sunlight or too much blue light, he does not eat, and then when he is in the sun for like 2-3 days, he starts eating again. I think his body is "perfectly" regulating, which is why his weight does not fluctuate.

However, his energy level DOES of course fluctuate. Not eating for 2-3 days does equal = no energy, just sleeping.

As long as there is no excessive weight loss, I wouldn't be concerned and agree.

I won't go into too much detail here, however I was dealing with a similar situation with what you are dealing with and had to help someone through it who was involuntary admitted to a facility (in psychosis) and diagnosed with BPD who had a history of complex PTSD. This only happened in December of 2023 (around summer solstice). Psychiatrist said the person would never get of meds and would go straight into psychosis if they tried to. I asked this person if they trusted me and explained that they do not have the capacity to make there own decisions at this stage and I will be making the decisions for them. I had to drop everything to help them.

Organised a consult with Jack, with the sole intention of the message I was delivering to them of what needs to be done delivered by someone else to ensure compliance.

I took their phone, made sure they saw sunrise every morning, rain hail or shine, outside all the time (like Jack said If it was him he would hang them up to dry like a fish in the sun), plenty of seafood, protein, fats etc, CT (this part was hard), no ALAN and staying very dark (no TV's no anything).

In March/April this year completed the full taper of all meds (no rebound psychosis or any issues) and all other markers returned to normal (due to stopping the meds). The majority of centralised psychiatrist's, do not understand how their own medications work. In my opinion they are useful for acute / emergency issues, however, not much more as peoples lives are being destroyed by following their long-term instructions.

It was a bumpy road, however the person has recovered in a very short period, that surprised everyone who are now curious and asking what they did (including the docs).

Not at the best latitude, however we still made it happen with 100% consistency (no exceptions).

My two cents - would be try to figure out the custody issue and try to keep your son in one place (at a good latitude with light stability) where you can build consistency with these habits that are needed to save his life. Soon enough there will be no more battles as he will see the light and realise what needs to happen for him to have a good & happy life.

All the best with his recovery.

Mat I
I am very grateful for your post, and if you don't mind, would love to hear more or get more feedback. Each time a member responds with experience and knowledge or both, I learn something. And each time, another aspect gets added.
I have now heard it loud and clearly and will implement the no-ALAN in my house very strictly. No more WiFi, wired connection. ZAP the phone at night. EMF measurement and EMF Safe (need to contact Anthony when I return). TVs or games are long gone. No Nintendo or iPad he has access to. I already have blackout curtain and red light and purple light, blue blocking, and a plan for cold bath in the morning and in the evening before bed. I also tapered his medicine down to one type, Lithium. Because he is such a picky eater, I am planning on supplementing with DHA and also Vitamin D + K2 on top of maximizing sun.

If we live for now in a not-great-latitude of 43N, do you agree that trying to keep the wake-up and sleep schedule consistent with augmented light is better than letting him be subject to the Chicago timezone which is 5.30 am - 8.15 pm in the summer and 7.15 - 4.30pm in the Winter? It simply is not possible, even with cold therapy that I will implement (and assuming that we are both uncoupled haplotype looking at my mother and grandmother's lineage that is very fair skinned Asian even though I am dark because of my dad who is sort of Pacific islander Asian, but I will get 23-and-me done soon). I am planning on keeping his light schedule consistent at 6.30 AM WAKEUP, eat at 5pm, blue blocking after 6pm, and black-out and sleep at 8.30pm. Cold bath at 7am and 8pm. Do you have a better version? I would be open to any input. Even the above protocol was adjusted after one of the fabulous doctor member told me that earlier dinner time is important. The earliest I can do is 4.30 or 5pm, so I will at least try to keep that consistent.

> One option is to get all-spectrum plant growing lights. They are truly 360 - 800 nm.
> Another option is IR/near IR panels for a few hundred $$ plus UVA/UVB from a reptile store
> And of course the fancy option is EMR Tech
>
> 7) Blue blocking glass when looking at any monitor. I ordered Daylight saving computer, so that should help. And replacing my iPad with a e-ink Kindle should also help. Obviously, reading analogue books is best, but that is not always possible. If I need to look at the monitor in the evening, turn on red light.
>
> 8) Eat before sunset. That is not difficult in the summer. This will be nearly impossible in the winter since sunset is 4.30pm. I will try my best, and if that is difficult, eat under Redlight panel.
>
> 9) I have not understood under what circumstances (besides being out in the sun as much as possible, but if there is no sun) having a UV lamp is helpful. I presume it's emulating the mid-day sun. Hence, UV light would only be good during day if there is a snow storm and what not and we need to be indoors, we can turn the UV lamp on, but I should not have UV lamp on early in the morning when waking up and in the evening at dinner time or before going to bed since that's not Nature's light.
>
> P.S. Do NOT comment on how we cannot biohack the sunlight. I know that full well, but sometimes circumstances are out of our control, so after doing everything possible, I am asking what to do in situations where we have to be indoors due to extreme weather in higher latitude.
>
> 10) Fly to Yucatan (3.5 hours from Chicago) and stay weeks at a time. However, this would not help for a long weekend (say 3-4 days) because the harm from flying might reverse any benefit that we got from Yucatan? The benefit is worth the flying when we can stay 1 week + ideally longer?
>
> Or can we mitigate the damage of flying by taking CoQ10, drinking a lot, methylene blue, full-coverage plastic (polyester) clothing to mitigate blue light.
>
> 11) Sleep on a Magnetico Bed (we already have it) should help in a bad environment?
>
> 12) Drink deuterium depleted water (only my son) should help since he is in need of more support?
>
> 13) Eat protein and saturated fat (butter, steak, mackerel, salmon) rich food, as close as possible to Ketogenic Diet, or at least Paleo Diet. Avoid Sugar and Carb as much as possible. If my son is not hungry, he can just not eat. It's the best option anyway.
>
> Here, I have another question. I noticed that his "not eating" for 24 hours to 72 hours at a time got more extreme. I think it is a sign that he is "blue light toxic". The question is then why is the blue light toxicity only now showing up in a better environment? I have a theory and I want somebody to fact check me.
>
> \- Blue light toxic + no sunlight + high latitude (i.e. bad all round, and the environment he was in): my son was not eating much or craving sugar. I learned from Dr Jack's podcast with the dentist that sugar is body's natural way to fight ROS. So my son was fighting his blue light toxicity with sugar/carb.
>
> \- Blue light toxic + sunlight + lower latitude (our current environment): my son is eating even less, and not even sugar/carb. Now, the sunlight is "feeding" him, but he is still blue light toxic. Otherwise, his body's natural hunger should come back?
>
> What changes would have to occur where he craves normal food again or gets hungry again for the good stuff (fish, meat etc). Would that be when his blue light toxicity is mitigated?
>
> I think that's ALL that I have so far learned. Did I miss something?
>
> And yes, my goal is to grow my money and move to the tropics.

This plan is not going to reverse your son's problem FYI.

### drsongihan — 2024-07-29T01:26:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-337683>

I

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=337677)
>
> This plan is not going to reverse your son's problem FYI.

I agree and am aware. I am trying to buy some time by decentralizing my money, survive the winter and make a plan in which I can move with him for an extended period of time to lower latitude. Currently my money is tied to a house that I am trying to sell, I have custody issues that I am trying to defuse and I don’t have any sabbatical credits having moved institution. In other words I am stuck and he is tied to me for now.

Because of the economy it’s easier to find partner institutions in Asia and find an excuse for me to stay longer without sabbatical credit. So, if it’s for 9 months not a few weeks, can Singapore be a good place, despite the time change? Of course nnEMF is bad there but I could find a house in which I can control the WiFi Router and mitigate. The Latitude is excellent at less than 10.

I am also looking for the best place to fly with a direct flight from Chicago for shorter stays. Flying into Cancun is pretty horrible but maybe it’s worth the pain since it’s only 4 hours direct.

Alternatively there is a direct flight from Chicago to Guatemala City and then I can drive up to Antigua in 2 hrs. It’s at the foot of a Volcano, it’s higher altitude and low latitude, so combining the best of all worlds? It’s also pretty cool there in the low 70 all year round.

The idea is to spend 10 days in Nov, 3 weeks over Christmas break there and see whether we can find connections there. And then make a plan for a long term stay starting April through December in El Salvador, Antigua, Singapore … wherever I can find footing.

Meanwhile I am welcoming any input how to extend time, alleviate his condition and get him well enough that HE is actively working with me to improve.

### drsongihan — 2024-07-29T18:39:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-337717>

> [drsongihan said:](/goto/post?id=337683)
>

>

Of course nnEMF is bad there but I could find a house in which I can control the WiFi Router and mitigate. The Latitude is excellent at less than 10.

OK scratch Singapore based on what the member community gave me input on. She convinced me that its a no-go.

I have a good friend and a former student in the oasis of Bangalore, India. While this place can be very dense, there are patches especially around the Universities that are in protected nature and are green oasis. By the inverse square law, if I am within a green belt and in a stand-alone house (not Apartment) then it should be possible to mitigate. Its at the 12th Latitude. I have to find a research institution where I can go to do remote work without having sabbatical credit. IISc Bangalore is one of those places (Singapore would have been also, but on that peninsula I understood I cannot mitigate, really. Its too dense, too crazy, and too plastered with nnEMF). And by having good friends there changes everything.

I will keep everyone posted. I think it matters to share our journeys with all the imperfections we are going through. There gotta be a way to make things work in thousand different ways through knowledge and wisdom.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-30T00:34:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-337751>

I think you need to engage Dad at once. If not your kid is going to become a problem chronically

### Gene Kalmens — 2024-07-31T04:44:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-337829>

[@drsongihan](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/31459/) I also live in a Chicago suburb and regularly swim in Lake Michigan, with the campus where you work in direct view. April-May and October-November are great months to get cold in the lake.
My personal circumstances are entirely different, but here is what I do in my house:
1\. Router wifi is disabled. A wifi access point exists and can be turned on if necessary, using an easy outlet switch. Rarely on, but always off for the night.
2\. Wired internet is implemented using Trendnet power adaptors. This way, you can even get wired internet on smart phones and tablets anywhere in your house.
3\. [@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/) 's all house emf filter installed, with a kill switch for the bedroom. In Illinois, electrical conduits are enforced by the code, so very little EMF gets emitted from the walls as long as nothing is plugged in.
4\. Amber and red incandescent bulbs are used throughout the house.
5\. I have two EMR-TEK devices (they are flicker-free unlike from other manufacturers), an IR heat lamp from Home Depot, and a Spertis Vitamiin D light. I hardly use them in summer. I use these lights based on Jack's recommendation during a consult.
6\. I use IRIS software on my computer and an eye care flicker-free monitor with a blue blocking function. When I work remotely, I have a setup to work outside May-September, with minimal clothing. Always a standing desk.
7\. In the office, I have a red bulb and a yellow daylight bulb plugged in next to my computer, modified screen RGB color settings, and bue-blocking glasses at all times. I go outside regularly and take a full hour during the lunch break to take a dip in the lake or walk on lake front exposed to sun.

These are my 2 cents. If you would like to meet up somewhere near the beach, it will be an easy task for me.

Gene

### drsongihan — 2024-07-31T12:37:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-337856>

> [Gene Kalmens said:](/goto/post?id=337829)
>
> [@drsongihan](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/31459/) I also live in a Chicago suburb and regularly swim in Lake Michigan, with the campus where you work in direct view. April-May and October-November are great months to get cold in the lake.
> My personal circumstances are entirely different, but here is what I do in my house:
> 1\. Router wifi is disabled. A wifi access point exists and can be turned on if necessary, using an easy outlet switch. Rarely on, but always off for the night.
> 2\. Wired internet is implemented using Trendnet power adaptors. This way, you can even get wired internet on smart phones and tablets anywhere in your house.
> 3\. [@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/) 's all house emf filter installed, with a kill switch for the bedroom. In Illinois, electrical conduits are enforced by the code, so very little EMF gets emitted from the walls as long as nothing is plugged in.
> 4\. Amber and red incandescent bulbs are used throughout the house.
> 5\. I have two EMR-TEK devices (they are flicker-free unlike from other manufacturers), an IR heat lamp from Home Depot, and a Spertis Vitamiin D light. I hardly use them in summer. I use these lights based on Jack's recommendation during a consult.
> 6\. I use IRIS software on my computer and an eye care flicker-free monitor with a blue blocking function. When I work remotely, I have a setup to work outside May-September, with minimal clothing. Always a standing desk.
> 7\. In the office, I have a red bulb and a yellow daylight bulb plugged in next to my computer, modified screen RGB color settings, and bue-blocking glasses at all times. I go outside regularly and take a full hour during the lunch break to take a dip in the lake or walk on lake front exposed to sun.
>
> These are my 2 cents. If you would like to meet up somewhere near the beach, it will be an easy task for me.
>
> Gene

Let's meet up! I think I have your email, I will drop you an email. What you wrote sounds amazing. I have started to implement most of it. I would love to hear about your swim routine and when you do that (morning or evening?) and where? Interesting that you mention Spertis light. I just ordered it yesterday--there was a choice between one that has a 5 min switch with 75% UVB or a 20 or 30 min switch with 25% UVB (rest UVA), so I chose the 75% UVB one since I got plenty UVA through black light. I also am planning to install Anthony's kill switch and I would love to hear about the Trendnet power adaptors.

My son started to implement many changes, but I also recognized that I have to myself do everything as optimally as I can and he will follow suit. He Zaps his phone after 7pm now, we have blackout curtains that he uses, and I blocked the standby blue light that often are on the button of extension cords. It makes a difference because once all the lights are blocked, you can clearly see the many photons coming from those blue buttons. He has blue blocking glasses---I need to remind him to use them at all times indoors. I changed the bathroom light to amber but I still have the hallway lights that are hard to change, so I taped up the switches so we just don't use it. I want to ask you also about where to get amber recess lights. I need to get red-light flash light because I saw him yesterday going to the bathroom. He thought he was doing a good job by not turning on the lights but using the phone flashlight, and of course its not. I also noticed that it helps a lot when I open the windows in his room and in the kitchen in the morning and evening. Its humid and we do have some air conditioning on, so water is condensing on the window, but maybe that's the least of the problem. We are now keeping a strict routine of breakfast at 7am and dinner at 5pm (I cannot do earlier but I am thinking of changing my schedule to shift to 4.30pm dinner). With these changes plus 4 weeks in El Salvador my son's anger has calmed down finally. Even in the summer, that anger was always there, so even few days of calm feels good. In my observation, the zapping the phone and black out of light in the evening dramatically improved his sleep. But I want the electricity also killed. What made a difference in his anger was to have the red IR/near-IR panel on at all times when he is indoors between 6-8am and 5-7pm + open all windows + Zinc supplement (small dose) in the evening. His energy is very low, so I need him to get out with me to see the sunrise -- working on it, and to spend the entire afternoon outside, as opposed to just the windows open and dipping out.

I am trying to optimize my own environment, no matter whether I appear to have an issue or not. I do have near sightedness since teenage time, grey hair in the frontal area and easily scared legs and also some attention/focus problems, so I thought I will challenge myself to reverse some of these condition. My office has a great view and natural sunlight but the windows cannot be open. So I realize that this is almost as bad as having blue light one since my windows filter everything. So I am bringing in red and black light also into my office now, and have to eventually negotiate with my Chair whether I can trade my fancy office with Lake view with a less fancy one where I can open the damn window! Oh and going on a walk today at 5.20am to see the Sunrise at 5.40am with my dog.

P.S. I had another revelation regarding my little poodle. She is very healthy, quirky and has good energy. And I realize she does most things right. She has a doggie door that she uses all day long, she goes out and suns at the patio a lot. But because she is a picky eater and is soooooo against Kibbles, I did give her regularly human food scraps and felt like a "bad dog mom" who is not tough enough to stick to the doggie kibbles that are good for the dogs. And then it dawned on me that this was the Centralized thinking being burnt into me. Why the hell would it be "best for the dogs" to give them fully processed, dry, doggie pellets? They are also part of nature, they also went through evolution like all of us did, since when do dogs eat fully processed, pelleted, dry kibble? Its our stupid invention, the dog food industry making a ton of money, and the veterinarians make even more money because most dog owners have problems with their dogs from kidney stones to digestion problems to cancer. Its the Indoor life + artificial food they eat making them sick.

Most dog owners cannot give any other food to their dogs because apparently they started having stomach problems. The breeder gave the dogs, as soon as they were old enough to eat food, real food. Even better would be raw food, but that would be hard for me to do. So I am thinking me giving her regularly real food scrabs that inevitably have some salt was after all not wrong. And besides who invented the idea that dogs should under no circumstances eat salt?!

I feed my dogs raw and my oldest is almost 19 and healthy. I also don't do vaccines or heart worm just natural dewormer. The vets always lecture me but I lecture them right back. You can do raw dehydrated if raw frozen isn't doable, there are places on line. When I found Jack, I started watching my dog too and noticed she goes outside every morning around 5:20 and wants to eat by 6. My horse too loved to be out in the morning sun. He would find the sunniest part and lay down with the sun on him as the sun was rising. Also since incorporating a healthier light environment, my bald patches at the front of my hair line have almost completely grown back in. I am in the process of figuring out the nnEMF. I need someone to come just tell me what to do as this is an area I am lost with. The light environment I have down even my fridge lights are red. Thank you for sharing your son's story I have learned by reading your journey.

[@drsongihan](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/31459/) - it would be of benefit to check with Jack before you use any artifical UVA/UVB lamp on your son as due to his condition as he would be lacking melanin in certain areas of the brain. I would procced with caution on this and figure out the implications of using these artificial lights to re-build his melanin to hack a bad environment. You are obviously accross what needs to happen (i.e. get to a lower latitude with greater UV and light stability), however have chosen to try to hack your environment for the reasons outlined.

For context - the person (adult) I helped was slightly higher than 30S and at 800m altitude above sea level. Your current latitude as you know is not good whatsoever for what you are dealing with. What is going to happen with your son if he is not homeschooling? Already at a less than ideal latitude and he will be at school in a nnemf hell hole which will more than likley trigger more BPD symptoms and degrade melanin even more. Noting due to his age I assumme that you understand that his brain is not fully mylenated. Doing something is better than not trying in your current environment, however consider that you may not be able to fully heal your Son's issue with executing your plan. You need a long term solution, such as figuring our the custody issue, home-schooling, getting to a lower latitude etc. I'm sure you could find a way to make this work long term. As I beleive with your current plan you would be swimming against the currents.

As always just figure out what works and doesn't for your n=1.

All the best
Mat

Already at a less than ideal latitude and he will be at school in a nnemf hell hole which will more than likley trigger more BPD symptoms and degrade melanin even more. Noting due to his age I assumme that you understand that his brain is not fully mylenated. Doing something is better than not trying in your current environment, however consider that you may not be able to fully heal your Son's issue with executing your plan. You need a long term solution, such as figuring our the custody issue, home-schooling, getting to a lower latitude etc. I'm sure you could find a way to make this work long term. As I beleive with your current plan you would be swimming against the currents.
>
> As always just figure out what works and doesn't for your n=1.
>
> All the best
> Mat

Example Florida and surrounding areas? There seems to be a few UNI's around there - you could look at a new position assuming they have the same faculty there. Just spit balling some ideas for you.
