### La-Shawn Hill — 2025-01-01T18:01:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-344597>

> [Gene Kalmens said:](/goto/post?id=337829)
>
> [@drsongihan](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/31459/) I also live in a Chicago suburb and regularly swim in Lake Michigan, with the campus where you work in direct view. April-May and October-November are great months to get cold in the lake.
> My personal circumstances are entirely different, but here is what I do in my house:
> 1\. Router wifi is disabled. A wifi access point exists and can be turned on if necessary, using an easy outlet switch. Rarely on, but always off for the night.
> 2\. Wired internet is implemented using Trendnet power adaptors. This way, you can even get wired internet on smart phones and tablets anywhere in your house.
> 3\. [@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/) 's all house emf filter installed, with a kill switch for the bedroom. In Illinois, electrical conduits are enforced by the code, so very little EMF gets emitted from the walls as long as nothing is plugged in.
> 4\. Amber and red incandescent bulbs are used throughout the house.
> 5\. I have two EMR-TEK devices (they are flicker-free unlike from other manufacturers), an IR heat lamp from Home Depot, and a Spertis Vitamiin D light. I hardly use them in summer. I use these lights based on Jack's recommendation during a consult.
> 6\. I use IRIS software on my computer and an eye care flicker-free monitor with a blue blocking function. When I work remotely, I have a setup to work outside May-September, with minimal clothing. Always a standing desk.
> 7\. In the office, I have a red bulb and a yellow daylight bulb plugged in next to my computer, modified screen RGB color settings, and bue-blocking glasses at all times. I go outside regularly and take a full hour during the lunch break to take a dip in the lake or walk on lake front exposed to sun.
>
> These are my 2 cents. If you would like to meet up somewhere near the beach, it will be an easy task for me.
>
> Gene

#2 sounds like something I need. I've had difficulties disabling wifi at my house. My parents live with me and have cell phones. I called AT&T and told them I telework and was trying to hardwire my internet. They said they could disable wifi from their end, but then my parents would be without wifi for their phones. Can you tell me if the Trendnet power adapters are universal and work for all mobile phones? I'm not the most tech-savvy to begin with, but I'm willing to try and set something like this up. Or, [@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/) , do you have any product suggestions? I'm so frustrated because I want to make my home environment safer EMF-wise, but I don't know how to do it.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-02T16:57:49+0300

Today I give you a gift. It is my mitochondriac anniversary on April 2nd. I post this for my Mom. She used to call me at this time every year. Now i am calling you, my tribe. It is time to wake up.

[https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-40-124743154](https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-40-124743154)

"The sage doesn't seek to be profound but is useful. A sage himself is a former fool who got tired of themselves."

This insightful quote paints a picture of wisdom born not from an innate desire for grandeur but from a humble journey of self-awareness and growth. A decentralized sage, in this light, is not someone who sets out to dazzle the world with lofty thoughts but rather an individual who, through the weariness of their own folly, discovers a practical clarity. Their usefulness emerges from their centralized experience, mistakes made, lessons learned, and a quiet resolve to move beyond the foolishness of their centralized past. It’s a reminder that true depth often comes not from chasing profundity but from the simple, steady act of outgrowing one’s former self.

From Jack's teachings I think this is blue light toxicity, and his magnetism if off, pregnenolone steel and 100% something in is environment is throwing off his labs.

He lives in Kenya (currently assisting in the facility where I work in miami, so he travels a lot) but he says he's moved a lot in last 8 years so he doesn't think it can be anything where he is living as this has been a long term issue with his labs.
Where he lives in Kenya he lives middle nowhere.
He's a "biohacker" (is you can envision type of person) & uses topical testosterone cream
LABS:
high LDL
high HDL
normal triglycerides
Thyroid free T3 2.87pmol.L ( hasn't had rT3 tested)
Vitamin D 38 despite lots of sun exposure (explained we want this at least 60) (he's also supplementing too which I told him to stop)
Testosterone only 250
Low pregnenolone
He has tested where he sleeps with EMF reader and has a low reading.
He spends all day on devices with no blue light blockers
He walks in the sun every morning at 6.30am

The blue light man has made via the MKULTRA experiment that is now at global scale will destroy this heme cytochrome by vitamin A liberation. NO = nitric oxide is also destroyed. Recall that NO controls the behavior of our stem cell depots. This is why every snakeoil salesmen is selling lemmings stem cell injections now. That state is also controlled by 380 nm light. This was a great optical arrangement for the survival of mammals long ago 65 million years ago, but it is now a big problem when you realize what cytochrome P450scc is designed to do in modern mammals' insides under the power of ALAN where POMC is boss. This enzyme carries out the so-called side chain cleavage reaction, consuming cholesterol to produce pregnenolone, the precursor of cortisol, and all other steroids. This is why males are losing testosterone at record rates and why females sex steroid hormones are being demolished. Glucocorticoid synthesis is tightly regulated at the level of cholesterol metabolism, which responds to ACTH stimulation over a period of minutes and ceases equally quickly when this hormone is removed. Remarkably, this dynamic process is modulated under most circumstances not by control of the intrinsic enzymatic activity of P450scc, but rather by substrate availability. For this reason, cholesterol transport within the mitochondrion has emerged IN ALL MAMMALS as the key control point for steroidogenesis. All your hormone panels are controlled by it. MKULTRA found this out in 1964 and the government has weaponized it to put in screen technology to use against you since screens replaced paper. I have often heard [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) refer to his postulate "If you make it, don't take it." But was wondering about biotech devices like the Nano Vi ([https://eng3corp.com/](https://eng3corp.com/)) Are these devices disruptive or beneficial to signaling pathways in our body?

It's more difficult to understand Jack's postulate when it concerns biophysical technologies vs biochemical with food, supplements, etc.

Thank you to all who may input.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-07-26T20:41:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nano-vi.29215/post-337602>

I went to the Nano Vi web site and as far as I can tell it is a very expensive way of getting water vapor. Maybe it is breaking down the distilled H2O into hydrogen and oxygen ie ”Browns Gas” but they don’t say what it is actually doing - which makes me very suspicious. If you live in a dry climate sit outside in the sun with a cup of hot water (tea) and breath the vapors.

**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2

### Amber Ament — 2024-07-27T11:39:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/room-for-rent-queensland-australia.29217/post-337623>

I know this isn't a rental page but I'm going bonkers trying to find someone on the same page, here is my ad, please share if you know of anyone.
I cant afford rent on my own unless I work every weekend and I'm exhausted by then being a 46yrd old with a 3yr old on my own.

ROOM FOR RENT AVAIL NOW - HUGE ROOM approx 2.5 x6.5m!!
Looking for a tree change?
Beautiful split-level share house in Mt Nebo, 25mins from Samford or The Gap. I have a very active 3yr old and no pets. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Suited to someone who....
is used to living remotely, tank water conscious

loves living (mostly)without wi-fi - hardwired options avail and works well
prefers a chemical free, low tox environment
likes long winding roads to get anywhere, everytime
prefers the outdoors/hiking/rockpools
likes dim lighting
loves growing their own food
doesn't mind removing rubbish to a communal spot

COSTS: Rent $240 a week + bills. Gas stove, electricity, internet not included. P.O. Box costs about $25 a year.
OTHER: Share kitchen and bathroom - very spacious throughout. HUGE room with walk in robe, 2m74cm x 6m47cm plus robe (150cm x170cm)
Tank water, no rubbish removal (there is a local public use dump for residents).
One dog permitted, with approval from landlord.
I run a very small business from home selling edible seedlings, plants and natural products, you are welcome to join in, this does not interfere with daily life in the home.
Mt Nebo offers a great hermit life while also being very community minded. Lots of community events - Muso's night, ping pong, handball arvo, 'Pub' night, Bunya night, etc.
It has a very small local school with around 40 kids, 2 cafes, and one post office.
Major shops are a 30 minute drive (either The Gap or Samford).
Please get in touch directly for more details, 0410026250, if you would like to see more pictures.

# Thread: What is the benefit of eating this part of the fish?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-benefit-of-eating-this-part-of-the-fish.29224/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### thejessallen — 2024-07-29T18:55:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-benefit-of-eating-this-part-of-the-fish.29224/post-337720>

I recently moved to El Salvador, and I have to say one of the lovely things is the affordability of having someone who cooks and helps with housework.

The beautiful lady that helps me, cooks a lovely fish. One time she cooked the ones with the eyeballs. That was really tough to see eyeballs rolling around on the plate. I did not eat the eyeballs. Should I have eaten the eyeballs?

So she’s gone back to buying the fillets from the fresh fish market. In these photos, it looks like maybe this is the spine? It’s kind of a darker color than the rest of the fish.

If you tell me, there’s tons of extra vitamins here. I’ll most likely eat it.

It’s just like the rest of the fish. I might let it go

### Saichi — 2024-07-29T19:28:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-benefit-of-eating-this-part-of-the-fish.29224/post-337721>

I eat fish heads often as my local fish mongers sell them for usually $3/lb at the farmers markets since most Americans are culturally averse to sophisticated flavors. Tilefish eyeballs are gooey and delicious, you just have to spit out the semi-hard 'shell' that's in it.

### rtd — 2024-07-29T20:04:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-benefit-of-eating-this-part-of-the-fish.29224/post-337726>

Fish eyes and stuff in the socket are salty and tasty. I spit out the lens

### NikkiYork — 2024-07-30T06:33:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-benefit-of-eating-this-part-of-the-fish.29224/post-337767>

> [thejessallen said:](/goto/post?id=337720)
>
> I recently moved to El Salvador, and I have to say one of the lovely things is the affordability of having someone who cooks and helps with housework.
>
> The beautiful lady that helps me, cooks a lovely fish. One time she cooked the ones with the eyeballs. That was really tough to see eyeballs rolling around on the plate. I did not eat the eyeballs. Should I have eaten the eyeballs?
>
> So she’s gone back to buying the fillets from the fresh fish market. In these photos, it looks like maybe this is the spine? It’s kind of a darker color than the rest of the fish.
>
> If you tell me, there’s tons of extra vitamins here. I’ll most likely eat it.
>
> It’s just like the rest of the fish. I might let it go

That’s just regular fish meat and it often turns brown around the spine. I would just eat whatever you like eating. It can taste less good. I cut it out of salmon.

I don’t eat fish brain and that stuff, but some do.

The cheeks of a fish are the best part (when it’s a big enough fish that has cheeks, like a trout or bigger). It’s under the cheek scale, the greatest thing to find for a kid, hahahaha. It’s true. It’s a very tender little piece of fish.

Europeans cook and eat a fish whole and it’s really only what you are used to. It would be awful for me, if somebody poked out the eyes before cooking. Like, what kind of monster did that?

If the fish is fried dead, which is sometimes done by the Salvadorian street vendors - probably a good idea, if it wasn’t a great fish - you can eat the whole thing. And it tastes all the same. I doubt there are much nutrients left.

Pretty much everything in an animal has vitamins and paleo technically we adapted to eating every bit that is somehow eatable. Our ancestors ate all of that… and made a soup from the rest.

Eat the eyeballs, Lady! Hell no, hahahaha.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-07-30T17:06:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-benefit-of-eating-this-part-of-the-fish.29224/post-337782>

Catch 22 . . . You have to eat the eyes to see the advantage.

### thejessallen — 2024-07-31T04:59:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-benefit-of-eating-this-part-of-the-fish.29224/post-337832>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=337782)
>
> Catch 22 . . . You have to eat the eyes to see the advantage.

that's funny... they were rolling on the plate... doing all sorts of new things these day - I'll seeee

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2024-07-31T09:03:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-benefit-of-eating-this-part-of-the-fish.29224/post-337850>

The eyeballs are the highest source of DHA and other nutrients. Its part of the reason sheep's eyes are such a delicacy in the arabian peninsular. Theyre the best source of nutrients. Its literally brain food. Because eyes are brain parts.

2vitamin C content, fish eyes are also **a great source of omega-3 fatty acids, vitamin A, and protein**. "

>
> i use laptop 5-10 hours a day, no wifi though

If my iPad is plugged in charging with an ungrounded power cord my body voltage (BV) goes up to 40,000mV when I touch it.

IF your laptop (tablet) is plugged in to an ungrounded power cord while you use it you are being zapped with a 60Hz (50Hz) AC field the whole time. Either use the laptop on battery or use a grounded power cord. Measure your BV to know what you are getting.

All nnEMF has a cumulative affect and for most people, along with blue light, the 60Hz (50Hz) AC field in their home (& office) is the largest source of daily nnEMF - and the easiest to mitigate.

Hello. I watch Dr. Kruse podcast interviews daily, and I distinctly recall him saying that skin peeling from sun exposure was actually a good thing, as it’s a way of the body eliminating deuterium. Deuterium is good for babies, not good for fully developed adults. Your redness may not have resulted in your skin peeling. In any event, you can research ways of working into building your “solar callus.”

That said - the BBI recommended levels for electric field at night (using the body voltage method) are <10mV “no concern”, 10mV - 100mV “slight concern” - obviously the lower the better but if we can get someone down to <100mV for most that is good enough and makes the difference. Worry kills - I would rather someone be <100mV and happy than 20mV and worried they are not <10mV.

In my experience most people are not willing to do what it takes to get down to <10mV - IF - in their house it can even be achieved.

# Thread: Warning - Hidden sources of RF you need to measure
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 54
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mat.I — 2024-07-31T06:51:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/post-337840>

When measuring / checking your environments it is important to leave no stone unturned as every day items could be a source of high RF.

Example, I recently measured a place and checked some smart TV/google remotes and found these remotes are all Bluetooth and continually pulse (even when not in use). The remote was pulsing 100 to 200 mW/m2 not connected (with receiver and TV off). With TV and receiver on 100 to 300 mW/m2 with button presses ~550 mW/m2. This was tested and confirmed on 3 x google chromecast setups. **Solution -** make sure you only use IR remotes for TV's or smart boxes and connect via LAN. It is important to check to make sure certain functions can be disabled on your smart TV (and confirm with testing) e.g. wifi scanning and Bluetooth. In the event you can do this and your smart TV has both Bluetooth and IR remote (on some you may be able to only use the IR function and not activate the Bluetooth smart functions etc). If you cannot, however you can disable BT, then you can get an aftermarket universal IR remote OR get a basic tv and a set top box (connected via HDMI) that does not use BT and has an IR remote. Example in AUS the basic / older Gen Fetch TV smart boxes can be setup with LAN, WIFI disabled, No BT & IR remote only.

This also applies to any smart devices that use WIFI & BT (too many to name). Nothing "smart" is actually smart for your health... Also just because you can buy LAN adaptors for smart devices does not mean that they are not constantly pulsing RF (this is why it is important to measure) as some functions can be bypassed, however not disabled..

Also, just because something says it is disabled does not mean it actually is (hence the importance of measuring this). E.g. when disabling WIFI on some routers I have tested via the on/off toggle switch and/or via advanced setting schedules it is still transmitting RF (just not your specific networks)!! Make sure you check this and turn off at the power adaptor or via a mechanical/digital GPO timer switch that kills the power. Solution is obviously hardwired, however, to avoid disappointment make sure you test prior to purchasing or just purchase a modem without in built WIFI router (if you need more ports use a hub also). If moving to hardwired LAN make sure you use decent shielded cables and earth as required (based on the cable run and hub used).

Hope this helps.

### Mat.I — 2025-02-03T22:56:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/post-346163>

Just a follow up to this - any smart devices (such as washers, dryers, fridges etc) can be a pain to completly disable RF. I recently did a smart LG washer/dryer and had to dismantle it to disable the wifi module (unplug it). Note depending on how the PCB's are built some will not work if you unplug, therefore internal shielding (Faraday) is the only option if you wish to keep the device. This will typically involve detaching the module and shielding then refitting. Fortunately, the LG still operates with the module disconnected..

### Chlo_e — 2025-02-04T11:00:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/post-346172>

Thanks for highlighting this. We have a smart picture frame TV that I refused to use once I realised what it was doing and measured the RF. There was no way to disable the bluetooth (the WIFI could be disabled but not the bluetooth) so we looked up how to remove the module, opened up the back of the TV and now the problem is solved  we use it with a shielded ethernet connection and IR remotes as you've outlined above (it's unplugged completely when not in use).

We found instructions to disable bluetooth on a bosche washing machine in the manual - and the same with an older cannon printer. Pain in the backside though..

### Mat.I — 2025-02-04T23:57:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/post-346193>

We were able to disable our HP via software settings and have confirmed it with a meter. We do not have any wifi in our house and we are fully hardwired everywhere. Phones are used with ethernet adaptors (the non-charging type). Only issue is normal calls or text (outside whatsapp or similar services) however for the ppl that matter to you, you can advise to contact you via whatsapp and/or use a VOIP dedicated phone handset without POE (works great). Then you can have a time where you can activate cell data to check your phone, then disable again..

### eve001 — 2025-02-05T14:07:04+0200

### Mat.I — 2025-02-06T03:53:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/post-346259>

We wouldn't be able to achieve this without heavy shielding at our previous home closer to the CBD approx. 20km (12 miles) with high pop density. We moved a few years back due to C19. We live over 130km (80 Miles) from the CBD in a more rural based area with large lots and low pop density. No 5G that serves our suburb. Nearest 3G/4G tower from memory is no less than 1 mile away. The process was very easy due to the house being built to our 2010 Building Code which mandates certain construction requirements for specific climate zones.

Our house is brick veneer - so brick external wall on slab on ground, internal wall is timber stud with plasterboard, cavity between internal / external wall is packed with insulation bats and foil faced wall wrap. Roof - high pitch timber frame, quite dense concrete roof tiles and backed by what we call sarking in AUS (foil face wrap) to address thermal / condensation / last line of defence for water ingress etc..

When all the internal sources were eliminated I could have stopped there, however, when you know you do better, so I mitigated further. Only had to shield 3 x windows with tightly woven (small aperture) insect screen mesh these come in stainless steel or aluminium. Simply fitted directly over the window opening only (not going any further past the opening) after pulling taught and anchoring to the grout between the bricks with concrete nails (clouts). You can't even really notice it...

Additionally, we are on a corner block with quite a bit of buffer zone between nearby lots. The construction definitely helped as power density was already quite low externally thus attenuation & reflectance factor of the existing construction was sufficient. I think If we had a older house without the sarking and wraps we would have had to potentially shield more areas.

### Mat.I — 2025-02-06T04:10:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/post-346260>

A longer term solution for the EF when I get some spare time will kick off (hopefully) over the next couple of months. Currently, reconfigured (split) circuits and keep them permanently isolated in the bedrooms. Long term will be permanent whole house DC lighting, reconfigure power circuits with metal conduits (in certain areas for required power outlets) that is grounded, my internal office will likely run completely on DC (via a battery pack that I'm going to build).

### Anthony Smith — 2025-02-08T18:38:25+0200

### Mat.I — 2025-02-09T01:22:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/post-346444>

Only caveat that I'll add is - make sure before doing a DIY EMF assessment you have a thorough understanding on how to measure and use the equipment correctly.. If you are asking about meters and equipment that tells me you are quite "green" to this area.. which is fine just do your research. I generally recommend that people get their area measured professionally to have a baseline to work from (if you are not confident using the equipment or have a good understanding of nn-emf). This is even more important if you are dealing with health conditions. There is alot more to it than what we are discussing here, especially in certain situations where you are only isolating circuits for bedtime (you could still be exposed to DE in the day) which is even more critical to identify and address. BV is good it will tell you push/pull forces based on the pressure differential to your earth/ground ref point, however does not tell you the frequency of the field etc.. It all depends what approach is taken to mitigate the issues. If you only use partial measures such as isolation at night but you are still exposed in the day to EF outside acceptable limits then you would want to make sure there are no artifacts in the wave (DE). This is where you could look into addressing the actual wiring route issues, looking into filters or alternative to limit exposure look at auto demand current switches (these shut off when circuit is not in use). I could go on for a while with other points and scenarios, I think you get the picture that it is a mine field, however, don't let that discourage you. Just do your research and understand what you are doing, what you can and can't measure with basic equipment and understand the risk of that given your individual n=1 situation.. To confirm the Cornet & the BV meter is a very good starting point and will get the basics done. If you mitigate to acceptable levels, you shouldn't have to worry too much about the issues I mentioned above unless there are case specific issues that need to be looked into further.

### Mat.I — 2025-02-09T01:52:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/post-346446>

See my thread last yr re: Cornet Meter
Thread 'New 2024 Cornet EMF Meter' [http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-2024-cornet-emf-meter.28911/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-2024-cornet-emf-meter.28911/)

After purchase and talking to many others in the BB industry and using the Cornet in areas measured under the same conditions with the Safe and sound & gigahertz solutions (highest end RF meters), NFA 1000 (for mag/EF) it will do the job of identifying sources, levels pre & post mitigation. It is a great allrounder convenient (pocket sized) meter that has great accuracy/sensitivity for the price you are paying. It also has lots of advanced functions that most do not even use, such as the data log and frequency waveform analyser. From memory this is up to 50hours of rf data and you can even log the EF and Mag data via usb cord.. I'm comfortable and confident with this meter as a decent all rounder with an added bonus of a light flicker meter that is also very accurate when compared to others..

### Mat.I — 2025-02-09T04:33:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/post-346465>

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=346417)
>
> That's because the meters can't occupy the same space and levels are usually very different just a couple inches apart when not in a well shielded space or outdoor space comparable to Mat's yard.

Spatial variations are quite possible within close proximity due to reflection, refraction, diffraction, constructive and destructive interference etc.. and so on. Or it could be what we refer to as calibration drift accross the devices.. If within tolerances no issue providing the accuracy needed for a particular project is achieved.. If outside the tolerance range then you would need to establish the cause e.g. environmental conditions or calibration drift.. Depending on what ISO standard the units are calibrated to this may be in the range of + or - 1 to 3 dB. From memory the s & s had a less than desirable range of + or - 6dB which isn't the best e.g pd could be 4 x lower or higher than measured.. if actual pd is 100uW/m2 the measurement deviation could display values from 25 uW/m2 to 400uW/m2 due to this tolerance... Hence why S & S isn't the end all be all.. I.will add more to this in the future. Cornet is advertised at + - 3 to 6dB (frequency depending) where as S & S is fixed at 6dB based on advertising. If I had to pick my ultimate professional meter for rf it would be the top end gigahertz solutions in the HF series.. great meters if you have the money to spend.

### Kristuune — 2025-02-21T03:09:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/post-347058>

### Anthony Smith — 2025-02-21T03:34:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/post-347060>

> [Kristuune said:](/goto/post?id=347058)
>
> You are right- I am very green and a lot of this is over my head- right now I am drowning in it all

Take a deep breath and relax. There is a lot to learn and figure out, but you will. With EMF measure and you will know BUT In the mean time, just turn off as much as you can at night (WiFi & circuits in your sleep area) and know you are better off than before.

### Woodstock — 2025-02-24T04:59:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/post-347199>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=337840)
>
> When measuring / checking your environments it is important to leave no stone unturned as every day items could be a source of high RF.
>
> Example, I recently measured a place and checked some smart TV/google remotes and found these remotes are all Bluetooth and continually pulse (even when not in use). The remote was pulsing 100 to 200 mW/m2 not connected (with receiver and TV off). With TV and receiver on 100 to 300 mW/m2 with button presses ~550 mW/m2. This was tested and confirmed on 3 x google chromecast setups. **Solution -** make sure you only use IR remotes for TV's or smart boxes and connect via LAN. It is important to check to make sure certain functions can be disabled on your smart TV (and confirm with testing) e.g. wifi scanning and Bluetooth. In the event you can do this and your smart TV has both Bluetooth and IR remote (on some you may be able to only use the IR function and not activate the Bluetooth smart functions etc). If you cannot, however you can disable BT, then you can get an aftermarket universal IR remote OR get a basic tv and a set top box (connected via HDMI) that does not use BT and has an IR remote. Example in AUS the basic / older Gen Fetch TV smart boxes can be setup with LAN, WIFI disabled, No BT & IR remote only.
>
> This also applies to any smart devices that use WIFI & BT (too many to name). Nothing "smart" is actually smart for your health... Also just because you can buy LAN adaptors for smart devices does not mean that they are not constantly pulsing RF (this is why it is important to measure) as some functions can be bypassed, however not disabled..
>
> Also, just because something says it is disabled does not mean it actually is (hence the importance of measuring this). E.g. when disabling WIFI on some routers I have tested via the on/off toggle switch and/or via advanced setting schedules it is still transmitting RF (just not your specific networks)!! Make sure you check this and turn off at the power adaptor or via a mechanical/digital GPO timer switch that kills the power. Solution is obviously hardwired, however, to avoid disappointment make sure you test prior to purchasing or just purchase a modem without in built WIFI router (if you need more ports use a hub also). If moving to hardwired LAN make sure you use decent shielded cables and earth as required (based on the cable run and hub used).
>
> Hope this helps.

Yes I think my TV remote does this pulsing. I am using a shielding pouch you like you can put your cell phone in to block RF. But of course it will pulse on you every time you use it to change the channel, etc. I missed this at first because of the pulsing so you have to test with a good meter and be patient. Mine will pulse every time you touch it!

### Mat.I — 2025-02-24T23:59:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/post-347247>

> [giedremail said:](/goto/post?id=347227)
>
> Hi Mat, we have moved the country and are renting now. There is a WiFi router in the house. I am not very good with technology myself. I have asked around but no one could recommend me a person who could come round and set up the internet without WiFi. What can I do, anyone/anywhere you could recommend? Thank you

If I were renting and wanted to minimise issues with the commercial rental agreement and wanted something that could be easily removed with minimal damage (in summary DIY version on the assumption there are no hardwired points) -
1\. disconnect any router with WIFI.
2\. Purchase a modem such as a Draytek Vigor 160 (without any wifi) with bridge or router mode.. The actual product will vary based on your incoming ISP line configuration e.g. ADSL, fibre, coax etc.. This product is just an example and if you had ADSL you could use something like this.
3\. Figure out your location and how many LAN ports you require (for your devices).
4\. Once you know how many purchase a basic non POE unmanaged switch with adequate ports to support your use.
5\. For cable routing - you can use mini duct (square) conduit and run around architraves, cornices, skirtings (with minimal holes to pass through to rooms). Or you can utilise the floor or roof space (subject to having one) for cable routing.
6\. In summary - incoming ISP connection if ADSL will go to modem (configured in router mode), then ethernet cable to the desktop based (basic) network switch and all your out going connections (to rooms/areas) will connect individually to the network switch / hub..

### Anthony Smith — 2025-06-26T06:47:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/post-355295>

I have measured RF from florescent bulbs including mini CFL bulbs. Unfortunately there are still a lot of these out there.
Getting RF from a “wall”?
RF can travel a significant distance. What is on the other side of the wall?
Modern WiFi routers can reach 200ft - 300ft.
Measure outside and try to see where the RF is coming from.

I have also seen people online (videos) talking about high RF readings from something and their meter was set on Electric fields . . .

### TB14 — 2025-06-28T08:06:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/post-355399>

> [michellejorge said:](/goto/post?id=355312)
>
> Does anyone have any contacts in south Florida to remove the smart aspect of smart appliances? I bought a remodeled house and currently disconnected both my stove and microwave (which I don’t need) because they were giving me high ratings while connected but not in use. Otherwise, I’m going to need to buy a new stove possibly used or older bc many are smart. There’s only so much I can make in the toaster oven!

I managed to get the RF chip out of the smart stove that came with my place. Was easier than I thought. The piece to remove is generally a little printed circuit board ("PCB") which is attached to a wire with a connector on the end that you just need to unplug. I think with stoves most of them are located somewhere behind the display where the digital clock is (at least that's where mine was). But before just taking stuff apart and looking for it (it can be hidden/tricky to find), I recommend first trying to find the service manual (different from the user manual) online and that should tell you where the part you need to find and remove is. They might call it a Wi-Fi/RF chip or Wi-Fi/RF PCB or something similar, but that's the part you need to remove. That said, there's no guarantee that the Wi-Fi components aren't welded directly onto the main printed circuit board (i.e., not just an easily unpluggable separate piece), and if that's the case it may be impossible to remove them without damaging the appliance (or at least more difficult and beyond the range of my current knowledge). Make sure you unplug the stove before you start taking it apart too of course.

### Mat.I — 2025-06-29T01:47:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/post-355448>

Yes, this can be a pain.. I just ordered a low emf FIR sauna for my wife. They address the MAG/EF in the heaters / wiring design but neglect the RF component. I requested a wiring diagram, which they never provided, so I opted for the more basic model (slightly lower powered) without BT, speakers and wifi etc. As I did not want it to be the case of the module being incorporated within the MCB or even separate daughter board but relied upon for handshakes and the boot up. The smart devices are a pain. Fortunately, all of the ones I have chosen based on prior research have been able to be disconnected, however, I have encountered some that will have a separate module/board and when disconnected the device defaults to error mode as it is reliant on that module for handshakes at boot up (like a system check).. This leaves 2 options - go to an electronics tech to do board level mods or just shelid the module (however, if incorporated within the MCB this becomes a pain and needs some creativeness).

Soon we will likley not be able to disable anything without board level mods.. Tying into the 2030 agenda to have us connected to the cloud..

### Saichi — 2025-06-29T01:55:38+0300

### TB14 — 2025-06-30T04:45:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/post-355512>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=355448)
>
> Soon we will likley not be able to disable anything without board level mods..

We seem to be almost there already with TVs, at least in the U.S. market. And it's also the fact that you can't really get functional stand-alone non-Smart TVs either. They still make non-Wi-Fi TVs but those also lack Ethernet, so you have to get a separate streaming device if you want to stream on them. There don't seem to be any more Ethernet-only TVs (like the ones I remember playing Xbox 360 online with my friends on as a teenager less than 10 years ago) being made. None of the recent SmartTVs that I could find have the ability to turn Wi-Fi totally off at the settings level, either. And a few Sonys were the only models I was able to find where people online confirmed that you can remove the Wi-Fi PCB without rendering the TV inoperable.

I almost wonder why they even left the Ethernet port in there for us. Same goes for why Tim Cook has kept the MacBooks and iPhones fairly simply Ethernet-compatible (though removing the port). It sometimes feels like they leave barely enough room where avoiding/addressing these bioweapons is practicable if you have the knowledge to know what to look for and how to fix it.

# Thread: THE QUICKEST WAY TO RUIN MITO REDOX: viral contamination thru gain of function research.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quickest-way-to-ruin-mito-redox-viral-contamination-thru-gain-of-function-research.29232/>
**Subforum:** Redox Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-31T15:37:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quickest-way-to-ruin-mito-redox-viral-contamination-thru-gain-of-function-research.29232/post-337863>

For those who do not believe viruses are real you might want to see the modern day version or Drs/ Mary Sherman and Sarah Stewart: Judy Mikovits, PhD is that person and she has been made persona non grata for her discovery on viral contamination in vaccines in 2011.

She is a biochemist and molecular biologist with more than 33 years of experience. Internationally known, a veritable “rock star” of the scientific world, she served as the director of the lab of Antiviral Drug Mechanisms at the National Cancer Institute before directing the Cancer Biology program at EpiGenX Pharmaceuticals. She later developed the first neuroimmune institute.

Her early work focused on cancer and HIV, her latest on Chronic Fatigue Syndrome and autism. She has published more than 50 peer-reviewed articles.

In 2011, she made the discovery that destroyed her career. She found that at least 30% of our vaccines are contaminated with gamma-retroviruses. Not only is this contamination associated with autism and chronic fatigue syndrome, it is also associated with Parkinson’s, Lou Gehrig’s disease, and Alzheimer’s. The retroviruses contaminating vaccines originate from mice used for research. Dr. Mikovits asked the world, “How many new retroviruses have we created through all the mouse research, the vaccine research, gene therapy research? More importantly, how many new diseases have we created?”

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-31T15:43:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quickest-way-to-ruin-mito-redox-viral-contamination-thru-gain-of-function-research.29232/post-337867>

When they destroyed all of her scientific work, and they discredited everything she or Frank Ruscetti had ever published on the topic, and arranged for the publication of my mug shot in Science, the NIH very deliberately sent the message to researchers everywhere about what would happen to any honest scientist who dared ask those important questions. This is exactly what they did to Sarah Stewart and Mary Sherman's of the world.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-31T16:49:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quickest-way-to-ruin-mito-redox-viral-contamination-thru-gain-of-function-research.29232/post-337872>

Viruses attack how mitochondria handle electrons.

The electron transport chain in the mitochondria of humans, and mammals as a whole, reverses the process of photosynthesis in chloroplasts. Electrons, hydrogen (H+), and oxygen (O2) make ATP and water (H2O), extremely simply put. Blue light (and nnEMF, both as some of the highest energy outputs) disrupts the complex enzymes of the ETC, as well as electron transfer capacity (electron leak), K+ signaling (crucial to mitochondrial water handling), ATP synthesis, thus disrupting H2O formation as end product of the ETC. The chromophores (light sensors) that make up the ETC shift from ground, to singlet, to triplet state, which transfers electrons to O2, forming superoxide (O2•-) and a photosensitizer radical cation. That triplet state chromophore transfers energy to O2 to form singlet O2. The leakage of electrons interacts with O2 to form more O2•-. That O2•- receives more electrons from this leakage to form other reactive oxygen species (ROS), such as hydrogen peroxide (H2O2), hydroxy radicals (·OH), and perhydroxyl radicals (HO2·). Electron leakage from the ETC is caused by the disruption and subsequent damage to complex/cytochrome enzymes.

Why is putting an excimer laser in the hands of a refractive eye surgeon like giving a serial killer a universal hotel room key?

Over 3.8 billion years, sunrise’s role in TCA/urea activation evolved with light, temperature and oxygen variations to optimize energy and repair, with thanatotranscriptomic genes adapting to these variables and these biological cycles. LASIK demoloshes this stiochiometry in nanoseconds.

Daytime TCA activity stores fat reserves, reducing sleep’s metabolic burden and enhancing repair (e.g., mitophagy).
Circadian Sync: Sunrise aligns melatonin suppression with TCA/urea cycle activation, ensuring deep sleep occurs at the end of a day that kills you slowly. It works this way so your colonies of mitochondria can recover every night to give you more time on Earth. If this fails you can die rapidly in ways you cannot fathom.

This blog tells you how the thanatotranscriptomic genes evolved and what their stiochiometry is. These genes were critical in evolving wakefulness from sleep. Enhancing AM light exposure (e.g., UV-mimicking therapies) amplifies TCA/urea benefits, complementing DDW production and ideal oxygen utilization to create precision in UPE output to extend life day after day. Temperature control is critically important to this thanatotranscriptomic method. This evidence is still seen in melatonin action today. It is maximally active in the brain after a 3-4 degree drop in temperature of the CSF. LASIK destroys this relationship.

### Katie P — 2025-07-23T03:45:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-sunglasses-glasses-contacts-lasik-cataract-surgery-destroy-redox.29237/post-356822>

I had PRK surgery in the military 17 years ago to correct my 20/400 vision so I could beome a pilot. I consider PRK the more painful cousin of LASIK, since you have to sit in a dark room for four days while the epithelium layer healed from being sanded down. Looking back, maybe that kind of forced sensory deprivation would’ve been slightly handy after a nuclear blast or maybe the opposite is true. I also didn't follow their instructions to wear sunglasses after that first week. I've always loathed sunglasses in part because I can’t stand anything that feels like an accessory masquerading as a necessity. Plus, let’s be honest, there’s a certain personality type that seems to gravitate toward sunglasses, either to avoid eye contact, or maybe they’re just running low on dopamine. Either way, I opted for squinting.

Lately, I’ve noticed the skin around my eyes never burns or tans, leaving permanent white rings like I’m wearing phantom sunglasses. I used to think it was a redox issue, but now I wonder if it’s just leftover damage from PRK.

Even if there is damage in my eye, I've felt better over the last 2 years of following Jack's advice then I had in the 10 years prior. I also haven't been sick in the last two years, only barely a throat tingle here and there, which is something I didn't think was possible after years of being sickly under any amount of additional stress. Having a year round tan and going to bed when the sun goes down makes a huge fucking difference.

Avoiding this decentralized struggle (e.g., via opioids over sunlight) poisons resilience and creativity.

Melanin, Melatonin, and Vitamin D Deficiency: Hypothyroidism reduces Coulomb charge in the skin and lens due to low melanin, melatonin, an an altered VDR, acting in unison to alter how Gauss law can be used and this impairs charge collection in cells leading to disease. This process opacifies the lens, causing cataracts.

Stars require darkness to shine, just as human biology needs light-dark cycles for optimal function. An opaque lens diminishes this balance, reducing the relative power of light to drive epigenetic programming and cellular evolution. Modern artificial lighting (e.g., blue light overuse) exacerbates this, further distorting the light-dark tension essential for health.

Leptin-Melanocortin Pathway: Leptin stimulates TRH via the leptin-melanocortin pathway, boosting α-MSH and POMC, which are essential for melanin production. In hypothyroidism, this pathway is disrupted, leading to pale skin, lens opacification, and increased sunburn risk. A cataract gives you a ciliary ganglionectomy just like sunglasses do and this leads to many diseases in modern humans.

Thermoregulation and TRH: TRH acts as a neurotransmitter, inducing hyperthermia when injected intracerebroventricularly. This heat stress, combined with a diminished heat sink (e.g., in the retina and lens), heightens cataract risk because it alters the production of high signal UPEs in the eyes and central retinal pathways leading to more diseases you never saw coming.

### DLO — 2025-08-18T01:50:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-sunglasses-glasses-contacts-lasik-cataract-surgery-destroy-redox.29237/post-358394>

The FDA has approved VIZZ, a once-daily eye drop developed by LENZ Therapeutics. It works by gently narrowing the pupil, creating a “pinhole effect” that sharpens near vision without affecting distance sight. Users can enjoy up to 10 hours of clear close-up vision, all without the discomfort or side effects of older treatments.
This breakthrough could transform daily life for over 100 million Americans who rely on reading glasses, giving them freedom to read, cook, or work up close without interruption. With a launch expected in late 2025, VIZZ represents a major step forward in eye care.
Science is giving millions the chance to see life up close again, naturally and safely.

### Saichi — 2025-06-07T14:17:23+0300

I thought you would know if he were recording on a transmitting phone the readings would be 100k to millions of µw.

The unshielded foyer space right outside my Brooklyn room were the same as the mud pit in Neil's recording of 'nature.' Decades of lead paint helped with that, ironically the city tried to "remediate for free" the lead paint in the unrenovated apts in my building but I refused. Outside my window ranged anywhere from 200 to 5k.

I was a member of World Spa which had a level of 2 in the 200 F deg main banya. The cold plunge area was around 300 which is why I wore an old non-nano emf beanie and emf safari hat while water's a natural faraday. I had a couple sunbathing spots in Prospect Park with peaks no greater than 10. I spent 80% of my living and sleeping time in safe spaces in NYC which I'm confident is better than almost everyone living out in "nature" even though the other 20% were in higher levels. But nnemf is non-linear in effect which is why I recommend people to always use the audio function on their Safe and Sound as you'll realize 30 is closer to 300 than 3.

I used to walk my dog in California in an idyllic marsh that used to have levels of 30 to 50 before the Fema test which I wasn't thrilled about and stopped after the levels went up 6 to 10 fold. "Optimal health" in my experience means maximizing time spent in safe spaces and we should all strive for Mat's environment but not let what's immediately achievable be the enemy of 'perfect' as there is no perfect left on this planet.

### Sirena — 2024-08-01T22:02:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataracts-hire-chauffeur-or-travel-for-better-iol-or-reverse-in-stable-light.29243/post-337946>

I'm very clear n='s 1. There's no doubt about that. Plus I'm very aware of Jack's comments regarding cataracts, contacts and sunglasses and agree; but also open to hearing from others who might have dealt with this little pickle.

So far, I've met with a few ophthalmologists and specifically asked about removing the cataract and leaving it alone without implanting an IOL This idea was a no go from all of them. However, a highly recommended ophthalmologists suggested the Bausch and Lomb EnVista Aspire IOL which apparently allows “some light"-- but I just got word. This particular IOL allows "minimal light."

With that said, what about the following:

—Forgo the surgery and hire a chauffeur until science catches up.

—Fly to Germany or Japan where one might find someone to implant an IOL like the Teleon IOL or better.

—Wait until ES has someone who’ll implant an IOL which absorbs the entire light spectrum.

—Move to ES where the stable light might reverse the cataract?

Thank you for your thoughts.

Sirena

### Sirena — 2024-08-02T18:26:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataracts-hire-chauffeur-or-travel-for-better-iol-or-reverse-in-stable-light.29243/post-337995>

I cancelled my procedure.
I want light.

Now the question is: How to proceed from here.

# Thread: Why do diabetics lose their superoxide pulse and why do their WBCs not work well?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-diabetics-lose-their-superoxide-pulse-and-why-do-their-wbcs-not-work-well.29248/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-02T06:33:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-diabetics-lose-their-superoxide-pulse-and-why-do-their-wbcs-not-work-well.29248/post-337976>

The family of NADPH-dependent oxidases is called Nox. What do NOX do? They create ROS in cells for a PURPOSE, and that purpose is not bad. The sole function of this enzyme family is the production of ROS specifically, superoxide (O2−) Centralized science perpetrated the lie that ROS was only associated only with detrimental effects on cellular structures. The first described family member was Nox2 & Nox2 is responsible for the respiratory burst in phagocytes that drive immune function. That certainly is not a bad thing. It is necesary for life and longevity.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-02T06:36:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-diabetics-lose-their-superoxide-pulse-and-why-do-their-wbcs-not-work-well.29248/post-337977>

All Nox enzyme family members share a membrane-bound catalytic core structure, a glycoprotein consisting of six trans-membrane α-helical domains (the actual gp91phox in Nox2), which contains two conserved HEME groups near the N-terminus. Why is the heme domain a big deal in infection risk in a nnEMF blue lit world? Blue light destroys all heme based photoreceptors. This means blue light destroys NOX and this decreases ROS and superoxide. This is why type 1 and 2 diabetics have no superoxide pulse from cytochrome 1. NO B12 and many other non visual photoreceptors get blown up by blue light and nnEMF. I hope some of you are beginning to see how all this stuff links to the light you ABUSE. This is where chronic disease occurs.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-03T15:38:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-diabetics-lose-their-superoxide-pulse-and-why-do-their-wbcs-not-work-well.29248/post-338034>

Diabetics often get dementia; it is often called type 3 diabetes = Alzheimer's.

If you have dementia never wear a hat or sun glasses.

Transcranial Near Infrared light stimulation of the brain improves dementia and cognition.

Only 6 MINUTES twice a day was enough to stimulate significant improvement in memory and other symptoms

The patients exhibited less anxiety, improved mood, energy, and positive daily routine after ~14-21 days of treatment.

Infrared light enhanced the clearance of amyloid plaque through phagocytosis, lower cytokine levels, increased NAD+, and increased ATP

This is all very beneficial to brain function.

And of course... these are frequencies of light found in SUNLIGHT

This bodes well for the safety of the treatment, as we've been exposed to infrared light from the sun for billions of years

But it also means getting outside in the sun is an accessible free therapy that is highly neuroprotective.

### [Transcranial Near Infrared Light Stimulations Improve Cognition in Patients with Dementia](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8219492/)

Dementia is a complex syndrome with various presentations depending on the underlying pathologies. Low emission of transcranial near-infrared (tNIR) light can reach human brain parenchyma and be beneficial to a number of neurological and neurodegenerative ...

 www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

### Jack Kruse — 2024-10-09T17:52:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-diabetics-lose-their-superoxide-pulse-and-why-do-their-wbcs-not-work-well.29248/post-340697>

New blog out today on MKULTRA disease diabetic neuropathy.

Does insulin work by taking its cues from the solar spectrum? Yep.

Most people blame food for what poor choices in light really cause = Cally & Cassie Means

If so, how is insulin’s action quantized?

When the energies of electrons are higher because of the action of the photons of the sun, that signal is transduced by the cytochromes in the electron chain transporters. Most electrons from carbohydrates enter cytochrome one because NADH is a fluorophore protein that controls hydrogen flows in the matrix. It absorbs best at the 340 nm range of light which is strongly in the UV range of the solar spectrum. This couples photoelectrically with insulin release from the pancreas. It uses another solar hormone, melatonin (in mitochondria), to help it achieve its goals in the gut. Melatonin is first made with the interaction of UV light and aromatic amino acids in the mitochondria of the central retinal pathways.

Melatonin is produced first by the mitochondria in the eye and then ocular melatonin helps liberate melatonin in the pineal gland in response to the absence of light. If light, especially from 400nm -465nm is present none is released in the pineal. Melatonin levels are quite low during the day and reach their peak 4 hours after darkness. Like other hormones, melatonin works by receptor-mediated bonding using calcium as a controller. This is the current belief paradigm. i believe this paradigm is wrong. I believe all bio-molecules are linked and operate via electromagnetic resonance.

### Johan Lindøm — 2024-08-22T10:00:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meet-up-europe.29249/post-338779>

Madeira a geopathic stess zone? I do not thing so:

["Madeira Island is the emergent top of a massive shield volcano that rises about 6 km from the floor of the Atlantic Ocean and forms the largest island of the 90-km-long Madeira Archipelago. Construction of the volcano along E-W rift zones from the Miocene to about 700,000 years ago was followed by a period of extensive erosion and possible edifice collapse. Two steep-walled amphitheaters open to south in the central part of the island. Late-stage eruptions are scattered throughout the island and lasted until the Holocene, producing scoria cones and intra-canyon lava flows covering rocks of the older eroded edifice. The youngest activity lies in the west-central part of the island, and consists of cinder cones in the upper Sao Vicente valley, a series of intra-canyon flows, and a tephra layer on top of the Paul da Serra plateau dated at about 6,500 years ago."](https://volcano.si.edu/volcano.cfm?vn=382120)

Cape Verde a geopathic stess zone? I do not thing so: [The Cape Verde Islands were created by magma rising through the ocean, which is why they never were part of the African continent.](https://www.capeverde.com/country-info/volcanism) My biggest concern about traveling to Cape Verde is the American military. They have invested half a trillion dollars in military infrastructure, and a portion of these funds is certainly used for communication. However, I have not been able to understand how this affects the island of Santa Maria, where we will be spending New Year's.

Another vacation destination I am considering is Gambia in Africa. Flights are arranged there from Norway.

### Pablo — 2024-08-25T00:27:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meet-up-europe.29249/post-338867>

Thanks [@Johan Lindstrøm](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/22853/) for suggestions! Cape Verde seems best option latitude wise (15-23), especially interesting for shorter days in december/january... Maybe a bit drawbacks could be language, their currency and maybe not super secure place, I heard?

The geopathic stress of Canary Islands (lat 28), I had not heard from Jack. I don´t know, it could be, but my intuition tells me the stronger than my latitude Sun should compensate, and that other things like dirty electricity, RF/MF, eleectric fields from the wiring of the particular house are probably more important to check out in any place we go. I´m not expert, maybe wrong. But good things are language (for me), probably less paperwork to travel there, etc...

And Madeira (lat 32), still decent latitud for months like October, March... And yes de BTC stuff makes it interesting. So I see those 3 pretty good.

And Gambia (lat 13), I´m all ears if your experience is good. I´m not rich and for job reasons my idea is to do several short visits South during Winter, like 7-10 days, so for me it is probably not worth to do very long and expensive travels. But when I´ll be able to, I am open to ecuatorial Africa places. I got a friend from Ecuatorial Guinea and he says is super cheap and pretty secure place (if you don´t question the goverment too much there...).

Lets see if we can meet!

### Michalis — 2024-09-08T15:53:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meet-up-europe.29249/post-339401>

You can use Xapo to buy bitcoins or alternatively use HodlHodl which is anonymous. You will pay more though. I think Xapo is a good solution as banks in the UK are blocking purchases. Ideally open a Xapo account and also open a Wise account as well.
Use the Wise account to transfer money from your legacy bank account to the Xapo account. Use it as a proxy in case your bank catches a whiff of what you are trying to to so be safe and continue buying.

As for meetups Fuertaventura looks great on paper. Actually I might be going soon if the plan I got for the winter falls through. La Gomera seems also good but a bit off the beaten track.

### Pablo — 2024-09-11T22:57:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meet-up-europe.29249/post-339496>

> [eve001 said:](/goto/post?id=339481)
>
> Hi Pablo,
> That would be great. Are you Spanish?
> Do you happen to know of a good map to check for nnEMF antenas for Spain?
> I am searching for one easy to navigate for EHS. No success yet.
> Wanna check locations at Canary Islands and the Iberic Peninsula.

I do not have a map for antennas. I think it is much more effective to just not go to a very high population area (not big cities) and then testing anyways. Low pop generally only will provide low RF/MW but you still should really test for dirty electricity, electric and magnetic fields IMO (and testing also anyways RF/MW even if you go to a low pop place, since sometimes antennas can be hidden).

Yes I am Spanish, I live in this 250k city at the 43 latitude: [https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oviedo](https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oviedo)

I guess where I live is far from optimal in lower light months (november-february), so I would not recommend for living year around. I live here especially because of job, but I try to do very well other things, like CT, maximizing time outside, etc. Also that´s why I´m planning to do some winter vacations South and in the mid - long term moving South. But if anyone wants come visit and talk here I´m super happy.

Depends on your situation, but in the Mediterranean part of Spain the weather is much better, more Sun, etc. Places like Malaga, Alicante, Valencia, etc. I think are better than mine. [@metabolicdaria](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/29467/) lives there so she may be able to tell you better. Even though they have more Sun, they have the sea, etc, those places are not super low lat, so maybe Canaries could be even a better idea. I think it depends a lot on your n=1. Also how much money you have is quite important. I have not been to the Canaries yet but I heard acommodation is getting quite expensive.

# Thread: El Salvador Water - Reverse Osmosis or Other Options?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-water-reverse-osmosis-or-other-options.29250/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### thejessallen — 2024-08-03T02:21:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-water-reverse-osmosis-or-other-options.29250/post-338014>

Hola amigos who live in El Salvador. How do you get decent water here? Can I buy reverse osmosis water somewhere? (I'm in La Libertad)

Does anyone sell countertop machines?

Would the under the sink filtration system actually improve the water quality from the sink?

Thanks in advance for your time and insights

### Laudy Cincotta — 2024-08-03T05:45:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-water-reverse-osmosis-or-other-options.29250/post-338021>

Hola Jess!

Water is difficult, i still dont have a solution i am totally happy with yet.

I would prefer to test my tap water to know exactly what is in it but it is very expensive to do so.

RO is the best you will find and can have an undersink unit installed but you will need to get a remineraliser or use sea salt to add back minerals.

You can see the units on display at Vidri and likely get advice there.

Haven't looked for a bench top here and don't imagine they have them, but I did bring an Acqua Tru with me from the US which is a bench top RO unit which became cumbersome and we couldnt keep up with filling the small tank so its not being used any more.

Currently using Perlitas in big bottles or Evian when i can find it at Pricesmart.

Good luck.

### thejessallen — 2024-08-03T16:09:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-water-reverse-osmosis-or-other-options.29250/post-338036>

> [Laudy Cincotta said:](/goto/post?id=338021)
>
> Hola Jess!
>
> Water is difficult, i still dont have a solution i am totally happy with yet.
>
> I would prefer to test my tap water to know exactly what is in it but it is very expensive to do so.
>
> RO is the best you will find and can have an undersink unit installed but you will need to get a remineraliser or use sea salt to add back minerals.
>
> You can see the units on display at Vidri and likely get advice there.
>
> Haven't looked for a bench top here and don't imagine they have them, but I did bring an Acqua Tru with me from the US which is a bench top RO unit which became cumbersome and we couldnt keep up with filling the small tank so its not being used any more.
>
> Currently using Perlitas in big bottles or Evian when i can find it at Pricesmart.
>

thanks for this insight Laudy! curious why you have chosen not to do an under sink RO unit?

### rtd — 2024-08-03T17:12:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-water-reverse-osmosis-or-other-options.29250/post-338039>

> [Laudy Cincotta said:](/goto/post?id=338021)
>
> Haven't looked for a bench top here and don't imagine they have them, but I did bring an Acqua Tru with me from the US which is a bench top RO unit which became cumbersome and we couldnt keep up with filling the small tank so its not being used any more.

Agree. A counter top AquaTru is great for low volume, yet requires many filters and power. I do not like the handle either; it should be one solid brass handle IMO because hands have germs, and all the little cut out slots are pockets I find are not easy to clean. I can smell it after a few uses.

Berkey gravity filters have many different sized filtration systems. I own a Berkey system for emergency situations. A multi filter system and buying bottled water on sale maybe a good approach. I buy 5 gallon Poland Spring where I live.

Are there any local springs to fill up? I have done that, it can be a pain over time. I have a lot of respect for the saying, "Carry your own water".

### Sirena — 2024-08-03T17:55:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-water-reverse-osmosis-or-other-options.29250/post-338042>

Hi there! I have an "Leogant" activated carbon filter here in Germany which makes structured water. It's portable and easy to install on any tap/sink. Or is this not enough as a filter?

### thejessallen — 2024-08-13T15:59:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-water-reverse-osmosis-or-other-options.29250/post-338370>

I’ve not seen glass bottles although I’ve only been here about a month now.

Portable enough to bring new one from Germany ? Although with water quality here unsure if it would be enough. I am also figuring these things out.

### Laudy Cincotta — 2024-08-16T21:17:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-water-reverse-osmosis-or-other-options.29250/post-338513>

> [thejessallen said:](/goto/post?id=338036)
>
> thanks for this insight Laudy! curious why you have chosen not to do an under sink RO unit?

Hi Jess,

If you are living here now you will understand how difficult things are to organise, it just hasnt been top of my list right now.

I don't have city water, the water here comes from an underground aquifer that i would like tested, mainly for levels of heavy metals, pesticides and agricultural chemicals, it will all of course need to be filtered out but in time.

Using Perlitas and having a variety of minerals we take as well as coconut water so going with that until i have the headspace to do a deep dive into it all.

Hope you manage to find a solution that works for you.

### DLO — 2024-08-19T17:40:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-water-reverse-osmosis-or-other-options.29250/post-338634>

My concern is that they dont use copper piping it's all plastic including most of the above ground storage tanks.

### Mat.I — 2024-09-10T05:05:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-water-reverse-osmosis-or-other-options.29250/post-339440>

> [DLO said:](/goto/post?id=338634)
>
> My concern is that they dont use copper piping it's all plastic including most of the above ground storage tanks.

This could also be problematic for leaching especially if exposed to temps outside the normal rated temp.. I'm wary of crosslinked polyethylene (PEX) pipes that are widely being used. Best choice would be copper with compression (non-solder) fittings.

# Thread: Glioblastoma diagnosis for my brother seeking help and hope
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glioblastoma-diagnosis-for-my-brother-seeking-help-and-hope.29254/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Punzilla — 2024-08-04T15:23:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glioblastoma-diagnosis-for-my-brother-seeking-help-and-hope.29254/post-338063>

Hi all
My brother has recently had a glioma tumour surgically removed from his brain. The procedure seemed to have gone well but the professor who performed the surgery has warned us that there is no cure and it doesn't seem to be curable or treatable with a keto diet as per the recommendation of Dr Thomas Seyfried.
He has started radio therapy and chemo last week and I don't want to accept the defeat to cancer without a fight.

A friend of ours has been assisting my brother and has been recommending him to get daily light exposure, he has given him a pair of amber blue blocking glasses and my brother is in the process of ordering the red coloured ones. He's also organised supplements and a diet for my brother to follow low in meat to assist with the cancer fight.

What would be the ideal way to keep my brother alive?

He has always been fit and healthy I'm not sure whether the nature of his shift work has gotten in the way and caused this cancer.

Any beginners advice of where to start. The stakes are quite high so want to explore as many options as I can.

Thank you.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-08-04T15:44:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glioblastoma-diagnosis-for-my-brother-seeking-help-and-hope.29254/post-338065>

I would avoid radio and chemo if at all possible

Maximize sunlight exposure (especially light from dawn to ~9AM)

The shift work has definitely gotten in the way. If your brother values his life, he will drop that permanently.

### John Warner — 2024-08-04T22:02:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glioblastoma-diagnosis-for-my-brother-seeking-help-and-hope.29254/post-338072>

> [Punzilla said:](/goto/post?id=338063)
>
> Hi all
> My brother has recently had a glioma tumour surgically removed from his brain. The procedure seemed to have gone well but the professor who performed the surgery has warned us that there is no cure and it doesn't seem to be curable or treatable with a keto diet as per the recommendation of Dr Thomas Seyfried.
> He has started radio therapy and chemo last week and I don't want to accept the defeat to cancer without a fight.
>
> A friend of ours has been assisting my brother and has been recommending him to get daily light exposure, he has given him a pair of amber blue blocking glasses and my brother is in the process of ordering the red coloured ones. He's also organised supplements and a diet for my brother to follow low in meat to assist with the cancer fight.
>
> What would be the ideal way to keep my brother alive?
>
> He has always been fit and healthy I'm not sure whether the nature of his shift work has gotten in the way and caused this cancer.
>
> Any beginners advice of where to start. The stakes are quite high so want to explore as many options as I can.
>
> Thank you.

### John Warner — 2024-08-04T22:03:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glioblastoma-diagnosis-for-my-brother-seeking-help-and-hope.29254/post-338073>

> [Joseph LeBlanc said:](/goto/post?id=338065)
>
> I would avoid radio and chemo if at all possible
>
> Maximize sunlight exposure (especially light from dawn to ~9AM)
>
> The shift work has definitely gotten in the way. If your brother values his life, he will drop that permanently.

Go to the ask Jack page, or email them and set up a consult. Probably a consult would be in order.

### Mat.I — 2024-08-06T03:28:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glioblastoma-diagnosis-for-my-brother-seeking-help-and-hope.29254/post-338109>

[@John Warner](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/25255/) - Check out Gabor Somlyai (PhD) book -

### Deuterium Depletion: A New Way in Curing Cancer and Preserving Health​

Also, consult with Jack.
All the best.
Mat

### Mat.I — 2024-08-06T06:59:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glioblastoma-diagnosis-for-my-brother-seeking-help-and-hope.29254/post-338112>

Also, you could check out Dr Zsofia Clemens - who deals with GBM's quite a lot through nutritional interventions. Note she uses a very strict Paleolithic Ketogenic Diet (much higher fat than your traditional keto). Not sure of Jack's opinion on her work. She operates out of paleomedicina in Hungary.

[https://www.researchgate.net/publication/345606592\_4-Year\_Long\_Progression-Free\_and\_Symptom-Free\_Survival\_of\_a\_Patient\_with\_Recurrent\_Glioblastoma\_Multiforme\_A\_Case\_Report\_of\_the\_Paleolithic\_Ketogenic\_Diet\_PKD\_Used\_as\_a\_Stand-Alone\_Treatment\_After\_Fai](https://www.researchgate.net/publication/345606592_4-Year_Long_Progression-Free_and_Symptom-Free_Survival_of_a_Patient_with_Recurrent_Glioblastoma_Multiforme_A_Case_Report_of_the_Paleolithic_Ketogenic_Diet_PKD_Used_as_a_Stand-Alone_Treatment_After_Fai)

### [Treatment of high-grade brain tumor using the paleolithic ketogenic diet (PKD): Three cases](https://www.paleomedicina.com/en/glioblastoma_paleolithic_ketogenic_diet)

Authors: Zsófia Clemens, Andrea Dabóczi, Mária Schimmer, Péter Barsi, Csaba Tóth DOI 10.28973/lindeberg.pm/po1Conference: The Staffan Lindeberg Memorial Conference, Lisbon 2017 ABSTRACT Introduction:

www.paleomedicina.com

**Obviously, she is missing the light story, however, do your research and see what works. Just getting some options out here for you to look at.**

Also, I have come across some information in the past that I assume Dr Seyfried would also be across that relates to Glutamine blocking with 6-Diazo-5-oxo-L-norleucine (DON) is a glutamine antagonist. Again not sure on Jack's view on this and I haven't looked at it in great detail either, however somewhere to also look further. I assume it would be experimental "research" only however, if deemed appropriate you could get your hands on it (obviously under the guidance of someone who knows what they are doing).

There are also quite a few studies on specific IR wavelengths that have been shown to help.

I can't recall which podcast, however, Jack talks about GBM's in one of them.

As time is very sensitive here - best to consult with [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) as soon as you can.

### Mat.I — 2024-08-06T06:59:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glioblastoma-diagnosis-for-my-brother-seeking-help-and-hope.29254/post-338113>

> Also, you could check out Dr Zsofia Clemens - who deals with GBM's quite a lot through nutritional interventions. Note she uses a very strict Paleolithic Ketogenic Diet (much higher fat than your traditional keto). Not sure of Jack's opinion on her work. She operates out of paleomedicina in Hungary.
>
>
> [https://www.researchgate.net/publication/345606592\_4-Year\_Long\_Progression-Free\_and\_Symptom-Free\_Survival\_of\_a\_Patient\_with\_Recurrent\_Glioblastoma\_Multiforme\_A\_Case\_Report\_of\_the\_Paleolithic\_Ketogenic\_Diet\_PKD\_Used\_as\_a\_Stand-Alone\_Treatment\_After\_Fai](https://www.researchgate.net/publication/345606592_4-Year_Long_Progression-Free_and_Symptom-Free_Survival_of_a_Patient_with_Recurrent_Glioblastoma_Multiforme_A_Case_Report_of_the_Paleolithic_Ketogenic_Diet_PKD_Used_as_a_Stand-Alone_Treatment_After_Fai)
>
>
>
> ### [Treatment of high-grade brain tumor using the paleolithic ketogenic diet (PKD): Three cases](https://www.paleomedicina.com/en/glioblastoma_paleolithic_ketogenic_diet)
>
> Authors: Zsófia Clemens, Andrea Dabóczi, Mária Schimmer, Péter Barsi, Csaba Tóth DOI 10.28973/lindeberg.pm/po1Conference: The Staffan Lindeberg Memorial Conference, Lisbon 2017 ABSTRACT Introduction:
>
> www.paleomedicina.com
>
>
> **Obviously, she is missing the light story, however, do your research and see what works. Just getting some options out here for you to look at.**
>
> Also, I have come across some information in the past that I assume Dr Seyfried would also be across that relates to Glutamine blocking with 6-Diazo-5-oxo-L-norleucine (DON) is a glutamine antagonist. Again not sure on Jack's view on this and I haven't looked at it in great detail either, however somewhere to also look further. I assume it would be experimental "research" only however, if deemed appropriate you could get your hands on it (obviously under the guidance of someone who knows what they are doing).
>
> There are also quite a few studies on specific IR wavelengths that have been shown to help.
>
> I can't recall which podcast, however, Jack talks about GBM's in one of them.
>
> As time is very sensitive here - best to consult with [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) as soon as you can.

I just found this article re: DON - [https://www.bc.edu/bc-web/bcnews/science-tech-and-health/biology-and-genetics/targeting-cancer.html](https://www.bc.edu/bc-web/bcnews/science-tech-and-health/biology-and-genetics/targeting-cancer.html)
