### Punzilla — 2024-08-12T13:56:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glioblastoma-diagnosis-for-my-brother-seeking-help-and-hope.29254/post-338301>

Thanks everyone. I'll need to show these messages to my sister-in-law and discuss. There's quite a lot of information out there. We're heading into the warmer months in Melbourne and my brother is spending a lot of time outdoors. I'm not fully immersed in dr Kruses work at the moment but after watching a recent interview of Dr Gabrielle Lyon and Dr Alexis Cowan I've downloaded the circadian app and am trying in the meantime to get my own circadian cycles in order too. I've previously downloaded the d-minder app on the mobile. I know in relation to getting vitamin d I'd virtually need to expose 90 percent of my skin by the solar noon to get any decent level of vitamin d amount.

I'm the meantime he is trying a (mostly) vegetarian diet whereby the first 2 meals can be cooked and the final meal of the night is raw so the body doesn't need to spend time digesting and can focus on its repair. (He is allowed to eat fish 3 days a week).

He is doing radio and chemo I think Dr Zsofia Clemens recommends not doing that which I get anxious with as it's the standard of care and I don't feel qualified to recommend against it. I'm hoping whatever else we can incorporate can include a mixture of western and natural medicine.

Thank you all for your replies appreciate it.

### Mat.I — 2024-08-12T14:18:06+0300

Mat.

### jonjacobsen — 2025-01-27T04:02:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glioblastoma-diagnosis-for-my-brother-seeking-help-and-hope.29254/post-345810>

My 10 year old daughter has a glial tumor that causes seizures, currently taking oxcarbazepine. The tumor won't be surgically removed as it's scattered throughout her right temporal lobe. She's a typical kid and loves the sweets, though my wife and I are pretty good about eating healthy she still gets things from school etc. The Cleveland doctors recommend this wait and see approach. Wish your brother the best - kick the cancer - good luck! Thanks to all on the thread for posting, I'll be checking them out.

# Thread: Pre-bipolar, chromosome quadruplication and light
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-bipolar-chromosome-quadruplication-and-light.29255/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Emmanuela — 2024-08-04T15:38:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-bipolar-chromosome-quadruplication-and-light.29255/post-338064>

Ok so I am using a friend’s situation to practice my understanding of the posts and webinars i have absorbed so far.

A good friend of mine and her youngest son both have quadruplication on chromosome 8p22.
She has some physical signs of the chromosome abnormality but no mental ones.
He went from being a relatively normal and high energy child until schools reopened during covid.
His mom homeschooled almost fully outdoors instead of doing the virtual learning.
But when he returned, the schools here had teachers in suits, masks, visors, the whole 9 yard and the kid snapped.
He has been recently diagnosed as pre-bipolar.
When unmedicated, he has “random” boughts of aggressive behavior to the point of being dangerous, pulling a knife on his dad, and the like, then 5 minutes later he is sweet like it never happened.
He cannot sense the need to pee or poo until last minute.
He is much calmer and less likely to have an episode when camping or outdoors in general. His urgency to use the washroom even diminishes when camping.
He struggles with math.
The mother is working on switching light, hardwiring the house, etc.

My thoughts:
How does the eye clock go awry to that extent “all of a sudden”?

From reading the Quantum Engineering 47 and listening to the june 13 webinar, my understanding is that he probably needs his mom to be very anal about the light environment, sunrise, and high dha seafood.
I was thinking the bathroom urgency has to do with the vagus nerve connection once i read the vagus nerve mito hacking blogs.

Probably the leptin reset would help the boy too although he isn’t chubby by any means, my understanding is his circadian rhythm is way off.

I am still trying to piece together the chromosome quadruplication to understand what that really means for him.

Also I keep thinking there had to be a physical trigger other than how the return to school would have affected him emotionally.

Ill go back to digging and post my continued thoughts.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-07T15:08:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-bipolar-chromosome-quadruplication-and-light.29255/post-338145>

The take home lesson is in this blog: The periodicity of our clocks determines the shape of our lives. Time sculpts us by using light and dark. What happens in your colony of mitochondria every AM writes a story in the arteries of your flesh. [https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-8-109636921](https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-8-109636921)

### Emmanuela — 2024-08-07T16:59:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-bipolar-chromosome-quadruplication-and-light.29255/post-338154>

Wait, this makes sense. So the mother’s mother has severe depression and was abused by her bipolar husband. And i was just reading a couple papers on the connection between nitric oxide signaling problems and violent behavior in children.

Then, i am reading this: [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC7764698/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC7764698/)

And it says the SLC7A2 is located on chromosome 8p22, that’s his and his mother’s quadruplications.
The paper mentions the role of cat2 in l arginine supply for NO production.
Could the quadruplication be reducing the NO activity in general?

This means he is extremely, extremely sensitive to anything that will further reduce nitric oxide.
Maybe the question shouldn’t be why did he start exhibiting the behavior all of a sudden but rather, the constant time outside delayed the onset of the behavior?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-moon-has-a-lesson-about-alan.29260/post-338093>

CAN THE MOON MAKE YOU CRAZY?

Before the invention of artificial light at night, also called ALAN, you had to wait for a full moon to stay up late and do bad things.
In this study we see how sleep patterns are effected by the lunar cycle. The word lunatic comes from luna = moon.

[https://www.biorxiv.org/content/10.1101/2020.06.01.128728v1](https://www.biorxiv.org/content/10.1101/2020.06.01.128728v1)

### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-05T16:47:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-moon-has-a-lesson-about-alan.29260/post-338094>

studies of artificial light at night (ALAN), we find each person has a different threshold for amount of brightness/lux they can tolerate at night. This is what they also find when studying moon/lunar cycle. Some people handled it much worse than others. A long time ago...They called them LUNATICS. Or werewolves.... The problem is that today we have infinitely more lunatics because you don’t have to wait for a full moon, you can simulate a 50-100x more powerful super moon every night with high powered LED tech screens & ALAN.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-05T17:01:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-moon-has-a-lesson-about-alan.29260/post-338096>

Wisdom from @DrJackKruse from CPC #4

\- this is hard to 'super simplify'- Genes encode proteins- most of our DNA does not code for proteins- it is NOT 'junk' as many smart people once proposed- epigenetics is the connection between our environment (at all scales) and how/which genes and not-junk areas are transcribed into proteins and give us ability to 'try out' new configurations that can help us survive/adapt to environmental changes.
As is clear from Jack's work and speaking- the common pathway between our numerous environmental sensors (skin, eyes, chromophores, taste buds, light sensors, temp sensors, diet etc) -- are our mitochondria - which convert those signals down into their quantum state of electrons and protons -- and are the primary driver of epigenetic adaptation/response to the environment.
to get from 'get morning sun, live circadian and cold adapted' to the next step in understanding Jack's connection between quantum physics and biology/biochemistry -- you have to spend the time to digest and understand the above.
He makes multiple evolutionary biology cases for this even at this early stage in his blogs. (which is remarkable since I am >90 in and and still very 'early' in the process).
"Because the K-T event used a sped up epigenetic signaling program to allow eutherian mammals to sense changes in their environment quickly and adapt, modern humans have the most adaptable and fastest ability to change epigenetic program on this planet today. We use junk DNA to first change DNA expression and with sustained pressure selected for the genomic change and expanded it in survivors. This is a great modern example of how a sped up epigenetics works in us. We need to think about the good and bad it can do. It is definitely a double edged sword for modern humans."
[](https://x.com/eric_novack/article/1820454876612370694/media/1820454556171706368)
[https://jackkruse.com/cpc4-evolutionary-friend-or-foe/](https://jackkruse.com/cpc4-evolutionary-friend-or-foe/)

### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-31T16:44:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-moon-has-a-lesson-about-alan.29260/post-339105>

All planets with life will eventually be found to have massive ambipolar or polarization fields associated with them. Why? It tells you the planet is loaded with electrons in the ionosphere that surrounds the rock below. This global electric platform is the key to understanding why all electric currents precede basic chemistry and biochemistry and why LIGHT > FOOD at all levels of understanding. The food guru narratives continue to implode as new data comes in from space. This ambipolar electric field on Earth is generated exclusively by the outward pressure of ionospheric electrons. Most of those electrons are coming from oxygen radicals.

The ambipolar field of Earth controls the structure of the polar ionosphere, where aurora manifest, boosting the scale height by 271% based on new data generated by NASA's suborbital Endurance rocket. NASA has inferred that this increases the supply of cold O+ ions to the magnetosphere by more than 3,800%! This is a big deal because it gives us a clue why this blog is deadly acccurate in its science. This now fully explains why other mechanisms such as wave–particle interactions can heat and further accelerate them to escape velocity. The electrostatic field of Earth is strong enough by itself to drive the polar wind by itself and is probably the origin of the cold H+ ion population that dominates much of the magnetosphere that I talked about in the Kruse for Dummies lecture on H+ and D. \- this is hard to 'super simplify'- Genes encode proteins- most of our DNA does not code for proteins- it is NOT 'junk' as many smart people once proposed- epigenetics is the connection between our environment (at all scales) and how/which genes and not-junk areas are transcribed into proteins and give us ability to 'try out' new configurations that can help us survive/adapt to environmental changes.
As is clear from Jack's work and speaking- the common pathway between our numerous environmental sensors (skin, eyes, chromophores, taste buds, light sensors, temp sensors, diet etc) -- are our mitochondria - which convert those signals down into their quantum state of electrons and protons -- and are the primary driver of epigenetic adaptation/response to the environment.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-05T17:10:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/changes-in-light-predict-epigenetic-programming.29261/post-338098>

Stars cannot shine without environmental darkness.

Light has no relative power without understanding darkness.

This is why you must embrace the suck the life.
Life has a tension between comfort and discomfort. Discomfort affords us the perspective to ask better questions about our current assumptions. In my opinion, discomfort emerges from our minds when our choices are suppressing our true identity.
A great way to poison a human is to remove any hardship or struggle from his life. One of the most essential things for any leader is never letting anyone else define who you are and what you choose. Being unconventional does not mean the idea of a mito-hack cannot work. Unconventional means the prescription has not made it into conventional textbooks. This is what quantum biology is and the recent melanopsin data show us; you better pay deep attention to Black Swan mitochondriacs as your environment is made unfavorable by the trends of modern human networking.
Darwin gave us the false meme that life is about the survival of the fittest and that competition is critical to success. It is not. Why do I say this? It is about the survival of the wisest. Unless you know the epigenetic code, which is our software program that runs the genes, which are the hardware in cells, the gene of DNA has no fundamental meaning. That has made Darwin and Watson Crick's perspective meaningless for us and for medicine. This paradigm of "genomic belief" has been utterly toxic for success with illness.

Letting go of toxic environments you put yourself in can be one of the hardest things to do in life, but it is one of the most wise things one can do to change their life. Life is much less a competitive struggle for survival than a triumph of cooperation and creativity. Build your career and network around this concept and you'll win. The most important thing in understanding life's cipher is hearing what isn't being said aloud. It is analogous to what we've found in genetics. Ignoring the genes and paying attention to the junk is where the rainbow really lies. Nature never gives up her secrets easily. We must learn to ignore the noise genes create in professional science and focus on the junk DNA.

The same is true with talk. Words we use are not as critical in human communication as we believe. Just as it is the silent part of the genome that runs our DNA, in language, it is the things not said in words, but present between words that are critical. They hold the key to successful understanding in life. The art of reading between the lines is the quest of the wise mitochondriac. Always pay attention to the pauses, the nonverbal chatter, and the movement that occurs between words to understand what is really going on.

Learning is like the fuel that moves the electrons of your body toward a destination of success. Learning requires an ear that listens and an intellect that is aware and has the patience to endure mistakes. Errors are the growing pains of wisdom. The invariable mark of wisdom in you is when you see the possible in the improbable and the miraculous in the common. Wisdom is not built by time and does not comes not from age, but from education, learning, and thinking.

The irony of modern centralized science is this: Physicists/astronomers believe that if they want to understand the beginning and end of the universe they should aim understand the life cycle of the stars to unlock the mystery of the evolution of the universe. They believe if they can work out how stars change and evolve over time they believe they could begin to understand how the universes changes over time. The irony? Stars emit light and it is the light that changes the universe. None of the scientist in biology have ever thought once about how light causes the evolution of cells in living beings. Amazing contraction to me. The lesson to be learned and tattoo'd into your brain?

Stars cannot shine without environmental darkness.

Light has no relative power without understanding darkness.

If you get it wrong,

### Jack Kruse — 2026-04-24T17:02:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/changes-in-light-predict-epigenetic-programming.29261/post-371963>

The study I'm referring to and pictured below, recently published in **[npj Aging](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41514-025-00244-x)** (2025) by researchers at **Emory University** and **Baylor College of Medicine**, found that psilocin (the metabolite of psilocybin) increased the replicative lifespan of human skin and lung cells by up to **57%**. In aged mice, monthly doses increased the 10-month survival rate from **50% to 80%**, while visibly reducing signs of aging like fur whitening. I'm not into mice studies but this needs to be studied in humans.

While the researchers focused on **SIRT1 expression**, telomere preservation, and reduced oxidative stress, my framework suggests a deeper, subatomic mechanism involving **deuterium depletion** and **melanin biology**.

**Psilocybin as a "Deuterium Filter"**
In my framework, psilocybin is a "power up" because it may solve the **Kinetic Isotope Effect (KIE)** problem at the mitochondrial level:

**Mitochondrial Efficiency:** The study noted that psilocybin improves mitochondrial respiratory capacity and ATP production efficiency. In my view, this is because psilocybin-treated cells are better at delivering **deuterium-depleted water** to the ATPase pumps, preventing the "mechanical friction" caused by heavy hydrogen.

**Melanin's Role in Mushrooms:** Many mushrooms use melanin to capture ionizing radiation and convert it into metabolic energy. If psilocybin synthesis is linked to these melanized pathways, the molecule itself might be "pre-depleted" of deuterium, acting as a high-quality **methyl and acetyl donor** that doesn't "stall" enzymes like CYP2C19.

**Solar/Light Mimicry:** Interestingly, the research shows that psilocybin induces gene expression changes in the visual cortex that **closely mirror light-induced changes**, even in total darkness. This suggests psilocybin might act as a "chemical surrogate" for the solar frequencies needed to maintain the **proton tunneling** efficiency of the NF-κB and SIRT1 switches.

**Why Longevity Researchers Missed This**
Mainstream geneticists attribute the 50% lifespan extension to "gene regulation" (SIRT1, p16, p21). However, if the **Inner Mitochondrial Membrane (IMM)** charge is restored by psilocybin’s impact on De-fragging **structured water**, the "genetic" changes are merely the downstream result of a restored **40-million-volt mitochondrial battery**.

Researchers at Emory University and Baylor College of Medicine have discovered that psilocin, the active metabolite of psilocybin, can dramatically delay cellular senescence, the process where cells stop dividing and begin to age. In preclinical trials, human skin cells treated with the compound lived 51% longer, while fetal lung cells saw a staggering 57% increase in lifespan compared to untreated samples. These findings suggest that the impact of psychedelics may extend far beyond the brain, potentially influencing the fundamental biological pathways that govern how our cells resist stress and decay. This sounds like a deuterium story I talked about in the Kruse for Dummies lecture.

**A logical step for future study:** If psilocybin’s benefit is truly about deuterium depletion, we should see a **blue shift in UPEs** (increased coherence) and an increase in the **dielectric constant** of the cellular water following treatment, essentially reversing the "biological brownout."

**CITES**
1\. [https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12322706/](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12322706/)
2\. [https://blogs.bcm.edu/2025/07/22/from-the-labs-can-psychedelic-mushrooms-slow-down-aging/](https://blogs.bcm.edu/2025/07/22/from-the-labs-can-psychedelic-mushrooms-slow-down-aging/)
3\. [https://www.mdpi.com/2077-0383/13/6/1727](https://www.mdpi.com/2077-0383/13/6/1727)
4\. [https://www.sciencealert.com/psilocybin-extends-life-of-human-cells-by-50-in-wild-new-study](https://www.sciencealert.com/psilocybin-extends-life-of-human-cells-by-50-in-wild-new-study)

# Thread: Does anyone know much about the MPO cardiovascular marker
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-know-much-about-the-mpo-cardiovascular-marker.29263/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Daniela Lawler — 2024-08-06T17:40:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-know-much-about-the-mpo-cardiovascular-marker.29263/post-338118>

My hubby tested very high and when I looked into it discovered it is an enzyme released by the white blood cells in response to inflammation and damage to the arteries. does anyone know how much research they is behind this marker and have any info I can take away?

### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-07T15:06:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-know-much-about-the-mpo-cardiovascular-marker.29263/post-338143>

Myeloperoxidase (MPO) is an enzyme stored in azurophilic granules of polymorphonuclear neutrophils and macrophages and released into extracellular fluid in the setting of inflammatory process and is associated with increased ROS/RNS creation due to altered mitochondrial metabolism and altered biophoton release. This is due to environmental changes that are not light/dark controlled.

The excess release of endogenous light is not creating enough UV light endogenously and this is not causing translation of POMC to create alpha beta or gamma MSH = less melanin inside and melanin inside deals with excess ROS/RNS production. As a result of this cascade, myeloperoxidase rises and it has been correlated with CVD disease because excess MPO has been linked as marker of plaque instability in PAD disease and coronary heart disease.

The cascade has many other parts associated with it discussed on the forum. See here ----> [https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decentralized-research.29101/page-2](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decentralized-research.29101/page-2)

### Daniela Lawler — 2024-08-07T17:10:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-know-much-about-the-mpo-cardiovascular-marker.29263/post-338155>

thank you jack I will take a read

> Myeloperoxidase (MPO) is an enzyme stored in azurophilic granules of polymorphonuclear neutrophils and macrophages and released into extracellular fluid in the setting of inflammatory process and is associated with increased ROS/RNS creation due to altered mitochondrial metabolism and altered biophoton release. This is due to environmental changes that are not light/dark controlled.
>

As a result of this cascade, myeloperoxidase rises and it has been correlated with CVD disease because excess MPO has been linked as marker of plaque instability in PAD disease and coronary heart disease.
>

So this is a nnEMF/5G story/ALAN story.... ? I did some digging into MPO a few months ago and never found anything that made this much sense! Dimming the light created inside has consequences

My husband has an amazing vitamin D level, he was at 82 in march so even before the summer solstice. He has just purchased the yellow and red lenses for indoors just this week, so let's see if this was the change he needed. He is addicted to devices - laptop, phone and he is a musician (DJ) so over the years has had a disrupted circadian biology. # Thread: Where to start with fatigue?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-start-with-fatigue.29267/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### adam915 — 2024-08-07T15:52:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-start-with-fatigue.29267/post-338151>

I am a complete beginner here. I have been binging anything on YouTube with Jack's name in the title lately. Looking for some specific advice on what to do to combat feeling tired all the time. I am a 38 year old male, no previous health issues before taking the covid jab. I went through a couple years of issues ranging from severe chest pain to neuropathy to anxiety. I powered through everything but am currently taking an SSRI for the anxiety as the panic attacks can be debilitating. I get a full 8 hours of sleep, and sometimes more. I do take a supplement with anti inflammatory agents like bromelain and nattokinase which is advertised to help with issues aggravated by spike protein. I try to eat clean within reason. I just feel wrecked all day every day. Could take a 2 hour nap every day if I was afforded the luxury. Any help on where to start?

### Thijs Oosting — 2024-08-07T16:20:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-start-with-fatigue.29267/post-338152>

could have to do with adrenal fatigue perhaps. Can you handle coffee? Do you have reactive hypoglycemia? Do you sometimes feel lightheaded when standing up? Do you have brain fog? Do you feel you need to eat something every 2-3 hours? You can look at Brain Gut 16: Adrenal Fatigue

Unfortunately fatigue can be due to many reasons so I can also be something else than Adrenal fatigue. I am myself am dealing with chronic fatigue for 8 years. Can't work or exercise etc.

### adam915 — 2024-08-07T16:29:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-start-with-fatigue.29267/post-338153>

I don't drink coffee, but can handle caffeine just fine. No lightheadedness while standing. Yes to the brain fog. Sometimes can't come up with the correct word I'm looking for in conversation. I eat three meals a day and rarely anything in between. I do annual bloodwork and as of June everything was in the target range. Blood sugar is toward the higher end of the scale.

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2024-08-07T18:19:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-start-with-fatigue.29267/post-338159>

Which SSRI? Prozac has fluoride in it as does citalopram. How much sunshine do you get ?

### 5G Canary — 2024-08-07T19:59:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-start-with-fatigue.29267/post-338160>

Your environment is stealing your energy... if you want it back you’ll need to change it. Redox before detox!! Disconnect and reconnect with Mother Nature...

I concur with 5G Canary above. I was the same state you describe 5 years ago, after 24 years of shift work the last 6 on graveyard, too much stress, a toxic relationship I refused to see for what it was, depression, etc. Today I am in a much better spot but still not optimal. What I changed was spending regular time in nature, getting a lot more sun, blocking blue light at night and during the day at work in front of screens, changing my diet to epi-paleo, getting regular cold exposure and red light panels. I've easily spent a couple thousand hours listening to Dr. Kruse podcasts, his private member videos, reading his blogs, and in general challenging a lot of beliefs I used to have in those 5 years.

### Rusty — 2024-08-07T22:20:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-find-what-cell-towers-you-have-near-by.29268/post-338170>

Cell tower mapping websites for India

### [Cellular Tower and Signal Map](https://www.cellmapper.net/map)

CellMapper is a crowd-sourced cellular tower and coverage mapping service.

 www.cellmapper.net

### [Home Page | Tarang Sanchar](https://tarangsanchar.gov.in/)

 tarangsanchar.gov.in

### [3G / 4G / 5G coverage in India - nPerf.com](https://www.nperf.com/en/map/IN/-/-/signal)

Compare the network coverage of mobile operators and check their performance at home !

 www.nperf.com

(i use this one)

### [India's leading provider of Prepaid & Postpaid Mobile Services in India - Airtel.in](https://www.airtel.in/opennetwork/)

Airtel is India's leading provider of prepaid & postpaid mobile, Airtel payments bank, broadband, 4G & DTH services in India. Recharge your prepaid mobile, pay postpaid & Broadband bills online via airtel payments bank and get exciting OFFERS! Know more about India's first open network.

 www.airtel.in

# Thread: Hello Everyone
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-everyone.29271/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### alleerielle — 2024-08-08T04:35:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-everyone.29271/post-338178>

I am so glad to be here. I have been reading as much as I can and listening to every Jack Kruse podcast I can find. There's so much to learn but it is so exciting and interesting. It definitely feels necessary.

In 2011, I was told I most likely have a rare blood disorder, Essential Thrombocythemia, which is a high platelet count. I never got it confirmed with a bone marrow biopsy because I didn't see the point of going through that hell for something that has no cure. I had an IV iron transfusion 2017 to see if that would get my platelet count down, however, it did not. Fast forward to today and my recent blood test showed all my blood counts are high, especially red blood cells. So I'm thinking I may have Polycythemia Vera instead. I am currently trying to find a hematologist that specializes in Myeloproliferative Neoplasms to find out.
[https://www.lls.org/myeloproliferative-neoplasms](https://www.lls.org/myeloproliferative-neoplasms)
Either way, I am committing myself to getting as healthy as I possibly can. I get morning sun everyday and get out in the afternoon to raise my Vitamin D levels. I'm also eating more seafood, which is just sushi right now. I know I need to try to expand my menu in that area. I wear my blue blocking glasses at night and am working on getting prescription ones for working on the computer. I just started drinking 125ppm deuterium water to see if that helps lower my blood counts. I just ordered Gabor Somalyai's book "Deuterium Depletion: A New Way in Curing Cancer and Preserving Health" which comes tomorrow. I have always hated sunscreen and sunglasses so my instincts were on point there. I have been a lifetime night owl and always wondered why I felt so awake at night. Now I know it was because I wasn't watching the sun rise. I feel silly that I never knew it was that simple and I am grateful that I know now. I still feel the urge to stay up, however, a lifetime of a lack of sleep and too much blue light has most definitely caught up to me. Melatonin is a priority now. Looking forward to connecting with like minded people here and of course I would love to hear any wisdom about my condition that anyone may offer. Thank you!


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: PolyPhenols etc

### Cameron — 2024-08-08T15:16:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/polyphenols-etc.29272/post-338182>

Can someone explain how polyphenols and other secondary compounds (metabolites) in food help our mitochondria. I would like to mentally tie the ingestion of foods rich in these compounds with the Kruse insights regarding ATP production, circadian health etc. I assume that lowering inflammation, of course, improves mitochondrial efficiency but is there more subtle signaling and other effects from eating real, local healthy foods when it comes to these secondary compounds? I am fascinated by the work of Steven Van Vliet and Fred Provenza and just trying to tie it together. [https://stephanvanvliet.com/](https://stephanvanvliet.com/). He is going way beyond the regular studies and tying in soil health and environment to the food quality in objective ways. For example, regenerative raised Bison versus feed lot finished bison (yes, most bison are the latter).

### Cameron — 2024-08-08T17:23:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/polyphenols-etc.29272/post-338189>

Today Jack reposted this re Tryptophan seems to indicate that foods rich in metabolites and secondary compounds may simply improve biological signaling and therefore reduce chronic disease....? "In my Vermont 2018 talk I warned the audience that light and aromatic amino acids are the key to understanding light. Why do I believe this? Typtophan has only one codon and acts like a time crystal in life and we can see that effect in its biochemistry. The photonics of typtophan pushes my belief paradigm even further down this rabbit hole because it is the only amino acid with an indole moiety, which makes it an ideal precursor for metabolites involved in biological signaling."

### Brent Patrick — 2024-08-09T00:41:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/polyphenols-etc.29272/post-338214>

> [Cameron said:](/goto/post?id=338182)
>
> Can someone explain how polyphenols and other secondary compounds (metabolites) in food help our mitochondria. I would like to mentally tie the ingestion of foods rich in these compounds with the Kruse insights regarding ATP production, circadian health etc. I assume that lowering inflammation, of course, improves mitochondrial efficiency but is there more subtle signaling and other effects from eating real, local healthy foods when it comes to these secondary compounds? I am fascinated by the work of Steven Van Vliet and Fred Provenza and just trying to tie it together. [https://stephanvanvliet.com/](https://stephanvanvliet.com/). He is going way beyond the regular studies and tying in soil health and environment to the food quality in objective ways. For example, regenerative raised Bison versus feed lot finished bison (yes, most bison are the latter).

The most impressive effects of polyphenols are via their epigenetic effects not as antioxidants!

"Epi-"means on or above in Greek, and "epigenetic" describes factors beyond the genetic code. Epigenetic changes are **modifications to DNA that regulate whether genes are turned on or off**. These modifications are attached to DNA and do not change the sequence of DNA building blocks.

[Checking your browser - reCAPTCHA](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9570183/)

Chaga is the bomb

Thanks. But still sorting mentally. The focus seems to be on scavenging free radicals but we know from Jack that ROS is also critical to energy production. no? So then it all comes
down to the regulation of free radicals to keep them at the proper level. What is confusing is the focus here is properties in food (polyphenols) and nothing about sun, melanin, etc. I guess my question is why these external inputs make things better and it isnt clear to me that always scavenging ROS is good. It also implies a central role for food. no? thinking out loud….

ok. i went further. ur right. goes way beyond ROS and is about methylation and histones and DNA etc. thank you. great article

### Cameron — 2024-08-09T22:09:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/polyphenols-etc.29272/post-338241>

but i am still confused some. These articles are like a Rhonda Patrick thing where some substance inhibits cancer by interacting with our genome etc but that still seems bio-chemical in approach. There are hundreds if not thousands of such substances which may have that effect and yet we may interact with only a small subset. Cant our bodies signal and turn on or off genes without these or convert/transmute other substances to do the same thing?. Overall, I cant get comfortable with, for example, a broccoli sprout shake being a great thing even if super rich in a “good” chemical compound (sulforafane). It just seems like that goes back to food guru world. Trying to reconcile…..

### Brent Patrick — 2024-08-10T06:35:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/polyphenols-etc.29272/post-338249>

> [Cameron said:](/goto/post?id=338241)

Overall, I cant get comfortable with, for example, a broccoli sprout shake being a great thing even if super rich in a “good” chemical compound (sulforafane). It just seems like that goes back to food guru world. Trying to reconcile…..

The way I reconcile this question is to think of polyphenols and alike as downstream packages born from light for us to consume. Just as Jack says food is lower on the list of priorities, important yes but not the most important.

Yes you are right there are thousands of these substances a whole other rabbit hole to dive down in relation to how they interact with Mitochrondria.

I do think that Rhondra Patrick falls into the catagory of wanting to isolate substances for their virtues but nature is way to smart and gives us what we need in the right proportion via food/fermentation and teas etc in its natural package based on the environment it is in.

Transmutation possibly ...Corentin Louis Kervran done such experiments with chickens,not sure how those experiments stand up today.

**"Cant our bodies signal and turn on or off genes without these"**

Sunlight does this via circadian cycle ,in fact if your Vitamin D levels are low, it will turn off the epigenetic switches on genes that turns on the immune system protector cells and keeps us free of diseases from viruses, bacteria and fungal infections.

**"Our genes don’t control us, but our thoughts do**. Thoughts are epigenetic signals too, but too few think that way. What we do with our thoughts control gene expression. **In a sense, in a mammal with an expanded neocortex thoughts are biologic endocrine secretions that change behavior by altering dopamine levels in our brain**."JK

### Cameron — 2024-08-10T16:39:11+0300

thanks for engaging! The Rhonda Patrick broccoli sprouts shake is when I became ready for Jack. Attia and her lost me and these conversations help me sort out a more accurate perception. # Thread: Visiting El Salvador Aug 20-29th
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/visiting-el-salvador-aug-20-29th.29273/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### moldguy-jc — 2024-08-08T17:30:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/visiting-el-salvador-aug-20-29th.29273/post-338191>

Greetings crew! I'm a new member, and making my first visit to El Salvador soon: Aug 20th to 29th!
I look forward to meeting other health-oriented folks and learning more about Bitcoin while I'm there.

I am staying at MT's place for most of this visit.

My hometowns in the U.S. are Dallas and Los Angeles and I'm looking to relocate for 3-6 months in El Salvador, Colombia, or Mexico in a peaceful, natural setting to do a **mold detox** wherever I land (I got very sick from being in moldy buildings).

Questions I have:

-   Are **saunas** easy to find in El Salvador, or should I just bring my portable infrared sauna? And is **cold plunge** a thing in El Salvador?
-   How is indoor **mold** in El Salvador? I've noticed it varies greatly across Latin America depending on environment and local practices.
-   For high quality olive oil, MCT oil, etc. do you fly back to the U.S. regularly to reload and put into checked luggage? Or **can you have items shipped to San Salvador efficiently**?

Additional info on me:
Being a perfectionist I don't call myself fluent in Spanish, but I can certainly get around and have lived in various Spanish speaking countries.

I'm an INFJ and when I leave my cave I love exploring various ideas and concepts in conversation with others...science, society, spirituality, etc. I get bored with small talk, gossip, and such conversation topics of most people. I prefer deeper, introspective and/or provocative topics that can lead to learning and growth vs. monotonous repetition of the same.

### India Rose Hopper — 2024-08-15T21:01:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/visiting-el-salvador-aug-20-29th.29273/post-338464>

Hope you enjoy your time here

MT’s is a great place to stay, not sure who else will be there at that time for company/conversation etc. It is very close to the beach, so between the big light in the sky (42% IR) and large body of water . . . Not sure why you would need a sauna or CT while you are there.

# Thread: Light water and magnetism - a Dr Jack original?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-water-and-magnetism-a-dr-jack-original.29277/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### thejessallen — 2024-08-09T00:34:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-water-and-magnetism-a-dr-jack-original.29277/post-338213>

I gave a presentation recently, the final piece of getting certified as a functional medicine health coach

I introduced Dr Jack Kruse (and Dr Alexis Cowan) as my mitochondriac mentors

I said Dr Jack coined the “light water and magnetism” as a 3 legged stool for mitochondrial optimization

I just came across a guy called Dr Jalal Khan. He’s speaking like Dr Jack but mentioned jack just at the very end along with others.

He even used these slides- this is what made me wonder… maybe it wasn’t jack who framed the famous “light water and magnetism” phrase?

He used the clock analogy and even mentions bitcoin.

I’m so grateful for Dr Jack and feel everyone should know his name and work.

Jalal often says “what I think is” and often followed by something I heard jack say.

It was my first presentation in many years, so maybe giving kudos to others while presenting is not cool?

He quotes Dr Doug Wallace as the expert on mitochondria

Only at the very end did he have a credits slide and quickly mention Dr Jack Kruse

Is this professional? The way it’s done? He sounded so much like Jack. It made me wonder is this what Doug Wallace sounds like? Or did this guy straight copy jack and leave that part out.

maybe because it’s I’m new - I haven’t given a presentation in decades. It felt right to mentioned Jack and Alexis often…

When someone had a health question - I said joining Jacks group is best place to get answers and Dr Alexis has lots of free content that’s worth learning.

Kind of wild - was curious what you might think?

### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-09T04:56:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-water-and-magnetism-a-dr-jack-original.29277/post-338226>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=338226)
>
> His answer about magnetism was pathetic.

It felt gross to hear him present as if these are his ideas.

My original curiosity still stands… was it you who coined the term “light water and magnetism”

I read an old post where you shared an image that said “sunlight water and magnetism” then I’m guessing it got upgraded due to blue light and nnEMF as light

you are the king

### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-10T17:10:26+0300

I only slept 1.5 hours and feel pretty bad. I haven't had insomnia that bad in a very very long time, maybe since 2017 / 2018. Sleep is super important for me to function well.

Anyone have a rough go at first with the Magnetico? I'm a bit nervous to try and sleep with it tonight since if I did that poor of sleep two nights in a row I would be really wrecked.

They said it could be from Detox Symptoms and recommended taking the booster off or taking binders. Curious to know other people's experiences with the product.

### TaylorJamesTJ — 2024-08-10T02:49:04+0300

Last night I was laying down on my bed with the Magnetico beneath it. I felt wide awake and alert. I could see I might sleep poorly again.

I got up and went into another room and laid down on that bed. I started getting groggy within 2-3 minutes and fell asleep and slept through the night.

So I'm nervous to use the Magnetico again.

### eve001 — 2024-08-10T13:19:32+0300

Hi Taylor! No kidding! I get that. I would get nervous too.
I have been reading only a bit bout the magnetico. I heard that depending on which hemisphere (N or S) one may need to turn it around or upside down bc of tyhe orientation of the magnets. Also I read your house better be low in nnEMF (LF, RF and dirty electricity). This is why I myself do not (yet) use earthing sheets or so as I am not sure bout the requirements for a healthy house nnEMF-wise for these products. Also consumer meters may not be specific enough to measure. I am yet to find an affordable building biologist in Belgium that I feel understands this topic sufficiently. Most of them have no idea of biophotons, biophysics and quantum biology. They seem deterministic-mechanistic old-school newtonian thinkers - uninformed by lessons from space travel regarding Schumann and magnetic field. But maybe I am being too picky here.
Could it also be that your cells sense something's odd - as the magnetico is new? All of a sudden so much energy to do what they used to do at a much slower pace? And so they and you get stressed and wide-awake due to stress bout this novelty added to stress you may already have? Some kind of hypervigilance induced by this bizar novelty? Was poor sleep an issue for you apart from the magnetico?
Have you considered calling the seller for more customer support?
Let us know how that works for you. May consider purchasing a magnetico or similar in the future too.

I am doing quite well, probably 80% and just want to get to 100%, 100% for me would be if I can lift weights again. Other than that, I feel pretty good a lot of the time, run a business, etc. Just want to get to optimal and be able to do more. If I take it easy on exercise and have low stress, I'm feeling energy and enthusiastic nearly every day. Just need to increase my capacity for more.

I wake up early and walk in the sun for an hour within an hour of the Sunrise. I do a cold plunge every morning. I have already had someone come to my house to help minimize nnEMF in it years ago. I turn off bluethooth on my phones during the day. I turn my WiFi router off and my phones off at night when I go to sleep. My computer has IRIS software on it. I wear blue light blocking glasses at night and keep all my lights off in my home as much as possible. Etc. Etc.

So my UV is great (people comment on how tan I am often), I'm outside a lot, I do cold plunges nearly every day, and I have minimized nnEMF. I don't think your hypothesis here is accurate for me and there is something else going on here.

Slightly unrelated but I had a terrible experience with infrared light panels (and the panel itself was excellent from an amazing brand where I've used other products). Tried it multiple times over the course of many months and it completely exacerbated certain symptoms every single time. I know other people hail it as miraculous but it had totally the opposite effect on me when I kept other factors consistent (and I'm very consistent with circadian health and nnEMF avoidance and mitigation). Anyway what I'm saying is if it doesn't work for you, it might just not be what your body wants at this particular time in your life- although obviously trouble-shooting any possible hiccups is worth exploring as others have mentioned. Sounds like you've already been pretty thorough about it.

Dirty Electricity “DE” is an artifact, harmonic of the 60Hz (50Hz) electric field - turn off the bedroom circuits - no electric field and therefore no “DE”, no magnetic field and no RF from those circuits. “DE” is less than 5% of the electric field, the “DE” is not the problem the electric field is. A barrier/shield is good to reduce the RF coming from outside, why use a barrier to reduce “DE” - turn off the circuit. If you have electric field in the room the Magnetico COULD be picking them up and “amplifying“ them, but I don’t know, I have never tested a Magnetico.

This is not my area but a thought is - could the magnetic field from the Magnetico, because of orientation, be in conflict with the natural magnetic field of your location?

Body Voltage Meter/Kit here: [www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

### Mat.I — 2024-08-15T03:27:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terrible-insomnia-with-magnetico.29278/post-338423>

> [TaylorJamesTJ said:](/goto/post?id=338407)
>
> I would say the room I have slept in being lower EMF isn't the argument here because I was sleeping good before the Magnetico. I am only using the other room **_because_** of the Magnetico. Only after the Magnetico did the sleep become dramatically affected. That is the **only** factor here. I think people are forgetting my sleep was good before the Magnetico and the Magnetico was the only change and **_then_** sleep became consistently terrible. I have not had sleep so bad that I only slept 1.5 hours in a night before for many years and that happened the first night with the Magnetico.

I understand what you are outlining here, however, it does not solve what is actually creating the issue. I know via the process of elimination you could say well if I eliminate the Magnetico the issue goes away. Test it with all the power isolated. You are truly flying blind here if you cannot measure what is going on when introducing a new variable (there is no baseline here). An absolute minimum would be to check body voltage. Measuring takes any assumptions and/or qualitative opinions out of the equation.

> [TaylorJamesTJ said:](/goto/post?id=338407)
>
> When the EMF expert came to my home a few years ago he did those measurements. My room was pretty good and we used a barrier behind myy bed and below it to lower the EMF and "dirty electricity" even more for sleeping, and with his tools he was saying my exposure to dirty electricity or EMF's was excellent for my bed area.

DE is only one small component. Define pretty good? This varies from one Building Biologist to another. Just because you measured a few years ago, it does not mean you will have the same results if measured today. There are many factors that could be at play here.

> [TaylorJamesTJ said:](/goto/post?id=338407)
>
> I did not experience vertigo or nausea while using the Magnetico. I have not killed the electricity in that room to test it out. What tool do you prefer to use to measure body voltage on / off?

Body voltage can be measured with a very basic multi-meter providing you already know what you are doing and can make up some earth jumpers with extensions, plenty of youtube videos out there showing you how to do this OR you could purchase Anthony's kit which I just looked at - it appears to have all you need plus extras and would be very beginner friendly and looks to be good value for the amount.

Long term - I would look at researching the EMF space so you don't need to pay a BB to measure your place and get some decent semi-professional meters. They will last a very long time providing they're looked after. When introducing new things &/or modifying existing I always measure, e.g. even if something gives you the option to disable BT or WIFI it doesn't necessarily mean it will disable it (hence the importance to measure) do not assume.

All the best.
Mat

Living here while looking for a better place.
\= How can we block LF EMFs?
\= How sensitive must a LF EMF meter be?

I have this esi22 meter 16Hz-3kHz. [https://www.radonshop.com/epe-conseil-indikator-esi-22-nf-electrosmog-emf-detector-16-hz-3-khz](https://www.radonshop.com/epe-conseil-indikator-esi-22-nf-electrosmog-emf-detector-16-hz-3-khz)

I can measure electric fields from the radiator (central heating) under the window. There also are ELF electric currents on the ceiling even though the circuits are turned off. I cannot measure them with the ESI around my bed.
How could one shield from that in a mobile way? (so I can take my shielding material with me when I move)
Could it be jump conduction?
Would a travel shielding tent be an idea?
I would not attach it to the ceiling given the LF fields there. = I would prefer a tent above a canopy.

RF EMF peak readings on SSPro2 fluctuate :
0,5-2 microwatt/m2 in this bedroom at bedlevel.
Around 3 microwatts at hip level standing up
Celltowers at +- 2-3km.
Plenty of solar pannels in street. (DE?)
In-house powerlan devolo finally eliminated last week! Yay!

This is way better than the 5G city I fled from. (3 cell towers, radar, 5G antennas around the building).
Many Belgians report: they feel way more horrible in Belgium than in France - when returning from holidays they report sudden worsening of symptoms as soon as entering Belgian territory. (DAB? Small territory high cell tower density? Different frequency combinations?)

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terrible-insomnia-with-magnetico.29278/post-351052>

The magnetico is not the cause of poor sleep. A lack of redox is.

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2025-04-22T02:26:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terrible-insomnia-with-magnetico.29278/post-351071>

### digitald3 — 2025-04-22T03:08:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terrible-insomnia-with-magnetico.29278/post-351074>

I live in Las Vegas and I sleep on a Magnetico pad... Am sun, lunch in the sun and spend most weekends outside as much as possible, blocking blue light, Daily CT and the Magnetico have fixed my insomnia.. I routinely get 6-8 hours of sleep..

### S Hudson — 2025-04-25T13:08:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terrible-insomnia-with-magnetico.29278/post-351245>

Strangely, since fully implementing the light aspect of Jack's ideas, this last week I've had broken sleep. I'm putting this down to changing my sleeping patterns (bed at 9-10pm rather than 1-2am, and waking at 5.30Am rather than 8-10am) as my body adjusts to the new reality. I've got decades of tech abuse to reverse, so its not going to be an easy transition.

# Thread: HOLD YOUR BREATH! - A UNIQUE 3-day Retreat in El Salvador
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hold-your-breath-a-unique-3-day-retreat-in-el-salvador.29281/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Erwan Le Corre — 2024-08-10T05:26:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hold-your-breath-a-unique-3-day-retreat-in-el-salvador.29281/post-338247>

Hi everyone

You may have heard my name from my dear friend, "Uncle Jack," who kindly permitted me to post on this forum.
A few months ago, I was honored to be the first speaker at the first Age of Light conference in El Salvador, where I talked about my natural approach to exercise and lifestyle.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

I held a two-day BreathHoldWork & Natural Movement workshop on the beach of El Zonte just before the conference, and many of you expressed interest in attending the same kind of event.

I've got great news!

I'm returning to El Salvador for a 3-day BreathHoldWork retreat from October 11 to 14!

[](https://www.breathholdwork.com/breathholdwork-elsalvador-retreat)
(CLICK ON THE IMAGE TO VISIT THE EVENT PAGE)

This masterclass in breathwork, breath-holding, meditation, and nervous system downregulation is set in the stunning San Luis La Herradura on the Costa Del Sol (“Sun Coast” ).

I hold the US National Record for Static Breath-holding with the CMAS organization in the Master category, with an official performance of 7 minutes and 29 seconds.
My personal record stands at 8 minutes and 3 seconds.

To hold your breath for such a long time, you need an impeccable lifestyle and specific physiological adaptations. Most importantly, you must master meditation and relaxation like you master high-level skills.
During this masterclass, I will teach you breath control, meditation, and relaxation in a unique and powerful way through various exercises and by sharing many profound insights.
**You’ll feel the most mentally clear, emotionally relaxed, and happiest you’ve been in a long time!

**

The venue is a private beachfront property with green grass and lush palm trees, providing a beautiful and intimate setting in a serene atmosphere ideal for your transformative experience.

If you live in El Salvador already or need another great reason to visit this amazing country, this is it!

I hope to see you there.

~ Erwan

# Thread: What 2 months of listening to Uncle Jack did to me
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-2-months-of-listening-to-uncle-jack-did-to-me.29283/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### thejessallen — 2024-08-11T03:54:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-2-months-of-listening-to-uncle-jack-did-to-me.29283/post-338272>

Today Apple (and my karma) gave me a memory video and I was shocked to see myself and the contrast in just two months.

I was scared to move my teen and I to El Salvador. I was living in a yoga ashram in Tennessee. Peaceful. Safe (ish) But not allowed to show much skin. I would hide in the woods and find patches of sun to shine on belly skin I would show.

Now I’m blessed to live in El Salvador - in a house with no neighbors. Investing in many long hours of “junk maintenance” mode as Uncle Jack calls it. Making sure I’m turning as many white areas as tan as possible.

After lightening storms I walk to the beach which is a 5 minute walk and cover myself with wet sand. I meditate with hope that eyes closed meditation is still allowing the red light into my retina.

My work and zoom meetings are outside as often as possible with my bare feet on the ground.

Eating fresh seafood from the La Libertad market at least 3-4 times a week

I’ve enjoyed fresh sourdough bread from Bitcoin farmers market. And am not attempting to be keto. Although soon I may do that again.

The picture of me in white is mid may 2024 exploring El Salvador to decide if I wanted to live here. It was an easy yes.

The picture of me in blue is about 2 months later.

My favorite upgrade is how much thicker my hair is getting.

I’m thinking Uncle Jack might be on to something with his “light, water and magnetism” theory

I’m living a dream and I’ve been through enough tough times to be certain, that no matter what will happen in the future, I know everything is all good if I’m alive for another day.

This day… I’m overwhelmed with gratitude and perspective about how quickly things can get better when choosing to live life as a black swan mitochondriac

# Thread: A deeply spiritual song / chant I love to listen to on repeat many times
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-deeply-spiritual-song-chant-i-love-to-listen-to-on-repeat-many-times.29284/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### thejessallen — 2024-08-11T05:02:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-deeply-spiritual-song-chant-i-love-to-listen-to-on-repeat-many-times.29284/post-338274>

I find this song so beautiful - then I learned what the lyrics meant and fell even deeper in love

God is One, I am One with all beings

Truth is God’s Name… The vibration of God’s Name is what created all beings…. that vibration is within me…. therefore in my core I am true.

God is the Doer…. God does all things… God works through me…. God works through everyone in my life.

God is fearless… I am fearless.

God has no revenge…. I exist in my heart… I stay present… I don’t hold on to the past…. I live in this moment in full compassion and acceptance of what is.

God exists in the undying form… God is in the purity of our efforts to live in a spiritual way, and these efforts never die… these efforts live on forever.

God is self illumined… I am self illumined. Within me I have the power to find light, to find love, to find joy. I don’t need anyone or anything for this.

it is the Grace of the Divine One…. that give us Grace.

Meditate!

True in the Beginning

True throughout the ages

True here and now

forever and always true. Spirit, Truth, Love, and Light…. these are the things that are true, that have always been true.

# Thread: Beige fat & weight loss article

**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ColinGorham — 2024-08-11T22:46:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beige-fat-weight-loss-article.29285/post-338288>

[https://www.sciencefocus.com/news/beige-fat-weight-loss](https://www.sciencefocus.com/news/beige-fat-weight-loss)

### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-13T05:08:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beige-fat-weight-loss-article.29285/post-338357>

Brown adipose tissue (BAT) utilizes large amounts of fat fuel for thermogenesis, but the mechanism by which fuel substrates are switched in response to changes in energy status is poorly understood in cnetralized science. My current podcast with Cameron Borg uncover that mystery.

The role of Kruppel‐like factor 15 is linked to a lack of UV light and cold temperatures as a transcription factor that is more expressed in adipocytes to help in the regulation of fuel in BAT.

### [retimer light therapy glasses | Wake up to better sleep! | Official Website](https://www.re-timer.com/)

Wake up to better sleep with Re-Timer Light Therapy Glasses. Worlds's most preferred sleep wearable to help align your circadian rhythm for a brighter, healthier lifestyle!

### Anthony Smith — 2024-08-12T20:05:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jet-lag-glasses.29288/post-338321>

Their ad says “fix your sleep the natural way” - 500nm light pluses at 50 to 166 hertz (and Bluetooth?). . .
Looks to me like these are exacerbating the problem of excessive blue light and nnEMF in our environment ON STEROIDS . . .

Now with a little modification of replacing the blue/green light with non pulsing, near infrared and a little UV (no Bluetooth) . . .
For extreme unavoidable situations like air travel and extended blue screen exposure . . . Maybe some benefit . . . OR . . .

You could actually “fix your sleep the natural way” by AVOIDING blue light and watching the sun rise (and set) and save the $$$.

# Thread: Corporate Income Tax in El Salvador.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/corporate-income-tax-in-el-salvador.29289/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Heerink — 2024-08-12T18:53:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/corporate-income-tax-in-el-salvador.29289/post-338313>

Dr. Jack,

With access to President Bukele, can you convince his government to lower the Corporate Income Tax in El Salvador?

The current corporate income tax (CIT) rate in El Salvador is 30%. There is a reduced CIT rate of 25% for companies that obtain taxable income equal to or less than 150,000 United States dollars (USD) in the fiscal year.
However this 30% rate is more than the 21% in the USA and in the top 10% of the world.

A lower CIT would benefit their economy because companies would invest and start/move their business to El Salvador (including mine)
Especially a low rate for online business would spur economic growth.

Something like this (several countries use this model):

-   For taxable profits up to $200,000, the rate should be ~15%.
-   For taxable profits exceeding $200,000, the rate should be ~20%.

Thanks,

### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-12T21:44:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/corporate-income-tax-in-el-salvador.29289/post-338339>

Hire an attorney for this information

# Thread: El Salvador - Recommondations
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-recommondations.29293/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bennefit-coaching — 2024-08-13T15:13:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-recommondations.29293/post-338368>

Hi guys,

I booked my flight ticket now on October 9th. My first option for a stay is a room in Atami in a "mitofriendly" house. It's 1000USD per month. My questions are the following. It would make me very happy when you could answer at least one of them. I'm looking forward to meeting you guys there.

_Isn't that a bit expensive for El Salvador? What would be a good price for a month?

Are there other "mitofriendly" homes you know?

Is it easy to get organic food there in Atami or in El Salvador in general?

Is it possible to get water in glass bottles there or should I bring my activated carbon filter for the tap water?

Who else is in Atami?_

Best, Benne

### thejessallen — 2024-08-13T17:55:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-recommondations.29293/post-338372>

Welcome Benne - I visited in May to explore living. Accommodation prices for travelers seem to be pretty aligned with United States pricing, but with more features.

I would say $1000 for a month and a mito friendly house is a great deal - the mito friendly part is worth that alone IMO

I haven’t seen water in glass bottles. Your host should know more. Curious what they say

I don’t know about organic food. I just focus on eating locally and get tons of sun and grounding hearing that helps with detoxing

El Salvador is amazing. I’m not in Atami - about 15 minutes away - I’m sure I’ll see you at a Bitcoin farmers market though! That’s a common time I venture out

Welcome - many blessings for rapid healing and fun for you here

Jess

Hello Benne,
Atami is a lovely place! I was there for 9 months but have currently moved to Shalpa where a friend, Jack McKenna, and I have have a house right across the road from the beach. We have a couple of rooms for rent if you still need a place. We are pretty hardcore “Mito” here with red lights and Ethernet cables only etc
Drinking water generally comes in BPA-free, plastic 5 gallon bottles.
If you would like more info feel free to private message me on here.
Hope your stay here is amazing!

### Anthony Smith — 2024-08-15T21:05:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-recommondations.29293/post-338465>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-recommondations.29293/post-338498>

The great advantage of this mitofriendly house is, that you don't need your own car, because there are shopping tours that you can join.
I wouldn't bother about organic food, the strong sun makes everything else less important, you will see. That being said, there are a couple of organic shops and even a delivery service that delivers to Atami.

# Thread: Hello from Northwest Indiana, the armpit of Chicago
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-northwest-indiana-the-armpit-of-chicago.29296/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AustinBrewster — 2024-08-13T20:46:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-northwest-indiana-the-armpit-of-chicago.29296/post-338380>

Hello everyone, I am so happy jack punched me in the mouth a year or so ago, although I was looking for this punch of truth for a long time. When I started reading his blogs, I knew why his information had been hidden from me. I’ve always intuitively known there was a deeper level to everything in the universe and nature was the answer to it all. I have been obsessed with becoming a better athlete and seeking truth since I was 14. I became a registered nurse in 2018 and was professionally disappointed from the start, but got my degree because I knew I’d at least have financial stability and some free time. I have done a lot of my own research into biochemistry and the benefits of circadian biology and food from that lens. When my son had a seizure at 5 months old in February of 2023 and his Vit D came back at 18 and was positive for an MTHFR SNP, my world changed. My wife was breastfeeding at the time so we went strict carnivore for the rest of the winter together, supplemented him with Vit D and K2, as well as Methylfolate/methylcobalamin, gave him full spectrum CBD, and took him off his Keppra. He hasn’t had a seizure since that hospital visit, he turns 2 years next month. During that hospital stay the fire was lit inside me and I am getting emotional even writing this because of the anger, frustration, and sadness I felt for the next parent that would have no clue there is a deeper level to your child’s health problem that the pediatrics departments wont tell you about. No longer was I going to allow my lack of knowledge affect my family and my mind the way it was, and no longer could I hold up the lies I knew the doctors I worked next to go on. I became outspoken amongst my peers and in the hospital system this falls on deaf ears, but I am doing this for myself and for the ones that aren’t deaf. Since I “fixed” my sons seizures through these methods, I thought I was onto something amazing, and delved into the carnivore and keto rabbit hole. Huberman also helped me quite a bit. I justified it as the best possible diet for our health because I was buying grass fed local beef that I knew wasn’t raised with the standard care of beef. I thought it was the least toxic option we have since clean fruits and vegetables were harder to come by in my area. I stumbled upon jack through Sara Pugh’s YouTube channel among others a couple months after my son had his seizure. I heard, “he wont dumb it down for you” So I kinda played it off like it was too high level for me and I couldn’t understand it. (Low agency as f\*\*\*) I kept his name in the background, thinking “Ill get to his work one day”. Through my journey I decided to become a Nurse Coach, and honestly the program made me believe in myself because I saw a way to get paid for my knowledge not my time. Spring of 2024 I found decided to finally delve into his work by listening to the 10 hours of podcasting on Tetragrammaton. I can’t stop listening or reading his work. He is basically like google for the curious people, he really stimulates thinking. Since then, I have been doing everything I can to keep my family strong and healthy, protected from my shit environment. Luckily, I live in a rural area, but it is surrounded by glyphosate rich fields.

Hey AB-- I am originally from north central IN & a Purdue grad. I've been living by the ocean the past decade in NE Florida. Seeing the sunrise and sunshine daily is Huge, those grey sky months get tough up there. UV levels are tough, I believe that's why a lot of folks get addicted with going to the tanning salons up there, not just to look good but their bodies crave the UV.

With everything you guys got going on ^^ I would recommend relocating down here (or even further south). All of my family is still in Indiana, which can be tough, but if you moved beachside they will all have a reason to come visit you. It can give them something to look forward to. The attached photo is from my front porch overlooking the intracoastal & out to the Atlantic.

One of the best things you can do for your family is provide a positive environment for them. Chicago & "The Region" are definitely not that. Writing this note to be a resource of information for you, hope for the best for you and your family. You can message me directly if curiousity strikes. Cheers man......MM

# Thread: School
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/school.29301/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Clancybenton — 2024-08-15T00:00:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/school.29301/post-338419>

Dr. Kruse,

I am in the very early stages of a huge project that I intend to start on in the very near future.

I am in the process of developing my own school. I am currently a public school teacher and I do not believe in this system at all. So I will be developing my own school with the help of professionals that I truly believe in. (You, of course, being one of them).

My goal is to develop a micro-campus that allows the students to be in the best possible learning environment, which means that we need to be in natural light. As much as I would like to just “have it outside”, there will be days in which being inside is the only option. Of course, we could opt for a lot of windows allowing for natural light to come in but we know that even the windows will block full spectrum lighting/allow in too much blue light.

Would you have any thoughts or recommendations on what an optimal school would look like if you were to build one out?

This school will be starting off very small but as we become more successful, I would like to build it out into a larger campus that has the heath and well-being of the students as a top priority.

Thank you for your time!

All the best,

\-Clancy Benton

# Thread: Vitamin D lab question (Argentina)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-lab-question-argentina.29308/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### martin.v — 2024-08-15T16:50:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-lab-question-argentina.29308/post-338446>

Hello Kruse community!

I'm not sure if where you reside happens similarly, but here we can't ask for more than **one** Vitamin D lab per year, otherwise you need to pay for each one of them (very expensive). Doesn't happen with any other normal value.

Funny right? I know what you may think, this looks twisted!

I was still wondering if the Vit D study was somehow more complex in the way the biochemist can take it, do some of you know if this is the case or is it just a regular lab that Big P doesn't want you to have?

This happens in Argentina, and happens with one of the best medical providers, which makes it even more suspicious.

Anyway, wondering if you had similar stories!
Have a nice one,
Martin

### NikkiYork — 2024-08-16T08:20:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-lab-question-argentina.29308/post-338481>

They took Vitamine D off the annual check up panel.
In California you need to specifically request it and otherwise pay for it, but it’s not expensive with… lifeextension I think is the name of the company.

Just noticed: last year Vitamine D insufficiency was <20mg/mol this year they lowered it to <10.

I spoke with a doctor from the East coast, and he said they want to really not rely on any of that test anymore, it would not indicate anything. Oh well.

> In California you need to specifically request it and otherwise pay for it, but it’s not expensive with… lifeextension I think is the name of the company.

> Just noticed: last year Vitamine D insufficiency was <20mg/mol this year they lowered it to <10.
>
> I spoke with a doctor from the East coast, and he said they want to really not rely on any of that test anymore, it would not indicate anything. Oh well.

Doc said that Vit D value doesn't indicate anything?

### NikkiYork — 2024-08-18T04:47:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-lab-question-argentina.29308/post-338568>

> [martin.v said:](/goto/post?id=338497)
>
> Doc said that Vit D value doesn't indicate anything?

Yup, like it’s old school thinking. That sort of jist and it’s not a reliable value. (Not my doctor, I tell you that).

### drsongihan — 2024-08-18T22:17:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-lab-question-argentina.29308/post-338597>

As someone who used to think that conspiracies are silly: I think this is not just ignorance or accidental, but a much more cynical situation. Its the doctors and insurance lobby suppressing information on purse because nowadays vitamin D levels look bad across the board, and they are not willing to say that really we should be exposed to UV from sunlight ideally, or if not possible at least via a therapy lamp because that would mean the entire dermatology society would have to put their hands up and say "mea culpa".

My friend from Germany (mostly 50's latitude) reminded me that the situation looked very different in the 70ies and 80ies. In Germany, kids and adults received UV lamp therapy in Fall and Spring (like monthly). His dad was a family doctor and its a therapy that he routinely used. Such practice is gone. Also, getting into a tanning bed in the winter was a thing in Germany that everyone did. Now only some do it, but most are afraid of the UV. I myself remember being told (I grew up in Germany) by my parents to get as deep of a tan as possible in the summer so that I stay healthy in the Winter. I don't know what my mom knew, but she went religiously to the village's pool every day where I spent all hours between school end and dinner time in the sun, swimming, sunning and playing. I turned black in the summer, and of course nobody used sunscreen. Maybe some adults took tan oil but none of the kids used sunscreen. All of this changed. It's a new conspiracy spread by doctors in the 2000's, and I heard that there was apparently even a plan to explicitly suppress any research papers that emphasize the importance of vitamin D.

Another friend of mine who grew up in a village near Quebec told me that she grew up knowing that the sun is extremely precious. All kids in the village were sent 30 minutes before the school bus comes to the bus station and were told to face east and watch the sun rise. They were told that if they don't see the sun rise, they will get sick and crazy in the winter. She told me that the entire village did that, and she grew up doing that all her childhood. All kids were sitting at the bus station bench, cold and shivering, watching the sunrise, but it was normal since the entire village did that. During recess the kids were sent out no matter the temperature, explicitly because they needed to get UV exposure during the solar noon. And they walked back around 3.30pm from school when the sun was setting. These village Canadians knew something and worshipped the sun. But of course today, all the wisdom that was practiced 20 years ago is lost because there is no more village culture anyway, it's all city and because people got all brainwashed. Now, kids are watching the iPhone in the warm room or heated car, definitely not watching the sunrise outside 30 min before the school bus comes, and definitely not as a group event.

Isn't it crazy that the intuitive wisdom was there even in the 80ies, but got lost somewhere between the 90ies and now?

### martin.v — 2024-09-02T06:09:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-lab-question-argentina.29308/post-339150>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=338737)
>
> Vitamin D covered here:

Wops! great podcast, in fact I can literally take with me the explanation for people when they try to get away with the Vit D supplements instead of getting into the sun:

"We know for a fact that Vit D is a fat soluble molecule, when sunlight hits your skin, it takes cholesterol, converts it to Vitadmin D, but it actually sulfates it too, in order to make it water soluble so it can get pass through to your arteries and get to the liver and kidneys where it's converted (to 25OH and 1.25OH right?). When you take a Vit D supplement, it comes in seed/olive oil, so it raises your triglycerides and LDL as a consequence."

Got it now
