# Thread: Adrenal Fatigue
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adrenal-fatigue.29309/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Thijs Oosting — 2024-08-15T17:32:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adrenal-fatigue.29309/post-338450>

Hi All.

Since 8 years both me and my twin brother have been experiencing extreme fatigue, which suddenly appeared during a dry-fast. Now 8 years later we are still not better at all. We suspect we have adrenal fatigue because we have low metabolised cortisol (see 17 hydroxysteroids on laboratory work). We have been following circadian lifestyle for a year (also went to the yucatan for 2 months) and have build up a very nice tan. No change in energy though.

Symtoms:
\- chronic fatigue (unable to go for walks, mostly chair/couch bound, unable to sit up for extended periods of time due to fatigue/exhaustion. Not being able to work at all, no social live, no exercise. Lie down or sit down all day. We receive sickness benefit from our ex company/government).

\- besides the constant fatigue we also have mid-morning and mid-afternoon crashes when we need to lie down. Too exhausted to even lift a finger.

\- often too tired to talk

\- large parts of the day too weak to read or concentrate on anything

\- horrible overall feeling of wellbeing. Just hanging on to make it to the end of the day

\- sometimes we have reactive hypoglycaemia

\- Reactive hypoglycaemia after drinking coffee. We don’t drink coffee but we tried 4 cups in 8 years.

\- Brain fog

\- Nausea (which seems to do with hypolgycemia)

\- Blurry vision on days of little sleep

\- Extreme hay fever during May-June. Rest of year no issue

\- Premature ejaculation

\- Muscle soreness

\- muscle weakness

\- late onset fatigue

\- Gut issues all life

\- Cold hands & feet

We have also noticed the following:

\- we seem to feel even worse when we go low carb/carnivore/ketogenic

\- No more allergic rhinitis (probably due to eating strictly carnivore). It now only arises when around pets, perfumes, smoke , dust and hay fever

\- In the past, whenever we allowed a cheat meal (a lot of ice-cream and cookies) we have experienced a temporary bump in energy.

\- We tried activated niacin a few times and we get a very intense niacin flush (only when we take activated niacin).

\- If we eat beef liver we have hypoglycaemic symptoms all day. Even eating carbs does not seem to help. Does beef liver contain insulin? (Could it be that we are deficient in cortisol and that cortisol is needed to counteract insulin?)

Thanks

### rtd — 2024-08-15T19:39:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adrenal-fatigue.29309/post-338455>

I suggest you delete the attachment, scrub your personal information from it, and repost. Not a good idea to have personal information, especially HIPA stuff, unsecured online.

### Thijs Oosting — 2024-08-24T12:14:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adrenal-fatigue.29309/post-338853>

[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)

Hey Jack. Your consult with us was great and we have decided to move to the equator for a year! We have found a great place on the vietnamese island Phu Quoc (10 degrees north latitude). It is a resort called: Cay Sao Beach Resort. The adres is: [63CF+VCC, Cây Sao, Phú Quốc, Kiên Giang, Vietnam](https://www.google.nl/maps/place//data=!4m2!3m1!1s0x31a7891044c22b89:0x6a81df65514d339b?sa=X&ved=1t:8290&ictx=111). The resort is located on the east side of the island in a low tourist, quiet area. Lots of affordable seafood available.

We will purchase a cold plunge bath that reaches a maximum low of 40 degrees fahrenheit in 86+ degrees environments.

### [Polar Pro | Powerful Cold Plunge For Daily Recovery](https://polarplungemy.com/products/sub-zero-v2)

Polar Pro is a premium cold plunge ice bath built for daily recovery. Drops to 3°C with a powerful chiller, filtration system, and durable indoor outdoor design

 polarplungemy.com

We prefer to set up our cold bath outside our bungalow so that we can be outside while taking baths. However, since the cooling machine needs to run for 6 hours a day and because we dont know how loud it is, we might have to install it inside our bungalow.

We are planning to book a bungalow with ocean view. However, all bungalows have acces to wifi. We contacted them and the owner is ok with removing the wifi station/booster in our bungalow so we can get cabled internet instead. However, we wonder if the wifi from the neighbouring bungalows would still be a problem?

Here are some photos in which you can see the distance between the bungalows. Would than be fine?

Thanks
Daan & Thijs

# Thread: Repeated concussion recovery
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/repeated-concussion-recovery.29312/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### inthesun — 2024-08-16T02:04:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/repeated-concussion-recovery.29312/post-338473>

Would be curious what is recommended beyond [Jacks concussion protocol](https://jackkruse.com/concussioncte-prescription/). I got a concussion yesterday. Relatively minor as I didn't get knocked out or vomit but saw stars for a few seconds and had symptoms since. Now I am consuming daily 2600mg EPA and 1800mg DHA, 600mg turmeric, 1g magnesium glycinate, 1 cup coconut oil and plenty of water in addition to my regular diet. Removed all alcohol from my diet for forseeable future. Sitting outside in the sun or in a room illuminated by red light.

I want to recover fully from this concussion and do what is possible to heal my brain from the additive effects of prior head injury as well. Looking for guidance that could be stacked on the Epi-Paleo Rx and EMF Rx.

### BLT — 2024-11-12T21:32:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/repeated-concussion-recovery.29312/post-342267>

kind bump on this thread from a newbie seeking the same

### Kpack12 — 2024-11-12T22:55:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/repeated-concussion-recovery.29312/post-342270>

If you still have lingering effects from concussions you may want to look for a neurofeedback practitioner. I've been offering brain training with neurofeedback for almost 9 years and worked with several clients who had concussions. It's a very effective way to retrain connections that may have been affected by a concussion. Wait at least 3-6 weeks post concussion before doing any neurofeedback

### BLT — 2024-11-13T00:37:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/repeated-concussion-recovery.29312/post-342275>

never heard of that, thanks Kpack12. i will check this out.
have you had experience resolving sensitivity to light issues?

### Kpack12 — 2024-11-13T02:53:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/repeated-concussion-recovery.29312/post-342279>

> [BLT said:](/goto/post?id=342275)
>
> never heard of that, thanks Kpack12. i will check this out.
> have you had experience resolving sensitivity to light issues?

Yes, I have worked with lots of children and adults with sensory processing issues including light sensitivity. Neurofeedback can work wonders for these issues.

### BLT — 2024-11-14T05:38:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/repeated-concussion-recovery.29312/post-342347>

awesome to hear that, thank you!

# Thread: From Blood Disorder to Optimal - w/Labs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-blood-disorder-to-optimal-w-labs.29313/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### alleerielle — 2024-08-16T03:55:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-blood-disorder-to-optimal-w-labs.29313/post-338476>

I wanted to log this journey for myself and for anyone else it may help with a similar diagnosis. I would love any feedback because my head is spinning and I am seeking more clarity. There is so much to learn and I want to know it all. Thanks in advance to everyone here for any insight. I am so grateful to Dr. Kruse for all the work he has done and for creating this forum. It is such a relief to finally feel like I am getting the truth.

I am Female, 54 years old and live in a suburb of Dallas, TX. Haplogroup H1. My Mom is 87 and still active and healthy. I work from home and I do spend a lot of time on my computer. And a couple of years ago, they put a 5G "tower" on my street. They make it look like a street light. It's not fooling anybody. I am working on moving out of here.

A little about my health issue...around 2011, I had surgery to remove a cervical polyp. It took 2 years for doctors to find the polyp and during that time I was bleeding a lot. Because of this, I became borderline anemic and my platelet counts went high. After the surgery, the bleeding stopped. I assumed the platelets would return to normal, however, even after an iron infusion, they did not. I saw a hematologist who suspected Essential Thrombocythemia. He monitored it for a few years but my counts, although high, were pretty stable. Since I had no symptoms, other than occasional fatigue, I really wasn't too concerned when my doctor moved to another city. I never really believed that it wouldn't go back to normal. My theory was that it was something like an epigenetic switch that got turned on. I obviously haven't figured out how to turn if off.

The past couple of years, I have been feeling more fatigue, headaches, brain fog and at times having difficulty concentrating. Also, my hair has been steadily falling out for about a year. Fast forward to a month ago, I started getting a weird symptom that felt like a pin was pricking me after I got out of the shower. When I looked it up, I saw that it was a symptom of Polycythemia Vera, so I went and got some labs done. And wow, they looked really concerning. I am trying to find a specialist just to find out exactly what I am dealing with. I'm also concerned about it being Myleofibrosis.

Currently I am watching the sunrise every morning. I go outside at lunchtime for my Vitamin D. I am increasing my sushi consumption. Drinking 1.5-2 liters 125ppm DDW for the past month and just started 105 ppm DDW per day. I wear blue blocking glasses every night. I try to get to bed by dark so that I don't even have to use them. I eat a meat based diet. I walk 3 miles a few times a week. I am researching more about what to do for my environment regarding NN-Emf. I'm highly concerned about it.

I have considered taking baby aspirin, however, my Dad had a slow brain bleed after taking it for years, and it concerns me. Any other ideas for safely thinning the blood? I've got to train myself to eat raw oysters. I have also considered Cold Thermogenesis, however, I am not sure if it would be a good idea for me since my blood is probably so thick. I am taking cool showers and baths, but mostly because I feel hot.

I am trying to get an appointment with a hematologist. Just sent in my labs today. I hate going to these centralized Cancer Centers. Something has always felt so "off" to me about them. It creeps me out. I'm going because I just feel like I need more information. So we'll see.

I attached the labs just taken a couple of days ago. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

>
> I attached the labs just taken a couple of days ago.

I hope you find some answers. I would recommend a private consult with Jack before you see these doctors so you have the right info. I wanted to pop in and say hi since we are "neighbors". I live near DFW and surrounded by cell towers too.

### alleerielle — 2024-08-16T19:07:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-blood-disorder-to-optimal-w-labs.29313/post-338499>

> [sastewie said:](/goto/post?id=338492)
>
> I hope you find some answers. I would recommend a private consult with Jack before you see these doctors so you have the right info. I wanted to pop in and say hi since we are "neighbors". I live near DFW and surrounded by cell towers too.

Hi, and thank you. I'm sure I will find the answers. I feel like I'm am in the right place. It's nice to meet you on here. I used to live in Colleyville and loved it there. By any chance, do you happen to know of a good place for raw oysters around here? I have never been interested in trying them before, however, I think it's time to try it out.

### alleerielle — 2024-09-28T20:58:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-blood-disorder-to-optimal-w-labs.29313/post-340126>

I wanted to update here. My lab results are pretty much unchanged. I received a call from the hematologist informing me that I tested positive for the Jak2 mutation. This confirmed the diagnosis of Polycythemia Vera. He indicated that I needed to do a phlebotomy asap. I am hesitant because my iron is already so low that my hair is falling out and I feel like the fatigue is affecting my work. I don't want to do something that will make me feel worse. Plus I am small 5'1", around 105 lbs. They want to take 500ml of blood. He said he would give me fluids if I felt dizzy.

He also said to take baby aspirin once a day. I am hesitant to do that also. When I conveyed this to him, he just said that is how we treat this. I told him I started taking the supplement lumbrokinase which is supposed to affect fibrin in the blood, break up clots and lower the blood viscosity. He had never heard of it. I don't believe I have any clots, however, it seems like a good preventative measure.

I have yet to meet this doctor in person. My first appointment is in a couple of days. My instincts are telling me to not follow his protocol. He is saying that he wants to keep my iron low indefinitely so that my body does not create more red blood cells. I am trying to understand this better. I cannot reconcile this in my mind though.

On a positive side, I am continuing with morning sun and sunset. And getting my entire body exposed to UVB mid day. Also I have continued the DDW. My BUN/Creatinine ratio has dropped from 19 to 12.5 since I started that.

I am also learning more about nnEMF side of this. I purchased the Body Volt Meter and am now shutting off the electricity to one room and sleeping in there. I feel like I am sleeping better. It has just been one week though.

Does anyone have any thoughts or experience regarding this?

My low back is better and I have more vivid dreams. I would highly recommend doing this, you need to know how much you are being radiated. \- See if you can let in sunlight
\- Get a meter safe and sound 2 pro is good for RF
\- The body voltage meter is good to monitor LF EMFs in your home from wired devices
\- Unplug devices not in use

Anthony Smith sells here is savy on all this here on the forum. His company is EMFsafe.

# Thread: When Rx’s don’t reverse your condition. Next steps?  YOU MISSED THE nnEMF story
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-rx%E2%80%99s-don%E2%80%99t-reverse-your-condition-next-steps-you-missed-the-nnemf-story.29315/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 40
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### dantothep — 2024-08-16T14:36:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-rx%E2%80%99s-don%E2%80%99t-reverse-your-condition-next-steps-you-missed-the-nnemf-story.29315/post-338490>

“This means all diseases that are tied to the loss of electrons are COMPLETELY REVERSIBLE.” - Jack Redox RX

I thought I would write as much detail as possible in the hope that this may trigger some ideas in this learned community. I firmly believe the quote from Jack above & I know I can reverse this rather than simply manage it.

I haven’t been here for a while. I started following the protocols in 2015, they really got me out of a nightmarish hole that I found myself in whilst living and working in London for 15 years.

In case anyone views this post as negative; it is in no way. Without finding Jacks work there is no doubt in my mind that I would either be very seriously ill, sectioned or dead had I tried to work through fixing my issues (severe amnesia, energy collapses, mental health issues) in the same environment that made me sick.

I moved to southern Spain in 2016 after Jack’s advice, and my life significantly improved as a result. I moved for the 300+ days of clear sky and sun per year, the access to the beaches, the nature, the change of pace of life. I currently live about 400m above sea level, in a very low EMF house (tested regularly) surrounded by a few neighbours and nature mostly. I have had the least financial and work stress in the past 2 years than ever before.

**Symptomatology**

This is a quagmire of cycles with no consistent pattern \[apart from the seasonal one - more on that later\]. I have periods of good health, characterised by high energy, high libido, clear focused thoughts and ideation, great memory recall, easy breaths strong connection to source through meditation etc. I know what it is like to feel VITAL. I still get some symptoms in this state, and these can come on very quickly and sometimes leave just as quickly.

My issue is a lack of consistency with energy, inflammation & mood. The severity depends on where my redox is at that point in time. This can shift hourly, daily, or weekly. I have had some years with very few flare cycles. Currently this has been the worst year since 2014 when I was living in London. The “shit” cycle started in grass pollen season (late April), and I have only had 1 or 2 weeks of normal cycle since then. Its very hard for me to see a high level pattern that I can use to come up with a treatment plan.

I am here as I feel I may have missed something in Jacks teachings or maybe someone can see something that I do not.

**_General symptoms:_**

A. Energy control. I can switch between being able to perform fairily athletic abilities (vaulting, climbing, martial arts high kicks), to being out of breath when walking up stairs. This switch can be intra-daily. Although I must caveat I have never had any stamina for med-high intensity aerobic activities since I was younger. However my ability to tolerate exercise has dimished massively in the past 10 years.

B. Solar Plexus pain, distention & Mood swings & Brain Fog. It feels like a tension, that when palpitated is most sore in the midway between my back and front. This pain affects my ability to breathe from the dan tien \[belly\] as it feels like energy is unable to flow up or down past this point, as if something is swollen and prevents my diaphragm from moving fully. When this hits above a 4/10 in severity it starts to affect mental processing & my mood quite severely. My thought process becomes like wading through mud and anger and frustration present themselves. At an 8/10 I will start exhibiting severe mental health issues. Sometimes this is accompanied by blaoting and gas. Stress is the trigger here

C. Pain / Inflammation / tightness of hips, legs and feet or thoracic spine and shoulders. I do have congenital scoliosis and had a severe head trauma at the age of 16 months (falling from 2nd story window onto my skull and fracturing it). Sometimes the leg / foot pain is crippling. General tightness in the front myofascial line i.e. when arching spine.

D. Continuously swollen glands. I cannot remember a time when they weren’t swollen.

E. Stool variance. Colour and consistency vary without pattern. More vegtable fibre generally improves consistency. Wiping is more yellow than brown (every time). Sometimes greasy.

F. Muscular weakness. Comes and goes very quickly.

G. Athletes foot / Tinea Scalp. I am barefoot most of the time, I would concur that when I wear shoes from more than a day it gets worse. I have thick hair so maybe the

H. Inability to gain weight.

I. Lack of consistently deep sleep. A lot of movement in the night. But I consistently dream most nights.

J. Libido very inconsistent and not tied to energy levels. E.g. Can have low energy and high libido and have large ejaculate size.

K. Loss of urine control when urinating (intermittent).

**_Symptoms when in a longer term shit cycle:_**

-   Severe loss of weight. From 62 to 55kg in a week or two. Max weight ever was 65KG. Height is 173Cm.
-   Suicidal tendencies.
-   Side of head pressure headaches.
-   Tinnitus
-   Swollen hands and face with reynauds (used to be only winter, but now any time).
-   Hands break out in blisters (which taste of iron), then are itchy and crack and bleed.
-   Extreme exhaustion, needing to sleep up 18 hours per day.
-   Amnesia
-   Dizziness
-   Short of breath (Hypoxic)
-   Tremors
-   Piles
-   Mild Jaundice (bilirubin levels always within ref).
-   Pins and Needles
-   Tremors
-   Heart palpitations
-   Jaw pain

**Background Context

_Home Environment:_**

0.0010 mw/m2 or lower \[RF\]
0.06 uT or lower \[Mag\]
3.75 \[Elec\]
400 PPM CO2
30 to 50 RH
0.006 or lower TVOC
9 PM2.5
11 PM10

**_AM Routine \[when working\]_**

-   Up at sunrise or just before.
-   Meditate 10-30 mins
-   Qi Gong / general movement / 40 min walk
-   Breakfast
-   Work outside on laptop \[I run a semi-automated UK business from Spain\]
-   Strength training if energy allows.

**_RX’s that I implement regularly (at least 80% of the time):_**

1.  Sungazing at sunrise and sunset.
2.  Sunbathing through the year, with 90% of clothing removed. No skin burn.
3.  Water delivered from the best local spring (approx 100km away).
4.  No Phones near body without being on airplane mode.
5.  Eating food seasonally and as locally as possible. Try and make the last meal of the day the smallest
6.  In winter regular Redlight sessions (x2 per week), sauna x 1 per week

Whilst all these protocols helped, I still had issues that would appear cyclically. So I continued to search, I’ve seen many, FM docs, natropaths, healers of varying types. Some worked for a while, but none of the treatments held for long. Things that have helped:

A. Regular meditation. Various techniques learned. From vipassana to dynamic meditation (living in every moment with more awareness).

B. Fighting Monkey (western style Qi Gong), has been very helpful in regaining child like mobility. [https://fightingmonkey.net/](https://fightingmonkey.net/)

C. Plant medicines & therapy (helped to heal past traumas and some unconscious behaviours)

D. Tuning forks.

**_Allopathic Tests (over the past 4 years) with some clinical significance._**

+Using SNPs to find that I have \[very mild\] Hemachromatosis. Initial ferritin was over 200, after 2 sessions (approx 400ml of blood letting)

It was back to around 50, and maintenance is needed once a year (sometimes longer). I am of the belief that environment trumps SNPS, so have put less focus on this. See attached for the profiles. Clearly challenged in serotonin and dopamine focused SNPS however.

+Very slowly declining pattern of insulin and peptide C and cyclically high Amylase and lipase. Although HB1AC is stabilised at 5.6 (pre pre-diabetic ref levels). Having never tested this at young age could I have always had low insulin? I did mange to get this down in winter in 2021 with Red Light Therapy 30cm/w2 for max 3 mins.

+MRI’s for Iron accumulation = clear.

+MRI’s liver, pancreas, and ducts for any sinister growth = clear

+Ultrasound of renal and gallbladder = clear

+Endoscopy & Colonoscopy = clear

+H Pylori = Clear

**_Past History_**

-   Jaundice at birth
-   Fractured skull at 18 months
-   Undiagnosed stomach ulcer from 12 to 23 years old. Fix was 3 days of antibiotics (such relief post treatment)
-   Asthma and Eczema as child.
-   Hernia (left inguinal)

**Triggers**

\+ Grass pollen season. In Spain it’s earlier than the UK. This always leaves me a state of exhaustion and puts me in the “shit” cycle.
\+ High levels of acute stress. Although I am MUCH better equipped to deal with this now.
\+ High food intake with little movement.
\+ Too much alcohol. TBH I am find alcohol less and less appealing as I age.

**Other Observations**

Vit D OH25 - is around 60-70 in summer and 30-40 in winter.

120 Min insulin curve was fine even with low insulin and pep C.

Butyeko course - I took on a Butyeko tutor and tried for 2 months, and improved my control pause. And then a shit cycle kicked in and my scores went back down and I lost the motivation to dedicate 1.5 hours a day to it. CP is around 20.

MDMA - After a dose I have noticed a decrease in the solar plexus pain, more focus and more stability in general, which has led to the belief that I may well be producing too much serotonin. However due to MDMA’s neurotoxicity this isn’t a long term solution. Other non selective agonists I have tried don’t have the same long lasting effect. Although I am yet to try micro-dosing of LSD25.

Cannot tolerate high fat meals especially. Lamb fat.

Frozen (3 week) raw organic calf liver smoothies. These always had a positive effect on me until this year.

Hair and nails grow very quickly compared to others in the family.

Methylene Blue makes me anxious and edgy.

Aspirin & Coffee - I know this isn’t a long term solution due to a-MSH down regulation, but it combined with microdosing of coffee (cannot handle more than half a cup of Arabica, due to it being highly stimulating for me) can give me some temporary relief.

Supplements - I use various minerals but not daily. I have tried most things with varying degrees of success, I don’t feel anything exogenous should be used for long periods.

Pharmacuticals - I haven't used them in years.

**Proposed next course of action**

A. Look into VDR Fok1 (+/+, GG) and test 1,25-hydroxy D. Could this be why as a rule I'm worse in summer?

B. High dose creatine. I have found a few anecdotal cases of people who struggled to put on weight, and had wild energy variance that have used creatine with some success.

C. Moving to the Spanish coast. Although a higher pop density, this would give me the ability to live at sea level and spend most of my time at or nr the beach.

D. Moving to low density equatorial zone to allow things to heal, before returning back to Spain. How long is enough? Where is best?

Hi All,

On the back of this I have contacted Building Biology society for a recommended electrical engineer who can look at:

1\. Non zero ground. L-E is 30v not 240v as expected.
2\. Non shielded wires in false ceilings above 2 rooms, causing high BV for those above or below.

Testing performed with Cornet ED88TPlus 2 (inc some 5G bands), multimeter, Trifeild & Stetzer

2024-08-17 18:41 RF 0.0010 Bed front plane mW/m2
2024-08-17 18:42 RF 0.0040 Bed right plane mW/m2

2024-08-17 18:42 RF 0.0018 Bed back plane mW/m2

Despite the odd ground issues and bad wiring (house needs this correcting for all the family), as you can see the readings are pretty low. It could be that the wiring is creating some other form of EMF that is beyond my basics skills to detect.

> 2024-08-17 18:48 Dirty Elec 45 GS

>
> However, I posit that as my ferritin goes up so does my sensitivity to nnEMF.

Readings all look good except the one that matters most- your bed! IF that is 1500mV the BBI says above 1000mV at night - extreme concern. Try turning off the breakers for the room and see if you can get that down at least below 100mV while you sleep.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-08-20T22:16:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-rx%E2%80%99s-don%E2%80%99t-reverse-your-condition-next-steps-you-missed-the-nnemf-story.29315/post-338722>

What is the “Comet” do you mean “Cornet”?
The body voltage meter measures electric fiends that have been induced into the body which acts as an antenna and by giving them a path of least resistance to ground. It does NOT measure RF. Electric fields in the home (60Hz / 50Hz) radiate out 6ft - 8ft from the source. The S&SP2 measures RF. You can pick up, measure RF from miles away. Electric fields are basically steady whereas RF comes in waves, or a better analogy in bullets. This is where sample rates come in. I believe the TF2 is missing many/most of the bullets because with 4G/5G they are too fast for the sensor. I believe the TF2 would be great in El Salvador where, it is my understanding, there is only 3G. The electric field measurement on the TF2 and Cornet meters is a “spot” reading. Great for outside under power lines etc but not accurate enough for bedroom readings.

### rtd — 2024-08-20T22:19:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-rx%E2%80%99s-don%E2%80%99t-reverse-your-condition-next-steps-you-missed-the-nnemf-story.29315/post-338723>

> [dantothep said:](/goto/post?id=338679)
>
> How can one be sure that S&SP2 isn't giving false positives?
>
> Is there a way of a calibration testing?

Try finding a calibration lab? that could cost some $$. When I worked in automated testing, devices that took measurements- DMMs, frequency counters, etc- had a calibration schedule. Any test results captured outside the calibration date were considered null and void. There is an entire industry set around instrument calibration.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-31T16:44:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-rx%E2%80%99s-don%E2%80%99t-reverse-your-condition-next-steps-you-missed-the-nnemf-story.29315/post-339103>

> [dantothep said:](/goto/post?id=338490)
>
> “This means all diseases that are tied to the loss of electrons are COMPLETELY REVERSIBLE.” - Jack Redox RX
>
> I thought I would write as much detail as possible in the hope that this may trigger some ideas in this learned community. I firmly believe the quote from Jack above & I know I can reverse this rather than simply manage it.
>
> I haven’t been here for a while. I started following the protocols in 2015, they really got me out of a nightmarish hole that I found myself in whilst living and working in London for 15 years.
>
> In case anyone views this post as negative; it is in no way. Without finding Jacks work there is no doubt in my mind that I would either be very seriously ill, sectioned or dead had I tried to work through fixing my issues (severe amnesia, energy collapses, mental health issues) in the same environment that made me sick.
>
> I moved to southern Spain in 2016 after Jack’s advice, and my life significantly improved as a result. I moved for the 300+ days of clear sky and sun per year, the access to the beaches, the nature, the change of pace of life. I currently live about 400m above sea level, in a very low EMF house (tested regularly) surrounded by a few neighbours and nature mostly. I have had the least financial and work stress in the past 2 years than ever before.
>
> **Symptomatology**
>
> This is a quagmire of cycles with no consistent pattern \[apart from the seasonal one - more on that later\]. I have periods of good health, characterised by high energy, high libido, clear focused thoughts and ideation, great memory recall, easy breaths strong connection to source through meditation etc. I know what it is like to feel VITAL. I still get some symptoms in this state, and these can come on very quickly and sometimes leave just as quickly.
>
> My issue is a lack of consistency with energy, inflammation & mood. The severity depends on where my redox is at that point in time. This can shift hourly, daily, or weekly. I have had some years with very few flare cycles. Currently this has been the worst year since 2014 when I was living in London. The “shit” cycle started in grass pollen season (late April), and I have only had 1 or 2 weeks of normal cycle since then. Its very hard for me to see a high level pattern that I can use to come up with a treatment plan.
>
> I am here as I feel I may have missed something in Jacks teachings or maybe someone can see something that I do not.
>
> **_General symptoms:_**
>
> A. Energy control. I can switch between being able to perform fairily athletic abilities (vaulting, climbing, martial arts high kicks), to being out of breath when walking up stairs. This switch can be intra-daily. Although I must caveat I have never had any stamina for med-high intensity aerobic activities since I was younger. However my ability to tolerate exercise has dimished massively in the past 10 years.
>
> B. Solar Plexus pain, distention & Mood swings & Brain Fog. It feels like a tension, that when palpitated is most sore in the midway between my back and front. This pain affects my ability to breathe from the dan tien \[belly\] as it feels like energy is unable to flow up or down past this point, as if something is swollen and prevents my diaphragm from moving fully. When this hits above a 4/10 in severity it starts to affect mental processing & my mood quite severely. My thought process becomes like wading through mud and anger and frustration present themselves. At an 8/10 I will start exhibiting severe mental health issues. Sometimes this is accompanied by blaoting and gas. Stress is the trigger here
>
> C. Pain / Inflammation / tightness of hips, legs and feet or thoracic spine and shoulders. I do have congenital scoliosis and had a severe head trauma at the age of 16 months (falling from 2nd story window onto my skull and fracturing it). Sometimes the leg / foot pain is crippling. General tightness in the front myofascial line i.e. when arching spine.
>
> D. Continuously swollen glands. I cannot remember a time when they weren’t swollen.
>
> E. Stool variance. Colour and consistency vary without pattern. More vegtable fibre generally improves consistency. Wiping is more yellow than brown (every time). Sometimes greasy.
>
> F. Muscular weakness. Comes and goes very quickly.
>
> G. Athletes foot / Tinea Scalp. I am barefoot most of the time, I would concur that when I wear shoes from more than a day it gets worse. I have thick hair so maybe the
>
> H. Inability to gain weight.
>
> I. Lack of consistently deep sleep. A lot of movement in the night. But I consistently dream most nights.
>
> J. Libido very inconsistent and not tied to energy levels. E.g. Can have low energy and high libido and have large ejaculate size.
>
> K. Loss of urine control when urinating (intermittent).
>
> **_Symptoms when in a longer term shit cycle:_**
>
>
> -   Severe loss of weight. From 62 to 55kg in a week or two. Max weight ever was 65KG. Height is 173Cm.
> -   Suicidal tendencies.
> -   Side of head pressure headaches.
> -   Tinnitus
> -   Swollen hands and face with reynauds (used to be only winter, but now any time).
> -   Hands break out in blisters (which taste of iron), then are itchy and crack and bleed.
> -   Extreme exhaustion, needing to sleep up 18 hours per day.
> -   Amnesia
> -   Dizziness
> -   Short of breath (Hypoxic)
> -   Tremors
> -   Piles
> -   Mild Jaundice (bilirubin levels always within ref).
> -   Pins and Needles
> -   Tremors
> -   Heart palpitations
> -   Jaw pain
>
> **Background Context
>
> _Home Environment:_**
>
> 0.0010 mw/m2 or lower \[RF\]
> 0.06 uT or lower \[Mag\]
> 3.75 \[Elec\]
> 400 PPM CO2
> 30 to 50 RH
> 0.006 or lower TVOC
> 9 PM2.5
> 11 PM10
>
> **_AM Routine \[when working\]_**
>
> -   Up at sunrise or just before.
> -   Meditate 10-30 mins
> -   Qi Gong / general movement / 40 min walk
> -   Breakfast
> -   Work outside on laptop \[I run a semi-automated UK business from Spain\]
> -   Strength training if energy allows.
>
> **_RX’s that I implement regularly (at least 80% of the time):_**
>
> 1.  Sungazing at sunrise and sunset.
> 2.  Sunbathing through the year, with 90% of clothing removed. No skin burn.
> 3.  Water delivered from the best local spring (approx 100km away).
> 4.  No Phones near body without being on airplane mode.
> 5.  Eating food seasonally and as locally as possible. Try and make the last meal of the day the smallest
> 6.  In winter regular Redlight sessions (x2 per week), sauna x 1 per week
>
>
> Whilst all these protocols helped, I still had issues that would appear cyclically. So I continued to search, I’ve seen many, FM docs, natropaths, healers of varying types. Some worked for a while, but none of the treatments held for long. Things that have helped:
>
> A. Regular meditation. Various techniques learned. From vipassana to dynamic meditation (living in every moment with more awareness).
>
> B. Fighting Monkey (western style Qi Gong), has been very helpful in regaining child like mobility. [https://fightingmonkey.net/](https://fightingmonkey.net/)
>
> C. Plant medicines & therapy (helped to heal past traumas and some unconscious behaviours)
>
> D. Tuning forks.
>
>
> **_Allopathic Tests (over the past 4 years) with some clinical significance._**
>
> +Using SNPs to find that I have \[very mild\] Hemachromatosis. Initial ferritin was over 200, after 2 sessions (approx 400ml of blood letting)
>
> It was back to around 50, and maintenance is needed once a year (sometimes longer). I am of the belief that environment trumps SNPS, so have put less focus on this. See attached for the profiles. Clearly challenged in serotonin and dopamine focused SNPS however.
>
> +Very slowly declining pattern of insulin and peptide C and cyclically high Amylase and lipase. Although HB1AC is stabilised at 5.6 (pre pre-diabetic ref levels). Having never tested this at young age could I have always had low insulin? I did mange to get this down in winter in 2021 with Red Light Therapy 30cm/w2 for max 3 mins.
>
> +MRI’s for Iron accumulation = clear.
>
> +MRI’s liver, pancreas, and ducts for any sinister growth = clear
>
> +Ultrasound of renal and gallbladder = clear
>
> +Endoscopy & Colonoscopy = clear
>
> +H Pylori = Clear
>
> **_Past History_**
>
> -   Jaundice at birth
> -   Fractured skull at 18 months
> -   Undiagnosed stomach ulcer from 12 to 23 years old. Fix was 3 days of antibiotics (such relief post treatment)
> -   Asthma and Eczema as child.
> -   Hernia (left inguinal)
>
> **Triggers**
>
> \+ Grass pollen season. In Spain it’s earlier than the UK. This always leaves me a state of exhaustion and puts me in the “shit” cycle.
> \+ High levels of acute stress. Although I am MUCH better equipped to deal with this now.
> \+ High food intake with little movement.
> \+ Too much alcohol. TBH I am find alcohol less and less appealing as I age.
>
> **Other Observations**
>
> Vit D OH25 - is around 60-70 in summer and 30-40 in winter.
>
> 120 Min insulin curve was fine even with low insulin and pep C.
>
> Butyeko course - I took on a Butyeko tutor and tried for 2 months, and improved my control pause. And then a shit cycle kicked in and my scores went back down and I lost the motivation to dedicate 1.5 hours a day to it. CP is around 20.
>
> MDMA - After a dose I have noticed a decrease in the solar plexus pain, more focus and more stability in general, which has led to the belief that I may well be producing too much serotonin. However due to MDMA’s neurotoxicity this isn’t a long term solution. Other non selective agonists I have tried don’t have the same long lasting effect. Although I am yet to try micro-dosing of LSD25.
>
> Cannot tolerate high fat meals especially. Lamb fat.
>
> Frozen (3 week) raw organic calf liver smoothies. These always had a positive effect on me until this year.
>
> Hair and nails grow very quickly compared to others in the family.
>
> Methylene Blue makes me anxious and edgy.
>
> Aspirin & Coffee - I know this isn’t a long term solution due to a-MSH down regulation, but it combined with microdosing of coffee (cannot handle more than half a cup of Arabica, due to it being highly stimulating for me) can give me some temporary relief.
>
> Supplements - I use various minerals but not daily. I have tried most things with varying degrees of success, I don’t feel anything exogenous should be used for long periods.
>
> Pharmacuticals - I haven't used them in years.
>
>
> **Proposed next course of action**
>
> A. Look into VDR Fok1 (+/+, GG) and test 1,25-hydroxy D. Could this be why as a rule I'm worse in summer?
>
> B. High dose creatine. I have found a few anecdotal cases of people who struggled to put on weight, and had wild energy variance that have used creatine with some success.
>
> C. Moving to the Spanish coast. Although a higher pop density, this would give me the ability to live at sea level and spend most of my time at or nr the beach.
>
> D. Moving to low density equatorial zone to allow things to heal, before returning back to Spain. How long is enough? Where is best?

All planets with life will eventually be found to have massive ambipolar or polarization fields associated with them. Why? It tells you the planet is loaded with electrons in the ionosphere that surrounds the rock below. This global electric platform is the key to understanding why all electric currents precede basic chemistry and biochemistry and why LIGHT > FOOD at all levels of understanding. The food guru narratives continue to implode as new data comes in from space. This ambipolar electric field on Earth is generated exclusively by the outward pressure of ionospheric electrons. Most of those electrons are coming from oxygen radicals.

The ambipolar field of Earth controls the structure of the polar ionosphere, where aurora manifest, boosting the scale height by 271% based on new data generated by NASA's suborbital Endurance rocket. NASA has inferred that this increases the supply of cold O+ ions to the magnetosphere by more than 3,800%! This is a big deal because it gives us a clue why this blog is deadly acccurate in its science. This now fully explains why other mechanisms such as wave–particle interactions can heat and further accelerate them to escape velocity. The electrostatic field of Earth is strong enough by itself to drive the polar wind by itself and is probably the origin of the cold H+ ion population that dominates much of the magnetosphere that I talked about in the Kruse for Dummies lecture on H+ and D. ### dantothep — 2024-10-23T15:59:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-rx%E2%80%99s-don%E2%80%99t-reverse-your-condition-next-steps-you-missed-the-nnemf-story.29315/post-341428>

So, I got a full Building Biology test from a great assessor based out of Barcelona.
Biggest learningthanks [@Saichi](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/23447/) & Antony for the heads up that the Cornet TF2 is a DANGER by putting people in a sense of false security. It was accurate as a Gauss meter but not for anything else. ELF's were missed completely, RF's were under measured.
**High level results (using BuildBioGuidelines-2015-englisch ratings)**
ELF = severe anomaly in some rooms
RF = severe anomaly in some rooms
BV = extreme anomaly
Magnetic fields = Safe
Ground Potential of the electrical system = 74ohms. Should be below 4.
Glyphosate and Cypermethrin = extreme anomaly
\----
So all in all, environmental pollutants cause disease for me once again! Now to try and find somewhere safe to move!
Wish me luck.
\----
How's ES for agrichemicals?

>
> A. Energy control. I can switch between being able to perform fairily athletic abilities (vaulting, climbing, martial arts high kicks), to being out of breath when walking up stairs. This switch can be intra-daily. Although I must caveat I have never had any stamina for med-high intensity aerobic activities since I was younger. However my ability to tolerate exercise has dimished massively in the past 10 years.
>
> B. Solar Plexus pain, distention & Mood swings & Brain Fog. It feels like a tension, that when palpitated is most sore in the midway between my back and front. This pain affects my ability to breathe from the dan tien \[belly\] as it feels like energy is unable to flow up or down past this point, as if something is swollen and prevents my diaphragm from moving fully. When this hits above a 4/10 in severity it starts to affect mental processing & my mood quite severely. My thought process becomes like wading through mud and anger and frustration present themselves. At an 8/10 I will start exhibiting severe mental health issues. Sometimes this is accompanied by blaoting and gas. Stress is the trigger here
>
> C. Pain / Inflammation / tightness of hips, legs and feet or thoracic spine and shoulders. I do have congenital scoliosis and had a severe head trauma at the age of 16 months (falling from 2nd story window onto my skull and fracturing it). Sometimes the leg / foot pain is crippling. General tightness in the front myofascial line i.e. when arching spine.
>
> D. Continuously swollen glands. I cannot remember a time when they weren’t swollen.
>
> E. Stool variance. Colour and consistency vary without pattern. More vegtable fibre generally improves consistency. Wiping is more yellow than brown (every time). Sometimes greasy.
>
> F. Muscular weakness. Comes and goes very quickly.
>
> G. Athletes foot / Tinea Scalp. I am barefoot most of the time, I would concur that when I wear shoes from more than a day it gets worse. I have thick hair so maybe the
>
> H. Inability to gain weight.
>
> I. Lack of consistently deep sleep. A lot of movement in the night. But I consistently dream most nights.
>
> J. Libido very inconsistent and not tied to energy levels. E.g. Can have low energy and high libido and have large ejaculate size.
>
> K. Loss of urine control when urinating (intermittent).
>
> **_Symptoms when in a longer term shit cycle:_**
>
>
> -   Severe loss of weight. From 62 to 55kg in a week or two. Max weight ever was 65KG. Height is 173Cm.
> -   Suicidal tendencies.
> -   Side of head pressure headaches.
> -   Tinnitus
> -   Swollen hands and face with reynauds (used to be only winter, but now any time).
> -   Hands break out in blisters (which taste of iron), then are itchy and crack and bleed.
> -   Extreme exhaustion, needing to sleep up 18 hours per day.
> -   Amnesia
> -   Dizziness
> -   Short of breath (Hypoxic)
> -   Tremors
> -   Piles
> -   Mild Jaundice (bilirubin levels always within ref).
> -   Pins and Needles
> -   Tremors
> -   Heart palpitations
> -   Jaw pain
>
> **Background Context
>
> _Home Environment:_**
>
> 0.0010 mw/m2 or lower \[RF\]
> 0.06 uT or lower \[Mag\]
> 3.75 \[Elec\]
> 400 PPM CO2
> 30 to 50 RH
> 0.006 or lower TVOC
> 9 PM2.5
> 11 PM10
>
> **_AM Routine \[when working\]_**
>
> -   Up at sunrise or just before.
> -   Meditate 10-30 mins
> -   Qi Gong / general movement / 40 min walk
> -   Breakfast
> -   Work outside on laptop \[I run a semi-automated UK business from Spain\]
> -   Strength training if energy allows.
>
> **_RX’s that I implement regularly (at least 80% of the time):_**
>
> 1.  Sungazing at sunrise and sunset.
> 2.  Sunbathing through the year, with 90% of clothing removed. No skin burn.
> 3.  Water delivered from the best local spring (approx 100km away).
> 4.  No Phones near body without being on airplane mode.
> 5.  Eating food seasonally and as locally as possible. Try and make the last meal of the day the smallest
> 6.  In winter regular Redlight sessions (x2 per week), sauna x 1 per week
>
>
> Whilst all these protocols helped, I still had issues that would appear cyclically. So I continued to search, I’ve seen many, FM docs, natropaths, healers of varying types. Some worked for a while, but none of the treatments held for long. Things that have helped:
>
> A. Regular meditation. Various techniques learned. From vipassana to dynamic meditation (living in every moment with more awareness).
>
> B. Fighting Monkey (western style Qi Gong), has been very helpful in regaining child like mobility. [https://fightingmonkey.net/](https://fightingmonkey.net/)
>
> C. Plant medicines & therapy (helped to heal past traumas and some unconscious behaviours)
>
> D. Tuning forks.
>
>
> **_Allopathic Tests (over the past 4 years) with some clinical significance._**
>
> +Using SNPs to find that I have \[very mild\] Hemachromatosis. Initial ferritin was over 200, after 2 sessions (approx 400ml of blood letting)
>
> It was back to around 50, and maintenance is needed once a year (sometimes longer). I am of the belief that environment trumps SNPS, so have put less focus on this. See attached for the profiles. Clearly challenged in serotonin and dopamine focused SNPS however.
>
> +Very slowly declining pattern of insulin and peptide C and cyclically high Amylase and lipase. Although HB1AC is stabilised at 5.6 (pre pre-diabetic ref levels). Having never tested this at young age could I have always had low insulin? I did mange to get this down in winter in 2021 with Red Light Therapy 30cm/w2 for max 3 mins.
>
> +MRI’s for Iron accumulation = clear.
>
> +MRI’s liver, pancreas, and ducts for any sinister growth = clear
>
> +Ultrasound of renal and gallbladder = clear
>
> +Endoscopy & Colonoscopy = clear
>
> +H Pylori = Clear
>
> **_Past History_**
>
> -   Jaundice at birth
> -   Fractured skull at 18 months
> -   Undiagnosed stomach ulcer from 12 to 23 years old. Fix was 3 days of antibiotics (such relief post treatment)
> -   Asthma and Eczema as child.
> -   Hernia (left inguinal)
>
> **Triggers**
>
> \+ Grass pollen season. In Spain it’s earlier than the UK. This always leaves me a state of exhaustion and puts me in the “shit” cycle.
> \+ High levels of acute stress. Although I am MUCH better equipped to deal with this now.
> \+ High food intake with little movement.
> \+ Too much alcohol. TBH I am find alcohol less and less appealing as I age.
>
> **Other Observations**
>
> Vit D OH25 - is around 60-70 in summer and 30-40 in winter.
>
> 120 Min insulin curve was fine even with low insulin and pep C.
>
> Butyeko course - I took on a Butyeko tutor and tried for 2 months, and improved my control pause. And then a shit cycle kicked in and my scores went back down and I lost the motivation to dedicate 1.5 hours a day to it. CP is around 20.
>
> MDMA - After a dose I have noticed a decrease in the solar plexus pain, more focus and more stability in general, which has led to the belief that I may well be producing too much serotonin. However due to MDMA’s neurotoxicity this isn’t a long term solution. Other non selective agonists I have tried don’t have the same long lasting effect. Although I am yet to try micro-dosing of LSD25.
>
> Cannot tolerate high fat meals especially. Lamb fat.
>
> Frozen (3 week) raw organic calf liver smoothies. These always had a positive effect on me until this year.
>
> Hair and nails grow very quickly compared to others in the family.
>
> Methylene Blue makes me anxious and edgy.
>
> Aspirin & Coffee - I know this isn’t a long term solution due to a-MSH down regulation, but it combined with microdosing of coffee (cannot handle more than half a cup of Arabica, due to it being highly stimulating for me) can give me some temporary relief.
>
> Supplements - I use various minerals but not daily. I have tried most things with varying degrees of success, I don’t feel anything exogenous should be used for long periods.
>
> Pharmacuticals - I haven't used them in years.
>
>
> **Proposed next course of action**
>
> A. Look into VDR Fok1 (+/+, GG) and test 1,25-hydroxy D. Could this be why as a rule I'm worse in summer?
>
> B. High dose creatine. I have found a few anecdotal cases of people who struggled to put on weight, and had wild energy variance that have used creatine with some success.
>
> C. Moving to the Spanish coast. Although a higher pop density, this would give me the ability to live at sea level and spend most of my time at or nr the beach.
>
> D. Moving to low density equatorial zone to allow things to heal, before returning back to Spain. How long is enough? Where is best?

Reading through this I was noticing a lot of similarities. When I got to the part about MDMA and LSD I just wanted to let you know that I don’t think this will help and might mess up your brain even further. Let’s just say I was a guinea pig for a “guru” who said it would heal the synapses in my brain along with other substances used for “healing”. Let’s just say it was a learning experience. With zero help and a shit ton of gaslighting from centralized medicine, I was desperate at the time. It helped temporarily with pain etc but the rebound was hell and my overall health took a serious nose dive. Possibly microdose would be ok but with a history of head injury, I think you are better off following Dr Jack’s advice.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-10-23T18:39:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-rx%E2%80%99s-don%E2%80%99t-reverse-your-condition-next-steps-you-missed-the-nnemf-story.29315/post-341437>

### andrea39 — 2024-10-23T19:01:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-rx%E2%80%99s-don%E2%80%99t-reverse-your-condition-next-steps-you-missed-the-nnemf-story.29315/post-341439>

I'm wondering if you are having difficulty digesting fats. Your stool color variance is one indicator. Yellowish or pale colored stools might indicate low levels of bile being released from your gall bladder. Adequate amounts of bile are necessary for full fat digestion. Greasy stools is another sign that much of the ingested fat is passing through (and out of) your digestive system instead of being digested and absorbed. Have you had any stool analyses done that evaluate your ability to digest fats, and all your foods? An inability to gain weight is another indicator that you are not properly digesting and absorbing your food - proteins and carbohydrate digestion might also be incomplete. Of course, bloating and gas would be common with this scenario.

I also wonder if you have an imbalance of your gut microflora - I wouldn't be surprised if you have an overgrowth of yeast or fungus, given your other symptoms of fungal overgrowth on skin and scalp.

TBI's make one more susceptible to harmful EMF's, as your blood-brain barrier has already been compromised. I met a lady at Environmental Health Center-Dallas (EHC-D) who's disabled from EHS, and she told me to buy the book "EMF Freedom," which goes into detail about all the symptoms of EHS. I stay fatigued all the time, which she said is because of EMF exposure. I'm taking a cruise next week to Mexico in order to get my redox up, but I'm afraid it won't help. I've been reading forum entries of other people who have altogether moved or taken month-long sabbaticals to places closer to the equator, but unfortunately they're still suffering. Is improved redox the only thing that's gonna help me? I'm just so tired and desperate to get better. The Center has recommended chelation IV and hyperbaric oxygen therapy. They also had me do skin testing so they can formulate neurotransmitter antigens I'll have to self-administer intravenously. Microwave poisoning is insidious; these bad frequencies are all around us. I'm not religious, but I'm calling it "spiritual warfare" because you're being attacked by something you can't see. I hope you get better. I'm not giving up, so don't you, either.

### SanderC — 2024-08-16T15:46:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-chronotype-genetics-vs-epigenetics.29316/post-338493>

Hi everyone, new member here - I've been diving deep into the Rabbit Hole of uncle Jack in the last 6 months and can't believe I only just got on this forum!

I live in Belgium and there's no light stability here.

In summer, sunrise happens between 5 - 6.30 am and often it's an uphill battle for me to rise early and feel energized in the morning, even while following most of Jack's recommendations. I've always been a night owl, and if I can believe genetic reports (23andme, DNAfit), I have the genes too. (both my parents are night owls)

I scoured the internet and I haven't seen uncle Jack saying anything about the effect of genetics on sleep chronotype / timing.

Wondering if you can help me scratch my curiosity! Maybe I should just move to the Canary Islands

Thank you, Sander

### drsongihan — 2024-08-16T19:27:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-good-podcast-by-someone-telling-the-same-story-about-the-world-covid-and-human-behavior-as-jack.29317/post-338503>

I came across a podcast by someone telling the same story about the world, covid and human behavior as Jack Kruse. I thought it was nice to hear the same narrative from a different person, expertise, voice ... but strikingly similar in terms of thinking.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-08-16T21:21:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-good-podcast-by-someone-telling-the-same-story-about-the-world-covid-and-human-behavior-as-jack.29317/post-338515>

### drsongihan — 2024-08-18T21:16:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-good-podcast-by-someone-telling-the-same-story-about-the-world-covid-and-human-behavior-as-jack.29317/post-338592>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=338515)
>
> I have listened to Dr Bret Weinstein over the years. Always interesting.

I think in this forum many of us have the experience of listening to people and knowing intuitively after 1 podcast whether that person is seeking the truth or driving an agenda. The truth seeker can be wrong, in fact very wrong, but it does not matter because the truth seeker will keep renovating, revising and unlearning and relearning until he/she gets it right. Listening to Dr. Weinstein it was clear to me that he is a truth seeker, and that means we will always learn something from truth seekers. He did not quite get to the sunshine yet, but mentioned sun and outdoor enough, but his thinking is glass clear. I want to surround myself with people who are willing to turn over every stone, are willing to pause and think, and say "wait a moment" and are willing to loose. It is so much fun and liberating to follow that compass.

But the irony is not lost on me that I am now suddenly more aligned with folks and groups that are considered conspiracy theorists, mindless antivaccers, presumably racists or anti-feminists (of course this is not true, just a dumb narrative), and pseudo scientists!

The CIA came up with the term “Conspiracy Theorist“ as a put down, dismissive, belittling against anyone speaking out against the established view.
With COVID the “conspiracy theorists” have turned out to be the truth tellers. The term is now a badge on honor for free thinking, anti establishment, truth seekers/tellers.

There are others who also do not belong to the "intellectual dark web" that are also worth listening...

For example, George Galloway and the Mother of all Talk Shows... , more focused on politics/geopolitics.. The Duran or Scott Ritter and more..

All truth seekers **got biases** but we need to listen to all the sides to form an opinion that will probably lead us to other people with different opinions, more rabbit holes.. They also do not care about what is talked about in this forum, but there is also life outside what is talked about the subjects in here.

And of course follow the money.. Mostly everyone is getting paid by someone or have vested interests on specific outcomes.. People are getting paid in shekels, dollars, rubbles, yuan, bitcoin (no pun intended)… This is the most difficult task to check... Mostly we can infer it from the message they convey.

So the bottom line is that the theatre of operation has always been our minds.. There are a lot of really smart people out there that got amazing education, eloquence, erudition and that more importantly FREE TIME that can weave amazing stories that can put us in awe. So it is really really hard for us to separate the wheat from the chaff, so as i said above we need to look deep into the message and follow the money as a rule of thumb and god or whatever is out there help us...

### drsongihan — 2024-08-18T23:41:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-good-podcast-by-someone-telling-the-same-story-about-the-world-covid-and-human-behavior-as-jack.29317/post-338606>

> [Michalis said:](/goto/post?id=338599)
>
> There are others who also do not belong to the "intellectual dark web" that are also worth listening...
>
> For example, George Galloway and the Mother of all Talk Shows... , more focused on politics/geopolitics.. The Duran or Scott Ritter and more..
>
> All truth seekers **got biases** but we need to listen to all the sides to form an opinion that will probably lead us to other people with different opinions, more rabbit holes.. They also do not care about what is talked about in this forum, but there is also life outside what is talked about the subjects in here.
>
> And of course follow the money.. Mostly everyone is getting paid by someone or have vested interests on specific outcomes.. People are getting paid in shekels, dollars, rubbles, yuan, bitcoin (no pun intended)… This is the most difficult task to check... Mostly we can infer it from the message they convey.
>
> So the bottom line is that the theatre of operation has always been our minds.. There are a lot of really smart people out there that got amazing education, eloquence, erudition and that more importantly FREE TIME that can weave amazing stories that can put us in awe. So it is really really hard for us to separate the wheat from the chaff, so as i said above we need to look deep into the message and follow the money as a rule of thumb and god or whatever is out there help us...

Agreed in essence but I need to contest for a reason. Let me know whether it makes sense after reading my post. I purposefully said "truth seeker" and not intellectual dark web.

I found truth seekers everywhere. Deep within the establishment and outside the establishment, some ready to jump out, some operating in the mids of the establishment but raising questions and some of course charting a new movement. Some are just "thinking" and some are acting, and I found both contributions add value, even if somebody is not acting yet. I myself am half thinking and half acting, and certainly have not jumped out yet in full action. But critically, I found astonishingly many more people (maybe 80%?) who are clouded or even brainwashed by a centralized propaganda. I too was. But at the moment one is faced with a good question, we have a duty to pause and think. Many are unwilling to pause and even look into information in front of their nose. I find that astonishing. Yes, we should listen to all sort of people, but I am not differentiating between factions. I mentioned the names by which some of these people are called just to point out the hypocrisy. There are as many corrupt and brainwashed thinking in any faction, for sure.

Rather, I find truth seeking to be more of a mindset and a lifestyle that has to fulfill certain conditions, and is more of an individual compass that each of us have to follow. Certainly, follow the money is one way to differentiate. But there are many other factors that cloud people's mind. It could be your ego, fear of being wrong, desire of wanting to have clout, or simply fear to have to change one's comfortable life because seeing and saying the truth can mean loosing people, loved ones, being ridiculed etc.

What I am actually contesting is the idea that "truth seeking" (which is a very high bar and everyone aspiring to it does not mean is actually achieving it) is a relative term, and I am contesting that bias is a criteria in truth seeking. Bias implies there are many opinions that we we have to consider, like in democracy. But that is not how Nature works. There is one truth, and once we found the truth, it applies to everything and it works. That also means somebody will be right and somebody will be wrong. Some theories are correct and some theories are flat out wrong. The truth seeker is NOT necessarily right (yet) but his/her core purpose is to find the truth. And once any of us think we found "the truth" we need to test it to see whether it can withstand the storm or will fall apart, abandon it when it falls apart and keep searching until we find something closer to the truth. Btw, our understanding of the universe will not be complete in any of our lifetime for sure. In that journey we know when we are directionally correct or not. But it should not be hard to immediately admit that one is wrong at the moment we realize that we are wrong or at least unsure, cost it what it may. We have to say it when we realize we are wrong. If somebody cannot do that or hides knowledge, that is a red flag and the same as lying or deceiving.

At the end of all of it, there is one truth. That truth does not need to consider all opinions or viewpoints, as long as it's the truth.

So far, I found the understanding of the world and biology to be most comprehensively understood and portrayed by Jack Kruse. He is not always right, and his understanding is incomplete. His use of terminology is sometimes rough and imprecise, but he is shining the light broadly in the right direction, and I have to say in my 20 years of career among the so called Intellectuals I have not found a better and more comprehensive truth seeking mission than this one. Dr Weinstein does not have anywhere close to a comprehensive understanding of human biology, but I could tell that he is seeking and it was refreshing. And I would be looking for the names you have listed for sure.

P.S. No worries. What you are saying is probably an important criteria in judging and looking for what is the truth. Potentially we are saying the same thing, or maybe not, I was not sure. But the reason I am responding to this is because a frequent encounter in Academia is: "why can't we all be right?". "can we not consider all viewpoints?" And my answer to that is No. Either one is right, the other is right or both are wrong. And if both parties that had differing viewpoints are "right" then this means both were wrong because the entire logical framework must have been a false one to begin with.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-08-17T00:55:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/having-dr-kruse-consult-soon-do-i-need-to-do-labs-before.29318/post-338522>

> [aruetenik said:](/goto/post?id=338520)
>
> Yes, all done with Trifield TF2 and Body voltage meter. (my husband was texting you yesterday
> 0 Body voltage in the bed. No magnetic either.
> Magnetico incoming (currently on grounding sheets, grounded outside)
> Have optional EMF canopy that we can use.
>
> RF inside bedrooms is under 0.050mW/m2
> Outside apartment its around 0.05-0.2mW/m2
>
> Should I get BUN/Creatine labs ?
>
> I havent had labs done in 5 years, and now im TAN

Zero body voltage on the bed is very suspicious and IF you are double grounded with grounding sheets is a false reading. The body voltage meter works by giving the electric field being induced into your body a path of least resistance to ground, through the meter. IF you have grounding sheets on the bed you are double grounded and the field is going to ground via the sheets NOT the meter. It is a false reading. UNPLUG the sheets and measure body voltage again. Let me know.

On the TriField RF 0.05 mW/m2 = 50 µW/m2 (units on the Safe & Sound Pro 2)
Also the TriField in not picking up all the RF that is there.
In my space I just measured with a TriField and the Safe & Sound Pro2, side by side. . . TriField reads 0.005mW/m2 (=5µW/m2) but the Safe & Sound measures 500 µW/m2 . . . Only 100x more!
Todays RF (ie 5G) frequencies require modern meters because of the way 5G technology transmits/handles the signal.

Just got the magnetico, so will install that next week. Then the grounding sheet comes off anyways...

Hey all, hope this is the right spot for this post.

I recently had an MRI done on my left knee after slipping on a dock getting off of a jet ski and falling on it pretty hard.

Attached a copy of the results.

Just wondering if there's any protocol that can be recommended to help recover from this. I lift weights quite often and it's been nearly impossible to do my usual loads due to the knee injury. Have been slowly trying to ease back into it doing 20-30 lb single leg extensions for 20 reps + lots of daytime walks. Patella traction is somewhat painful especially at the top of the contraction when load is added. Been doing CT every evening before bed and added glycine / bone broth to my diet a bit more to try to help collagen repair/synthesis. Obviously following circadian rules as well.

# Thread: FORGING A NATION
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/forging-a-nation.29324/>
**Subforum:** The Kruse Longevity Center
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-18T15:35:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/forging-a-nation.29324/post-338580>

### The 12 Jungian Archetypes​

1.  Ruler
2.  Creator/Artist
3.  Sage
4.  Innocent
5.  Explorer
6.  Rebel
7.  Hero
8.  Wizard
9.  Jester
10.  Everyman
11.  Lover
12.  Caregiver

A nation trying to rebuild itself needs archetypes.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

My belief is that the key figure in the transition is the THE REBEL who becomes THE HERO.

### The Rebel​

-   Motto: Rules are made to be broken
-   Core desire: revenge or revolution
-   Goal: to overturn what isn’t working
-   Greatest fear: to be powerless or ineffectual
-   Strategy: disrupt, destroy, or shock
-   Weakness: crossing over to the dark side, crime
-   Talent: outrageousness, radical freedom
-   The Outlaw is also known as: The rebel, revolutionary, wild man, the misfit, or iconoclast.

### The Hero​

-   Motto: Where there’s a will, there’s a way
-   Core desire: to prove one’s worth through courageous acts
-   Goal: expert mastery in a way that improves the world
-   Greatest fear: weakness, vulnerability, being a “chicken”
-   Strategy: to be as strong and competent as possible
-   Weakness: arrogance, always needing another battle to fight
-   Talent: competence and courage
-   The Hero is also known as: The warrior, crusader, rescuer, superhero, the soldier, dragon slayer, the winner and the team player.

**What do I think is my role in forging a country?
I want to focus my energy, guard my time, constantly train and retrain the mind & body, think for myself, curate my friends and the light environment I live under, keep my promises the best I can, & stay optimistic and constructive to my tribe while upgrading the world.
**

### Michalis — 2024-08-18T16:43:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/forging-a-nation.29324/post-338582>

Having looked into Jung at some point and obsessed by the notion of the shadow while I was trying to look into my dark side I can say that there might be actually be value in the whole archetype thing and particularly in the Jungian psychologist Robert Johnson.

However after some research into Jung I have started taking him with a bit of salt. Really good salesman and a bit of a charlatan too.

### [The mysterious Jung: his cult, the lies he told, and the occult](https://www.hgi.org.uk/resources/delve-our-extensive-library/interviews/mysterious-jung-his-cult-lies-he-told-and-occult)

Ivan Tyrrell asks Professor Richard Noll, author of ‘The Jung Cult’, to unravel the lies Carl G Jung told to aggrandise himself and his charismatic psychoanalytic movement.

 www.hgi.org.uk

I am.not convinced that the solution is to try to be hero's it is just unattainable for most. There are a lot of rebels out there also that they are just plain idiots.

Collective work is needed in my opinion with the brightest to lead through a democratic process. And for those that their allergies break out hearing this I don't mean collectivism. Just saying

### Anthony Smith — 2024-08-18T21:58:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/forging-a-nation.29324/post-338595>

>
>
> ### The Rebel​
>

> -   Core desire: revenge or revolution
> -   Goal: to overturn what isn’t working
> -   Greatest fear: to be powerless or ineffectual
> -   Strategy: disrupt, destroy, or shock
> -   Weakness: crossing over to the dark side, crime
> -   Talent: outrageousness, radical freedom
> -   The Outlaw is also known as: The rebel, revolutionary, wild man, the misfit, or iconoclast.
>
> ### The Hero​
>
> -   Motto: Where there’s a will, there’s a way
> -   Core desire: to prove one’s worth through courageous acts
> -   Goal: expert mastery in a way that improves the world
> -   Greatest fear: weakness, vulnerability, being a “chicken”
> -   Strategy: to be as strong and competent as possible
> -   Weakness: arrogance, always needing another battle to fight
> -   Talent: competence and courage
> -   The Hero is also known as: The warrior, crusader, rescuer, superhero, the soldier, dragon slayer, the winner and the team player.
>
>
> **What do I think is my role in forging a country?
> I want to focus my energy, guard my time, constantly train and retrain the mind & body, think for myself, curate my friends and the light environment I live under, keep my promises the best I can, & stay optimistic and constructive to my tribe while upgrading the world.**

I am not that familiar with Jung, so I looked up the 12 archetypes.
The first thing I noticed was half the charts say “Rebel” the other halve say “Outlaw”.
In my mind an “Outlaw” is not the same as a “Rebel”.
All these Archetypes may have been more accurate in 1919 but I’m not so sure they work in the same way today.

That being said, with respect, I do not see you so much as the “Rebel” becoming the “Hero” but the “Sage” - (all be it a rebellious one) .

In the “Hero’s Journey” analogy you are not the “hero” . . . You are the “Mentor” . . .You are Yoda . . . You are Gandalf - YOU invite, challenge, motivate, inspire all the other archetypes to become ”Hero's“ . . . And as such you are our hero.

I believe they would work today , society is crashing so all these "look" crazy, which is exactly why it needs to be done.
