# Thread: Poor Posture - a Biomechanics or a ROS problem?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poor-posture-a-biomechanics-or-a-ros-problem.29326/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### SanderC — 2024-08-18T17:49:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poor-posture-a-biomechanics-or-a-ros-problem.29326/post-338584>

When I learned from Dr. Kruse that Sleep Apnea is PROTECTIVE, this got me thinking about forward head posture.

If I understood correctly, sleep apnea is a bodily response to consume less oxygen at night because it can't handle the ROS.

What if a 'tech neck' or forward head posture follows the same process, and is not just about slouching or looking down?

Typically people who are chronically on the phone or laptop are exposed to lots of blue light and nnEMF --> Hypoxia --> Melanopsin destruction --> Melanin degradation and mitochondria dysfunction --> Body incapable to handle excess ROS --> Postural changes to consume less oxygen (neck breathing instead of diaphragmatic breathing).

Could it be?? Looking forward to hearing your thoughts!

### digitald3 — 2024-08-18T19:33:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poor-posture-a-biomechanics-or-a-ros-problem.29326/post-338587>

I’ll wait for Dr Jacks response as well but will chime in that the pathway you described is what makes sense to me as well. And add that some will have tissue growth/expansion, tongue, posterior pharynx etc as mitochondrial power reduces and gives the appearance of a closed off airway. Which is why sleep physicians, ents and dentists work to “open” the airway without considering the underlying quantum biology.

It’s also a reason that most of these approaches will make “AHI” better they dont necessarily lead to a better or longer life.

And this is going to be a long slog to get through to the centralized docs. I got in a bit of trouble this weekend even suggesting that apnea and mitochondrial function are connected

### SanderC — 2024-08-19T14:11:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poor-posture-a-biomechanics-or-a-ros-problem.29326/post-338624>

> [digitald3 said:](/goto/post?id=338587)
>
> I’ll wait for Dr Jacks response as well but will chime in that the pathway you described is what makes sense to me as well. And add that some will have tissue growth/expansion, tongue, posterior pharynx etc as mitochondrial power reduces and gives the appearance of a closed off airway. Which is why sleep physicians, ents and dentists work to “open” the airway without considering the underlying quantum biology.
>
> It’s also a reason that most of these approaches will make “AHI” better they dont necessarily lead to a better or longer life.
>
> And this is going to be a long slog to get through to the centralized docs. I got in a bit of trouble this weekend even suggesting that apnea and mitochondrial function are connected

Thank you Doug! From a Quantum Biology perspective, it would make sense that poor posture is a symptom and not a cause persé.

Let's see if [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) can illuminate us!

P.S. Really enjoyed your podcast with uncle Jack

### digitald3 — 2024-08-19T15:13:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poor-posture-a-biomechanics-or-a-ros-problem.29326/post-338625>

In the centralized world forward head posture is a response to maintain an airway. Ie if the airway is small or the tissues are enlarged a human adjusts their head forward to open the airway to breathe. As a centralized approach is always how do we more air to the lungs

I actually agree that this is a symptom of too much blue light and ALAN and nnEMF (and this is where I’m a bit fuzzy in the science)which leads to the mitochondria using the ROS/RNS system for energy. I know I butchered that. Regardless of the mechanics there is too much ROS in the cells and the body is doing what it needs to do to squash the extra ROS and O2 by not allowing more in to maintain normal cell processes.

So yeah the blue light devices are increasing the amount of ROS in the system and then the body is working to limit extra o2 in to the system which leads to hypertrophy on the tissues and eventual forward head posture.

And we definitely need studies done with these ideas.

### digitald3 — 2024-08-19T15:40:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poor-posture-a-biomechanics-or-a-ros-problem.29326/post-338630>

I’m not sure I’m making sense. Forward head posture is usually in place to create more air flow. Or that’s what we use to check for poor airways. So if the tech screens and nnEMF are creating too much cellular ROS then I’m not sure they are directly correlated. As the cells don’t want more o2. Which is what centralized apnea therapies try to do. Add more o2 phone and tech use do affect posture. So I can make a connection I just don’t think it’s as direct as forward posture reduces airflow.

And forgot to add -Thanks for listening and glad you enjoyed it!

### SanderC — 2024-08-19T17:53:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poor-posture-a-biomechanics-or-a-ros-problem.29326/post-338639>

Thank you for spotting the flaw in my thinking!

What if it is actually both: 1) if you're sitting in front of a screen/phone your airways are compressed so your body compensates (centralized advice) but also 2) blue light and nnEMF causing hypoxia so your body tries to compensate with a first line of defense to get more oxygen in (before you have melanin degradation / mito dysfunction)

### digitald3 — 2024-08-19T18:45:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poor-posture-a-biomechanics-or-a-ros-problem.29326/post-338644>

At this point I will say that all of the above is possible.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-20T19:22:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poor-posture-a-biomechanics-or-a-ros-problem.29326/post-338706>

Fundamentally a melanin and water problem

### Mat.I — 2024-08-26T12:29:47+0300

OK they were monsters outside and terrorizing society, but to keep them cramped like that like tortured animals? I dunno man, it depends if you believe in future lives and stuff or not, but I think locking away energy like that and forgetting about it is doing no favours for anyone in the long run.
> Have a look at Norway's prison system. Do I agree with it? Not really. Too comfy. Somewhere in the middle would be appropriate, and better conditions can be earned with good behaviour etc.
>

After understanding some of the crimes they have committed, can they still be considered human? Can they ever be rehabilitated? You could look at it on a deeper level, however, in my opinion would not do any good or change the facts of the matter. I agree with DLO' comment above that this happens in other parts of the world.. There are some truly bad & evil beings out in the world..

### LuciizaP — 2024-08-26T13:31:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-jails.29329/post-338920>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=338917)
>
> After understanding some of the crimes they have committed, can they still be considered human? Can they ever be rehabilitated? You could look at it on a deeper level, however, in my opinion would not do any good or change the facts of the matter. I agree with DLO' comment above that this happens in other parts of the world.. There are some truly bad & evil beings out in the world..

Well the fact that you've questioned their humanity is what I have a problem with. They are traumatised children/teenagers in adult bodies; pressured by men to do crimes so they can ensure their own safety. That's what they've grown up with and consider normal.

Will some of them be a genetic psychopath? Yes a few. Are most of them? I expect not.
Are they redeemable? Well according to that Scandinavian video above, recidivism rates are lower, so it is achieveable. Do they have comparable populations? Probably not, because of the gang affilitations and culture. But is the tabula rasa the same in that each human has an innocent template? I believe so.
How to start solving the problem? Well, split them apart from other gang members, so they're in a smaller groups that don't know each other. Move group members according to personality dynamics. Perhaps maximum 7/8. Turn off ALAN. Maximize sunlight.
Then the key? Decondition them through meditation over multiple years/decades. Get them to talk to the victims families; the non-psychos will eventually give-in an apoligize.
I just don't think penning them up in those cages with all this stuff is the right way to go about things. What's done is done, and it's terrible. Humans aren't going to get out of their own mess if they can't forgive each other, no matter how heinous the crimes.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-26T17:25:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-jails.29329/post-338930>

I have a problem with your take. You do the crime you get what you deserve.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-26T17:26:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-jails.29329/post-338931>

# Thread: Any fellow Software Engineers?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-fellow-software-engineers.29330/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 9

### Sid_Nandakumar — 2024-08-18T23:24:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-fellow-software-engineers.29330/post-338604>

Just wanted to see if there are any fellow software engineers. My current goal is a remote job with the ability to work out of el salvador/digital nomad. Would be nice to see if others have the same vision or are currently living that life.

### Mat.I — 2024-08-19T02:51:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-fellow-software-engineers.29330/post-338612>

> [Sid\_Nandakumar said:](/goto/post?id=338604)
>
> Just wanted to see if there are any fellow software engineers. My current goal is a remote job with the ability to work out of el salvador/digital nomad. Would be nice to see if others have the same vision or are currently living that life.

I'm an engineer/expert witness, however not for software and have been working remotely since 2020. As directors we implemented WFH for staff after C19 restrictions and kept it in place to provide better balance and health outcomes for the staff. As I can fully work remotely, due to not being too hands anymore and only responsible for technical QA/QC of staff with the odd expert case here and there, I have the same goal in mind with doing this from ES in the future.

### eaandkw — 2024-08-19T18:39:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-fellow-software-engineers.29330/post-338641>

I work cybersecurity so effectively the same. I stare at computers all day. Probably the biggest changes I've made lately are walking outside during my lunch break when WFH. I also ordered night-time blue light-blocking glasses and waiting for them to come in. Right now I am only hybrid WFH but hopefully soon I can make that full time.

### jackmck — 2024-08-19T20:20:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-fellow-software-engineers.29330/post-338649>

I’m mostly in project management and still do web development work here and there. I began working remotely in ES since the beginning of this year. I do freelance jobs so I can have control over my time. I work 3 to 4 hours a day in an outdoor set up. Iris installed, no wifi, and I don’t use my phone. Sun is king. Great way to have some control over the environment you’re working in.

Thanks for these questions, that's really helpful. I worked as a developer for seven years at seek.com.au. So, I had a good bunch of connections already. Most of my work comes from friends and from referrals from friends. The referrals have been some of the most interesting work, including video editing. Though not my primary area of expertise... really fun. I've been using Corsera to drive up my skills in Sales. And to see what other areas I might want to advance.

# Thread: I'm living a dream - don't pinch me
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-living-a-dream-dont-pinch-me.29334/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### thejessallen — 2024-08-19T18:56:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-living-a-dream-dont-pinch-me.29334/post-338645>

Yesterday's live Q&A with Jack was awesome as always.

I asked for insights on how to my frozen shoulder heal

I was not surprised - that a big part of the answer was be at the beach, grounding, putting my hands on the sand to get electrons into my shoulder.

I moved to El Salvador a month ago, a huge reason was because of the health benefits and Jack's consistent message about the health benefits of equatorial sunlight and grounding at volcanic sand beaches.

So my sunrise this morning was at the beach. As I sat there, with rarely another human in sight, looking at the beautiful ocean and rising sun... the message I kept hearing in my head was

"I am living a dream"

In what world did we ever imagine that the prescription for getting healthy is "spend more time at the beach"

I also knew I'd be returning home, to a place where I can sit outside for some more grounding and junk maintenance while starting my work day.

Eventually putting on some clothes because a wonderful local lady will be arriving to cook fresh seafood and take care of house chores.

My dream morning got a little better because when i returned home, my mom was awake and we had a nice chat that started with a lot of laughter. She got out to the beach yesterday and had a wonderful time. It had been many months since she had been to the beach. Although every day she's been getting her sunshine. She's been listening to Dr. Jack too

it's so important to slow down and appreciate dreams manifested. To savor the simple things and to find reasons to be grateful no matter what.

I've had horrible days and traumatic times and learned how to find the silver lining and be grateful there too. The silver lining for me is that in this vast cosmos, the opportunity to be human for a minuscule moment of time is a blessing. Cherish it. Be grateful I woke up today.

Days like this make it easy to be grateful.

thanks Jack, I am grateful for you and all the tribe members that help your wisdom reach more people

# Thread: Optimal Journal, profanities abound.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-profanities-abound.29336/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rachael Bowen — 2024-08-19T20:53:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-profanities-abound.29336/post-338650>

Hello all - trying to keep it concise here in the first post of my optimal journal. Grateful to be here and to have already learned so much devouring threads on this forum, Jack’s Patreon, podcasts and in my third read through of EpiPaleo RX. I’m pretty fired up these days and drop a few f-bombs below.

Maternal grandmother was born and raised in North Carolina (35th latitude) to a farming family - tobacco, cotton and hogs. She married my grandfather and moved to Iron Mountain, MI which is at 45 latitude. She was healthy, active and outside for much of her life though she did have diagnosed osteoporosis later in life (mid 60’s) and congestive heart failure towards the end (she lived to 89, had a stroke alone in her home and passed away a day later in hospital). She was one of her only siblings (there were 11) to not have type 2 diabetes.

My Mama: Born in Iron Mountain, MI 1948 at the 45th lat, natural birth, breast fed. **Breakdown basics: osteopenia, pre-diabetic, spine surgery, neuropathy, diverticulitis, elevated cholesterol = statins.**

-   Healthy, normal childhood spent outside in summers. Lots of lake time and skiing in the winter. No health concerns as a child.
-   Motorcycle accident in her early 20’s, compound fracture and hospital stay where she went septic and almost died.
-   Moved to Nederland, CO at the 39thn latitude in the 60’s. where she spent a ton of time outside and in nature living a half way hippie lifestyle.
-   My older brother was born naturally in 1981 - he was a tough baby, colic and some developmental issues. Later diagnosed with a seizure disorder and learning disabilities. He was reliant on my parents and would never have lived alone - he passed away ‘suddenly’ (though now I am sure there were plenty of signs his neurologists and doctors didn’t see or couldn’t understand) in 2008 after not feeling well, going to the hospital, having a ‘dangerously low” white blood cell count, being put into a medical coma, discovering a small hole in his heart and then his organs started failing. He was on life support for about 5 days and my dad didn’t want to do an autopsy. Cause of death was ruled organ failure but they didn’t know why.
-   I was born naturally in 1984 in Montrose, CO. I was breast fed for \_\_\_
-   In 1995, she had a back injury and ruptured a disc in her low back. After surgery to repair, she recovered but has always had issues with this. Mostly neuropathy, back pain etc.
-   High fasting blood glucose around 105-115 over the last 20-25 years. Bouts of diverticulitis.
-   Osteopenia for the last 25 years or so, I don’t believe she’s ever taken medication for this but they’ve tried.
-   High cholesterol and a statin script that she barely takes though her PCP are insisting now. She’s reading EpiPaleo RX and even gave it to her naturopath which warms my little heart.

Me: Born at 38th latitude currently living in Denver, CO (39th latitude). **Breakdown basics: history of disordered eating/yo-yo-ing, two moderna jabs, live in a big city, work in a high nnEMF environment, have a good amount of tattoos (99% black ink, no areas fully covered), osteopenic, likely leptin resistant, beginnings of insulin resistance, shitty environment.**

-   I was raised on the western slope (38th lat) on a farm/ranch in my baby days.
-   Breast fed for 18 months
-   Moved to Boulder area and grew up there - lots of outside time in the summer and I was a tan kid.
-   Had a bout of ear infections when I was a baby and going into kindergarten.
-   Had skin issues (only ever in my scalp and behind my ears) that presented as a flaky, gooey scab. A friend diagnosed it as eczema. It was bad for years on and off. I went to a derm in college and was told it was sebborrhic dermatits - prescribed clobetsol. I haven’t needed it since I got myself back in the sun starting June of this year and at this point it is pretty much gone.
-   I’ll never forget when we got AOL dial up though my parents were very strict with me being online. I loved computer games and video games but they closely monitored my screen time until high school.
-   Started menstruating at age 11 - I’ll never forget the pain of that first period and have had mostly awful cramping and pain with my cycle since then up until very recently.
-   High school - we had computers with internet in the library. We typed up papers to hand in. I cruised chat rooms. I got my first pager and then a Nokia brick cell phone my junior year. I still spent a lot of time hanging outside with friends, smoking weed and drinking cheap vodka.
    -   I had a fasciotomy surgery in high school to relieve pressure in my lower left leg. Shin splint caused aches and pains in the lower legs. Never did the right leg. This has been my only ever surgery.
    -   I also started hormonal birth control pills in high school - I was told it would help with my cramps - it did a bit. I got off of hormonal birth control in 2008 and have never touched it again.
-   With the advent of screen time, me finally getting my own Playstation and TV and more and more communication with friends in chat rooms, i became aware for the first time of weight gain (having been a ‘normal healthy’ weight for all of my life).
-   College - marked mostly by disordered eating, “Freshmen fifteen”, lots of time inside on the computer, recreational drug use, a year long period of extremely restricted eating and rapid weight loss and many trips to the OB/GYN for ovarian cysts and an interstitial cystitis diagnosis. I was prescribed flagyl a lot though i remember feeling very suspicious as none of the things the OB was doing helped very much.
-   After college - lots of diet fads, bouts of exercising a lot and then not at all. More time inside, less time outside. Frustration with diets and exercising never ‘working’ the way it was supposed to even though I was a strict rule follower.
-   First jobs after college were in tech - spent 9 years in front of a computer screen in buildings filled with servers and screens and nerds and phones and literally every gadget you can think of. Weight continued to do ‘weird things’ despite best efforts.
-   No “major health issues” during this time - I didn’t have a PCP. I had my teeth cleaned regularly and had a blood draw every couple of years - all “normal”.
-   Quit tech in 2016 and became a nail technician. Started my own business which exploded after 6 months. I felt better - I wasn’t starting at a screen all day. My weight normalized easily with basic efforts. I started eating Paleo and then Keto. I felt like superwoman. I was still inside a lot though, working 6 days a week to build my clientele.
-   As business got busier, I threw myself into it. Few days off, if I wasn’t working I was thinking about work. I started training apprentices, the business became my biggest focus and time suck. It also became quite the little fiat farming operation which is how I’m framing it these days.
-   2020 shut all of it down. Didn’t work for two months, came back and followed all the rules because “they" were shutting businesses like mine down for non-compliance and “fellow community members”were ratting out these businesses for the same.
-   March and April 2021, got two moderna jabs. May, June, July, Aug, Sept, Oct 2021 - had the most insane periods of my life. Clotted blood so dark it seemed black and had full blown covid symptoms for the first two days of my period. No one believed me that it was jab related. No doc would even talk to me about it .
-   November 2021, got covid….BAD. I was furious. Wasn’t I supposed to be ‘protected’
-   December 2021 - FINALLY got my head out of my own ass, started listening to my ball and chain who RAGED against me getting vaccinated and began the long journey of re-educating myself towards what I now know to be a decentralized life. Spoiler alert - I fuck up a few more times.
-   July 2022 - Nov 2023 - began a lifestyle challenge and start counting macros and exercising in a gym 3-4x/week first thing in the morning. Something doesn’t feel right after months and still no ‘results’. My coaches solution is to cut more calories and eat things like yogurt and dates right before bed. At this suggestion, I opt out. I know something is wrong but I don’t know what yet.
-   December 2022 - I get a dexa scan and it shows I am ‘almost’ osteopenic and I have a high body fat % (38%). Which shouldn’t be the case with all the macro counting and exercise… HA!
-   February 2023 - I find a functional medicine NP and she’s the first person who will listen to me about the side effects of the jab. We start doing tests (duh). And I start taking supplements (also duh).
    -   I’ll post labs down below but I had a high fasting blood glucose and A1C (5.7) as well as thyroid hormone issues and a high CRP. She \*did not test vitamin D levels\*.
-   Feb 2023 - December 2023: Prescribed liothyronine for thyroid from functional NP - 25mg. Started feeling better. Was still counting macros and working out and not seeing any benefit to that either in my body composition or on the scale. Energy sucked, brain sucked, eating was towing the line disordered, I was very concerned about my elevated A1C and fasting blood glucose and was able to bring those numbers down over the course of a year.
    -   I had a SARS COV2 Spike IgG antibody test run in an effort to see what the jab was doing in my system - the first value was 15828 and the second, done in july of 2024 was higher at 20003. Would love to know if this means anything while also knowing that redox is the best medicine for jabs and also tropic living.
-   January 2024 - After reading more and educating myself, I ask functional med lady for a vitamin D test, feeling incredulous at this point that she didn’t order one for me initially.
-   February 2024 - Vitamin d is in the absolute toilet at 25. She starts me on supplements. Never suggests I go outside.
-   April 2024 - In the process of listening to as many RFK Jr podcasts I can, I’m introduced to the Uncle Jack, RFK, Rick episode by a dear friend and my mind is blown into smithereens. An obsessive, almost manic devouring of podcasts, blogs, posts, threads, books and conversations follow and continue.
-   **April 2024 - present:**
    -   Started eating from the farmers market only in June.
    -   Eat within 45 min of waking, BAB
    -   Switched from filtered water to spring water from local spring.
    -   Watch the sunrise every morning, kiniki or similar, eyeballs staring for as long as I can (easily can do 5 min now and up to 10)
    -   2-5 hrs of early morning and afternoon sun 7 days a week
    -   Grounding 30-60 min a day
    -   blue light blockers at work and at night after sunset
    -   no lights on in the house in the evening, circuits cut to most of the house after dark
    -   in bed 30-45 min after the sun sets in complete darkness
    -   Started CT up in the mountains at the river 3-4 times per month, have a CT set up on its way for the fall and winter months. For now doing cold showers and some face submersion.
    -   mitigated my bedroom for sleep sanctuary thanks to Anthony’s help so my nnEMF is about 20. (haven’t done the RF readings yet though I know they will be insane living in Denver).
    -   Found out that my nnEMF at work is very high - 5-6,000 on the BVK. Working on mitigating that as much as possible while I fiat farm
    -   Reordered Vitamin d test - i’m up to 85 though I know the reading is inaccurate as I was supplementing as well. I stopped taking the supplement after this last test and will test again after 8 weeks or more to see the difference.
    -   Stopped taking liothyronine and all supplements from functional NP - very happy to have that $400 every other month back!!!! Also I feel better than I did when I was taking all that stuff now just doing what I've listed here.
    -   HS CRP - 2.0 as of 7/2024
    -   A1C 5.5 (up from 5.3 in february)
    -   Fasting Blood glucose (7/16/24) 90 (up from 86 in February)
    -   LDL 104 in feb 2024
    -   Fired functional NP.
    -   Planned southern latitude trips for Sept, November (See ya in El Salvador?!) December and February so far.
    -   Increasing seafood - I have always gotten pretty violently ill from raw oysters so working on cooked for now. Anyone have any thoughts on this? Haven’t tried a raw oyster in 6+ years as it is a wretched experiment but I am game and appreciate any tips!

Not short but hopefully more on the concise side. I have really enjoyed reading the optimal journals so a big thank you to all who have shared there. I find it fascinating, encouraging and often times more helpful than anything else I can find online.

Some things I’ll be working on over the next year:

Redox
nnEMF mitigation @ work
I’d like to become a CT savage by end of winter this year
Meditation (quantum breathing blog)
MovNat
Buy more BTC
Researching Business opportunities for long term plan in ES
Making connections with more decentralized humans, upgrading the close circle of friends and community

I’d also like to get better at distilling what I’m learning into ‘friends and family’ morsels. I talk to a lot of people all day and they really “want to know” what I’m up to. This is just part of my job and always has been. I am not holding out a lot of hope here of changing minds but I have to say, I really like, as Jack might say, “punching people in the face”. I also get fired up thinking about how this learning has already completely changed my life and I do have some altruistic tendencies.

Before Covid, I was slightly more okay not standing out or having a different view. That whole mess really changed the way I think and I guess I can be grateful for it now that I’m out the other side. Especially now that my brain is working well for the first time in what feels like decades. I’ll never be the same person as I was before 2020 and I truly feel that way now, too.

Thanks for reading if you got this far! Below are a few labs - salivary cortisol, Thyroid and a leptin test (when I mentioned to my functional med that I thought I was leptin resistant, she ordered this test for me).

### Rachael Bowen — 2024-09-03T18:03:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-profanities-abound.29336/post-339200>

A few things that I've noticed improving these days:

\- Skin texture and moisture improvements: living in Colorado, I have always relied heavily on creams, lotions and oils after a shower or being in water of any kind. I no longer need any of these products at all and my skin stays soft and does not feel dry. I do use a beef tallow moisturizer on my face (no longer using any products my esthetician recommends, she thinks I'm nuts but can't deny my skin looks happy).

\- Thirst: hardly ever have a regular feeling of thirst (especially since switching to spring water) and notice myself drinking less water (down from a gallon or more a day to about 80-90oz) and peeing way less.

\- Digestion: bubble guts and "oh shit" situations greatly reduced over the last few months, even if I'm traveling or away from my regular food sources.

\- Sebborhic Dermatitis: nearly gone, have stopped using all topical steroids and haven't needed them at all. Scalp stays moisturized though still a bit flaky, nowhere near what it was.

\- Light sensitivity: I consider this an improvement in that my eyes are getting more sensitive at night time which greatly incentivizes me getting my ass in the dark and going to sleep. It's nearly impossible for me to be driving a car in the dark now without blue blockers as the headlights give me an instant headache.

\- Body composition: slow but steadily improving for the first time in many years. I believe mitigating my bedroom and adding 5 min cold showers daily has been the corner turned in this along with everything else. Excited to see where I'm at a year from now.

\- Oysters!!!! Tried my first raw oyster in over 7 years last weekend with no negative effect. Very happy about this one!

### Rachael Bowen — 2024-10-05T19:28:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-profanities-abound.29336/post-340461>

Working on a rig for this Vogel crystal with a vortex. TBD!

### Anthony Smith — 2024-08-20T21:28:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-screen-iris.29338/post-338717>

> [John Patrick said:](/goto/post?id=338699)
>
> Anthony, Michael Moreau here and please do keep us posted on the screen you referenced as Ruby Lith - if needed: in addition, I have put the deposit down on the Analog Daytime computer Jack introduced to us during his "pool side - solar chat", some time ago. But in the meantime, I will look up Iris screen protection, you and many have mentioned. I have never dated a gal named "Iris," but she sounds like a "nice" girl to date during this waiting for the 21 St., century computer - which should be available in early 2025.
>
> Michael here, can you update us and me on this grounded technique you are utilizing, that works?

I have found using a grounded charger (Apple) grounds my whole network ethernet included. To ground just the ethernet, use a Cat6A or Cat8 cord (metal plugs) and either add a grounding ethernet plug/cord into the bus or clip an alligator clip onto the plug. [Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/store](http://Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/store)

### Daulatwant — 2024-08-20T22:06:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-screen-iris.29338/post-338720>

> [optimal2024 said:](/goto/post?id=338695)
>
> I am using a laptop without charger and connected to an Ethernet cable. I have iris installed and have set it to zero night time blue light setting
> Is this enough or do you think I need to add rubylith film to the screen the blue light as well? Thanks

Any screen, except maybe oled. Will have some light bleeding through regardless of what the colors are set by software.

My samsung amoled phones, still produce green light when I set blue and green all the way to zero. That is because the red pixels of the oled are not clean red but rather orange.

The effect is far worse on lcd, since the backight is always putting out white light and it leaks through.

I would probably use a film.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-08-20T22:28:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-screen-iris.29338/post-338724>

If you have to spend time in front of any screen, certainly use software and/or filters but also have as much natural light around you Or add a red light and/or NIR light to counter the blue. Any screen time after sunset especially, no matter what action you take - is just bad.

The use of the laptop is mainly for day use. Thais for your help

All the network needs is a ground somewhere either the cable or the box.

Touching external mouse - 0.149

Sat in front of the laptop not touching - 0.044

My name is Vivian (Viv) and I live in NYC and Connecticut, and have been struggling with Lyme, mold poisoning, and recurring SIBO and SIFO, chronic insomnia and gut issues and bad brain fog. I have become extremely EMF sensitive so a place like NYC with 5G density has become hard for me, I often get a fever after just a few days back for work. When I get to CT and ground on the beach and swim I start to feel better. It's been a vicious cycle. I'm planning a trip down to El Salvador with two dear friends who are also new members here, and would love any recommendations on homes to rent in walking distance to the beach. ### GFam — 2024-08-23T18:27:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anthony-body-voltage.29353/post-338824>

This is a question for Anthony. Sent him a direct message but he didn't reply (maybe didn't see it) so putting it here.

I was browsing the forums the other day and came across a post where you had mentioned your business partner was developing a device to test stray voltage in the ground (can't find it again now). I think this is different from your body tester. What was the outcome of that development? Did a device get created?

Another quick question. When using the body voltage tester outside should we be connecting the BVK to ground or not since we are grounded ourselves (assuming our skin is touching the earth).

### Anthony Smith — 2024-08-23T18:52:13+0300

I think this is different from your body tester. What was the outcome of that development? Did a device get created?
>
> Another quick question. When using the body voltage tester outside should we be connecting the BVK to ground or not since we are grounded ourselves (assuming our skin is touching the earth).

Hi, I did not receive a direct message here on the Forum so thanks for posting here.

We put the ground test project in hold. The meter required was quite expensive.

The body voltage meter works by giving the field you are in a path if least resistance to ground thru the meter so you can measure it. If you are also grounded in some other way, bare feet, ground pad etc the BV meter does not work. Outside wear shoes and connect the white ground cord to the ground spike.

# Thread: Becoming cold adapted
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/becoming-cold-adapted.29360/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Emmanuela — 2024-08-25T06:41:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/becoming-cold-adapted.29360/post-338872>

I have been fairly regular with CT at about 55-57 degrees for 30-45mim every other day. For about two months now. Prior to that i did 6 weeks of the face plunging.
But, This week i went camping where it was approximately 8deg C at night and my tent and sleeping bag were not that warm.
Honestly, i was just freezing every night and felt so unadapted to the cold. Now i have a slight cough.

I have not been doing the cold water prior to CT.
What else am i missing? I really thought i would be a little more cold adapted by now.

### Johan Lindøm — 2024-08-25T12:10:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/becoming-cold-adapted.29360/post-338874>

I can definitely relate to your experience. I live in a cold country, and I feel that I’m better adapted to the climate than most of my friends. However, I’ve had moments similar to what you described, where I struggle to regain my body temperature and end up shivering through the night. That said, I’m no expert.

I find cold therapy quite challenging. While I can run or ski in the winter without a t-shirt, sitting still in cold water or swimming in it is much more difficult for me. My body constantly urges me to quit as soon as possible. That’s why I prefer to run in nature wearing only shorts; I can do that for hours. I tell myself that this is also a form of cold therapy, and it gives me the much-needed exposure to sun light! Even though the sunlight here is shitty and without UV.

edit: A small addition. At that time when I was freezing at night, I was in the middle of Hardangervidda, in winter, far from people. I was sleeping in a sleeping bag with a sleeping pad, but without a tent, and the temperature that night dropped to below minus 24 degrees Celsius. Hardangervidda is a large mountain area in Norway with almost no people, and large parts of the area are nature reserves.

### rtd — 2024-08-25T19:25:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/becoming-cold-adapted.29360/post-338890>

an extra layer? Lol you mean more clothes?

# Thread: Cataract Surgery alternatives?

### Strictlylauren — 2024-08-26T09:22:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-alternatives.29362/post-338910>

I developed a cataract in my right eye at 36 years old, out of the blue (last year Dec) while staying for a week in a friends mouldy/blue light ridden flat - the only option I’m given is the cataract surgery. I absolutely don’t want this but there are no other options! Do you know anything that could help guide me as to what to do besides doing the sun things and using blue light blocking glasses?? Most importantly, will the artificial lens they insert in the surgery affect my body with regards to how much light I can filter? Please help!!

### Espresso — 2024-08-26T16:03:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-alternatives.29362/post-338926>

I really don't think that the cataract developed suddenly.
The artificial lens that they fit will block UV as far as I know. Jack says that a cataract is a way that the eye tries to block blue light. I assume that this means that you must avoid all un-natural blue light and see as much natural sunlight as you can. I know that this will stop further damage, but I don't know if it will reverse it.
Unfortunately for me I discovered Jack too late and I have had the surgery. As to whether there are long term adverse effects, I will just have to wait and see.

### Espresso — 2024-08-27T12:27:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-alternatives.29362/post-338956>

NNEMF is non native emf.
This includes all man made lights, the suns rays altered by going through glass or plastic, radio signals (Wifi, Bluetooth, 1G, 2G, 3G, 4G, 5G), electric and magnetic fields generated by alternating current flowing in wires.
Pretty much everything that out modern society revolves around.
How much of this you need to avoid will depend on how sick you are, and how well you want to become.

### Espresso — 2024-08-28T14:22:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-alternatives.29362/post-339003>

Lauren, watch this talk first.

I discovered it all too late.
He hints that he may be working with lens manufacturers to try to make a lens that does allow unfiltered light through. I don't know if it is actually feasible? After watching this, I asked the eye surgeon who did my cataract surgery if my lenses blocked UV light. **Oh yes** she said very proudly!!!!
You may be able to slightly mitigate the effects of not getting proper light in your eyes by being sure to get a lot of light on your skin, but I personally aren't sure of this.

### Wolfpleasures — 2024-08-28T19:50:01+0300

I am going through the same thing, trying very hard to resist this surgery. And there is another point about it: they use local anesthetics in the eye, and I have now heard that those anesthetics have nanoparticles. I really don’t want to have those injected into my eye. But I haven’t yet figured way around it. I’ve been putting off the surgery for years, and my eye doctor said I finally really need to do it. I’m going to talk to him about an alternative to local anesthetics and see what he has to say.

### Rocky — 2024-09-14T20:55:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-alternatives.29362/post-339583>

[@Strictlylauren](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/31861/)

There is a lot you can do to prevent a cataract, not much you can do to reverse one, if at all.

Although cataract surgery isn't optimal, it's not like you have much choice if you can't see. So I would avoid listening to those bringing fear about having surgery, i.e. 'surgery is like chopping off your hand etc'.

The lens in the eye has no mitochondria and without them, making changes to lens contents is not going to be easy.

### maria@modernhuman — 2024-09-14T22:01:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-alternatives.29362/post-339588>

Anyone interested in cataracts should check out this post from Kruse, it's an oldie but goodie.

"In five early-stage PSC \[posterior subcapsular cataract\] patients taking 5,000 IU of 25-OH D daily for vitamin D deficiency, there was resolution of their cataracts during the 2-year follow-up period."[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/26132781](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/26132781)

"So if UV light exposure is bad for the eyes why does UV light make cataracts go away?

Member**: In this way bariatric surgery is exactly analogous to cataract surgery in that it fixes a "problem" which was actually a compensation without addressing the underlying cause, thereby worsening overall health in both cases.

Maria,

Maybe I'm not interpreting the results correctly, but this study doesn't seem to be all that exciting, per the conclusion for anything other than 'early stage'. If you are halfway or late stage you are kind of out of luck.

I wasn't told I had cataracts until last year at the age of 71, so there isn't much that can be done about them other than have surgery or go blind at some point.

Not sure why Dr. Jack would say: "Stop letting them take your patients cataracts out.", when there is no viable solution except maybe in early stage and most likely don't even know at that point.

**Conclusions:** Serum 25(OH)D levels were unrelated to nuclear opacities in this study sample. However, exploratory analyses suggest a protective association in women younger than 70 years. Further investigations of the relationship between vitamin D and nuclear lens opacities are warranted.

### Rocky — 2024-09-15T00:31:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-alternatives.29362/post-339590>

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=338972)
>
> People did not evolve to have foreign implants in our bodies. Blocking UV light in your retinal hypothalamus endocrine system is horrible.

You know what's more horrible? Not being able to see.

### maria@modernhuman — 2024-10-01T02:16:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-alternatives.29362/post-340208>

> [Rocky said:](/goto/post?id=339583)
>
> [@Strictlylauren](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/31861/)
>
> There is a lot you can do to prevent a cataract, not much you can do to reverse one, if at all.
>
> Although cataract surgery isn't optimal, it's not like you have much choice if you can't see. So I would avoid listening to those bringing fear about having surgery, i.e. 'surgery is like chopping off your hand etc'.
>
> The lens in the eye has no mitochondria and without them, making changes to lens contents is not going to be easy.

The lens does have mitochondria in the epithelial tissue and mitochondrial dysfunction is linked to cataracts, as well as other eye diseases.

Super short version of events: mitochondrial breakdown => less oxygen consumed by ETC => lens is less hypoxic => damage and cataracts

Fundamental solution: address the causative agent of mitochondrial breakdown

Check out this informative study: [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4996752/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4996752/)

"As described above, our previous study reported that the lens exists in a hypoxic environment which is important in maintaining lens transparency, and that oxygen levels are generally lower closer to the lens.[29](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4996752/#R29), [30](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4996752/#R30) Exposure to increased oxygen has been identified as a risk factor for nuclear cataract, the most common type of age-related cataract.[40](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4996752/#R40) Therefore, we hypothesized that decreased mitochondrial function in lens epithelial cells (LECs) plays a key role in cataractogenesis."

Thanks for this!

Hyperbaric chamber? (Never tried one)

Cardiovascular? Been cardio for years but tapered down lately and now wondering if the lack of steady and heavy breathing might actually be a culprit to cataracts. Or the opposite.

Or are we talking about something else entirely different? If so, this goldie gold chlorine salt water member who's read all of this over and over and over again--just don’t get it. So I'm asking. What in the heck does "exposure to increased oxygen" mean? How does it actually happen? The "exposure?" Do we make it happen? What is this action or inaction that makes this happen.

@Would swimming do this by breathing in and out so much?
answer: it's not the swimming that's the problem it's the mitochondria being impaired that they can't handle what's being done to them.

@why would increased oxygen exacerbate a cataract?
answer: did you read the study

@Why is oxygen not a good thing?
answer: oxygen is the terminal electron acceptor in the mitochondria. if something is impairing the mitochondria and they can't process the oxygen available, it turns into ROS and free radicals. it's like shoving gasoline into a donkey. poor donkey.

@Hyperbaric chamber
answer: any over-oxygenation whether from hyperbaric oxygen or high FiO2 in an intubated patient in the ICU can be damaging if it is counterproductive to mitochondrial equilibrium of electron processing

>

>

I’ve already had the surgery. I’m hoping some light makes it in through my lens or even another part of my eye. I do a grounded sunrise walk every morning and expose as much skin as I can depending on the weather. I never wear sunscreen or sunglasses. I have to hope this is enough. If anyone knows any other way to mitigate these IOL lenses I’d love to hear about it.

### wtsmg — 2025-03-26T21:49:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-alternatives.29362/post-349399>

> [WRC777 said:](/goto/post?id=348433)
>
> I’ve already had the surgery. I’m hoping some light makes it in through my lens or even another part of my eye. I do a grounded sunrise walk every morning and expose as much skin as I can depending on the weather. I never wear sunscreen or sunglasses. I have to hope this is enough. If anyone knows any other way to mitigate these IOL lenses I’d love to hear about it.

With respect to the important UV (and IR) part of the light spectrum, check out the [Vermont 2017](http://youtube.com/wthatatch?v=d7qjh4BIGbc) video at 1hr47min - 1hr49min. There was a question about this and Dr. Kruse replied that there is UV light assimilation through the sclera! He mentioned that he gazes about 15 degrees of angle of the sun for about 15-20 minutes. I highly suggest (re-)listening to the whole thing... It's amazing how much better you can understand things after spending time with all the information we are taking in here and there.

See this paper: [Influence of Circadian Rhythm in the Eye: Significance of Melatonin in Glaucoma](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7996162/#B43-biomolecules-11-00340). In it, it states,

> Focusing on the eye, receptors have been localized in different structures. On the ocular surface, **melatonin MT1 receptor has been detected in corneal epithelial cells in different animals \[43,44\] and humans \[45\]**, while MT2 receptor has only been identified in animals \[43,46,47\]. They have also been seen in rabbits in the iris and ciliary body \[48\] and in human uveal melanocytes and human uveal melanoma cells \[49\]. Presence of melatonin in aqueous humour \[50\] has also been confirmed as its enzymes AANAT and HIOMT \[51\]. Furthermore, within the lens, **MT2 receptor has been identified \[43\] being considered as another source of melatonin \[52\]. In sclera, both receptors are expressed \[43,44\].**

In Reality #4, Dr. Kruse mentions,

> It turns out **aromatic amino acids also have a specific absorbance spectra in the UV range** and purple light oscillation can control those amino acids to make the bio-genic amines in the eye. Those two bio-genic amines are dopamine and **melatonin. They both contain aromatic amino acids that absorb UV light 260-289nm that leaks into the eye in small quantities.**

I hope my understanding of this is correct and that Mother Nature is sticking it to the miscreants that give us these IOLs.

### Mat.I — 2025-04-16T11:00:24+0300

### Mat.I — 2025-04-17T02:12:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-alternatives.29362/post-350813>

> [eugene1000 said:](/goto/post?id=350807)
>
> ^^^ creativity is the manifestation of entropy / dissipation of order at the macroscopic level. but order can re-emerge as cave paintings, snow angels, shadow puppets, or home videos if the medium is present to capture the entropy in prigogine's dissipative systems. compare the depth of van gogh's creativity in his paintings from his early career to the end of his career. the scars are deep in the latter. he cut off his ear, checked into a mental asylum, and churned out "the starry night", "wheatfield with crows" and "sorrowing old man" all in 6 months before he killed himself one year later. someone like basquiat, for example, was all chaos from beginning to end. the chaos present at the time of their deaths are captured and reflected in their respective environments that house their legacies today.

Pretty much painted what there mito could not stabilise.. Art becomes the body's last ditch transduction of chaos into meaning. Creativity is biology's refusal to die quietly.. This would be encoded in all but expressed differently. It gets quite deep the more you think about it..

# Thread: How to test safe wifi distance bungalows vietnam?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-test-safe-wifi-distance-bungalows-vietnam.29366/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Thijs Oosting — 2024-08-26T13:52:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-test-safe-wifi-distance-bungalows-vietnam.29366/post-338921>

My twin brother and I are planning to book a bungalow in Vietnam on a beach resort. If we enjoy the bungalow we will book it for a year. Each bungalow has wifi. We contacted the owner and he will remove the wifi station/booster in our bungalow so that we can get cabled internet instead.

The question remains: will the wifi from the neigboring bungalows still be a problem? How do we test for that? Is it as simple as testing whether our phones can receive a wifi signal? And if they do, is 1 bar on the phone still safe? Or do we need other equipment to test?

On the photos below you can see the distance between the bungalows

### OptimalSkr — 2024-08-26T14:44:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-test-safe-wifi-distance-bungalows-vietnam.29366/post-338922>

You'll never know for sure until you get a real RF meter --- for $200 dollars its worth the peace of mind

### Mat.I — 2024-08-26T14:48:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-test-safe-wifi-distance-bungalows-vietnam.29366/post-338923>

> [Thijs Oosting said:](/goto/post?id=338921)
>
> My twin brother and I are planning to book a bungalow in Vietnam on a beach resort. If we enjoy the bungalow we will book it for a year. Each bungalow has wifi. We contacted the owner and he will remove the wifi station/booster in our bungalow so that we can get cabled internet instead.
>
> The question remains: will the wifi from the neigboring bungalows still be a problem? How do we test for that? Is it as simple as testing whether our phones can receive a wifi signal? And if they do, is 1 bar on the phone still safe? Or do we need other equipment to test?
>
> On the photos below you can see the distance between the bungalows

You need to measure this with an microwave RF meter and each n= 1 in terms of how RF effects you is different. If you're not RF sensitive then maybe you can handle more, however, if striving to be optimal and to limit as much EMF as possible especially where you are sleeping you will need to measure it to understand what your are dealing with.

### Thijs Oosting — 2024-08-26T16:43:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-test-safe-wifi-distance-bungalows-vietnam.29366/post-338928>

Ok thank you. We have a TriField EMF meter model TF2 which includes an RF mode. How many mW/m2 is safe? We are dealing with adrenal fatigue, ashtma and gut issues and need to maximize internal melanin production.

### Saichi — 2024-08-27T01:09:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-test-safe-wifi-distance-bungalows-vietnam.29366/post-338946>

Groundhog Day every day here. People who ask for help don't even read this forum and the same who need spoon-fed wouldn't do the mitigation work anyways.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-08-27T13:42:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-test-safe-wifi-distance-bungalows-vietnam.29366/post-338958>

> [Thijs Oosting said:](/goto/post?id=338921)
>
> My twin brother and I are planning to book a bungalow in Vietnam on a beach resort. If we enjoy the bungalow we will book it for a year. Each bungalow has wifi. We contacted the owner and he will remove the wifi station/booster in our bungalow so that we can get cabled internet instead.
>
> The question remains: will the wifi from the neigboring bungalows still be a problem? How do we test for that? Is it as simple as testing whether our phones can receive a wifi signal? And if they do, is 1 bar on the phone still safe? Or do we need other equipment to test?
>
> On the photos below you can see the distance between the bungalows

Your question is like someone who is allergic to cooking smells and so only eats salad, asking - will my neighbor‘s cooking bother me?

You seem to be going to a lot of effort to in part avoid nnEMF. Why would you not invest in the equipment to measure? Most WiFi routers have a range of 100ft to 200ft these days. Before making a one year commitment I would measure with a decent RF meter - Safe and Sound Pro 2 ($400). It is not just the Wi-Fi router, it’s also the computers and phones etc. connected to them. How close is the nearest cell tower? What are the electric fields like? All those bungalows look pretty close to each other.

### Thijs Oosting — 2024-08-27T19:55:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-test-safe-wifi-distance-bungalows-vietnam.29366/post-338978>

Thanks Anthony! We just purchased a Safe and Sound Pro 2 a few hours back haha. Do you perhaps know what measurements would be safe? Would that be up to 0.010 uw/m2?

### Anthony Smith — 2024-08-28T05:00:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-test-safe-wifi-distance-bungalows-vietnam.29366/post-338992>

I believe the BBI recommendation is <0.1 micro watts per meter square is “of no concern”
0.1 - 10 “slight concern”
10 - 1000 “severe concern”
\>1000 “extreme concern”

In this modern world with all the tech for most <10 consider good (unless you are already EHS)

### Thijs Oosting — 2024-08-29T12:59:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-test-safe-wifi-distance-bungalows-vietnam.29366/post-339038>

Thanks Anthony. This is really helpful!

### Thijs Oosting — 2024-08-30T17:45:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-test-safe-wifi-distance-bungalows-vietnam.29366/post-339086>

We may want to sleep in a tent on the beach and we wonder how we can ground ourselves? We have a bought a copper sheet to sleep on. We can connect a copper wire to it and stick it in the ground. Would that be a good way to ground? and do you think we need to buy a body voltage meter for testing? Or is testing on the beach not necessary?

This is the sheet:

[Amazon.com](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B07WTQGGNM/ref=pe_386300_440135490_TE_simp_item_image)

This is the copper wire:

[https://www.amazon.com/dp/B07GD8WL5V/ref=pe\_386300\_440135490\_TE\_simp\_item\_image?th=1](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B07GD8WL5V/ref=pe_386300_440135490_TE_simp_item_image?th=1)

### Anthony Smith — 2024-08-31T19:49:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-test-safe-wifi-distance-bungalows-vietnam.29366/post-339122>

> [Thijs Oosting said:](/goto/post?id=339086)
>
> We may want to sleep in a tent on the beach and we wonder how we can ground ourselves? We have a bought a copper sheet to sleep on. We can connect a copper wire to it and stick it in the ground. Would that be a good way to ground? and do you think we need to buy a body voltage meter for testing? Or is testing on the beach not necessary?
>
> This is the sheet:
>
> [Amazon.com](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B07WTQGGNM/ref=pe_386300_440135490_TE_simp_item_image)
>
>
> This is the copper wire:
>
> [https://www.amazon.com/dp/B07GD8WL5V/ref=pe\_386300\_440135490\_TE\_simp\_item\_image?th=1](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B07GD8WL5V/ref=pe_386300_440135490_TE_simp_item_image?th=1)

The ground IS the ground . . . If you are sleeping dirrectly on the ground you are grounded. If you want something between you and the ground because of dampness, sand critters etc the copper sheep could work instead of a plastic ground sheet that would insulate you. Why add a wire? You could use a canvas cot with metal legs. Still need to measure to find a good low RF location.

# Thread: Daylight Blueblockers Myopia
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daylight-blueblockers-myopia.29369/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BabyBecca — 2024-08-27T16:15:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daylight-blueblockers-myopia.29369/post-338964>

My eyeglass prescription is -750. I stopped wearing contact lens 4 months ago. Anyone know of place to get prescription daylight blue blockers with my prescription? Looks like RA Optics glasses go up to -600. Thanks!

### Johan Lindøm — 2024-08-27T16:46:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daylight-blueblockers-myopia.29369/post-338965>

You can visit any optician. In Oslo, we have an independent optician that is not part of a chain, where I can request glasses designed to block specific wavelengths, such as everything below 465 nm or 550 nm, based on your preferences. For added assurance, I brought a spectroscope to verify my order. Opticians should carry a wide range of test lenses, and if they don’t have what you need in stock, they should be able to order the appropriate lenses from their suppliers. Your prescription will of course be tailored to the glasses you need.

### Daniel Renaud — 2024-08-27T17:05:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daylight-blueblockers-myopia.29369/post-338968>

I have worn glasses for more than 60 years. Myopia and astigmatism. Pretty strong prescription. I stopped wearing almost entirely a few years ago, and started getting more sun (basic Uncle Jack protocol) and the last time I went to the optometrist my prescription changed for the better significantly for the first time ever. The optometrist was shocked. Amusingly he had no interest in how I achieved that miracle.

### Emmanuela — 2024-08-28T04:08:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wonderjunkie-podcast-with-jack-kruse.29371/post-338990>

Have you all listened to it yet? It’s goooood. The guy made a BS intro where he makes it sound like Dr Jack was attacking him. But really it’s a history lesson and brief summary of Dr Mary’s monkey.
I read Dr Mary’s Monkey but this goes deeper into the history piece and I love real history.
Plus, the whole “people are just trying to raise their kids and be good people” line makes me go completely bonkers. If you can’t go the extra mile for your kids, it’s no good. Not for the world we live in. Everyone with children has the responsibility to do better, ask more questions, and try harder. Makes me mad when people use that excuse.
As always, thanks Uncle Jack for all your blogs, podcasts, and information you freely share.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-29T14:53:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wonderjunkie-podcast-with-jack-kruse.29371/post-339042>

This one is BETTER.

New pod alert.

If you are silent about your beliefs because you are worried about offending others or a paradigm in power youre doing life wrong. The message it sends out to the cosmos is that you have no conviction in your beliefs. It tells you that YOUR beliefs really are not that important to you and are superfluous. No one will listen to your ideas.

The other message this sends out to world is that you care more about what people think about you is more important than your ideas. That tells everyone youre doing life wrong.

No one will listen to you.

Here is the lesson of this podcast: When you stand up for what is right and true, you will receiver hate & love from the cosmos. One thing is certain, the entire cosmos woll know what you are really fighting for. And there will be no mistaking of your intentions.

Knowing a concept incorrectly is more dangerous than skipping a concept when you are searching for wisdom. The illusion of knowledge is just like drinking too much wine with your beliefs. I'd suggest you never get drunk on your own dogma. This includes the history that the "victors" wrote for you in history books.

This idea is simply stating that ignoring may not harm you as much as partial or incomplete knowledge may do. Today this idea is harming millions in the centralized world of healthcare and few realize it.

They are inebriated with many false beliefs. Some people out there truly believe think they can help others with their incomplete knowledge but the reality is they are only creating deeper problems than a firm solution. This is the deep problem with the illusion of knowledge.
This podcast will shake you to you core. That I am positive of.

"To a seed the process of growth is a personal disaster". Let the disaster transforms you. The Disaster, is The Teacher. When the lesson is learned, the teacher will leave. -Make It Matter.

Never judge each day by the harvest you reap and the seeds you plant. Some of those “seeds” have no idea of their potential when we decide to bury them and drown them in water. They think our intentions harmed them when they were done with great intention. To a seed, the process of growth is a personal disaster where the force brought to bear on it seemingly destroys it, but what really looks destructive to the seed is the growth and discomfort that the seed needs to become a tree in its future. Initial appearances may not tell you what the mitochondriac in me has in store for you.

**THE WINDEX EVENT IS NOW AVAILABLE**

Here’s the webinar page for the event: I am only opening up 50 slots for Saturday's Q&A now. I hope you all enjoy Monster. It was a lot of work to complete this and deal with all the Big Tech assh@le platforms.

Do you have a network around you that acts like Windex for you? Windex is used to help clean debris from your lenses. Windex is a catalyst for change. Confused? Instead of better glasses, our dopamine network of people around us can help give us better eyes to see things. This is especially true because of what the government has done to millions with MKULTRA. This is gang warfare at global scale to brains.

If we choose incorrectly, it can harm us. I love meeting people in real life, and I remain pretty ambivalent about virtual introductions on social media. That all being said, networking is NOT just how many people we meet or how many followers or likes we get. It is how many people we educate, help, recommend, and introduce to others to get them closer to their goals. This presentation will help you understand what they have done to our world.

### eaandkw — 2024-08-29T17:14:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/unplugged-phone.29374/post-339054>

If you are worried about security and privacy you basically should stop using a cell phone. They are insecure by design. Now, you may not be that worried that you would stop using the cell phones. But understand you can be geo-located just by using the cell towers. 5G is probably the most accurate and gets less accurate as you go down the radio spectrum. If you use 'apps' they have more access to your phone and data (to possibly include conversations), than they would if you used a browser on your phone. This is why there is always a push for you to use the app and not the browser.

# Thread: How to chose a career?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-chose-a-career.29375/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-29T02:18:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-chose-a-career.29375/post-339024>

**HOW TO CHOOSE YOUR CAREER IN LIFE?**

Growing up as a human being, one of the most important choices you will make is the choice of what job to do.
That is because, it is your job that will put food on your table, take care of your family, give you the kind of life you want etc.
By job here, I mean either a job, or a skill, or a business.
It can be anyone.
But what is the thing that should determine the career you will choose?
You see, there are a few things to look at, before picking a career.
First of all, choose what career you want to be in. Something youre passionate about.
Yes, what do you want to be?
You may want to be a doctor, a lawyer, a singer, a footballer etc.
Go for what you want.
There is a reason for this.
It is easier for you to work hard when you are doing something you want to do, rather than something you don't want to do.
And hard work always brings success.
However, if you are in a situation where the thing you want to do, cannot give you the type of money you want to have, then you should look for the career that will give you money.
Yes, this is important because, sometimes, you may want to do something, but if the thing you want to do does not give you money, you will soon get frustrated in it.
So as you are choosing what you want to do, make sure it is something that will give you money.
There are some careers however, that will actually give you money, but not at the starting point.
These types of careers will only begin to bring you money much later as you go on through life.
If you are in such a career, then you must learn to combine that career with a job for the time being, until you get to that phase where your career gets you money.
So when choosing a career, it should first be about what you want to do.
And if what you want to do does not have the potential to give you the money you want to have, then leave it and choose what will give you that kind of money.
And if your career is the type that does not pay immediately, then combine it with a job, especially at the beginning.

My advice to anyone enter adulthood and into a any career:

**Focus your energy, guard your time, train your mind and body, think for yourself, curate your friends and environment, keep your promises, stay optimistic and constructive to your tribe while upgrading the world.**

### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-29T02:27:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-chose-a-career.29375/post-339026>

**IF YOU DON'T FIND YOUR PASSION, DON'T EXPECT YOUR LIFE TO WORK AS YOUR DREAMS**

You have been told by some teachers and speakers that if you want to succeed in life, you have to find your passion.
According to them, your passion is that thing you can do for free, without even getting paid.
But they ignore the fact that, to succeed, you may need back breaking hardwork.
You will get tired sometimes.
You may want to give up sometimes.
Your passion is not the way to success.
A lot of young men are very passionate about sports, but how many of them are succeeding through sports? Very few.
When you find your passion, and you try to sell your passion, you will most likely end up realizing that you are the only one passionate about your passion.
The people you are trying to sell to are real human beings, with their own needs and wants, who do not care about how passionate you are with what you do.
Rather, find your purpose instead.
It is your purpose that will sell.
What is a purpose in this case?
Your purpose is that thing you can do to positively impact the lives of others.
So while your passion focuses on you, your purpose focuses on others.
If the focus of your production is on you, then you are the only one who will love and buy what you produce.
A good case study of this fact is a car produced in the 1950s, called the Ford Edsel.
You see, the staff members of Ford corporation wanted to produce a supercar.
They loved the idea, the concept design and everything about the car.
They named the car, Edsel.
If you are intelligent, you will Yandex about the Ford Edsel after reading this.
So they were able to come up with a good design for it.
In fact, during the design period, they closed up the studio from the mass media so that nobody will be able to copy the Edsel design.
After everything was done, the Edsel was produced.
The result?
No sales.
Why was that?
Because even though the Ford members of staff and management were passionate about the car, the members of the public were not interested in the passion of the Ford designers.
That is because, when you get passionate about something, you are passionate because it serves your interest.
But when you find a purpose, that purpose serves people's interests. And people are going to buy into things that serve their interests far more than they will buy into things that serve your interest.
So pursue purpose rather than passion.

### Mat.I — 2024-08-29T03:16:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-chose-a-career.29375/post-339032>

Thanks Jack.
My thoughts on this -
Your career could also be something you are really good at to earn dollars, and eventually fuel your life purpose. Your purpose could be something completely unrelated to your career that does not necessarily need to be the primary source of income. I struggled with this quite a bit in the past accepting the fact that just because you are good at your career and an "expert" in the field, does not necessarily mean you are fulfilling your life purpose. My life purpose is in a different field, hence, why I'm on here. I know I must play the long game to get there, build and get the necessary things in place to be able to step away.

### Justin Goldberg — 2024-09-18T06:45:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-chose-a-career.29375/post-339683>

What do you say for people who are lonely? Not a regular lonely. Sometimes being the smartest person I know, I feel terribly alone. Hanging with bitcoiners in real life has certainly helped this.

For example, I never felt at ease at a bar because I can't strike up conversations with regular people.

My faith helps too with feeling loved and wanted, but the congregants aren't on my level of understanding.

Thanks again for the advice on clubhouse, I will never forget it and I have taken it to heart. I am making efforts in exiting the USA thunderdome.

### eaandkw — 2024-09-18T17:57:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-chose-a-career.29375/post-339699>

I'll give my two cents. First, try to understand the lifestyle you want to live. From there look for jobs or careers that can provide that lifestyle and not make you want to off yourself. Second, understand what you are giving up or what you are missing out on while pursuing that job or career. And lastly, always have an exit strategy. Meaning try not to have all your eggs in one basket. This doesn't necessarily mean working multiple jobs or having multiple streams of income. But don't lock yourself into a situation you can't get out of by having lots of debt.

25-50 years ago having a stable job was ideal and the ticket to a good life. Today I think mobility is more important. Being able to move to where jobs are, to move careers when needed, or even to work for yourself I think is more important today.

Granted, most of us are probably on the older side of life. This advice is more effective the younger you are but it still applies to us. You just have to acknowledge reality. For example, I might have been really good and strong when I was younger but, at about 50 years old I'm not going to be a world-class powerlifter. I can, however, pivot my experience or knowledge into something that involves the gym or powerlifting competitions. I could be a coach or personal trainer.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-09-18T20:21:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-chose-a-career.29375/post-339707>

Love what you do (do what you love) . . . keep your expenses lower than your income . . . spend more time with positive people than negative ones.

### Brogan — 2024-09-19T01:12:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-chose-a-career.29375/post-339713>

Recognize that it's part and parcel of being a free-thinking human; the farther you develop yourself from the herd, the less you'll be able to relate to most people, and vice versa. Find tribe by forcing yourself to do different things around different people. Get as comfortable with your mind and heart as possible, and have compassion for your all-too-human need for social interaction.

> So pursue purpose rather than passion.

Hi Dr Kruse,

Wise words – you truly are an inspiration and have offered so much to so many people. I was listening to one of your interviews on YouTube the other night and heard you talking about how much death you deal with as a neurosurgeon. I also heard you talking about the surgery you performed for the teenage girl who had been attacked and had many shards of bone lodged in her brain and how you were able to retrieve them successfully even without the assistance of imagery. What an awe-inspiring story! It was apparent how connected you are still to that experience.

Hearing you talk about the work you do and recount that story also really puts your personality into perspective. Some people may find you harsh and abrasive (which I know isn’t news to you) but if those people understood the way you are in the context of the kind of things you deal with, I think your no-bullshit approach to things would make much more sense to them.

I’ve had many interests and passions thus far in my life, like reading, writing, making art, learning languages, photography, acting, philosophy, activism and much more. I’ve also been inspired by many talented, wise, and impressive individuals. But I have to say that you are my biggest inspiration so far. For the last year and a half, I have been quite hell-bent on the idea of studying medicine. And whilst it remains to be seen whether or not that comes to fruition, I feel very called to bridge the gap between what modern medicine currently is and what it should be. The crap people are fed versus the truth that they need.

Thank you for doing the work that you do. I truly believe that it has a ripple effect and that if enough people choose to wear the truth, rather than hide from it, then the truth will shine its light over great distances.

Thanks again,

Kayla

### Sandy Abram — 2024-10-10T03:31:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-chose-a-career.29375/post-340728>

As a Mum of two teenagers the journey of helping to guide my children as they explore their future careers, studies and dreams has become so much more difficult.

Once upon a time, parents would be in tears "what do you mean you're not going to University?!". Now I see myself in a few years saying, "why would you want to do a crazy thing like go to University?!".

Universities are not what they once appeared to be. Once a placed students aspired to go (and parents pushed them to go) it's now become very apparent that Uni's are a key part of the centralised corrupt system. Young adults become debt slaves before they walk out the door, they undertake studies sponsored by corrupt corporations and in regards to the University itself, they are subjected to woke agendas and dangerous ideologies.

So now what? What do you do if your child's passion and purpose intersects with an evil education system that is broken? Or worse, a profession where unless you comply, all your hard work and experience is gone in an instant.

As we try and build a more decentralised world, hopefully one day soon, we see decentralised education platforms a part of that picture.
