> These types of careers will only begin to bring you money much later as you go on through life.
> If you are in such a career, then you must learn to combine that career with a job for the time being, until you get to that phase where your career gets you money.
> So when choosing a career, it should first be about what you want to do.
> And if what you want to do does not have the potential to give you the money you want to have, then leave it and choose what will give you that kind of money.
> And if your career is the type that does not pay immediately, then combine it with a job, especially at the beginning.
>
>
> My advice to anyone enter adulthood and into a any career:
>
>
> **Focus your energy, guard your time, train your mind and body, think for yourself, curate your friends and environment, keep your promises, stay optimistic and constructive to your tribe while upgrading the world.**

Dr Jack Kruse, I wish I had found you sooner!

### Valerie888 — 2025-11-27T04:02:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-chose-a-career.29375/post-363243>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=339024)
>
> Focus your energy, guard your time, train your mind and body, think for yourself, curate your friends and environment, keep your promises, stay optimistic and constructive to your tribe while upgrading the world.

I'm going to write this in calligraphy and put it somewhere I can see every day.

### DLO — 2026-05-01T18:02:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-chose-a-career.29375/post-372734>

> [Sandy Abram said:](/goto/post?id=340728)
>
> As a Mum of two teenagers the journey of helping to guide my children as they explore their future careers, studies and dreams has become so much more difficult.
>
> Once upon a time, parents would be in tears "what do you mean you're not going to University?!". Now I see myself in a few years saying, "why would you want to do a crazy thing like go to University?!".
>
> Universities are not what they once appeared to be. Once a placed students aspired to go (and parents pushed them to go) it's now become very apparent that Uni's are a key part of the centralised corrupt system. Young adults become debt slaves before they walk out the door, they undertake studies sponsored by corrupt corporations and in regards to the University itself, they are subjected to woke agendas and dangerous ideologies.
>
> So now what? What do you do if your child's passion and purpose intersects with an evil education system that is broken? Or worse, a profession where unless you comply, all your hard work and experience is gone in an instant.
>
> As we try and build a more decentralised world, hopefully one day soon, we see decentralised education platforms a part of that picture.

Lotsa boomers phasing out businesses, take over the business and pay it off with monthly business income. 0 down.

# Thread: WHAT AN EDUCATION DOES NOT TEACH YOU?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-an-education-does-not-teach-you.29376/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-29T02:23:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-an-education-does-not-teach-you.29376/post-339025>

**LECTURERS IN SCHOLL WILL NEVER TELL YOU THIS IN THE UNIVERSITY OR COLLEGE.**

If you are invited for a job interview, at the entrance of the gate or within the Organization's premises:

1.Politely greet everybody. I have seen MDs and Managers disguise and stand at the gate to observe interviewees for a deep reason.
2\. Do not go inside and start charging your phone or begin to look for where to charge your phone.
3\. Do not go inside and begin to chat with your phone or make unnecessary calls. Put the phone inside your bag and turn it OFF. They could be monitoring you through the camera.
4\. Avoid too much talk with fellow interviewees. Don't go there and begin to argue about the government of the day, escalated food prices and even lack of employment opportunities. Just be calm and positive.
5\. Don't start praying and speaking in tongues to demonstrate your religiosity and closeness to God. Pray at home before going.
6\. If they offer you Tea or food, Please reject it politely and thank them. You can take after the interview. If you take that Tea or food, you are out.
7\. Don't chew gum, biscuits or anything. Go with a book and be reading it. Have a notebook and pen ready to write during the interview.
8\. Never ever criticize your previous employer even if they deserve it.
9\. If you see somebody you know in the organization, either as a staff or a fellow interviewee, greet him officially. Avoid creating an impression that you know him.
10\. Do not appear too poor, helpless and inferior before the interviewer. Forget about problems we face in the USA, your personal and family problems. Focus on the job.
11\. After the interview, put up a smiling face, thank them and leave quickly.
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. # Thread: How to read blogs, tweets, and more on a e-ink screen (no blue light)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-read-blogs-tweets-and-more-on-a-e-ink-screen-no-blue-light.29379/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### SanderC — 2024-08-29T19:50:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-read-blogs-tweets-and-more-on-a-e-ink-screen-no-blue-light.29379/post-339060>

Hi everyone.

I read all uncle Jack's blogs, Twitter posts and other things from the internet on the _Reader_ app on an e-ink screen. This SIGNIFICANTLY reduces my blue light exposure, and it's super readable vs blue screens in the sun.

**Hardware**

E-ink tablet. I use BOOX: [https://shop.boox.com/products/tabx](https://shop.boox.com/products/tabx)

I will be switching to the Daylight computer once it arrives (pre-ordered). The Daylight computer is basically an e-ink screen but with fluidity (60 hz).

If you don't use the backlight, it's blue-light free. You can also enable Airplane mode. It has an android app store so you can download any app just like on another tablet.

You also have this in [phone size](https://shop.boox.com/products/palma?gad_source=1&gbraid=0AAAAAC9FI3055B4M4d1UJgax1vX2jG32k&gclid=CjwKCAjwuMC2BhA7EiwAmJKRrLHDZTTd5BX0lZy4aqpKA0IZ83nHJPkuLrHfijPs-7z--TjHwhSb7hoCbP4QAvD_BwE) if you're looking to use have a blue-light free phone (wifi only). Not sure how good it is.

**Software**

Readwise Reader: [https://readwise.io/read](https://readwise.io/read)

It's basically one feed where you can import all the stuff from all over the internet (pdf, ebook, twitter feeds, online webpages...)

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

You can also have a [Readwise paid account](https://readwise.io), which enables you to save highlights from these blogs, twittter feeds etc, and receive one email newsletter a day with random highlights/quotes.

Download this Reader app on the e-ink tablet, read them in the sun and off you go!!

Hope this helps and let me know if you have any questions.

### metallikat36 — 2024-08-30T02:03:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clothing-that-doesnt-block-light.29383/post-339072>

White linen is apparently best, but still seems to block 80% of light:

[https://pismin.com/10.1111/php.12292](https://pismin.com/10.1111/php.12292)

Some companies have made special fabrics designed to let more light through, but none publish data, one has a contradictory statements (like "blocks UVB while not blocking vitamin D production"), none specify which types of UV, and none specify effects on other wavelengths. Plus the lines of apparel are very limited and expensive. Aside from getting naked, anyone find any clothing that helps substantially? Though I am not exactly interested in wearing nothing but fish net or see-through items. This guy's top is just shouting, "I'm ready to big my presentation to the executives":

### metallikat36 — 2024-09-04T11:03:36+0300

### metallikat36 — 2024-09-04T11:12:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clothing-that-doesnt-block-light.29383/post-339235>

There are some really scandalous see-through net / mesh shorts for when you are alone, but ain't gonna try that in public...

In theory, I have an idea. Image a chess board. The black squares and the white squares are out of phase with each other. Imagine the black squares are like one layer of mesh fabric, and the white squares are a second layer of mesh fabric. Then imagine the inside of the first layer of fabric is highly reflective. Light would pass through the holes in the first layer of fabric, and reflectivity on the inside of that layer would help light reach your skin. All while blocking direct vision. In other words, maybe someone can invent clothing that "bends" (or rather redirects) the path of light. Or to say it another way: The visible outside layer of fabric is somehow "one-way" for light. Light can pass through its gaps, but not get out again, and bounce around until it hits the skin.

This one looks good so long as your admirers are at a distance:

[https://www.amazon.com/YiZYiF-Through-Shorts-Underwear-Watershort/dp/B07TWQ8BZH/](https://www.amazon.com/YiZYiF-Through-Shorts-Underwear-Watershort/dp/B07TWQ8BZH/)

### metallikat36 — 2024-09-04T13:59:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clothing-that-doesnt-block-light.29383/post-339238>

So it occured to me that windows can have one-way tinting. We need that, but for clothes. Apparently, in theater they use a fabric called scrim which sounds like it is some sort of one-way fabric:

### [Sharkstooth Scrim Fabrics](https://www.rosebrand.com/fabric-stage-curtains-backdrops/sharkstooth-scrim-fabrics.aspx?)

Extra-wide widths and colors. Scrim is an open weave net used extensively in theatre.

 www.rosebrand.com

Hmm...

### Michalis — 2024-09-09T11:10:33+0300

### alipankey — 2025-06-03T00:50:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dubai-vs-uk.29384/post-353795>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=339415)
>
> Dubai has more sun . . . But that requires being outside and exposed to far more nnEMF 5G.
> It is hard to mitigate 5G outside but easy inside . . . But inside there is no sun.

I thought 5G had a very short distance, and that is why you need more antennas, say 150 feet apart in a city, for comprehensive coverage. If that is the indeed the case, and assuming you can avoid regular cell towers, then shouldn't there be few 5g frequencies reaching a human at a beach while grounding?

### Anthony Smith — 2025-06-03T02:34:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dubai-vs-uk.29384/post-353812>

> [alipankey said:](/goto/post?id=353795)
>
> I thought 5G had a very short distance, and that is why you need more antennas, say 150 feet apart in a city, for comprehensive coverage. If that is the indeed the case, and assuming you can avoid regular cell towers, then shouldn't there be few 5g frequencies reaching a human at a beach while grounding?

Totally depends where you are.
The most widely used 5G frequencies (in the us) are below 4 GHz so travel further and penetrate more. 4G travels a good distance and goes through most things including YOU. If your phone works you have RF.

Grounding outside, especially at the beach has massive benefits but does not stop any RF from reaching you. Being protected by a cliff and/or in the water helps.

### S Hudson — 2025-06-03T16:34:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dubai-vs-uk.29384/post-353830>

Some might argue that much of the shite about the UK that falls outside quantum biology (lefty politics, DEI, violent crime... and yes, I accept the MKULTRA link to these) can still be mitigated by living in rural areas. Avoid the cities and built-up areas and you can almost pretend none of it is happening.
Dubai strikes me as a blue light hell

### S Hudson — 2025-07-28T14:09:43+0300

I was thinking about this on the drive home today as a friend of mine has just bought property there. If we take everything we know, Dubai feels like a trap, as if it's there to suck up all those with the awareness to leave the West, only to get stuck in a nnEMF deserted dead zone. are the powers that be that determined to think that far ahead, though?

### Jack Kruse — 2025-07-28T15:34:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dubai-vs-uk.29384/post-357090>

Dubai is not a place I would go unless it was a segway from the UK to somewhere better.

# Thread: New member visiting ES in October
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-visiting-es-in-october.29388/>

**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### swanalamer — 2024-08-31T22:01:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-visiting-es-in-october.29388/post-339127>

Hello, I’m Meredith. I am new to the community and feel grateful for this forum with your welcoming and helpful posts.
I plan on coming to ES in October for 5-6 months to heal from an autoimmune illness and to explore ES as a possible place to move.  I haven’t yet purchased my ticket for I need to see my way to move from where I am, so my date isn’t set. Does anyone know whether if I come mid to late October if it will be much harder to find a rental with the influx of people coming in Nov for different events?

I would like to find a place for a few weeks once I land in ES where I can meet people in the community, then see if there may be solo or group rentals in quiet areas outside of the cities, for I am a bit of a naturalist and prefer living in the quiet of Nature.
Are there any shared households you might guide me to outside of the towns?
I have reached out to Maria Theresa to see if she might have a place, so may start there if dates work.

Does anyone live up near the lakes who can let me know what that area is like? Are the lakes accessible and swimmable, and are there any shared houses with other mito sensitives up there? Are cars essential when out of town? I would like meet people in the community so perhaps this isn’t as possible up there?

Because of the autoimmune illness I would like to be where I can access the magnetism, beach and seafood as much as possible. I read somewhere in the forum that since it is black lava sand that it has the magnetism needed rather than being up on the volcanoes. Is this true?
Can you walk on the black sand barefoot or is it too sharp?

Thanks so much for any guidance you might offer,

Meredith

Hi there. I am looking for recommendations for

\* a 1-2 person tent
\* a sleep mat = hard, flat and no cushion (sleep on belly sometimes)
\* available for EU, Belgium.
\* mild cold proof temperatures are dropping slowly as autumn comes
\* rain proof for Belgium
\* does not offgas (too many) chemicals/synthetic odour

I want to sleep in the garden. My aim is to improve sleep and energy.
If that works I may go camping - alone or w/ my dog.
I am new to camping. Want to spend winter/autumn South.

Has anyone good experiences w/ some tents/mats?

My concern is that the chemicals in tents may leave me wore of then I now am... - given that my brain seems to be struggling due to EHS (leaky BBB, neurotoxins?)

I already spent hours/days looking for tents+mats on amazon etc. but couldn't make up my mind.
The flat mats with no cushioning are expensive. Towards 150-200€. Is that worth it? Or are there good cheaper options? Sleep is an issue here...
What's your experience?

Thanks!

# Thread: LDL Subfraction success
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ldl-subfraction-success.29392/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Franko54 — 2024-09-02T12:03:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ldl-subfraction-success.29392/post-339157>

Had a high CAC score of 1661 and amongst other less than ideal numbers, small dense LDL Subfractions were high (LDL 4 was 0.25mmol/L and LDL 3 1.36mmol/L) - August 23. Eliminated all sources of nnEMF from the home; and changed all lighting to blue light blocking globes; 6-8 months of sunrise; CT and sauna; plenty of UV.
25 OH Vit D steadily increased over this period 134-158nmol/L. I spent a few months in Italy to escape the Australian Winter and I ate my share of carbs (and local wine and seasonal fruit). The markets labelled all their produce with their origin, which was helpful. I also tested the local tap water to check the deuterium levels out of interest, and it was 151.17ppm. The natural spring water I drink locally is sourced from about 100km away, has a D20 of 150.26ppm.
Significantly, I had never got so much sun in my life during this trip.
Anyway, the punchline is LDL 4 is now zero and LDL 3 within range at 0.1. Intermediate LDL also dropped back by 35%, moving me from Type B to Type A LDL phenotype. Hopefully this is a proxy for CAC score burden, and this will move in concert. So very pleased I discovered Dr Kruse and his teachings last year.
Although, my ferritin has moved up to 549 ug/L after this trip and was 500ug/L in August 23, dipped to 295ug/L and then back up. Iron; transferritin and transferritin saturation all within range. Not sure what to make of that?@Jack Kruse , will retest toward the end of the year. Overall, things appear to be heading in the right direction.

### Amandapants11 — 2026-02-05T04:29:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ldl-subfraction-success.29392/post-366378>

Wow, this all sounds very promising. Have you had any subsequent calcium scoring or any other tests? How are they all looking?

### Jack Kruse — 2024-09-02T18:47:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-youre-an-artist-here-is-where-your-journey-should-start.29393/post-339165>

New blog out today that covers how our use of technology is REFLECTED in our art and creativity today. This was the topic of my recent talk at the event held in El Salvador in August of 2024. How does art link to physics? The story is ancient. Democrtitus, in the fifth century B.C. had declared that all the world was composed of only two elements: atomes and the void. This reduction of the myriad of forms to only two was the ultimate in dualistic reasoning. Christianity adopted dualism when it created the strict division between good and evil and heaven and hell. Art is a reflection of the state of the semiconductors that are located between the environment all five of your sense organs. A neurosurgeon is both an artist and a scientist…

I believe the decentralized ones rely more heavily on the artistic side of their brains than the left side. Centralized NS are TAUGHT to rely more heavily on their left sided hemispheres. You can see this in the advice they give. The advice they give is a reflection of the light emitted from the surfaces of their semiconducting brains. Neurosurgeons should learn to rely heavily on their intuitive visual-spatial right-hemispheric mode because of our medical school curriculums. They are polluted with BigHarma ideas. At the same time, our training is obviously scientific. Left-brained logic, reason, and abstract thinking are the stepping-stones leading to the vast scientific literature’s arcane tenets. The need in my profession to shuttle back and forth constantly between these two complementary functions of the human psyche.

Art and physics seem like a strange coupling to the centralized mind. Of the many human disciplines, could there be two that seem more divergent? The artist employs image and metaphor; the physicist uses number and equation. Art encompasses an imaginative realm of aesthetic qualities; physics exists in a world of crisply circumscribed mathematical relationships between quantifiable properties. Traditionally, art has created illusions meant to elicit emotion; physics has been an exact science that made sense Yet, despite what appear to be irreconcilable differences, there is one fundamental feature that solidly connects these disciplines. Revolutionary art and visionary physics are both investigations into the nature of reality of the present. They reflect the state of the environment when they were created. This talk/blog shows you this is not hyperbole, but reality. It shows you how it happened 65K when there was Neaderthals and no modern humans. [https://patreon.com/posts/110094014](https://t.co/1n0a37Sloi)

# Thread: El Salvador visit
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-visit.29394/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ra Beam — 2024-09-02T19:10:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-visit.29394/post-339166>

I’m wanting to come to El Salvador next week, however I know it’s still rainy season. Is the health benefits still good during the rain or no? I’m assuming yes. Didn’t know how much the sun would be covered. Also I’m trying to get a consultation from Dr Kruse while I’m there

### Kirsten F — 2024-09-02T19:12:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-visit.29394/post-339167>

Hello, I am in ES and the sun is great. The most common pattern is sunny and beautiful in the mornings. If it does rain (we just had a few dry days), it is most likely that the rain clouds will gather in the mid to late afternoon.

### Ra Beam — 2024-09-02T19:23:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-visit.29394/post-339168>

> [Kirsten F said:](/goto/post?id=339167)
>
> Hello, I am in ES and the sun is great. The most common pattern is sunny and beautiful in the mornings. If it does rain (we just had a few dry days), it is most likely that the rain clouds will gather in the mid to late afternoon.

Awesome, that’s not too bad then. I figured it would be worse then that. Thanks!

### Kirsten F — 2024-09-02T19:38:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-visit.29394/post-339170>

They still have seasonal rain storms (we had one for 7-8 days in June) where it rained nearly non-stop. I have heard that they are common in October as well. My vote is to not worry about it, come and enjoy. Other than that time in June, it has been beautiful despite being "rainy season".

### Ra Beam — 2024-09-02T19:43:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-visit.29394/post-339171>

> [Kirsten F said:](/goto/post?id=339170)
>
> They still have seasonal rain storms (we had one for 7-8 days in June) where it rained nearly non-stop. I have heard that they are common in October as well. My vote is to not worry about it, come and enjoy. Other than that time in June, it has been beautiful despite being "rainy season".

Fair enough… I just heard that September can be the worst month during said rainy season so that had me questioning if it’s worth the trip. I really want to come though

### pfedorow — 2024-09-02T20:06:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-visit.29394/post-339173>

> [Ra Beam said:](/goto/post?id=339171)
>
> Fair enough… I just heard that September can be the worst month during said rainy season so that had me questioning if it’s worth the trip. I really want to come though

Sun during the day, and rain at night usually.

### jackmck — 2024-09-02T21:05:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-visit.29394/post-339175>

> [Ra Beam said:](/goto/post?id=339171)
>
> Fair enough… I just heard that September can be the worst month during said rainy season so that had me questioning if it’s worth the trip. I really want to come though

It's worth it. You'll get sun. The more time here, the better.

### thejessallen — 2024-09-04T22:05:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-visit.29394/post-339265>

The rain storms often come with lightening, which adds electrons to the earth - when Jack talked about this on his live - he actually said during rainy season is better because we get this extra boost from lightening. And like others said - it mostly rains at night - hardly during day now. When I was searching I read May/June were worst months.

That's when I traveled to explore living here... thinking if I could handle the "worst" months then everything else got easier from there.

### Ra Beam — 2024-09-04T22:21:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-visit.29394/post-339266>

> [thejessallen said:](/goto/post?id=339265)
>
> The rain storms often come with lightening, which adds electrons to the earth - when Jack talked about this on his live - he actually said during rainy season is better because we get this extra boost from lightening. And like others said - it mostly rains at night - hardly during day now. When I was searching I read May/June were worst months.
>
> That's when I traveled to explore living here... thinking if I could handle the "worst" months then everything else got easier from there.

thats an interesting point, I didnt think about that with the lightning. Do you have any beach recommendations? isnt there some black sand ones? idk if thats too hot to be on or what

### jackmck — 2024-09-05T02:51:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-visit.29394/post-339294>

> [Ra Beam said:](/goto/post?id=339266)
>
> thats an interesting point, I didnt think about that with the lightning. Do you have any beach recommendations? isnt there some black sand ones? idk if thats too hot to be on or what

Where are the beaches that aren't black sand in ES?
The black sand isn't too hot to be on. I recommend laying in the surf while you get sun.
Palmarcito is nice. Stay to the end of the beach where the Atami Resort is to avoid RF from the cell tower.
I like Shalpa. Less rocks than Palmarcito or Tunco.

### Ra Beam — 2024-09-05T16:53:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-visit.29394/post-339309>

> [jackmck said:](/goto/post?id=339294)
>
> Where are the beaches that aren't black sand in ES?
> The black sand isn't too hot to be on. I recommend laying in the surf while you get sun.
> Palmarcito is nice. Stay to the end of the beach where the Atami Resort is to avoid RF from the cell tower.
> I like Shalpa. Less rocks than Palmarcito or Tunco.

Thanks for the great tip about avoiding the RF areas of it. I will pay special attention to that.

### pfedorow — 2024-09-05T18:04:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-visit.29394/post-339310>

> [Ra Beam said:](/goto/post?id=339309)
>
> Thanks for the great tip about avoiding the RF areas of it. I will pay special attention to that.

Beaches change all year round in tunco and palmarcito. In my opinion in the summer here Nov - march, palmarcito is way better than shalpa due to the calmer waters and ability to swim way out there.

### Ra Beam — 2024-09-05T18:45:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-visit.29394/post-339313>

> [pfedorow said:](/goto/post?id=339310)
>
> Beaches change all year round in tunco and palmarcito. In my opinion in the summer here Nov - march, palmarcito is way better than shalpa due to the calmer waters and ability to swim way out there.

Love to hear that, enjoying a swim is more ideal so I’ll definitely take that into consideration, thanks!

### Kirsten F — 2024-09-05T20:25:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-visit.29394/post-339314>

I cannot comment on the RF levels, but playa San Blas has been wonderful for swimming in the rainy season (rock free). There are rip tides, but they are well-marked with red flags, and safe areas are also marked.

# Thread: new member, in Brooklyn -- HELLO! :-)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-in-brooklyn-hello.29396/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### meruprecht — 2024-09-02T20:30:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-in-brooklyn-hello.29396/post-339174>

Hello all, hello Dr. Jack Kruse,

Much thanks to Dr. Kruse for all your work and for creating this space. I am looking forward, as part of my journey to healthfulness and happiness, to being part of the community here and learning from folks. I am right now finishing Dr. Kruse's EXCELLENT book and have started my Lepsin Rx reset. Day Two. (I didn't need tests, I did what the doctor said, looked in the mirror and whoops, yep, obese person. ) My son is a big fan of Jack's, and he's the one who let me know about your/his work.

**My situation, health-wise:** I have struggled with obesity for the last 30 years, I just turned 63. I want to change my lifestyle, lose the weight, and be truly healthy for the duration. I was struck by the fact that Dr. Kruse's personal health journey started with weight gain AND a meniscus tear in the knee -- my story is much the same, and I imagine many peoples'. About ten years ago I tore the meniscus in my left knee, the first physical long-lasting debilitation I have had, other than the obesity. I need to lose around 133 pounds, and I now know, Jack, that you lost that amount too. So these parallels, as well as what I'm learning from the book, got me really interested, and now, started. I am, additionally, a dedicated intermittent faster, since 2022, and have always rejected (tried to reject?) the Western diet, which Jack calls SAD. (It is, sad! lol. I also teach Food Studies in my composition classes, which my students love, and which helps me stay on top of things somewhat.)

Also, I was diagnosed in 2021 with uterine cancer, whether it was pre-cancer or cancer I was never clear but the doc said the treatment is the same: full hysterectomy, plus some lymph nodes. After that I was declared by NYU Langone "cancer free," which I hope is fully true -- they said there was no spread, etc. What they did not tell me, and I only now know because a colo-rectal surgeon told me, is that after abdominal surgery people often develop gastro issues? Who knew. The colo-rectal surgeon told me that the uterus is a buffer for the colon, and that when you lose it your colon starts reaching out for other organs as buffers, and you develop "fistulae" (and there is another word he used), which I have. That diagnosis came after a diagnosis of diverticulitis, all of this following the hysterectomy. At the time, a year ago, I was told I needed colo-rectal surgery even though I have no pain and am asymptomatic generally. (The only reason this was diagnosed was because I got a CAT scan ordered by my gynecological oncologist, to make sure everything was good in there, but what it showed were these fistulae, so she sent me to the ER, a truly hell'ish night where they kept going back and forth, do I need emergency surgery or not? It was literal hell -- and, I had JUST had massively invasive surgery less than two years before that.) I felt at the time like jumping to surgery was too far of a leap -- and my doctor kept delaying the surgery, waiting for me to lose weight. Finally I said, if it can keep being delayed (twice), do I REALLY need this surgery? To which he said, Okay, no, let's put it off indefnitely unless I become symptomatic. I haven't.

All of what I am reading, Dr. Kruse, in your book is making me think about all this again. Do I have leaky gut actually? Do I have leptin resistance? Certainly I do, I must. And if I was to change my lifestyle, food intake, and all, as prescribed in the book and the Leptin Reset/PostReset, might my body repair itself through these natural means? Through autophagy? As you say, surgery without surgery. I have never been a believer in Western med., fully, I have never believed in popping pills to solve problems (unless truly medically necessary). My x-husband has terrible type 2 diabetes and takes all these meds which I fear are only making his sickness worse; having read the diabetes chapter last night, Dr. Kruse, I am sending him the book TODAY. (I already sent him _The Diabetes Code_ in response to which he changed nothing. Maybe this book will be the magic.) Anyhow -- that is just a bit about my health situation, why I'm here and reading the book and starting to make the prescribed/suggested changes.

**About me, as in my life:** In terms of my work, I am a professor and scholar, I teach at CUNY in Brooklyn, and I write and publish scholarly books, articles, chapters, alot of them , and the occasional Op Ed. (I'm an English prof., this is my page on MLA Commons: [https://mla.hcommons.org/members/meruprecht/](https://mla.hcommons.org/members/meruprecht/) ) I'm also a single mother, although my children are all grown up now, both in their 40's. My son owns two Brooklyn restaurants and he is very into Dr. Kruse and related; my daughter is a geoarchaeologist who does amazing work around cultural heritage rights for Native American communities.

As a final note, I've been thinking about how, when I was young I was never heavy, I was thin, fit, rode my bike for hours daily, climbed trees all the time, played softball---it was after a series of traumas leading up to losing my father in 1990 that, afterwards, I pretty much fell apart and started putting on this weight. But I've been thinking, along with all of what I have learned from Dr. Kruse, and hope to learn by being here, that we can re-find our original healthy selves, whether by that we mean "evolutionarily" or we mean literally our own life.

I am glad to be here, to have found Dr. Kruse's work, grateful also to my son for sending me this way -- and looking forward to learning everything Jack and everyone here will teach me, and also being a support to others however I can be.

Peace, love, solidarity--
~Maureen

### India Rose Hopper — 2024-09-11T21:36:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-in-brooklyn-hello.29396/post-339487>

Welcome!
I don’t know if you are a member and can watch the webinars but if so, I am re watching the one from December 2012 about female fat loss. It is really eye opening and I will be watching it several more time cause that’s how I learn
Anyway, I have been in El Salvador since last October and have lost over 33 lbs just by doing the things. It’s really amazing! Glad you’re here and that you listened to your son

I would highly recommend everyone learns to dowse or use a pendulum.
Great for checking if supplements & how many, food or drinks are good for you.
Also should I buy this?? or read/buy this book, or will a magnetico benefit my health?
Is there any geopathic stress here - can use it to locate underground streams etc.
Should I move here? Will this person be my soul mate / or be happy to follow Jacks protocols? - Can use this if join a dating app. Will this course benefit me? Etc.

Plenty of free info out there - [https://lettertorobin.wordpress.com/](https://lettertorobin.wordpress.com/)
Lots of interesting newsletters - [https://dowsingaustralia.com/](https://dowsingaustralia.com/)
Raymond has lots of interesting protocols including how to energize water & heal things [https://www.raymongrace.us/#/](https://www.raymongrace.us/#/)
Dale Olson - Pendulum charts are fantastic they include -
Health and healing with the right:
Foods, remedies, vitamins, minerals, diet, nutritional supplements, amino acids, tissue salts, anti-oxidants, enzymes, flower essences, herbs...

Probability of success for personal, career, business or consumer decisions, lost objects, water, land, house, locations, directions...

Determine: allergies, origin of dysfunction, infections, parasites, teeth-mouth health, cancer prevention, cancer reversal, immune system health, chronic fatigue, relationship compatibility, personal motivators, family lineage, what is going on, who is involved, what am I telling myself, how to change my life, healing remedies, source of condition, chakras, health, healing, and well-being...

Analysis: body, mind, spirit, plant/soil, house inventory, automobile diagnosis, business decisions...

There is FB pages with lots of help

# Thread: CT and recovering from covid
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-recovering-from-covid.29398/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sheas — 2024-09-03T17:07:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-recovering-from-covid.29398/post-339199>

I rarely get sick. Rarely catch things going around. However....my husband and I had to fly to MIA from DFW just for a night to take care of something and two days after we got home it hit me. And it hit me hard. I never tested but I knew it was covid just from my symptoms. I baked in the sun as much as I could even with fever and achy and feeling like hell. My routine was get up at 6, go outside with the sunrise then lay on a lounger until about noon. Then I would come inside, take an epson salt bath and sleep.. for 3 days that is all I did. I could not tolerate a cold plunge at all. So I stayed with the heat and listened to my body. Today is day 12 and getting much better just congested still and my energy is not back to 100%. Still baking in the sun and actually quite tan except today and yesterday it has been raining. Anyway, wondering what others have done and how CT could help at this point. I drained my tub yesterday and the cold water felt good when I reached in to pull the plug, so thinking it might be beneficial now. I have been cold plunging for a few years so quite acclimated and I only take cold showers. Except these last 12 days I have been wanting to feel warmth. Wondering what others have done to recover from this and get their energy back. Should I force a plunge or is it too much stress right now.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-09-03T18:18:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-recovering-from-covid.29398/post-339201>

I have read in several places (not just one source) that people have experienced relief of symptoms with nicotine patches or gum. People with long Covid report getting taste and smell back in days. They say cut a 7mg patch into 4 and use 1/4 each day.

### Saichi — 2024-09-03T19:31:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-recovering-from-covid.29398/post-339204>

Cold, flu, covid, corona, whatever you want to call it is not contagious hence all tests to determine if you have it are not real. You became sick because of the nnemf onslaught of flying and your environments.

### sheas — 2024-09-03T20:27:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-recovering-from-covid.29398/post-339209>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=339201)
>
> I have read in several places (not just one source) that people have experienced relief of symptoms with nicotine patches or gum. People with long Covid report getting taste and smell back in days. They say cut a 7mg patch into 4 and use 1/4 each day.

# Thread: CENTRALIZED PEDIATRICIANS ARE RUNNING DISEASE CAMPS FOR centralized medicine.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/centralized-pediatricians-are-running-disease-camps-for-centralized-medicine.29402/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-09-04T17:14:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/centralized-pediatricians-are-running-disease-camps-for-centralized-medicine.29402/post-339248>

I ran the numbers for a typical pediatrics practice and can confirm a least 50% of their revenue comes from vaccines. What's not mentioned here is that their overhead is 50%, so without vaccines they can't even stay in business. A single vaccine administration fee for the pediatrician is only $35, but they do so many of them that it really adds up: An average pediatrics practice has 1500 patients per provider (some have twice as much or more).

Those 1500 patients get 60 shots over the course of birth to 18 years, which is 1,620,000 total shots administered in 18 years, or an average of 5,000 vaccines administered per year. That’s $175,000 in vaccine administration fees alone at $35 a pop. This is essentially the median salary of a pediatrician. Of course most practices bring in about double to account for their overhead expenses, which run 50-60%. But without the $35 payments there would be basically nothing left over. Pediatrics is not economic for the most part without vaccines.

And this doesn't even count the bonus payments they get for vaccinating 85%+ of their practice.

Edward Mandell House (originally “Huis” which became “House”) was born July 26, 1858 in Houston, Texas. He became active in Texas politics and served as an advisor to President Woodrow Wilson, particularly in the area of foreign affairs. House functioned as Wilson's chief negotiator in Europe during the negotiations for peace (1917-1919), and as chief deputy for Wilson at the Paris Peace Conference. He died on March 28, 1938 in New York City.

DATA on why I am right: What was the real purpose of retirement account creation under the Reagan administrations? It began in 1981when Reagan hatched a plan with his military contractors who got him elected as California's govenor and then as President. What was the first payback?

The **Iran–Contra affair** was a [political scandal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Political_scandal) in the United States that occurred during the first and second term of the [Reagan administration](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reagan_administration). Between 1981 and 1986, senior administration officials secretly facilitated the illegal sale of arms to [Iran](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Islamic_Republic_of_Iran), which was subject to an [arms embargo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arms_embargo) at the time. Because it was successful, the Reagan legal team came up with the idea to launer money on a larger scale to fund military wars using the Public's retirements accounts in the future as a slush fund to fund covert operations

During 1981 the Reagan administration hoped to use the proceeds of the arms sale to fund the [Contras](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Contras), an anti-[Sandinista](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sandinista_National_Liberation_Front) rebel group in [Nicaragua](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nicaragua). Under the [Boland Amendment](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Boland_Amendment), further funding of the Contras by [legislative](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States_Congress) appropriations was prohibited by [Congress](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States_Congress), but the Reagan administration figured out a [loophole](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Loophole) by secretively using non-appropriated funds instead.

How did this scandal set the stage for COVID? The DoD took our retirement money that was started under the Reagan administration and laundered it for their programs and forever wars and they now came up with the repayment plan to lower life expectancy to pay off the money laundering. The Iran Contral affair was investigated by Congress and by the three-person, Reagan-appointed [Tower Commission](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tower_Commission). Neither investigation found evidence that President Reagan himself knew of the extent of the multiple programs. Additionally, [US Deputy Attorney General](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/US_Deputy_Attorney_General) [Lawrence Walsh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lawrence_Walsh) was appointed [independent counsel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States_Office_of_the_Independent_Counsel) in December 1986 to investigate possible criminal actions by officials involved in the scheme. In the end, several dozen administration officials were indicted, including then–[Secretary of Defense](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States_Secretary_of_Defense) [Caspar Weinberger](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Caspar_Weinberger). Eleven convictions resulted, some of which were vacated on appeal. Weinberger's trial however opened the door to prosecute George W. Bush whow was caught red handed.

The rest of those indicted or convicted were all pardoned in the final days of the [presidency of George H. W. Bush](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Presidency_of_George_H._W._Bush), who had been vice president at the time of the affair. W Bush has deep ties to the industrial military complex via his family ties that date back to the OSS that became the CIA under General Groves during the FDR and Truman presidency's.

How bad was it for W. Bush? Former Independent Counsel Walsh noted that, in issuing the pardons, Bush appeared to have been preempting being implicated himself by evidence that came to light during the Weinberger trial and noted that there was a pattern of "deception and obstruction" by Bush, Weinberger, and other senior Reagan administration officials. Walsh submitted his final report on 4 August 1993 and later wrote an account of his experiences as counsel, _Firewall: The Iran-Contra Conspiracy and Cover-Up.

June 11, 1981 at 1:00 a.m. EDT

Tucked away in President Reagan's latest tax cut plan is a provision to give a major new tax deduction to millions of working Americans by permitting them to establish individual retirement funds even if they are participating in company pension plans.

What was the REAL monetary effect of the IRA program?

Dallas Salisbury, at this time, was the executive director of the Employe Benefit Research Institute. **Salisbury predicted that with the new proposal, individual savings would increase by nearly $2 billion a year at a cost to the Treasury of $200 million to $300 million.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-09-15T14:58:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ww1-the-fed-and-the-link-of-the-royal-family-to-the-rise-of-hitler-and-formation-of-israel.29403/post-339601>

### **In 1948, 4 Star General Eisenhower met with General Leslie Groves, who was director of the Manhattan Project, Eisenhower presented Groves with a long list of complaints, ending Groves' career. Groves was furious. Groves had a beef with the Executive branch when Eisenhower wins the Presidency in 4 years. Groves left the military and becomes part of the defense contractor industry that morphs into the industrial military complex that in 8 years becomes a serious threat to Eisenhower and the Executive branch.** ​

### **Post WW2, Eisenhower didn't view Groves in a positive light, given the success of the Manhattan Project.** ​

### Jack Kruse — 2024-09-15T15:42:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ww1-the-fed-and-the-link-of-the-royal-family-to-the-rise-of-hitler-and-formation-of-israel.29403/post-339602>

Relatively little, however, has been written on the history of key Army engineer who made the Manhattan project a revolutionary success. This was done by design. Leslie Richard “Dick” Groves, Jr., a career officer in the Army Corps of Engineers, was tasked with assembling the crucial links between _**government, industry, science,**_ **_and the military_** beginning in September 1942, due to his imposing personality, iron will, and remarkable administrative acumen. Groves also had a grasp pertinent scientific principles, as well as a unique ability to choose among technical alternatives to deliver quick results. Most importantly, Groves saw the Manhattan project and the atomic bomb as the means to end the war. Eisenhower believed that Stalin and the Russians were more important in winning in Europe than Groves bomb was in Japan.

Eisenhower knew how Truman was selected by Groves and Jimmy Burns in Dec 1944. Eisenhower did not like that Groves perpetrated a covert coup with Truman.

Groves made a _lot_ of enemies throughout his military career, and Eisenhower's gripe with him in 1948 was not about whether he did a good job in 1945, but whether he was doing a good job in 1948. By 1948 he was running the Armed Forces Special Weapons Project, a very different sort of organization than the Manhattan Project.

With the Manhattan Project, Groves had the ability to essentially be a minor dictator of his own industrial military covert agency — he was insulated from criticism and audit, and had the coveted AAA priority rating that allowed him to requisition whatever resources or people he wanted without being significantly challengeable. His style of operation was very well-suited to the conditions of the Manhattan Project, where he was able to bulldoze his way towards a very specific outcome. It was extremely ill-suited for the postwar, military of rebuilding the world he helped destroy. He personality is where all the modern Neo-Cons come from. Groves second gig building the modern deep state, however, does not go as well as the Manahattan Project, because the outcomes were more nebulous and ill defined, and his authority was no longer absolute, and his oversized ego had become resented by other military brass who considered him to be perhaps a bit too big for his britches.

To Eisenhower, he wasn't some kind of war hero, because many felt he was not in the theatre of battle. Eisenhower felt that Groves was "Toy Soldier" who babysat a bunch of Jewish scientists for FDR. Groves harnessed the scientists in silos of that Allen Dulles harvested in Operation Paperclip from Europe. Groves not not viewed as a war hero by the military in WW2 in the same sense that Eisenhower or MacArthur were — he was an production engineer for the army Corp of Engineers that managed an industrial production project.

Despite all this, Groves had some supporters in the Senate, and some in the military. But he had a huge list of enemies, including David Lilienthal, the head of the Atomic Energy Commission, and the scientists on the General Advisory Committee secretly regarded him as a nuisance in transitioning the atomic weapons business from its "crash" wartime footing into something more regular and systematic in nature. It became a huge business.

Groves and Eisenhower never got along, ever. Their styles didn't mesh, their personalities didn't mesh, and their experiences didn't mesh. Eisenhower's specific complaints were almost exclusively about these kinds of things:

Groves was rude and ruthless when it came to other officers who he felt were standing in his way; he was arrogant; he was single-minded in his attempt to accomplish his goals, no matter who got in the way; he did not abide by rules; he repeatedly worked to achieve advancement through tricks rather than by putting in the "dues" that were required of others.

And — perhaps ironically — he was too young to be the Chief of Engineers, and so there weren't a lot of positions actually open to him for advancement at that point in post war America, and it was an "up or out" situation regarding promotion. The people Eisenhower wanted to promote, in any event, were the commanders who had succeeded in Europe and the Pacific — not those who had stayed on the home front.

All of which to say, there is a classic truism in manufactured history that says that Churchill was a great Prime Minister for war, but a terrible Prime Minister for peace. One can debate that particular example, but Groves was a great project manager for war, and the Manhattan Project probably wouldn't have been a success without him. But those particular qualities that made him good for a "crash" program made him awful for the day-to-day of peacetime operation, and he showed himself totally unwilling and unable to adapt to the culture clash that existed between him and the combat commanders who were going to be running the show in the Army for a long time.

The very things that made Groves successful in 1942-1945 made him fail in 1947-1948, to put it very simply, because the context of the latter was very different than the former. It is of note that much of the Manhattan Project's operations underwent many transitions in this time as well; Norris Bradbury was a very different sort of manager and scientist than J. Robert Oppenheimer.

The best biography of Groves is Robert Standish Norris' _Racing for the Bomb_, and the specifics of Eisenhower's complaints that I've given come from chapter 25, which goes into great detail on the specific circumstances that lead to Groves' retirement and into the world of defense contractors just outside the military. Eisenhower's Farewell speech is linked directly to how powerful Groves became as a product engineer for the Oilmen and Howard Hughes businesses. He comes back to capture the Eisenhower's Executive branch.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-09-16T01:45:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ww1-the-fed-and-the-link-of-the-royal-family-to-the-rise-of-hitler-and-formation-of-israel.29403/post-339612>

Albert Einstein and J. Robert Oppenheimer at Princeton, 1947.[](https://www.icp.org/browse/archive/objects/robert-oppenheimer-director-of-the-institute-of-advanced-study-discussing)

Almost all scientists could see three facts: that the Soviets would get the weapon; that there would eventually be another great power conflict. And that a resulting atomic war would mean the end of civilization. “The unleashed power of the atom,” wrote Einstein, “has changed everything save our modes of thinking and we thus drift toward unparalleled catastrophe.”

The United Nations had been formed just a few months before, but it had (and still has) limited power, thanks to a great power veto. Einstein saw the UN merely as “a transitional system toward the final goal... a supranational authority vested with sufficient legislative and executive powers to keep the peace.”

If supreme military power in the form of the atom bomb were held by a United Nations body, as Oppenheimer wanted, then it would almost be a world state. In fact, many scientists explicitly advocated a 'world federal government.' They published a book, _One World Or None_, with an accompanying film \[see film below\]. This view was not just held by scientists. One of the supporters was Gen 'Hap' Arnold, the head of the United States Air Force — the man in charge of dropping the bomb. The idea of international control even took root at the highest levels of government.

President Harry Truman had two advisers with diametrically opposed views. One was Henry Stimson, the elderly and aristocratic Secretary of War. The other was James Byrnes, Secretary of State. Stimson had famously prevented the Bomb's being dropped on Kyoto, a city he had once visited with his wife. Plus, he had an almost mystical view of how the world had changed. He thought the Bomb “should not be considered simply in terms of military weapons, but as a new relationship of man to the universe.” Stimson’s plan also included international control via the new UN agency: “It must be controlled if possible to make it an assurance of future peace rather than a menace to civilization.”

The Saving Privacy Act looks to Repeals the Bank Secrecy Act’s SAR and CTR reporting requirements while maintaining record-keeping provisions.

It repeals the Corporate Transparency Act.
It strengthens Fourth Amendment protections, bolstering warrant requirements in the Right to Financial Privacy Act of 1978.
It repeals the SEC’s Consolidated Audit Trail (CAT) database.
It prohibits the creation of a Central Bank Digital Currency.
It requires congressional authorization for financial regulations deemed major rules. (weak point) Needs SCOTUS
It requires congressional approval for any new databases that collect personally identifiable information of U.S. citizens.
It institutes penalties for federal employees who illegally seek constitutionally protected financial information = penalty should be treason for the industrial miliatry complex thiefs.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-10-09T16:59:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ww1-the-fed-and-the-link-of-the-royal-family-to-the-rise-of-hitler-and-formation-of-israel.29403/post-340693>

Much of Lansky's money was directed at cryptographic research. In 1955, not only were Steve Jobs and Gates born, but so was David Chaum. I believe Lansky funded much of David's work to build the answer to the Fed's programmable money to be used in the surveillance state.

Why do I think David Chaum was the real target of Murder Inc.'s covert banking empire?

After Chaum obtained his PhD in Computer Science from UC Berkeley, he founded the International Association for Cryptologic Research, the cryptography group at the Center for Mathematics and Computer Science in Amsterdam, to avoid the legalities of cryptography in the USA. He did this with no verifiable funding. I have done extensive work on his early investors, and this is the dryest tap I have found in my search of history. This makes NO sense when you consider the cost of this business. It does make sense when you understand how Lansky and the MOSSAD operate. They own people. They bought Chaum's time and put him to work for them.

At the same time this is all going on that he founds the research wing of cryptography, Chaum also funds projects DigiCash, the Voting Systems Institute and the Perspectiva Fund. These things are theoretical and not cheap to run. I have NO idea where he got the money from but his parents do have links to Israel and I have found some links to history of attorney Ira Malnick, but none are convicing enough for me to make a claim.

In the 1970's and early 1980s David Chaum seems to channel into his research most of what Lansky would need to run his monetary empire outside the Fed system. What do I mean?

David Chaum somehow made a quantum leap by envisioning many privacy concerns that did not yet exist in the world of the Federal Reserve. It is as if he predicted what the implications of the Bank Secrecy Act meant in terms of AML/KYC concerns. I do not think this is a coincidence at all. Lansky knew exactly what would come from the Fed/BIS, and he made sure Chaum did too before he died in 1983. His actions seem to predict that privacy concerns would soon erupt within the BIS and the Fed system.

His first discovery I believe comes from reviewing the work of Turing and Claude Shannon in the later 1970s. I think Chaum realized that the take-home of Claude Shannon's work was MESSAGES HAD TO BE UNUSUAL to be useful in networks.

This idea morphed into the concept of **“Shuffling”** incoming messages from multiple senders and sending them back in random order could confuse those eavesdropping. This was his first breakthrough cryptographically. In fact, I believe this is how **"mix networks"** saw the light of day for the first time.

This idea seemed to prompt him to create an anonymous PEER TO PEER payment system that would allow for secure transactions in the coming digital age. His idea is identical to Lansky's need for his covert banking empire to run parallel to the Fed system that was incorruptible. The Federal government looked for Lansky's money he made during his time as the main accountant of Murder Inc. using their best forensic accountants in intelligence from 1960-1973, and they could find nothing on him or Moe Dalitz even though they knew he was banking most of organized crime in the USA. I believe they parked a lot of that money away in art assets because this market was lucrative and small and they could buy the best assets privately and hide them over time while they had Chaum work on their new system that would run secretly and in parallel to the Fed system.

Lansky gave the idea for this new system, Chaum had to execute it, before it became reality. The plan worked, but not as many of them hoped.

Why? Chaum lost control of the process as he developed **Mix Networks** almost immediately.

Chaum's contributions built Tor networks, which brought many builders to the anonymity network project. This did not make the Murder Inc. crowd happy. However, it might be the best thing ever for the people of the world because it brought Lansky's idea out of the covert world and into the light of day for the public. Why?

In Tor, the communication protocol between nodes is nested like the layers of an onion. The network encrypts the next node's destination IP address and sends it through a virtual circuit of random Tor relays. The final relay decrypts the innermost layer and sends data to its destination without knowing the source IP address. The network surveillance normally relies upon knowing the source and destination, and at this point, can’t spot either a sender or a receiver. Because the link between them was broken by these crafty proxy servers mixing things up.

Guess who has invented the servers that take in messages from multiple senders, shuffle and encrypt them to send back in random order to the next destination? David Chaum, in 1981.

Guess who worked on TCP/IP architecture soon after? Cypherpunk Len Sassaman born in 1980. In 1998, at 18 years old, he was on the [Internet Engineering Task Force](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Internet_Engineering_Task_Force) responsible for the TCP/IP protocol underlying the Internet.

**WHAT HAPPENS BETWEEN 1981 and 1998?**

Not a lot.

His 1981 paper, "Untraceable Electronic Mail, Return Addresses, and Digital Pseudonyms", laid the groundwork for the field of anonymous communications research. The project stagnates until others enter the project who are not supposed to be involved.

**Mix networks and blind signatures** are innovated during this epoch. In 1982, blind signature is a type of a digital signature used in the protocols where the signer and message author are different parties, mostly, in digital payment or voting systems. Chaum's group of technologist are behind it.

It wasn't until 1990 that Lansky's vision for private money met Chaum's creation. From 1990-94, things ramped up because cypherpunks were all working on this project all over the world. Lansky's private project is now fully open-sourced. It became a reality through the company known as DigiCash in 1994. This is really Bitcoin 1.0. It has many features Lansky needs, but it is not yet ready for primetime.

This is when Industrial military complex corporations begin sniffing around Chaum's work. Why? The BIS plan, now called a CBDC, is going way slower than expected.

DigiCash had accumulated five thousand users by the time it went bankrupt in 1998. It was used by Deutsche Bank as well as an American financial institution, Mark Twain Bank. David Chaum had almost completed Lansky's dream. The rumor in 1994 was that Bill Gates, Visa, and investment firms linked to the future players in the PayPal mafia at Stanford were interested, but nothing really came to fruition in terms of an offer or financial backing. So, it seemingly died.

DigiCash declared bankruptcy in 1998 and subsequently sold its assets to eCash Technologies, another digital currency company, which was acquired by InfoSpace after the Patriot Act is brought on by the industrial military complex 9/11 activities with the MOSSAD on February 19, 2002.

InfoSpace was in the same business as Google who was given the BIS search algorithm by DARPA in 1995. The Fed, Google, and BIS continue to work on the CBDC project today.

Do you know who owns InfoSpace? **Naveen Jain.** Do you know Jain's first job out of college in 1983 was at [Burroughs](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Burroughs_Corporation) now known as [Unisys](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Unisys) in New Jersey as part of a business-exchange program. Who was associated with Burroughs? General Groves. If you read my thread on Groves on this site you'll see how he controlled Remington Rand merged it with Burroughs to become Sperry which became Unisys.

Why did Jain buy Digicash at bankruptcy? Because he knew Chaum had a connection to Lansky, who was linked to Groves because of his time at Microsoft to run the industrial military's covert banking system. He said he moved to Silicon Valley for its warmer climate and worked for "a bunch of startups" before joining key industrial military player Microsoft in 1989. Jain never harvested the key part Chaum missed, but Chaum advised a student who did make the connection. **TIMESTAMPING.**

**Claude Shannon taught the world that information flows via entropy. All clocks, including timestamps, should be considered flowmeters for entry. I believe Len Sassaman, Chaum's student, realized when he worked with Chaum that time controls how energy flows over matter. He added timestamping to the Proof Of Work metric developed in Hashcash and created Bitcoin's white paper on Jan 3, 2008.**

Not everything that passes under the label "Jewish" or "Israel" is one monolithic thing. There was a man named Abraham, called by God out of Ur of the Chaldees, who became the father of Isaac, who became the father of 12 tribes that formed an ancient ethnic nation named Israel, referred to in the Bible as the Israelites.

Gentiles were allowed to convert to the Judaic religion, undergo circumcision, and keep the Law. Those were called proselytes. They were not ethnic Israelites but were instead Gentile converts to the religion of Israel.

Around 50 BC, according to the historian Josephus in his 'Antiquities', Herod the Great killed most of the Israelite Levite priesthood families and replaced them with families brought to Jerusalem from Babylon. While some of these newly installed priesthood families were half Israelite, many were proselytes. That is, they had converted to JUDAIC RELIGIOUS PRACTICES, but they were not themselves Israelites.

These proselytes, who were not ethnic Israelites, later produced THE TALMUD, beginning about 1,200 years after Moses wrote the Torah, and 200 years following the death of Christ.

The Talmud, which Orthodox and Hasidic Jews consider superior to the Torah, written by an actual prophet, is generally thought to have been compiled between 200 and 500 CE. Yes, you are fully expected to believe that THE TALMUD, produced by a lot of Babylonian Gentile converts to the Israelite religion, more than 1,000 years after Moses wrote the Torah, was transmitted ACCURATELY for 1000+ years until someone finally started writing it down.

And wouldn't you know it, the Orthodox/Hasidic branches of modern Judaism hold the TALMUD over the TORAH. Have you ever read the Talmud and compared it to something a divinely inspired prophet like Moses wrote? There's some EYE-OPENING stuff in that Talmud, lemme tell ya.

Now, let me COMPLICATE THIS EVEN MORE, OK?

A modern nation called ISRAEL was created by a bunch of super-rich British elites, and a lot of Orthodox/Hasidic Jewish people settled there, for very understandable reasons following World War 2. And today American Christians are \*\*deliberately taught\*\* to conflate the ethnic Israelites from the 12 tribes Isaac fathered...with the modern secular government of Israel. Zionists formed and are the modern secular government of Israel.

You have to approve, endorse, and support whatever the secular government of Israel does because if you don't, you are going against the 12 tribes descended from Isaac...or something. See how this is conflated to confuse people?

Modern day Israel \*\*MAY HAVE\*\* some people in it who follow the Torah and not the Talmud. It \*\*may have\*\* some people in it who are actually descended from the 12 ethnic tribes that Isaac fathered. But the truth is...MOST of the people in the modern-day secular state of Israel, and in Israel's secular government...ARE NOT biblical Israelites.

They are descendants of GENTILE proselytes from long ago, who don't even follow the Torah; they place the Talmud above it, and when the Talmud contradicts the Torah \[as it often does\], they go with the Talmud. American Zionism is a front for elite British imperialism in drag.

As a Christian, I'm \*\*not called\*\* in the New Testament to support a secular state made up of people who have supplanted the actual words of Yahweh as communicated through prophets like Moses, for the much MUCH later interpretations written down over 1,000 years later by Gentile converts trying to 'fix' Judaism to their liking. They predated the American use of Lee Harvey Oswald in the American military coup of our Republic. Klaus Fuchs and Alan Turing, both British citizen working for the Crown and were prominent figures in the British scientific community during and after World War II. They were passing secrets on behalf of the Corwn and unfortunately your history books will tell you they were security risks. You were told this so that you'd never connect the Crown to propping up the Hitler regime to reclaim allodial title back from the Brits. To keep the plan quiet Fuchs was convicted of espionage for passing information to the Soviet Union about the atomic bomb project, while Turing was investigated for his homosexuality, which led to his prosecution and contributed to his suicide when the British used female hormones to make him a woman. In America, Oppenheimer became the first American patsy before Lee Harvey Oswald. Lewis Strauss turned evidence on Oppenheimer during a security clearance meeting to harpoon the career and life of Oppenheimer. This was done due to a complex mix of personal animosity, political differences, and security concerns. Strauss, then Chairman of the Atomic Energy Commission (AEC), harbored resentment towards Oppenheimer for past disagreements, particularly regarding the development of the hydrogen bomb. Strauss's nomination to be Secretary of Commerce in Eisenhower's cabinet was rejected by the Senate in 1959, in part due to the controversy surrounding the Oppenheimer case. JFK was the junior Senator who sunk him. He was the first nomineee reject by Congress since 1925. JFK became a focus of Groves and Strauss after this event. Now you can understand fully why this record on Patton exists. Patton believed The British were our real enemy, but your history books will say he was on General Groves side in making the USSR our new enemy.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-01-15T07:08:52+0200

Warburg Family Timeline:

1500s: Originate as del Banco in Venice, wealthiest bankers.
1559: Flee to Warburg, Germany; adopt surname.
1798: Found M.M. Warburg & Co. in Hamburg.
1902: Paul Warburg moves to US, joins Kuhn Loeb.
1905: Jacob Schiff finances Japan in Russo-Japanese War (~$200M).
1910: Paul at Jekyll Island, designs Federal Reserve.
1913: Fed established. WWII: Eric Warburg in OSS (precursor to CIA).
1966: Eric founds Warburg Pincus.
2008: Tim Geithner (Treasury Sec) awards BlackRock bailout contracts.
Today: Warburg Pincus manages $100B; ties to UBS ($6.9T).

### Jack Kruse — 2026-02-02T16:52:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ww1-the-fed-and-the-link-of-the-royal-family-to-the-rise-of-hitler-and-formation-of-israel.29403/post-366236>

HISTORY LESSON

August 1933.

Hitler had been in power barely six months.

The world was screaming boycott German goods.

Jewish organizations worldwide were starving the Reich economy in rage over the brownshirt beatings, the signs, the laws stripping rights overnight.
Yet right then a quiet deal was signed in Berlin.

Ha'avara. The Transfer Agreement.

Nazi Economics Ministry + Zionist Federation of Germany + Anglo-Palestine Bank.

Jews fleeing Germany could dump their money into a special account. That money bought German machines, cement, steel, goods. Those goods shipped to Palestine.
Sold there.

The proceeds, minus hefty cuts, handed back to the arriving immigrant in Palestinian pounds.
On paper?
A lifeline.

Around 60,000 German Jews got out between 1933–1939 with at least part of their wealth intact.

Many would have been trapped, later murdered.
So it saved lives.
But peel the layer.
For the Nazis?
A masterstroke in foreign policy.
They smashed the global Jewish boycott the one thing that could really hurt them early in the reign.
German factories hummed again exporting to the Middle East.
Tens of millions of Reichsmarks flowed out as Jewish capital, but came back as hard currency and broken boycott solidarity.

Jews paid to leave and subsidized German rearmament in the process.

For the Zionist leadership?

A brutal calculation.

They needed bodies and capital to build the Yishuv fast before the British slammed the doors shut with White Papers.
They needed German Jews' skills, money, know-how.

The "Jeckes" arrived with education, professions, cash infusions that doubled down on land buys, kibbutzim, industry.

That flood strengthened the future state while the Arab economy in Palestine fractured under the weight. And the Palestinians? They watched their country change overnight.

More land sold (often absentee landlords cashing out).

More German goods flooding markets, undercutting local artisans.

More immigrants now with capital the locals could never match.
Tensions boiled into the 1936–1939 Arab Revolt.
The very mechanism that saved German Jews accelerated the dispossession machine already turning.
The irony cuts deepest.
The same agreement that let some Jews escape the coming inferno also greased the wheels for what came after both for the victims who didn't make it out, and for the land and people already living where the escape hatch opened.

So when they say "Zionists collaborated with Nazis," it's half-truth weaponized. When they say "it was just humanitarian rescue," it's sanitized half-truth.

The full picture?

A cold intersection where desperation, ideology, and naked interest met.

Everyone took something.
Everyone paid something.
And in the middle, ordinary people German Jews who lost everything anyway later, Palestinians who lost their homes step by step paid the heaviest price because the Zionist won and this lead to modern day problems that still remain in Israel/Palestine.

### EDI AVDIC — 2024-10-23T20:17:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/artists-corner-post-something-artistic-a-poem-a-painting.29405/post-341444>

The Dance of Spheres and Cubes

In spheres, the divine crafts its cosmic art,
While Satan molds cubes, setting worlds apart.
Analog waves, smooth and flowing with grace,
Yield to digital pulses, in rectangles they race.

Centralized powers strive to square the circle's embrace,
In this pursuit, they seek some hidden place.
The bull of Wall Street demands its due,
From parents who chase the promise of wealth and so sacrificing their promised new.

For Satan craves time, the immortal quest,
To avoid God's judgment, he seeks the blessed.
From children, he steals life's precious span,
Their innocence harnessed in his grand plan.

New Jerusalem, a cube in celestial flight,
Twelve thousand stadia per side, a beacon of light.
A perfect form, nestled within the moon,
A harmony of shapes, where spheres and cubes tune.

Is it time to cease squaring the circle's dare,
And instead, learn to circle the square?
In this global city, this city of Bale,
We ponder our journey, our mythic tale.

### EDI AVDIC — 2024-11-01T18:51:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/artists-corner-post-something-artistic-a-poem-a-painting.29405/post-341849>

Through the Eye of the Needle

Why is the path to God through the needle’s eye?
Perhaps the way out is inward, where truths lie.
Like a black hole’s pull, our universe bends,
No escape outward; only inward it sends.

In hell’s tight grip, we’re bound and confined,
Yet deep in our essence, the light we must find.
As we journey from matter to realms that ignite,
We shed all that’s heavy, transforming to light.

Is death just a passage, a threshold to grace,
Where we slip through the eye, to a brighter place?
In this sacred transition, our spirits take flight,
Finding freedom and peace in the dance of the light.

Why may the way to god ie source be through the eye of the needle; maybe because the only way out is inward as the black hole (as physics now shows that our universe is a black hole and were all in it) ie hell will not allow you to escape out of it outwardly but only inwards; and in this process as we go from 3D ie matter to 4D ie light we shed all that isn't light ie your body ie death; is death the "passing" through the eye;

### EDI AVDIC — 2024-11-19T02:04:55+0200

### EDI AVDIC — 2024-11-21T06:40:00+0200

The inking is the magical science of casting spells into reality whether or not ink is involved th-inking is always involved; for example: the science of words is propaganda is the science attempting to govern-mental domains is government therefore government may simply be the science of words; is it any wonder our words are their biggest enemy ie censorship;

The Science of Inking

The inking is a magic, a craft unseen,
A spell to bend the world where thought has been.
Though ink may not be part of every cast,
In thinking, magic's power holds steadfast.

Words are the vessels, the power they wield,
Casting in silence, their truths unconcealed.
In the science of words, we shape and we mold,
Propaganda's reach is silent but bold.

Governance grows from the thoughts we convey,
A rule of the mind, where words hold sway.
For government, in essence, is simply this—
The science of words, in power's abyss.

Is it any wonder they fear our own voice,
For truth in our words gives us all the choice.
Censorship whispers, to silence the sound,
Afraid of the power that speech can unbound.

So let us remember, in words we create,
A world of our making, for love or for hate.
In the inking, the thinking, the science we find—
The world is a mirror of our spoken mind.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-10-11T02:48:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/artists-corner-post-something-artistic-a-poem-a-painting.29405/post-360866>

Pablo Picasso died in 1973, he left a treasure trove of artwork to his one legitimate son, Paulo. Per the New York Times, reporting on Paul’s death just two years later, on June 7, 1975, Paulo was one of the principal inheritors, along with the artist’s widow, Jacqueline Picasso, of the Picasso estate, which at the time included paintings valued at more than $100‐million but would quickly balloon to the billions. At that moment, the collection of ‘Picasso’s Picassos’ passed to Marina, Paul’s daughter. So I chatted her up and bought a few of them.
