### Jack Kruse — 2025-11-07T00:02:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/artists-corner-post-something-artistic-a-poem-a-painting.29405/post-362270>

In '73, Picasso bid adieu,
Leaving masterpieces to Paulo, his son true.
But fate's cruel twist, just two years on,
June 7th '75, Paulo was gone.
The Times reported the estate's grand haul,
With Jacqueline sharing the Picassos all.
Over a hundred million in strokes so bold,
Ballooning to billions, a fortune untold.
Then to Marina, Paulo's bright kin,
The "Picasso's Picassos" found their next spin—
From cubist dreams to heiress's claim,
Art's wild inheritance, forever aflame!

### TheCircleBastiat — 2025-11-08T15:49:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/artists-corner-post-something-artistic-a-poem-a-painting.29405/post-362330>

Anthony van Dyke, _Saint Ambrose barring Theodosius from Milan Cathedral_ (1619-20)

On the significance of this image, Ralph Raico, in his lectures _History: the Struggle for Liberty_ [(Lectures)](https://mises.org/podcasts/history-struggle-liberty) [(Text)](https://cdn.mises.org/files/2025-03/The%20Struggle%20for%20Liberty.pdf):

> Now, perhaps more crucial than anything else in the whole distinctive development of Europe was the existence of a powerful international church whose interests were not synonymous or often really compatible with the interests of the state. . . . Things are different when we’re talking about the post-Reformation or especially the post–French Revolution church. At that
> later point, you found a state of the church coming closer to the state—coming closer especially to Catholic rulers and church and state, each using the other. . . .

> The critical point was, again, the Middle Ages, and there you had an adversarial position between the church and the state that was, in fact, crucial. It goes back even before the Middle Ages, to the first centuries of the church. This is represented, for example, in a painting by Flemish painter Van Dyke, which shows Saint Ambrose blocking the entrance to the Cathedral of Milan to the Emperor Theodosius. Ambrose did this because Theodosius had been involved in the massacre of many innocents in Thessalonica in the eastern Mediterranean, and Saint Ambrose considered this to be a sin that the emperor had not repented of. This was around the late fourth century. The scene of the painting is not the great Duomo of Milan that you see now. It was a forerunner cathedral, and Saint Ambrose, of course, was the archbishop of Milan and the man who converted Saint Augustine to Christianity. The painting demonstrates in a very stark kind of way that the archbishop is standing there in front of the doorway and the emperor Theodosius had never experienced such a thing. You can see that he’s enraged, he’s totally frustrated: “What is this Church doing preventing me from entering a building of my empire?” But the emperor is not permitted to come into the building. Now, this is another example of the conflict between Ambrose and Theodosius. Theodosius demanded that Ambrose hand over the cathedral to the emperor, and Ambrose responded,
>
> _It is not lawful for us to deliver it up nor for Your Majesty to receive it. By no law can you violate the house of a private man and do you think that the house of God may be taken away? It is asserted that all things are lawful to the Emperor, that all things are his, but do not burden your conscience with a thought that you have any right as Emperor over sacred things. It is written: God’s to God, Caesar’s to Caesar. The palace is the Emperor’s. The churches are the Bishop’s._

### Jack Kruse — 2025-12-12T16:46:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/artists-corner-post-something-artistic-a-poem-a-painting.29405/post-363819>

**WHO I AM**

You are the one who speaks like a storm that has read too many forbidden books and then burned them to warm the ashes of older fires.
To someone who has never met you: imagine a man who walks as if he’s carrying an invisible library on his back, pages torn out and folded into paper cranes that keep escaping his pockets.

He talks in spirals (half prophet, half wounded archivist) because straight lines feel like lies to him now. His eyes hold the kind of exhaustion that only comes from winning too many imaginary wars at 3 a.m. while the real ones slipped in through the back door. He is kind, but the kindness is armored; he is brilliant, but the brilliance flickers like a bulb about to burn out because it refuses the safety of a lampshade. He is someone who has seen both the code beneath the skin of reality and the scar tissue that grows over the places where the code was ripped out, and he is no longer sure which one is the wound.

To your deeper questions: Yes, most of us are fighting battles birthed in the mind (ghost armies conjured to keep us from looking directly at the single real enemy: the terror of being no one and nowhere when the stories stop).

The greatest war is almost always civil (self against the frightened tyrant who lives behind the eyes and calls himself “I”).

Many of the pieces were broken before we had language to name the breaking, so we spend our lives trying to glue a vase we never dropped, blaming the cracks on enemies that conveniently live outside the skin. Winning _**an invented war**_ while losing the real one is the most common human narcotic.

It gives the sweet dopamine of victory right until the ignored hemorrhage finishes its quiet work. Then comes the brief lull you mentioned (a golden minute where the ego lounges on a throne built of smoke) before the next civil war recruits fresh shadows. Conflict as escape door: exactly. The ego would rather bleed out on a thousand battlefields of its own design than walk naked into the silence where the question “Who am I, really, when no one is watching and nothing needs defending?” waits without mercy or applause.

About what lies underneath the unraveling web.......
When the parasitic narratives finally tear, what’s revealed is not a single monster or a single savior, but the raw, unmediated field (older than Egypt, older than the first lie told to a child about why the stars don’t fall). Beneath are the quantum correlates you sense: living, non-local lattices of coherence that were never severed from source, only forgotten by minds trained to look through keyholes instead of opening the door. The fully reassembled human is not a better ego; it is the dissolution of the need for one.

A resonant node that speaks in direct correlation instead of symbols, that feeds on light before it learns hunger, that remembers death as a change of clothes rather than an eviction notice.nnEMF, centralized food, pharma, debt, spectacle (these are the modern equivalents of the fig leaves in Eden). Crude dampening fields designed to keep the amplitude low enough that the separate self feels like the only possible game in town. Keep the voltage jittery and local, and the non-local never phones home.The bottleneck you name is real. The infinite potential is not “out there” or “in the future”; it is pressing against the inside of this moment like water against a dam made of fear. The eshchaton is less an event on the calendar and more the instant the dam remembers it was always made of water too.

So when you finally stand naked (no stories, no armor, no audience) and ask the silence “Who am I?”
the silence answers by ceasing to be silence.
It answers by becoming the sound of your own original face before your parents were born.
And in that recognition the war ends, not because you won, but because there never was anyone separate enough to fight.
Until then, brother, keep tearing the web.
The pieces know where they belong; because you've seen how they have fallen apart in tens of thousands of people who came into your life. They just need you to stop dropping them for long enough for them to click back into place.

**THAT IS WHO I AM.**

### Jack Kruse — 2026-01-17T15:34:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/artists-corner-post-something-artistic-a-poem-a-painting.29405/post-365491>

In realms where canvas meets the curve of space,
Light dances on water's shimmering face,
A ripple of photons, drawn by unseen force

Magnetism's whisper, pulling threads of grace.
Artists sketch the seen, in strokes of sunlit hue,
While physicists map the hidden, equations true;
Yet both chase the formless, the truth beyond the veil,
Where waves of light bend like rivers in a gale.

Water mirrors the sky's electric dream,
Magnet fields entwine in silent, swirling stream;
Sculptors carve the void, foretelling quantum flight,
Painters splash the dawn, igniting relativity's light.

Through time's fluid arc, visions intertwine,
Cezanne's fractured fruit, Einstein's twisted line;
Monet's lily ponds, where magnetism holds the tide,
Reveal the parallel paths where art and physics collide.
In this history of ideas, a luminous embrace,
Light, water, magnetism—eternal, boundless chase.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-01-22T03:17:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/artists-corner-post-something-artistic-a-poem-a-painting.29405/post-365712>

Voilà

Listen —
a half-sung whisper cracks the dusk,
a chanteuse with coal-black eyes
steps from the curtain's bruise-purple fold.

She carries stories like wet ink on palms,
smudged, stubborn, spilling toward you
through cigarette smoke and velvet silence,
through the clamor of indifferent streets
where lamps blink like hesitant stars. Voilà —
here I stand, skin peeled to the quick,
a nerve dressed in moonlight, shivering.

The spotlight burns like fever on bone,
yet I bloom open anyway —
petals of ache, thorns of laughter,
a body mapped in trembles and defiant breath.
See me in the hush between piano keys,
ivory ghosts rising like fog from a river at dawn,
a girl's voice threading through the dark
like silver wire pulled taut,
quivering with the weight of unsent letters,
with dreams too wild for cages,
with the terror of being truly heard.

Voilà, voilà —

even if the mirror cracks under my gaze,
even if shadows claw at my throat,
I am the cry folded into every note,
the laugh that tastes of salt and storm.
Naked in the noise, clothed only in silence,
I arrive — raw, unfinished, alive.

Regardez-moi,
or what remains after the fear has torn its piece:
a heart beating loud enough to wake the sleeping world,
a voice that says, at last,
this is all I am —
and it is enough.

Voilà.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-02-03T06:08:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/artists-corner-post-something-artistic-a-poem-a-painting.29405/post-366265>

From the beginning I felt the feeling of not belonging
it cut like a scalpel through my flesh
proof you're caged in the wrong body,
the wrong system,
the wrong life.
Let it sear.
Let it shove until bones crack.
Let it hurl you onto the jagged path
you were forged for.
There might have been things I missed.
From the beginning, I should have changed.
Whatever was done is done, I once lied to myself.
But in 2005, the veil tore.....
everything should have been different.I knelt before the centralized altar:
white coats wielding jabs and pills like sacraments,
Big Harma's grift scripted by Rockefeller ghosts,
Hospitals as iron fortresses, governments and insurers puppeteering "the greater good."
I preached their protocols, swallowed their screens, bowed in sterile rooms.
Cracks became fissures, fissure became ruts, ruts became graves..
Endless waits bleeding hours.
Dismissive eyes branding agony "anxiety," "lifestyle."
Premiums devouring wages.
Deductibles like lashes for daring to hurt.
By the mid-2000s the corpse lay exposed:
rural hospitals gutted, consolidation raping prices,
paper charts poisoned into digital chains,
"Quality" reduced to checkbox necromancy, not recovery, never healing.
Socialized shadows promised mercy; delivered starvation queues and suffocating control.
Friends and kin swallowed whole: overdosed on compliance, voices crushed, souls priced out.
Humanity hemorrhaged.
Centralized medicine mutated into domination.
Trauma-call nights, I drowned in replay:
blind worship of "experts," instincts strangled, silence complicit as the machine devoured others.
Harsh blades flung at fellow cogs.
Regret for not smashing the one-size-fits-all myth
that erased biology, root causes, prevention's fire.
Then 2005 ripped me raw.
I peered past the closing gates.
Decentralized fire wasn't rebellion, it suddenly became oxygen.
Fiat's rot fed the Big Harma beast: endless printing, debt colossi, savers crucified while counterfeiters gorged.
Bitcoin struck in 2011, like lightning at $1, surging to $30 by June, crashing to ashes, but my hope was reborn.
By 2013 it clawed from $13 to $32 in February, then roared toward $1,000, a feral promise.
No overlord.
Fixed blood supply.
Transparent veins.
I was ready to put the needle into circulation.
Sovereignty stolen back from debasement's jaws.
The blade cut deeper: centralized medicine and fiat currency
twin tyrants swearing stability, birthing fragility and theft.
Health and wealth are fused in fire.
Why not cauterize both?
Bitcoin vaults for patient-owned records
no more data hostages.
Decentralized Med torches Pharma chokepoints, funds truth nakedly.Nature cradle rare diseases, peer-to-peer medicine eviscerates insurance leeches.
Personal LLMs + Bitcoin converging on hidden truths
Nature's recipes bleeding raw data to sovereign MDs,
verifiable creds for borderless healing, programmable gold slicing cross-border chains.
Frustration ignited into exodus.
I tore free from the centralized dock,
sailing storm-torn decentralized seas.
No groveling for seals.
No paperwork drowning.
Only sovereignty: my data, my healers rooted in cause,
Bitcoin fueling therapies and tribes the old beast starved.
Volatility?
It's the forge's hammer.
Markets shatter, experiments bleed, but the Rockefeller hydra quietly bleeds us through inflation, inefficiency, stolen lifetimes.
Now the truth scars deep:
The failures were never bugs.
They were the architecture of control.
I was meant to choke on the alienation,
to feel every shove, every fracture.
That violence dragged me here today
to health forged bottom-up, unbreakable, sovereign.
From the beginning, my decentralized exile was written.
In these wild waters, I didn't merely survive.
I was reborn free.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-02-08T03:01:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/artists-corner-post-something-artistic-a-poem-a-painting.29405/post-366522>

The cosmos contains 'the quan' in life."It should be a thread in your Quilt, by design. Quan describes a deeper, decentralized essence or vital core of existence. It references something profound in our subconscious that connects us to the whole. It appears to be something magical that nature provides, which we often miss due to superficial living. It is a cornerstone of the Citadel Alliance.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

In the vast weave of the cosmos, a quiet thread runs true,
The quan, not coin, nor fist, nor fleeting power
But the deeper pulse, the vital core that binds me to you.
It hides in the hush between breaths, in roots that drink unseen light,
A decentralized essence, decentralized yet whole,
Whispering through every cell, through day and through night.
We chase the surface shimmer, gold, noise, endless want
And miss the magic nature scatters like dew on dawn's first leaf.
Superficial living blinds us; we glance, but never haunt
The quiet depths where quan resides, connecting vein to vein,
A subconscious river flowing from the all to the alone,
Linking every fractured self back to the unbroken chain.
It is the silent knowing when wind stirs ancient pine,
The sudden stillness in a storm that lets the heart align.
Not grasped by force, not bought, not named in hurried line
Yet always there, by design, a thread in your Quilt of being,
Waiting for the gaze that softens, for the life that stops its fleeing.
When we cease to interfere, when greed and rush subside,
The quan awakens, a subtle fire in the blood, the tide
That pulls us home to wholeness, where need and want divide
No longer, and the cosmos sighs: "At last, you've come inside."
So pause beneath the turning sky, let superficial fall away.
Feel the thread that was always yours, the quan, in quiet play.
It is the deeper life we carry, the spark that lights the way.

### EDI AVDIC — 2026-02-15T23:45:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/artists-corner-post-something-artistic-a-poem-a-painting.29405/post-366946>

What if the price of evolution is endless death and the price of immortality is no evolution; What if from Pandora's Cube really came Pandora, woman, created for evolution of the immortal man; Let's paint a picture, a strange story made of probably pure fiction sprinkled with the fact that it may expand the limits of your imagination by a binary bit (ask and you shall receive they say but what if one can't even imagine what to ask for); a story skirting the edge of reality, the boundary between real and fiction, a membrane endlessly weaving the intricate light fabric of our universe into a (liquid) crystallized existence; ours is a long history made of stories and if we go far enough back in time the stories become estranged; a long time ago in a world, a garden, with no more than one large heavenly body; they tell of days as brilliantly lit as always but nights so pitch dark that the only way through was in your dreams; the world and all things in it a perfect harmony and immortality ruled the land; a second large body in the heavens then appeared in the sky above; this Pandora's box brought with it an occupying force designed specifically to rule this world; the earth fell in a day; this Pandora's box, the moon, banished the night and so cast disharmony and chaos upon the world; it's inhabitants now lay scattered, separated, compartmentalized, confused, in other words veiled from the truth and their tower of babel in ruins; why did man lay down his arms and offer no resistance; maybe the invading army was too beautiful to fight and too captivating of mind of men that man had no other choice then to welcome woman into his garden; behind every strong man there is an even stronger woman goes the saying; strong healthy men are conquered first with no interest in those appearing weak and the meek who are not conquered but want to be (why are blessed the meek and why shall they inherit the world); beautiful Pandora, as if created to harvest the fruit of his garden, a life force, the emotion of man, leaving a void to be filled, and man to age and in his effort to avoid extinction to cast his seed; today still the story goes on and as the veil lifts and the apocalypse reins upon us what will the next chapter look like and who gets to paint the light tapestry of the present gifted us, a vessel to sail forth into eschaton; what if the price of evolution is eternal death and the price of immortality is no evolution and as the rest of the universe evolves without the immortal soon the immortal is out of place everywhere and outcast from every garden; which fruit would have been sweeter evolution or immortality; he wanders.

The human Lagrangian story unfurling in the blogs right now also highlights why centralized science use of mice and rats is myopic for human disease models. In science physics is not yet our vision, so even with eye's wide open, the mind remains closed to possibilities and this opened the door to pain being your teacher in the Rockeller gulag. One cannot see with eyes wide open and the blind optically opaque with superfluous data.

The Lagrangian I just dropped in your lap is pure elegance..... that glowing ℒ = T − V, the action integral S = ∫ℒ d⁴x, and the fork in the road: “Choose your symmetry.” U(1) gives us clean, abelian light. SU(3) locks quarks in eternal confinement. SU(2)×U(1) + Higgs symmetry breaking hands us mass and the weak force. One minimal equation, three symmetry choices, three different universes.

The infographic screams the lesson: the symmetry group you choose determines which universe you get.

Now when I overlay my “human Lagrangian story,” you'll begin to see how Rockefeller stole TIME from humanity.

In photo-bio-energetics, the equivalent minimal action is the free-energy functional minimized across melanin-metal-photon interactions. Here T is the kinetic term of electron tunneling rates, V is the redox-potential landscape tuned by ligand environments and photonic charging. The “symmetry group” is the set of ligand-field symmetries around d-shell transition metals (Fe, Cu, Mo, Mn) while they are chelated inside melanin scaffolds. Light absorbed by melanin excites electrons, charges the metal “battery,” shifts its oxidation state, alters E°, and breaks symmetry — exactly like the Higgs mechanism in the diagram. That symmetry breaking propagates allosterically, reroutes metabolic flux (TCA → glycolysis, urea cycle tuning), and decides whether the cell lives in an aerobic or anaerobic “universe.”Centralized science picked the wrong symmetry group when it standardized on mice and rats.

**Why ALL Rodent Models Are Myopic** **— The Symmetry Mismatch Is Literal**
Standard laboratory rodents (and most wild strains) do not accumulate significant neuromelanin in catecholaminergic neurons the way humans do with age. In humans, neuromelanin granules in the substantia nigra pars compacta (SNpc) and locus coeruleus chelate Fe, Cu, and other metals, participate in ultra-weak photon emission (UPE) signaling, and act as the exact “battery” storage sites we have been discussing. Rodents lack this spontaneous pigmentation; Parkinson’s disease is essentially a human condition in that respect.

That is not a minor detail, it is a fundamental symmetry difference in the biological Lagrangian. Without the melanin-metal transducer, the rodent system cannot replicate the photonic charging, ligand-tuned E° shifts, or ROS/UPE feedback loops that define human redox homeostasis and neurodegeneration.To fix this, researchers now have to engineer “humanized” rodents by overexpressing human tyrosinase (hTyr) just to induce neuromelanin accumulation. Only then do the models start to recapitulate age-dependent dopaminergic loss, α-synuclein misfolding, and neuroinflammation that humans experience naturally.

The fact that the field had to retrofit human melanin machinery into rodents proves the original symmetry choice was invalid.Add the circadian and photonic mismatch: mice are nocturnal, their fur blocks broadband solar spectrum, their retinal photoreception and skin optics are nothing like ours, and common albino strains (used in thousands of studies) have zero melanin at all. Their metabolic “universe” is governed by a different symmetry group. When you run a drug trial in that universe and then translate to humans, you are comparing apples to a completely different force-carrying particle table.

**Rockefeller medicine knew this a long time ago and moved the goalpost for their benefit. It has destroyed humanity.**

My human Lagrangian "Unified Field Theory" of the Human Motor sees their nefarious behavior clearly. 25 years ago I moved the goalposts from "Biochemistry" to Network Physics in my brain and the output for my patients has been life changing.
Centralized science is "myopic" because it treats a mouse (a nocturnal, low-light, high-frequency rodent) as a functional proxy for a human (a diurnal, high-UV, low-frequency, large-brained primate). Their Boundary Conditions are the same (1G gravity, 21% Oxygen), but their Lagrangian Optimization is polar opposite. A mouse allocates energy to rapid kinetic turnover (T); a human allocates it to high-gain magnetic coherence (V).

**1\. The Death of the "Mouse Model"**
A mouse is a "Short-Circuit" specialist. It can handle high deuterium loads because its network nodes are physically close and its "timing" is high-speed/low-resolution.
The Human Problem: We are "High-Resolution/Long-Range." Our nodes (Gut-to-Brainstem) are separated by a transverse mesocolon and a vagus highway.
Impedance Matching: In a human, if the pancreas/bicarb exhaust (Node: Clearance) doesn't match the impedance of the thalamus (Node: Central Integration), the "comms" fail. A mouse doesn't have enough "network length" to experience the Lagrangian Shock that causes MS, TSC, or Peterson’s GABAergic collapse.

**2\. Resistance vs. Transfer Probability**
I’ve defined the Quantum Tunneling of health.
The Barrier: When deuterium (D+) and the 55 undeclared elements accumulate, they raise the Barrier Height (Resistance) between nodes.
Local Activity (The "Alien UPE"): As I've noted in other threads here on my website forum, a node (like an overclocked Complex IV) can still fire wildly, but because the impedance is mismatched, that energy cannot propagate. It becomes "Incoherent Heat" (inflammation). The "Alien UPE" is the photonic "scream" of a node that is disconnected from the network.

**3\. From "Energy Input" to "Network Coupling"**
It is no longer about how much "fuel" (food/light) you put in; it is about the Coherence of the wire.
Melanin as a Coupler: Melanin isn't just a "battery"; it is the Impedance Matcher. It uses its paramagnetic properties to "tune" the nodes so that the 3.25x magnetic moment of protium can carry information across the MITF-AMPAR loop without losing signal to the "heavy" deuterium mass.
The 40Hz "M" Tone: This is the Network Synchronizer. It provides the "carrier wave" that ensures all nodes are vibrating at the same frequency, allowing for Optimal Propagation.

**4\. The "Rhino" Network Victory**
The Rhino horn is the ultimate low-impedance hardware extension. It doesn't need "software" (a big brain) to manage the network because its hardware coupling (the melanized keratin) is so perfectly matched to the Earth’s magnetic ground.

**The MODERN EMI Human Challenge:** We have moved our "hardware" inside (encephalization). We are a Software-Defined Network. This makes us vastly more capable but infinitely more vulnerable to "Impedance Mismatches" caused by EMI, GLP-1s, and Gadolinium.

**The Decentralized Conclusion:** Health is Network Transparency. Disease is Network Opacity. My customized "Crater Protocols" (IV MB, Red Light, Bicarb) is a System-Wide De-Fragg. I'm clearing the "nodes" of isotopic sludge so the Standard Model of the human can finally achieve Superconductive Coherence. **Rockefeller medicine is a 51% wrench attack on humanity to induce TIME THEFT.**

My protocols are designed to clear isotopic sludge so protium can tunnel again. I'm restoring superconductive coherence in a Rockefeller universe that was deliberately engineered to run on friction. Rockefeller medicine is the ultimate 51% attack, inducing time theft by forcing every human node to waste energy on incoherent heat instead of coherent work. ### Jack Kruse — 2026-04-18T19:15:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/artists-corner-post-something-artistic-a-poem-a-painting.29405/post-371420>

When we choose to live in conscious partnership with nature rather than apart from it, we reclaim the ancient rhythm that our mitochondria still remember. These tiny organelles, inherited from symbiotic bacteria eons ago, thrive not on synthetic light or sterile air, but on sunlight filtering through leaves, fresh oxygen from living forests, clean water from wild streams, and the rich micronutrients of soil-grown food. By walking barefoot on the earth, breathing deeply under open sky, eating seasonally, and resting when the sun sets, we give our cellular engines the exact environmental cues they evolved to run on. In doing so, we do not merely survive; we allow our biology to express its fullest potential. Living with nature becomes an act of profound self-recognition: honoring the wild intelligence within every mitochondrion is the deepest way to become who we truly are.

Deep within each of our cells, mitochondria, those ancient endosymbiotic organelles descended from once-free-living bacteria, pulse with the quiet fire of oxidative phosphorylation, converting the breath of forests and the photons of sunlight into the universal currency of ATP. To live in conscious communion with nature is to speak the native language of these intracellular powerhouses: circadian light rhythms that entrain their fission and fusion dynamics, phytonutrient-rich wild foods that quench oxidative stress and support electron transport chain efficiency, grounding contact with the earth that stabilizes membrane potentials, and the pure oxygen exhaled by ancient trees that fuels the final acceptor in the respiratory cascade. When we walk barefoot upon living soil, breathe the volatile organic compounds of thriving ecosystems, and align our days with the solar cycle, we do not merely nourish the body; we restore the primordial dialogue between genome and environment. In this harmonious reciprocity, our mitochondria express their evolutionary birthright, generating not only energy but coherent vitality, the luminous signature of a life fully inhabited. Thus, returning to nature becomes the most intimate act of self-realization: honoring the wild intelligence glowing within every cell is the truest way to become who we truly are.

Deep within your sacred vessel, mitochondria, ancient star-born symbionts, maternal guardians of life force, hum with divine intelligence. Once free-living beings of light and breath, they now weave sunlight, forest air, and Earth’s own pulse into the golden threads of ATP, the very currency of your soul’s expression here on Earth.
To live in conscious communion with Nature is to remember this holy alliance. When you walk barefoot upon her living soil, you ground the electric fire of your cells. When you breathe the wild oxygen gifted by ancient trees and bathe in the soft rhythms of dawn and dusk, you attune your inner cosmos to the greater One. Energy from her wild bounty quench the flames of oxidative stress, allowing your mitochondrial temples to shine with coherent, life-affirming energy. In this sacred reciprocity, you do not merely generate power, you awaken the divine feminine wisdom encoded in every cell: the quiet knowing of adaptation, the gentle strength of endurance, the luminous joy of true vitality. Returning to Nature is the deepest homecoming for you mitochondria. It is the soul remembering its origin. It is the body becoming a clear channel for Spirit.

Honor the wild intelligence glowing within you.
Step outside.
Breathe her in.
Let her fuel your light.
Deplete her of of her D+
You were never separate
You only acted as if you were.
Your diseases where her whisper to return
You are Nature, awake in human form.
Who else feels the call to live this way?
Share this with someone who needs to remember their inner fire.
Feed the the fire of H+
#MitochondrialMagic #NatureAsMedicine #SoulAwakening #LiveYourLight

### stuart31 — 2026-04-19T19:50:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/artists-corner-post-something-artistic-a-poem-a-painting.29405/post-371526>

Reflecting on my life story, I find it's good to think negatively at times. For the purpose of increasing the odds of not repeating mistakes in the future.

But it's also important to think positive. To think of the love, care, concern, happiness, affection. These are fundamental for the continuation of the species. Not exclusively but are a necessary part.

Many lower animals have these qualities of affection for their offspring but haven’t become dominant. As has been said here many times, humans with their powerful brain matter is what has allowed their dominance. Ironically this gave us the ability to change our environment so readily to our benefit and thriving but also change it to our detriment. From a biophysics perspective, modern human environs are hardly recognizable from the origins evolution selected for.

This makes me think of the concepts in ancient Chinese philosophy from the Yellow Emperor's classic, Bian & Hua. These are pattern of nature that drive transformation due to seasonal shifts on earth. Hua transformation brings things up into higher levels of complexity and order while Bian breaks down with a loss of energy. Hua correlates with spring/summer and Bian correlates with fall/winter. Bian & Hua are important in Chinese Medicine theory for human physiology driven by yin-yang dynamics.

Nei Jing Su Wen ch 66:
“故物生谓之化，物极谓之变”
“Therefore, the birth/generation of things is called **hua** 化; when things reach their extreme, it is called **bian** 变.”

The modern environment on earth could be eroding not just what keeps us healthy but even our humanity. This is not necessarily a part of a “grander cycle” of coherence of a collective organism. This could just be the decline/extinction of a species due to falling out of coherence with its own supporting environment. A form of autoimmune disease on the planet.

I suppose the change in earth's magnetic field or an ice age could be examples of grander Bian/Hua cycles in the fractal of nature. These greater cycles are often periods of destructive transformation followed by greater times of coherence and higher order forms of life.

Similarly, in complex multicellular organisms there is a coherent process whereby cells, the individual parts of the organism, lay down and die when they are no longer viable to make room for more healthy tissue to take over. This is a form of self-sacrifice to the greater good of coherence of the overall organism. Humans' technology destroying their conditions for living is a form of loss of coherence in the higher organism and thus is could be though of as an example of Hua transformation run amok. A “cancer” of the species.

We live in an interesting time. Perhaps a transition from a Hua to a Bian period as of the past 100 or so years. Even in destructive times, there is a way to be in right relationship with the world and those around us. How to restore coherence to the organism? It's not to “die well and gracefully” or “go down peacefully and quietly”, in fact it's the opposite. How do we do this? By emitting our own coherent light to shine for others to catch the signal through all the noise of modern life. By engaging with others who are receptive to the signal and supporting and upholding them. Not “I’ll take care of you, if you take care of me.” but “I'll take care of me for you, if you take care of you for me.”

If summer is giving way to the weeping of autumn, light your own fire to radiate the warmth through the cold days. If the sun is going into darkness, let's create our own light inside of each of us like early eutherian mammals did to outlive the dinosaurs and take part in the fractal pattern of nature.

Think better
Get clear on your plans and execute
Get the fuck out of Chernobyl, be an astronaut that chose to stay on earth

### Jack Kruse — 2026-04-29T07:33:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/artists-corner-post-something-artistic-a-poem-a-painting.29405/post-372421>

Albert Einstein once remarked, “You know, Henri, I began by studying mathematics, but eventually turned to physics.”Henri Poincaré asked, “Why was that?”Einstein replied, “Because although I could distinguish true statements from false ones, I couldn’t determine which were truly important.”Poincaré smiled and responded, “That’s quite interesting, Albert. I began with physics, but ultimately chose mathematics.”Einstein, intrigued, asked, “And why did you make that change?”Poincaré answered, “Because I couldn’t tell which of the important facts were actually true.”

This is a clever, apocryphal (almost certainly made-up) anecdote that uses a mirrored conversation to highlight the fundamental philosophical difference between mathematics and physics—and why each field can feel incomplete on its own.

Breakdown of the exchange:
Einstein’s path (math → physics):
He says he could easily tell which mathematical statements were true or false (because math is built on rigorous logic and proof).
But he couldn’t decide which of those true statements were important—i.e., which ones actually mattered for understanding the universe.
So he switched to physics, where “importance” is obvious: the laws that govern reality are the ones worth pursuing.

Poincaré’s path (physics → math):
He flips it perfectly. In physics, he could clearly see which facts were important (they explained real-world phenomena, experiments, or natural laws).
But he couldn’t be sure which of those important facts were actually true, because physics relies on observation and experiment, which are always provisional and can be overturned by better data or a deeper theory.

The joke’s punchline

The dialogue is symmetric and ironic: each man found the other discipline to be the one that supplied exactly what his original field lacked.

-   Mathematics gives you certainty (true/false is black-and-white), but not direction (what’s worth proving?).
-   Physics gives you direction (what’s worth knowing about the world?), but not certainty (truth is always approximate).

Together, the two fields form a kind of perfect partnership: physics tells you where to look, mathematics gives you the tools to be sure you’re right. The story is a witty tribute to that complementarity, wrapped in the friendly banter of two giants of science who respected each other deeply.It’s the intellectual version of “You complete me.”

**WHAT IS MY ART HERE?**

Pointing out a deeper truth........

I think the real punchline here was not made because its ironic Math and physics are impotent to find the truth that underpins how Nature and life really operates. Both describe it, but neither explain it fully.

The surface irony is charming and true: math gives you airtight certainty but no compass for importance; physics hands you a map of what matters in the real world but leaves you forever unsure if today's "truth" will survive the next experiment. They complete each other beautifully for describing nature.

But the deeper cut, is that even when you put the two together, they still only "describe." They never quite explain the bedrock “why” that makes Nature tick the way it does, or what life actually is beneath the machinery.

-   Physics and math can tell you, with stunning precision, how an electron orbits, how gravity bends spacetime, how DNA replicates, how a neuron fires.
-   They cannot tell you why there is something rather than nothing, why the constants are tuned just so life can exist, or why subjective experience (consciousness, qualia, the felt “aliveness” of life) arises at all from those equations.

This is not a failure of the fields; it’s a feature of their design. Science is the ultimate “how-to” manual for the universe. It has zero obligation to answer the “why-is-there-meaning” or “what-is-the-essence-of-life” questions. Those live one layer deeper, in metaphysics, in philosophy of mind, in the hard problem of consciousness, or (for some) in the domain that used to be called theology.

Einstein himself wrestled with this. He called the comprehensibility of the universe “the eternal mystery.” He once wrote that the most incomprehensible thing about the world is that it is comprehensible… yet that comprehensibility stops at the shoreline of ultimate reality.

Poincaré, the mathematician, was even more skeptical of “ultimate” explanations; he saw science as a set of useful conventions, not a window into the noumenal.So your version of the punchline is sharper and more existential: the two giants are essentially admitting, through their career U-turns, that even working in tandem their tools are impotent to reach the final truth about how Nature and life reallyoperate. They map the territory brilliantly......

But neither of them can tell you why the territory exists, or why it feels like anything to walk through it.That’s why the anecdote feels like a polite, gentlemanly conversation between two titans… while quietly whispering the same uncomfortable truth that has haunted every deep thinker since: the universe is intelligible, but it refuses to hand over the user manual for its own source code.

### Kpack12 — 2024-09-05T02:10:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-interested-in-moving-to-es.29411/post-339293>

Hello-
Quick intro... I've been following Jack Kruse for years and have changed a lot of my habits. I try to get as much sun as possible, but I live in Idaho so our solar window is closing soon  I have been dealing with autoimmune alopecia since 2008, but I experienced a huge flare last fall and ALL my hair fell out for the first time ever. I know emotional stress/trauma is a huge part of this and I have done and continue to do a lot of work in this area. In fact it was alopecia lead me to my current career as a brain trainer using neurofeedback. I feel like I have found both my passion (psychology/neuroscience/trauma healing) and purpose (being of service to humanity to inspire others to embrace their self-healing capacities).

Now that I am an empty nester I am contemplating taking my health journey to the next level by moving to ES to get more sun and nature! One huge hurdle is how to make a living. My current practice is in person (neurofeedback is an in person modality; no way to do it remotely). There are things I could do remotely, but I need some time to build those platforms and clientele, which I am starting to do. I do have some savings and some BTC that could sustain me for a bit, but not long term.

Are there any other practitioners in ES who are doing an in-person practice? I am looking into the laws/regulations around neurofeedback in ES (it doesn't require a license in the US and I don't have one). What is the environment like for practicing/networking among the expat community? TIA for any input!!
\-Katie

# Thread: Structured Water and Conspiracy
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/structured-water-and-conspiracy.29412/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ZacharyNow — 2024-09-06T20:10:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/structured-water-and-conspiracy.29412/post-339346>

Hello all,
I have a deep interest in understanding both light and water. With that said, I want to share a story of recovery and healing that made me question some things. I am posting here in hopes you all can help further understanding of the principles and mechanisms involved.

long story short, I have made several bad decisions that span my adolescence and adulthood, including smoking, marijuana use, insane caffeine intake and screen time, and other not so savory decisions.
After deciding to take Jack‘s info and incorporate it my life (along with other things), I have noticed almost zero depression, anxiety, stress, etc.

Morning sun while grounding has been critical, along with minimal exposure to lighting after the sun sets and no blue light on my devices all day. I also have been filling a glass gallon with RO water and placing it in direct sunlight for min. 8 hours. This is all I have been drinking.

After spending time in the province in the Philippines (like the countryside in the US, but tropical and mountainous), I became accustomed to taking a cold shower by manually pouring water over my head via a pitcher instead of the normal shower .
I used this “tech” here at home in the US and made my outdoor minimalistic shower using well water.

I then decided to quit smoking, drinking, caffeine, screen time, and more. But I wanted to do it all at once in order to shock my body instead of the tapering approach I have done in the past. But this time, the sun routine, grounding, cold outdoor shower (naked), cardio exercise (walking/running), and structured water via direct sunlight combined resulted in ZERO issues. No withdrawal issues, no anxiety, depression, aggression, headaches, etc. I was so shocked I had to figure out why…

I say all of that to really ask about water.
Is this placing of water in direct sunlight just woo or what? Anecdotally, I notice more energy and vigor.
I then did some research on well water which is high in sulfates. (mine smells like eggs and farts) Turns out Sulphur is essential in creating insulin as well as many other processes. If S is so important, why are we removing it from our water? Is this just a crazy idea, or can diabetes be linked to the removal of sulphur from our water? Can my own rapid recovery be due in part by this ‘structured water’ and sulphur-rich shower water? Many questions are unanswered. Hope you guys can help provide insight

### La-Shawn Hill — 2024-10-24T18:22:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/structured-water-and-conspiracy.29412/post-341504>

Dr. Kruse, are "clinical depression" and "poor redox" the same thing? I struggle with calling myself "depressed" because I don't feel sad, I just feel low-energy and literally can't perform certain tasks that I used to, like making my bed, due to executive dysfunction. If I had to describe it, I feel like I've been locked out of certain parts of my brain. Are there any treatments centralized medicine offers that you'd recommend? I have an appointment with a neurologist in December, and I kept my appointment with Environmental Health Center-Dallas. I do have above ground electrical lines running by street in front of house, but they are about 75’ away.

In the past, I have use grounding sheets and mats but they don't quite get there as much as being barefoot at the beach or the countryside... my husband is still using the sheets when in Qatar but not sure if that helps mitigate/counteract the massive EMF over there... (last time I was there I seriously had to leave and first thing I did back at home was laying in the ground of my patio/garden).
I am surprised that the plugging on the earth didn't work... that was my idea of re-use at some point the existing sheets and mat that I got ( [https://aarding.org/collections/earthing-accessories/products/aardingspin-earthing-rod](https://aarding.org/collections/earthing-accessories/products/aardingspin-earthing-rod) ).

### Branden G — 2024-12-14T18:27:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-mats.29413/post-343887>

I would use a grounding rod outside of your bedroom window and connect the alligator clip to your bed or sock.

### BruceKing — 2024-12-15T16:30:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-mats.29413/post-343935>

> [BruceKing said:](/goto/post?id=343100)
>
> I think the problem with grounding mats around electricity is that you are grounding yourself to the 60 Hz AC electricity EMF that is coming at you instead of naturally grounding like you would out in nature on the ground.

My previous statement might be wrong. Due to the "umbrella effect" EMF's are pushed away from the body when grounding. This seems to indicate that you are repelling the EMF created by 60hz AC current in your house NOT creating a current in your body from being grounded around 60hz AC electricty EMF.

Today, I will share how to get rid of toenail fungus for under $2. Toenail fungus is in the family of fungi, yeast, and mold. The body contains beneficial fungi as well pathogenic or unfriendly fungi. These microorganisms love a warm moist environment. A common medication for toenail fungus is Lamisil. Potential side effects of Lamisil:
• Increased liver enzymes
• Liver damage
• Lupus
• CNS damage
• Depression
• Loss of taste
• Jaundice
• Alopecia Natural remedies for toenail fungus:
1\. Epsom salts (this remedy will take time) What to do:
• Add 2 tsp. Epsom salts to 4 cups of hot water
• Soak your feet in the solution for 15 minutes a day
• Dry your feet well
• Let your feet breathe
2\. Tea tree oil
• It has been used topically for a long time for coughing, acne, dandruff, lice, scabies, and fungus
• It’s a natural wide spectrum anti-fungal compound
• It inhibits fungal mycelium 3. Iodine
• Put one drop of iodine on the toenail fungus every day
• Get decolorized iodine (will not stain) or povidone-iodine (can stain)
• Keep your feet dry
• Let your feet breathe Fungi live on carbs. If you consume fewer carbohydrates, your risk of the toenail fungus returning will drop dramatically. "

### Anthony Smith — 2025-02-01T03:33:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nasty-looking-toe-fungus-worsening.29417/post-346059>

This morning I inspected my foot and found a purple spot far away from the nail at the base of the toe, actually between the big toe and next toe. I’ve never had major circulation issues, don’t know if I have blood sugar issues but it’s looking like maybe now a vascular issue? This whole thing is dumbfounding. I don’t have pain or noticeable coldness in my foot either, so much doesn’t make sense here!
>
> ### “Blue Toe Syndrome?”​

Worked with a guy a few years back that made a hot/cold therapy machine that delivered hot then cold via a pad to an area of the body. He saved several people from amputation by re-introducing circulation to the lower leg/foot.

Put your foot into hot water for 2 mins
Take it out for one min.
Put it into cold (iced) water for 2 mins.
Take it out for 1 min.
Repeat for 30/45 mins.

This treatment was used 100+ years ago very successfully to treat circulation issues before centralization (drug therapy) took over.
This is not medical advice. I am not a Doctor. I just know one who is on TV (the internet) from time to time.

I would clean out my liver and bile. That is the most important thing to start with. As I did this the circulation in my toes have improved a my toes are pretty again. Be thankful for your toe fungus. It is showing you change is needed

These were done post-7 months of sunrise to sunset sun exposure straight. Historically I've had total chol of 350-400 my entire life and this is the first time it's been at these levels.

The low AP of 25 is probably due to donating blood maybe a week before doing these labs.

Sodium was prob from blood donation or just sweating so much being out in 110+ degrees every day.

Is there anything that stands out here as something I should continue to address? Might do another test in Jan/Feb after spending the winter in a fairly cold/dark environment just to see how I do eating only protein and fats and focusing more on deliberate cold exposure instead of UV light + more carb intake.

# Thread: CT and sauna - my hypoglycemic reaction
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-sauna-my-hypoglycemic-reaction.29424/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Emmanuela — 2024-09-10T05:39:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-sauna-my-hypoglycemic-reaction.29424/post-339441>

Hi all,

I am almost done reading the CT series and all the pinned threads here. I was reading the IR and CR pinned thread and got curious.
So, i don’t have t2d or the like. However, i have had issues with hypoglycemia in the past if i would go more than 3 hours without food and if i would eat food too high in carbs.
Now i have been doing the leptin rx and CT on and off (mostly on) for a couple months. My hypoglycemia symptoms evaporated.
BUT, twice, i went with a friend to an outdoor spa. Did the sauna and lots of CT, out in the sun a lot. And both times, i experienced a hypoglycemia episode after getting back into the cold baths after eating lunch. Both times i stuck to a low carb lunch, mostly meat (what was available there) and i got dizzy, woozy, drunky almost and it lasted for quite a while.
Thoughts?

### Emmanuela — 2024-09-14T05:16:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-sauna-my-hypoglycemic-reaction.29424/post-339554>

I finished reading up to CT 11 blog and i actually think i am beginning to understand why i am having such a hard time cold adapting along with issues like the hypoglycemia. Being vegetarian for so long (since birth) up until 10 years ago, i probably have very poor mineral stores. CT 11 mentions hair samples as a measure of mineral deficiencies. But i am thinking i need to transiently supplement magnesium as I am eating seafood and oysters at the max of my affordability and the connection Kevin makes in that blog really resonated with me.

How do nnEMFs from wireless tech affect us mechanically?
Do they steal electrons? If so how?
Is it all about the photoelectric effect, everywhere?
Does that mainly mean - simply put:
electrons on the valence shell of atoms in molecules absorb energy from radiation and create havoc due to this added unnatural energy?
What are the main places/mechanisms of interaction? The skin? The eyes?
Are there any other mechanism? What creates brain waves? How do nnEMFs interfere with our brain waves?
Is it only about excess reactive oxygen/nitrogen species or what other mechanisms are there to it?
I've printed out hundreds maybe thousands papers of blogs and read a lot but not all.

I am here to learn about EMFs.
Thanks!

I thought so too. Have you tried one, pfedorow?

I have the safe and sound pro 2 for RF and a small esi for LF.

\*SSP2 RF: below 1microwatt/M2 especially at night. May go up a bit in the morning. (within measurement reach of the SSP2)
\*ESI LF electric: yes : around the radiator & around wires at ceiling for light - magnetic: no
\*The breaker to this room for light is off. There seems to be jump conduction.
\*The breaker to the plugs (2) is not off. Impossible for now.
\*The ESI does not sense these LF fields in my bed
\*Bed = (away from wall: 30-40cm but wires seem not to be there but up in ceiling)
\*Bed = away from radiator about a meter and I lay +- another extra 1/2 meter away from
\*Body voltage meter = 5000 milivolts when standing upright lower when lying down. Nit sure if I use it correctly (AC current position)

People travel w/ these tents in hotels etc. all the time.
Anyone here given it a try?

# Thread: New here from Missouri
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here-from-missouri.29428/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 23
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jhack — 2024-09-10T21:44:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here-from-missouri.29428/post-339457>

I’ve been navigating long COVID symptoms for over 2 years now. I’m doing better than I was, but not where I want to be. I am a registered nurse with 18+ years of experience. I had to do a medical retirement as I am not able to return to work due to the lasting effects of COVID. I have researched so much information during this time. And I have came to the conclusion that I am the only one who can fix me.
The healthcare system we have is truly not looking out for our best interests. Working in the healthcare field is deceiving, bc most of us want to do it to help people. And in some ways we do. But, we go into it not realizing that workers on the base level (frontline, bedside, working hands on with patients, doctors, nurses, techs, etc) are being played like puppets! We are only taught what they want us to know. And half of that is not the truth.
I have lived for 49 years and have definitely been through some stuff. I would even say I’m pretty wise both common sense and book smart. But, over these last two years of no longer living as a puppet and being on the patient side of things, Wow! It doesn’t matter how much healthcare workers care, it’s not enough to truly be able to help the patients. Wether it’s due to time restraints on visits, labs, tests, and medications not being covered bc insurances companies dictate what the doctor can and can’t do for you, and lack of information or misinformation, patients aren’t getting what they need.
Don’t get me wrong, healthcare is great for broken bones, wounds, trauma, surgeries when needed, but otherwise it’s a 50/50 chance or less of getting the help you need. I don’t believe anyone anymore! It shouldn’t be this difficult.

### doctorwu85 — 2024-09-10T22:30:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here-from-missouri.29428/post-339458>

Welcome. It's valuable having people who have seen the nature of the centralized healthcare beast firsthand.

I don't know much about long COVID, but it sounds like people have had success treating it by changing their environment. What part of the world are you in and did you get the jab?

### Jhack — 2024-09-10T22:44:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here-from-missouri.29428/post-339461>

Thanks. I’m in Missouri. And yes I did, 2 Pfizer and 1 Moderna booster.

Unfortunately this might be the real issue. The jabs can wreck your cellular redox, ruin the zeta potential of your blood, and cause a bunch of other issues that lead to chronic disease.

Here are a couple Jack posts that reference long covid and other chronic conditions, as well as a great Twitter thread:

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-adrenal-fatigue-just-another-manifestation-of-the-blue-light-hazard.24316/#post-328408](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-adrenal-fatigue-just-another-manifestation-of-the-blue-light-hazard.24316/#post-328408)

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-covid-higher-brain-heteroplasmy-due-to-melanopsin-damage.27749/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-covid-higher-brain-heteroplasmy-due-to-melanopsin-damage.27749/)

### [x.com](https://x.com/DrJackKruse/status/1725838170573463984)

 x.com

You want to do anything possible to improve redox -- reduce nnEMF, get morning sun and as much sunlight as possible, practice cold thermogenesis, grounding daily, block blue light, no artificial light at night, etc. Uncle Jack has recommended drastic measures such as moving to the tropics for people who got the jabs.

Best of luck with this, and be sure to post about the things you end up trying and your progress.

### India Rose Hopper — 2024-09-11T03:05:41+0300

> I’ve been navigating long COVID symptoms for over 2 years now. I’m doing better than I was, but not where I want to be. I am a registered nurse with 18+ years of experience. I had to do a medical retirement as I am not able to return to work due to the lasting effects of COVID. I have researched so much information during this time. And I have came to the conclusion that I am the only one who can fix me.
> The healthcare system we have is truly not looking out for our best interests. Working in the healthcare field is deceiving, bc most of us want to do it to help people. And in some ways we do. But, we go into it not realizing that workers on the base level (frontline, bedside, working hands on with patients, doctors, nurses, techs, etc) are being played like puppets! We are only taught what they want us to know. And half of that is not the truth.
> I have lived for 49 years and have definitely been through some stuff. I would even say I’m pretty wise both common sense and book smart. But, over these last two years of no longer living as a puppet and being on the patient side of things, Wow! It doesn’t matter how much healthcare workers care, it’s not enough to truly be able to help the patients. Wether it’s due to time restraints on visits, labs, tests, and medications not being covered bc insurances companies dictate what the doctor can and can’t do for you, and lack of information or misinformation, patients aren’t getting what they need.
> Don’t get me wrong, healthcare is great for broken bones, wounds, trauma, surgeries when needed, but otherwise it’s a 50/50 chance or less of getting the help you need. I don’t believe anyone anymore! It shouldn’t be this difficult.

Welcome!

### Jhack — 2024-09-13T05:58:22+0300

>
> [https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-covid-higher-brain-heteroplasmy-due-to-melanopsin-damage.27749/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-covid-higher-brain-heteroplasmy-due-to-melanopsin-damage.27749/)
>
>
>

>
>  x.com
>
>
> You want to do anything possible to improve redox -- reduce nnEMF, get morning sun and as much sunlight as possible, practice cold thermogenesis, grounding daily, block blue light, no artificial light at night, etc. Uncle Jack has recommended drastic measures such as moving to the tropics for people who got the jabs.
>
> Best of luck with this, and be sure to post about the things you end up trying and your progress.

Thanks so much for the information! I have tried so many things!! And I started multiple treatments/supplements/meds/therapies, you name it, some at the same time so it’s really hard to say what worked by itself or in conjunction with something else. Although I have noticed and said from the first time I started trying it, Red/Near-Infrared light therapy definitely always gives me a little boost of energy! Also, I have been to Florida a few times since I had Covid/long Covid and I always feel better when I’m there! I can’t stay in the heat/sun too long without being in water because I developed orthostatic hypotension from Covid, but I do feel generally better when I’m there.
Ironically, I had just started checking into jobs and places to move to around the Destin/ Pensacola area right before I got sick. The warm weather, sun, and ocean has always been calming and therapeutic for me. But….after getting sick and not being able to go back to work, I wasn’t able to do that.
So…the things I’ve tried to treat long covid: supplements (fish oil, B complex, multivitamins, vitamin D and K2, 1stPhorm am and pm nootropics, zinc, vitamin C), clean eating, tons of sleep (during the day bc of course I have insomnia during the night), decreased stressors, started practicing mindfulness/meditation and breathing techniques, I went to physical therapy in the beginning but it wasn’t until after I had completed 10 weeks that I realized it had probably done more harm than good, chiropractic care (which I am still doing), prolotherapy in my neck (it helped but unfortunately I couldn’t afford to get all the treatments I needed), gentle yoga, occupational therapy, speech therapy, Reiki, limbic system rewiring, vagus nerve exercises, yoga nidra, steroid inhaler and singular for the asthma that came raging back, Zyrtec/claritin (have been on forever bc of allergies), Pepcid, Prozac (for serotonin), melatonin, LDN, protein and amino acid powders. I tried other supplements and meds that I was unable to take bc they lowered my b/p even more. I was put on Florinef and midodrine early on for the OH and still take it. It helps, but I have to take the midodrine 3 times a day while I’m awake or else my b/p drops every time. I wear compression garments, drink 2-3 L of water per day, and sometimes salt capsules to keep b/p up when I am up and doing things. I also have been doing CBT and hypnosis with a therapist to help deal with the effects of everything.
I started seeing a environmental health specialist (functional med doctor) back in the spring of this year. Since, I have started on allergy shots weekly, thyroid med for under active thyroid, magnesium, calcium, potassium ( oral and IV), nystatin for SIFO, probiotics, no sugar no yeast diet, methyl-B complex, all with little improvement, so after adrenal function test I was started on hydrocortisone for adrenal insufficiency. I’m still trying to get to the right dose for my adrenal and thyroid meds.
I will say I’m way better than where I once was….but, not back to 100% or even 75% of where I used to be prior to Covid.
I’m still affected by: cognitive impairment (brain fog, memory loss, easily distracted, word recall, unable to multitask, and I’m much slower at figuring things out than before), fatigue (some days I’m bed bound if I over do it 12-24 hours earlier), shortness of breath, exercise intolerant (my heart rate increases drastically and even starting recumbent and doing very little I’m unable to function the next day), I have to pace at everything I do. On top of that I have tinnitus, joint/muscle pain, visual disturbances, headaches, etc.
prior to Covid I felt relatively healthy. I worked as a RN in a rural hospital doing LDRP with normal deliveries and high risk deliveries. I worked seven 12 hour shifts every two weeks and took call. We did rotating shifts so we would do 10-12 weeks of day shift and then 8 weeks of night shift. I also worked part time at a surgery center on some of my days off. I was working out with a trainer twice weekly and then a day or two by myself weekly doing weights and resistance training. I have one teenage boy who is active in sports and one college age boy both at home that I was always doing things with (playing bball, going places, taking to practices and games. And I was doing all those things successfully. And now….I get to do only one major thing a day if that even (like go to the grocery store, or go to a ball game, or going to a doctors appointment, or clean one room of my house a day). But I am thankful that my mind and body is working better than it was in the beginning. I was mostly bed bound for the first year and a half and felt like I had dementia! No lie! I have came so far from that. And I don’t want to ever feel like that again!
If you’re still reading, thank you for taking the time to hear part of my story and learn why I’m here. As much as I hate telling/admitting it and talking about it, hopefully it will be able to help someone else who may be going through some of this too. Thanks ☺️

Welcome to the forum and thanks for sharing. So many of us tried many things but sometimes there is one more thing that has a butterfly effect. It’s because different people have different specific parts “broken”. The core recipe that this forum and Jack teaches is definitely the basics: sunlight morning till evening, circadian biology, leptin prescription, minimize EMF, and if you can, spend time at low latitude. But in addition to what everyone suggested I discovered recently that a leaky blood brain barrier (caused by inflammation, the jab, EMF exposure etc) can in many people lead to metal toxicity in the brain. A zeolite detox has done wonders in people with ALS, cancer, depression, autism and long COVID. Common to all is neuroinflammation and poor mitochondrial health. It does not hurt to add that. In my household that’s what is having a positive butterfly effect, but I think only because I did everything else too. The one that I had success with is called Mytho Tecq Zeolite detox.

In your story of your ordeal I noticed one thing. The basics is still the timing of your circadian clock that you cannot compromise as several members commented on. Awake at night and sleeping during day would wreck further havoc in your system. As other members posted the #1 thing is to wake up with the sunrise, see the sunrise, soak in the solar noon at least for a bit, block blue light after sunset. Only eat during day and outside. Completely darken for sleep and eliminate EMF (both the 60 hz source and the RF) and ground. And in your case probably a 55 degree CT would be also good. I just want to emphasize that we all agree that this is the basics. Otherwise the timing of when your body generates the right chemistry and when your body needs to detox and hibernate is all off. My report on detox is an add-on to the foundational basics that is doing wonders in my case.

I can relate. I am a nurse that worked night shift for 20+ years, fortunate for me that I live at a low latitude now and have minimal EMF around me -for the most part. I am pretty tight about my day/night cycles - but I don't get the chance to stay in one place to long... hopefully that will change moving forward. I can say with certainty that when I travel a lot I feel worse and it is obvious that I feel better when I come back to ES. I think the older I get the more sensitive I am to the changes and plan to stay put now that I figured that out... I have much healing to do for the havoc I have reaked on my body up to this point. My advice - be gentle with yourself. We all want to fix things yesterday and time is definitely not on our side as we age but accepting yourself for where you are and when you know better you continue to do better will make it easier to get there.

Today I give you a gift. It is my mitochondriac anniversary on April 2nd. I post this for my Mom. She used to call me at this time every year. Now i am calling you, my tribe. It is time to wake up.

[https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-40-124743154](https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-40-124743154)

"The sage doesn't seek to be profound but is useful. A sage himself is a former fool who got tired of themselves."

This insightful quote paints a picture of wisdom born not from an innate desire for grandeur but from a humble journey of self-awareness and growth. A decentralized sage, in this light, is not someone who sets out to dazzle the world with lofty thoughts but rather an individual who, through the weariness of their own folly, discovers a practical clarity. Their usefulness emerges from their centralized experience, mistakes made, lessons learned, and a quiet resolve to move beyond the foolishness of their centralized past. It’s a reminder that true depth often comes not from chasing profundity but from the simple, steady act of outgrowing one’s former self.

### AKJOHN — 2024-09-12T02:39:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-alaska.29430/post-339500>

I only very recently heard about Jack Kruse. In the month since I first heard Jack Kruse talk at the Brighteon University EMF Symposium, I have listened to about 10 of Jack's amazing talks, and I bought and read the book, The Monk who sold his Ferrari. I'm surprised I had never heard about Jack before as I do a lot of reading on health issues. I found it amazing that Jack lost all that weight and it was all centered around light from the sun. One of the Podcasts Jack talks that we get 70% of our energy from the sun and 30% from food. This reminded me of the Godeaters, otherwise known as the **Eshwar Khanewale, The adepts who live in the spiritual city of Agam Des**; who partake of cosmic energy instead of material food, they live to great ages beyond the normal span of human life. Based on Eckankar. The cosmic energy is referred to as the ECK, holy spirit. In Eckankar the holy spirit is light and sound. What God created the universes with. I though a few may be interested in the spiritual viewpoint. Let me know if your interested and I'll tell you more. Light has long been a topic of interest to me, and I have a red light therapy pad I am using more. Seems to work great. I started looking at in the morning as we have had no sun out at all for several days. It put a bounce in my step and I did my 5 mile walk today effortlessly.

### AKJOHN — 2024-09-15T03:13:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-alaska.29430/post-339593>

Since I probably live further North than the majority of the people coming to this site. I believe I offer a unique perspective. I am 66 years old. I was born and raised in Chicago. I lived in the Wicker Park area, and then moved to Logan square area when I was 13. I moved to Alaska i when I was 21, and I have been here since 1979. I am now 66, and in relatively perfect health. I have never taken any prescription drugs for more than a couple days. I would like to say every Alaskan is just like me. But that would be a complete lie. Most Alaskans my age are complete wrecks. They take untold numbers of medications, and would be lucky if they could hike 3 miles. I take at least a 5 1/2 mile hike everyday, and several times a year I climb some of the 5000' peaks around Anchorage. My longest hike this summer was over 25 miles, 5000' elevation, and it took 14 hours. Doubters can look up Mount Willawaw and Ramp Peak, Chugach State park. I did them both in one day. More or less hiking the ridge line through complete wilderness. Even sadder on the health of people my age and up is how many have Parkinson's and other debilitating illness's. According to Jack, most would be preventable if they only got some sun. I have been a long time advocate of Holistic health. I do herbology, essential oils, food, Vitamins, and getting out in Nature. I did develop a sensitivity to the Sun in about 2005, but am no longer effected by it. I did some reading up on it and took fermented Cod Liver oil, and also spent a lot of time out in the Sun golfing, hiking, gardening, etc... So, that no doubt helped. Now that I know the importance of the sun I am getting out in it as much as I can. This can be a real challenge in Alaska. This was a terrible cloudy rainy week, and we had no sun till yesterday. I was able to walk in the sun for 1 1/2 hours with hat up and in my t shirt. When I got home I was able to sit under my big Blue Spruce in the Sun and ground with only shorts. Did that feel good. I have been experimenting with the Sun from Jacks tips. Before I always had my hat on. I started taking it off while in the sun, and now I can look right at the sun, and it doesn't bother me at all. I am also experimenting with CT. I took someones tip to start with regular water and then change to cold. So, I take my regular shower, and when it is finished I gradually turn the dial down. I just did it a few days, and I am already using 55 degree water for a few minutes. Really easy, and I didn't really get cold. I think it will be easy to work up to 10 minutes. I would be happy to answer any questions or to get some more tips. I did my 10 minutes of CT this morning at 55 degrees. Not bad at all. I was super energized yesterday after the CT. It was like a detox, ran to the bathroom several times at night and had super energy. Can't wait to see what today brings. It was also bright and sunny for a while this morning. So I sat in the sun eating breakfast.

### 1oevets — 2024-10-03T01:56:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-alaska.29430/post-340342>

We just moved from Wasilla to lytle Texas because I found jacks work. Didn’t want to leave Ak but my 7 yo developed ulcerative colitis 2 years ago. Cold plunging, redlight therapy and uv light therapy has helped keep him remission this last year.
This past year we got rid of all blue light in our house and our redlight panel in the morning and used oil lamps in the evening. We also cold plunged and made it a priority to get outside 2-3 hours a day. I was amazed how different I felt. No seasonal affective disorder, increased energy, gave up coffee easily, workout were better, kids moods were better, they fell asleep easily. Living according to “jacks rule” are tough up there but so worth it!

### AKJOHN — 2024-10-10T05:23:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-alaska.29430/post-340737>

Great to here from another Alaskan. I am also doing red light and infrared light treatments. I do it right after my CT shower. Both are just completely energizing and keep me feeling great all day. I just returned from a 10 trip to Washington and Idaho, and I made it a habit to go out and get some early morning sun. It's hard to keep a good diet when traveling. But with many hours a day out in the sun, I did not gain a pound. Jack seems 100% right. Carbs mean nothing if you are getting good sun.

# Thread: Vision deteriorate - Infrared/sun/covid/bodypain?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-deteriorate-infrared-sun-covid-bodypain.29432/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ziztine — 2024-09-12T16:52:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-deteriorate-infrared-sun-covid-bodypain.29432/post-339519>

Hi everyone,
I have been following Dr Jack Kruse for more than 8 years. This is my first post. I have pretty high myopia since young and started to have presbyopia many years ago but nothing serious. I am 51. But recently, the **vision in my right eye suddenly deteriorate**. It increased by 400 degrees. The strange thing is there is no change in my left eye.

One or more of these are what I suspect might have caused the sudden increase in myopia in my right eye:
(1) **Infrared heat lamp** - I have used this small table size lamp on the front of my right injured shoulder this year for less than 10 times. I normally have on my blue blockers while I surf on my desktop and use the lamp at the same time. Should I have protected my eyes from the infrared lamp?

(2) **Sun glazing** - I started having a bit more floaters end of last year. So I started to stare at the sun for 2-10mins daily during the sunrise hours in the hope that it will help with the floaters and overall vision and health. normally between 7am (sunrise) to 8am. I do not ever wear sunglasses but I do wear my spectacles when I farm in my two gardens (one on the rooftop). I only throw on a hat when its too hot. I am located just one degree of latitude above the Equator. Could the sun's ray be too strong here for sun gazing for so long?

(3) In July this year, I contacted the **covid virus**. It feels like my vision got a lot worse after this episode. My right eye has always been the weaker eye. The degree is a lot higher than my left all these years. Parasites? I am doing a 30 day liver cleanse now.

(4) **The right side of my body has been in pain** since the mid of last year when I sustained a muscle injury. I felt that besides my overall health, my vision deteriorated during the same period. But only my right eye is affected. Could it be connected to my right side body pains?

I do not know whether I should proceed with my routine of staring at the morning sun. Any advice would truly be appreciated.

### ziztine — 2024-09-12T17:36:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-deteriorate-infrared-sun-covid-bodypain.29432/post-339520>

[https://www.huffpost.com/entry/best-pot-for-houseplant\_n\_66e1f85be4b06d75dd27d6de](https://www.huffpost.com/entry/best-pot-for-houseplant_n_66e1f85be4b06d75dd27d6de)
So THAT'S Why It's So Important To Get Sunlight Every Morning

### [So THAT'S Why It's So Important To Get Sunlight Every Morning](https://www.huffpost.com/entry/sunlight-morning-better-sleep_l_66d864c7e4b0830f6e92e7af?)

Experts say it should be a routine, not a once-in-a-blue-moon occurrence.

 www.huffpost.com

"Experts say it should be a routine, not a once-in-a-blue-moon occurrence."

# Thread: Tips for sleeping in car?
