### eve001 — 2024-09-12T19:40:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tips-for-sleeping-in-car.29433/post-339525>

Practical tips for sleeping in car as comfy as possible and make it more fun?

I am at 50°. Autumn. Chilly and rainy often.
I have no camping experience.

There is a level difference between trunk and the back seats that are folded forward of about 12cm.
\==> how could I even that out best?

E-smog seems low here and my best option now.
I have no energy to go shopping, browse or flirt with the search function.

Body voltage seemed good. Very low.
I took my BV meter and put it on DC.
I then connected it to th earth (not too deep, impossible, wire too short).
Then I sat in trunk with rubber soles.

RF also. Peak below 1microwatt when measuring behind the metal.
Otherwise up to +- 4 when cars and so.

Loading my red/amber lights. Will take water.

Maybe put paper or maybe rescue blanket or aluminium foil against the windows... to block light and other nnEMFs?

So sick of this.

Hope this works for me to get more energy to leave this place.
Africa? That'd make for beautiful contrasts.

Cool song - Weird video clip - first time I see it. Expected a black skinned singer...

PS: If you think this can't happen to you... think again.
PPS: People with off-topic unhelpful responses will be blocked without goodbye.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-09-12T21:27:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tips-for-sleeping-in-car.29433/post-339527>

When sleeping in a car keep in mind it is a metal box. The car needs to away from any AC fields - buildings, overhead wires etc. You also want to be away from cell towers. You MUST turn of your phone and/or computer (WiFi, Bluetooth etc). Most people are not adversely affected by DC voltage from the car BUT some are. A friend has to disconnect the battery when he sleeps in his car.

### eve001 — 2024-09-12T21:41:33+0300

### Anthony Smith — 2024-09-12T23:34:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tips-for-sleeping-in-car.29433/post-339530>

It’s a car . . . Park where there is no nnEMF . . . IF DC fields bother you - disconnect the battery.
Rules for nnEMF mitigation:
#1 - turn off, disconnect, eliminate the source
#2 - distance from the source
#3 - shield from the source

### drsongihan — 2024-09-15T19:34:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tips-for-sleeping-in-car.29433/post-339605>

A trifold foam mattress or a custom foam mattress works well because the foam allows you to wedge it on uneven surfaces, push it into the foot space of back of car and it can take up many different shapes. Somewhat firm foam will help you not develop back pain. A trifold also allows you to bring that into a simple tent that you can always carry in the car whenever you are in a spot to put up a tent you have a full size mattress. If you have a tarp for wet ground, a tent and a trifold foam and a car, you can sleep almost anywhere anytime. Make sure you buy foam meant for sleeping — I worry about petroleum degassing for a while and the loose foam is bad because they degas for a while. But if you let it degas for a while and sleep with a crack of window open it should be fine. Foam soaks up humidity so important that you have a well absorbing cotton sheet around. I wish there was a shape shifting natural material but you could argue petroleum is “natural” as long as you don’t eat it

Driving to a low EMF place sounds great and maybe the car battery is not a problem for you. If you can drive and work remotely do you need to be at 50 Latitude? Wouldn’t it be better to drive to lower latitude? Even 40 is easier than 50. How about driving to a nearby Forrest or Mountain or at least a botanical garden? I think being near plants and trees are healing and EMF tend to be poor near Forrest’s.

I would always drive and sleep with window open. The windows will anyway let EMF and RF through (since your phone has reception in the car) so driving to a low EMF place would be best. But a closed car window filters all light except for visible in blue/green and only lets a bit of red through.

I would also take your water container and fill the bottom with quartz crystals. They are cheap, like $10 for a pound.

I think nature heals. Which continent are you in and which one is the closest National park?

If I have no kid to take care of, and if my health was to get into trouble or if I got sick from my environment or if I want to take a break from my work, I would totally get a truck and drive to my favorite mountain range, the Sierra Nevadas, like Yosemite and Kings Canyon or Sequoia or the Volcanoes in Lassen National park or to Yellow Stone National parks. Good light, water and magnetism there

### Uswebers1 — 2024-09-16T06:01:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tips-for-sleeping-in-car.29433/post-339616>

Zero degree (rated) sleeping bag

I am from Belgium, EU. Small country. Dense population. Few small/nature reserves. Wild camping forbidden.
We are entering autumn. Wet climate, chilly.
I tried my car 1 night and it needs tweaking.
Now moverd from 4000-5000mV BV bedroom (dark) to 50mV BV veranda (light comes in but Bodyvoltage (BV) significatly lower). I feel too weak to travel now. Hoped the melanin renovation past summer would have strengthened me. Only learned about BV last week. RF EMFs okay here.
Hopefully sleeping in veranda will allow me to gain energy.
Sleep not yet optimal here - adaptation I guess.
Will re-read your recommendations.
I hope to have enough energy/resources soon to travel through France/germany down South.

### MatthewT — 2024-09-12T21:18:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypoglycaemia.29434/post-339526>

from a quantum perspective what causes hypoglycaemia episodes and how would you attack it at its root?

I have had come out of nowhere the past two weeks what people describe as “reactive hypoglycaemia” when I wake up my BG levels are just right but the moment I have breakfast onwards from there my BG is like a rollercoaster, if I don’t eat every two hours I’ll have some seriously hypoglycaemic levels leading me with no choice but to consume fast acting carbs as I have got to levels like 1.2 mmol/L / 18mg/dl.

Had labs done and everything is fine, tested thyroid function, liver function, cortisol, a few other things but it’s all good, the Drs were suggesting it potentially some kind of viral infection as everything seems healthy but it doesn’t seem to be going away.

Rx for this situation?

Or

B: See sunrise then 10 mins after drive to the beach without the need for blue blockers but miss 20 minutes or so of the 1st hours infrared while driving?

I want to maximise the infrared and UVA I am getting by grounding in the sea with my top off.

# Thread: Advice for insomnia when early morning sun isn't enough?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advice-for-insomnia-when-early-morning-sun-isnt-enough.29439/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### vivianrosenthal — 2024-09-14T16:00:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advice-for-insomnia-when-early-morning-sun-isnt-enough.29439/post-339571>

Hello! I would love help and advice on how to help my insomnia. I have been getting up and seeing the sun for three months but unfortunately I am still not able to sleep through the night, and I'm exhausted and feeling sick from lack of sleep. Is there a red light machine that is recommended to increase light exposure since the morning routine hasn't been enough to help my hormones and melatonin production reset. Would love any advice or tips from folks who have struggled with insomnia, hormonal issues, melatonin production issues, etc. Thank you! Vivian

### rtd — 2024-09-14T16:11:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advice-for-insomnia-when-early-morning-sun-isnt-enough.29439/post-339572>

> [vivianrosenthal said:](/goto/post?id=339571)
>
> Hello! I would love help and advice on how to help my insomnia. I have been getting up and seeing the sun for three months but unfortunately I am still not able to sleep through the night, and I'm exhausted and feeling sick from lack of sleep. Is there a red light machine that is recommended to increase light exposure since the morning routine hasn't been enough to help my hormones and melatonin production reset. Would love any advice or tips from folks who have struggled with insomnia, hormonal issues, melatonin production issues, etc. Thank you! Vivian

A supplement mix of Magnesium BiGlycinate (400mg), L-theanine (400mg), and Inositol (1500mg) works for me. I also like NuCalm's Deep Sleep binaural beats.

### drsongihan — 2024-09-14T16:36:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advice-for-insomnia-when-early-morning-sun-isnt-enough.29439/post-339574>

I don’t know the answer to your situation but I can tell you what my friend and my son had to do for insomnia.

1\. Early morning sun. My friend went out at sunrise, I assume you are going out 30 min before sunrise or at least at sunrise and stay outside for at least 30 min.

2\. Blackout the room for sleep. No photons. So dark you cannot see your hand in front of your face. I closed crevices below door even. My friend also uses eye cover because her husband or kids open door sometimes to bedroom.

3\. Remove all electrical devices in your bedroom. Plug them all out. Remove WiFi, obviously remove phone from your bedroom and overall make sure there is no EMF pollution in your room.

4\. My friend takes over the counter micro dose of lithium. My son takes higher dose of lithium. Lithium helps anybody who has a weak electrical body and no robust bio electric. Lithium is different from supplements in that it does not alter your body’s receptors to down or up regulate things, unlike melatonin.

5\. Ground yourself as much as you can, especially before going to bed.

6\. No blue light after sunset. I have no blue light on after sunset in the entire house. Only amber or red light. If you look at electronics, wear amber blue blocking glasses. That’s critical that your eyes don’t get blue light at least 3 hours before bed.

7\. Go out at solar noon for at least 30 min to get UV light if you prepared your body to be able to do so. And wherever you go, in car, in office or at home, open windows. Far infrared, infrared and uv photons are all missing indoors.

That’s what helped two people close to me. I do the same and sleep better but I never had insomnia to begin with.

### maria@modernhuman — 2024-09-14T16:54:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advice-for-insomnia-when-early-morning-sun-isnt-enough.29439/post-339576>

Vivian it's good to address the fundamentals before spending money on gadgets, supplements, lotions, and potions. Re-assess your environment and remove things causing the assault.

### vivianrosenthal — 2024-09-19T23:50:28+0300

> I don’t know the answer to your situation but I can tell you what my friend and my son had to do for insomnia.
>
> 1\. Early morning sun. My friend went out at sunrise, I assume you are going out 30 min before sunrise or at least at sunrise and stay outside for at least 30 min.
>
> 2\. Blackout the room for sleep. No photons. So dark you cannot see your hand in front of your face. I closed crevices below door even. My friend also uses eye cover because her husband or kids open door sometimes to bedroom.
>
> 3\. Remove all electrical devices in your bedroom. Plug them all out. Remove WiFi, obviously remove phone from your bedroom and overall make sure there is no EMF pollution in your room.
>
> 4\. My friend takes over the counter micro dose of lithium. My son takes higher dose of lithium. Lithium helps anybody who has a weak electrical body and no robust bio electric. Lithium is different from supplements in that it does not alter your body’s receptors to down or up regulate things, unlike melatonin.
>
> 5\. Ground yourself as much as you can, especially before going to bed.
>
> 6\. No blue light after sunset. I have no blue light on after sunset in the entire house. Only amber or red light. If you look at electronics, wear amber blue blocking glasses. That’s critical that your eyes don’t get blue light at least 3 hours before bed.
>
> 7\. Go out at solar noon for at least 30 min to get UV light if you prepared your body to be able to do so. And wherever you go, in car, in office or at home, open windows. Far infrared, infrared and uv photons are all missing indoors.
>
> That’s what helped two people close to me. I do the same and sleep better but I never had insomnia to begin with.

### levkalmens — 2024-09-15T22:16:48+0300

I’ve disabled both wifi bands on my Xfinity Arris XB6 router. I tested the RF using the Cornet electrosmog meter and was surprised to see very high RF levels being emitted. I am wondering why that would be. Moving it is not an option as I am limited to only one coaxial input location. Did I incorrectly calibrate the settings or is there some other reason?

### doctorwu85 — 2024-09-16T18:15:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-rf-levels-from-xfinity-router.29442/post-339629>

Xfinity routers always broadcast a secret network that you cannot turn off. They say it's for their home security package, but who knows. I have not found a way to disable it. I guess calling them is worth a try, but I doubt they will help.

You have to switch to another ISP that uses different hardware if you want your wifi router to function only as a modem. I use AT&T and their Wifi gateways actually will disable wifi (verified by Safe and Sound meter) and function as a simple modem.

### EWO — 2024-09-24T08:14:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-rf-levels-from-xfinity-router.29442/post-339943>

I had this problem with equipment provided by Xfinity, and I solved it by purchasing another brand that was compatible with Xfinity. I can't remember at the moment if this other brand doesn't include wi-fi options or if I had to disable it. But the bottom line if there is no RF coming from that unit. I use a separate router for wi-fi on rare occasions.

### levkalmens — 2024-09-24T17:13:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-rf-levels-from-xfinity-router.29442/post-339952>

I can't remember at the moment if this other brand doesn't include wi-fi options or if I had to disable it. But the bottom line if there is no RF coming from that unit. I use a separate router for wi-fi on rare occasions.

Xfinity also broadcasts its hotspot network which can be disabled. On non Xfinity provided modem/routers when disabling the wifi radios it works perfectly. I have an access point set up and can turn on wifi when I need it but otherwise connect my laptop via ethernet. I wish I had tested my previous router, I thought I had wifi disabled for a few years when in reality it was broadcasting hidden channels, I never thought to check. Lesson learned.

### AlbertW — 2025-09-29T05:07:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-rf-levels-from-xfinity-router.29442/post-360144>

I literally just ran into this with a Cox modem at an Airbnb I’m staying at.

Turned off private WiFi networks, even asked host to log into their cox.com account and disable the public hotspot, but RF is still through the roof. Obviously hidden network still there.

I’ve read online that with Comcast, you can get escalated to their business support line to disable all radios, but that’s obviously a ridiculous ask of your Airbnb host, unless it’s a very long stay.

Of course I can just unplug the modem, but if I want to use hardwired Internet I’ll need to plug it back in.

What do you do in this situation? I have a Mission Darkness Faraday fabric to cover the modem (leaving open one side due to overheating), but I still need to keep a substantial distance to achieve < 10 uW/m2.

Any tips?

Can I “wrap” the router with something that won’t overheat it?

Maybe travel around with a Starlink travel dish?

I assume that if I bring my own modem, I’d need the Airbnb host to call the ISP to register it, so that’s a total no-go.

hi rusty! i just moved into a new apt and it already had this installed. can you tell me about it vs a usual router (like i had one from verizon previously)? i put the photo into Grok but i never know if it knows what its talking about! thanks!

### Rusty — 2025-10-07T13:27:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-rf-levels-from-xfinity-router.29442/post-360565>

> [HLG2025 said:](/goto/post?id=360417)
>
> hi rusty! i just moved into a new apt and it already had this installed. can you tell me about it vs a usual router (like i had one from verizon previously)? i put the photo into Grok but i never know if it knows what its talking about! thanks!

It's an in-wall Wi-Fi access point (AP) that's usually connected to the building’s main router.

You can log in to its admin page using the SSID shown on the label, find its IP (often 192.168.0.1 or 192.168.1.1) and try to disable WiFi

### HLG2025 — 2025-10-10T04:49:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-rf-levels-from-xfinity-router.29442/post-360806>

> [Rusty said:](/goto/post?id=360565)
>
> It's an in-wall Wi-Fi access point (AP) that's usually connected to the building’s main router.
>
> You can log in to its admin page using the SSID shown on the label, find its IP (often 192.168.0.1 or 192.168.1.1) and try to disable WiFi

thanks for the info! is it better/worse/same as the other kind of router in terms of the nnemfs/ef coming off of it if you are close to it?

### AlbertW — 2025-10-10T05:30:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-rf-levels-from-xfinity-router.29442/post-360810>

> [HLG2025 said:](/goto/post?id=360806)
>
> thanks for the info! is it better/worse/same as the other kind of router in terms of the nnemfs/ef coming off of it if you are close to it?

An AP (access point) is one of the functionalities typically provided in a router. It’s in charge of broadcasting WiFi so it’s just as bad in terms of RF as a typical router.

A “router” is really an Internet gateway plus a switch plus an AP all bundled together in one device.

### AlbertW — 2025-10-10T05:34:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-rf-levels-from-xfinity-router.29442/post-360811>

I recently stayed in an Airbnb that mounted a UniFi AP on a 20 ft+ ceiling powered by PoE via an Ethernet cable run through the roof then underground to some other home on the same property…couldn’t shield (during the day) since too high up, couldn’t unplug, couldn’t flip the breaker since it’s PoE from another house. Cutting the Ethernet cable with scissors would be the only option lol.

Can shield at night by sleeping in a bed canopy but not an option during the day.

### [JRS Eco 100 Era - Low EMF WiFi Router on Asus](https://www.jrseco.com/product/jrs-eco-100-era-low-emf-wifi-router-based-on-asus/?pk_campaign=JRS+Eco+100+Era+-+Thanksgiving&pk_kwd=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.jrseco.com%2Fproduct%2Fjrs-eco-100-era-low-emf-wifi-router-based-on-asus%2F)

WiFi routers and modems put out radiation 24 hours a day, even when you are not using the Internet. They broadcast so-called beacon signals with the name of the network, 10 times a second. So the biggest 24-hour EMF emitter is in your own home.…

 www.jrseco.com

This is basically an ASUS router that has custom software imbedded within in. It allows you to automatically have the router turn off when there are no WIFI-enabled devices nearby querying the router. Plus when it's in WIFI mode, it has 90% lower EMF output and you can set the output power directly. I use for ethernet connectivity most of the time, but set it up to allow the WIFI radio to turn on when a phone is trying to send/receive text messages over the ISP

### Uswebers1 — 2024-09-16T06:54:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/woodstock-we-are-stardust.29443/post-339617>

(Lyrics)
Well, I came upon a child of God
He was walking along the road
And I asked him, Tell where are you going?
This he told me

Said, I'm going down to Yasgur's Farm,
Gonna join in a rock and roll band.
Got to get back to the land and set my soul free.

We are stardust, we are golden,
We are billion year old carbon,
And we got to get ourselves back to the garden.

Well, then can I walk beside you?
I have come to lose the smog,
And I feel myself a cog in somethin' turning.
But then maybe it's the time of man.
And I don't know who I am,
But life is for learning.

We are stardust, we are golden,
We are billion year old carbon,
And we got to get ourselves back to the garden.

We are stardust, we are golden,
We are billion year old carbon,
And we got to get ourselves back to the garden.

By the time we got to Woodstock,
We were half a million strong
And everywhere was a song and a celebration.
And I dreamed I saw the bomber jet planes
Riding shotgun in the sky,
Turning into butterflies
Above our nation.

We are stardust, we are golden,
We are caught in the devils bargain,
And we got to get ourselves back to the garden.

### Chlo_e — 2024-09-17T13:15:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7-sided-heart.29446/post-339646>

Some of the sound and cymatics researchers point out that the bass frequencies produced by the heart beat also play a role in some of these processes. For example, John Stuart Reid found that whole-blood (in vitro) was better oxygenated when exposed to music with a strong bass compared with classical music with no bass, but that both types of music improved oxygenation compared to no music.

Steiner also goes into great detail about the hexagonal shape in the development of bee hives, and that the hexagonal shapes produced in nature imparts certain energetic qualities. This theme seems to keep cropping up in other areas like water research.

The solar IR mentioned as driving the gel phase of water- I also do wonder how much of it is solar IR and how much of it is endogenously-produced IR or a combination of both..

### Jack Kruse — 2025-02-18T17:04:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-sv-40.29449/post-346945>

Look at this tweet on X.........https://x.com/toobaffled/status/1891672692455092318

The data?

The presence of SV40 in monkey cell cultures used in the preparation of the polio vaccine from 1955 through 1961 is well documented in the literature. Investigations have consistently demonstrated the oncogenic behavior of SV40 in animal models. Early epidemiologic studies were inadequate in demonstrating an increase in cancer incidence associated with contaminated vaccine. Recently, investigators have provided persuasive evidence that SV40 is present in human ependymomas, choroid plexus tumors, bone tumors, and mesotheliomas, however, the etiologic role of the virus in tumorigenesis has not been established.

Using data from SEER, researchers analyzed the incidence of brain tumors, bone tumors, and mesotheliomas from 1973-1993 and the possible relationship of these tumors with the administration of the SV40 contaminated vaccine.

**Their** analysis indicated increased rates of ependymomas (37%), osteogenic sarcomas (26%), other bone tumors (34%) and mesothelioma (90%) among those in the exposed as compared to the unexposed birth cohort.

**What did these researchers conclude?** These data suggest that there may be an increased incidence of certain cancers among the 98 million persons exposed to contaminated polio vaccine (Cutter Incident) in the U.S.; further investigations are clearly justified considering SV 40 is now been proven to exist in the modern new mRNA vaccine of Pfizer. This is incongruous and seems intentional.

**CITES**

Comparative Study

Anticancer Res
. 1999 May-Jun;19(3B):2173-80.

### Cancer risk associated with simian virus 40 contaminated polio vaccine​

[S G Fisher](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/?term=Fisher+SG&cauthor_id=10472327) [1](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/10472327/#full-view-affiliation-1), [L Weber](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/?term=Weber+L&cauthor_id=10472327), [M Carbone](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/?term=Carbone+M&cauthor_id=10472327)

# Thread: High BUN:Cr Ratio
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-bun-cr-ratio.29452/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kirsten F — 2024-09-17T21:52:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-bun-cr-ratio.29452/post-339667>

New to posting! Hoping to optimize cellular dehydration and retest before reconsultation with Dr. Kruse.

Background: 43 yo F, living in ES. Hx: IOL replacements 3 years ago (vision correction for high myopia), PCOS, BMI 21. Consult with Jack in April 2024 re: long-standing migraines. I have been doing very well re: my light environment and using my “solar panels” since I have a “tarp” over my eyes.

On retesting, vit D rose to 59 from 30. BUN:Cr ratio is shit - 21 (Cr 0.7, BUN 15).

Ideas and questions re: ratio:

1.  Grounding - not easy on my property, but needs to be optimized
2.  Environment - needs testing and optimization. We turn off wifi at a night. Just purchased meters and ethernet cables.
3.  Diet: I assume caffeine and alcohol worsen the ratio? Will focus on decreasing carb intake further.
4.  Body composition: Is there any leeway to what the optimal BUN:Cr ratio is based on body composition? My Cr will always be lowish due to low muscle mass - given the low denominator, is my target still <10?

Any comments greatly appreciated, including areas I have missed. Otherwise, I can take up with Jack in a consult.

### Kirsten F — 2024-10-10T17:26:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-bun-cr-ratio.29452/post-340758>

> 2.  Environment - needs testing and optimization. We turn off wifi at a night. Just purchased meters and ethernet cables.
> 3.  Diet: I assume caffeine and alcohol worsen the ratio? Will focus on decreasing carb intake further.

>
> Any comments greatly appreciated, including areas I have missed. Otherwise, I can take up with Jack in a consult.

Excited about progress:

\- no caffeine for 2 weeks
\- environment: learned to use RF meter, and was horrified by readings from WiFi and near cell phone. WiFi now off, and using ethernet only. Phones were already distanced from us at night, but now distancing during the day too.

Will be retesting to measure effects on ratio, though unclear how responsive the ratio is (ie. does it fluctuate on a day-to-day, week-to-week, or month-to-month basis once consistent efforts to improve redox have been made?).

# Thread: Circadian etiology of, or role in, brain fog
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-etiology-of-or-role-in-brain-fog.29461/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ajohnson90 — 2024-09-19T00:16:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-etiology-of-or-role-in-brain-fog.29461/post-339712>

I’ve been dealing with brain fog for about a decade now, and I’ve noticed that it gets worse in the afternoon, around 3-4pm.

Where would one go to look into why that is the case, what circadian mechanism is at play, and how to resolve the issue overall?

### Mat.I — 2024-09-19T02:22:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-etiology-of-or-role-in-brain-fog.29461/post-339715>

> [ajohnson90 said:](/goto/post?id=339712)
>
> I’ve been dealing with brain fog for about a decade now, and I’ve noticed that it gets worse in the afternoon, around 3-4pm.
>
> Where would one go to look into why that is the case, what circadian mechanism is at play, and how to resolve the issue overall?

Have you tested your environment for nn-emf? Looking at that level may not be relevant when the overall environment that you are in is not good. Every n=1 is different meaning nn-emf affects us all in different ways.. are you staying dark at night? Seeing sunrise in the morning etc?

### ajohnson90 — 2024-09-19T23:42:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-etiology-of-or-role-in-brain-fog.29461/post-339734>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=339715)
>
> Have you tested your environment for nn-emf? Looking at that level may not be relevant when the overall environment that you are in is not good. Every n=1 is different meaning nn-emf affects us all in different ways.. are you staying dark at night? Seeing sunrise in the morning etc?

I haven’t tested my environment for nn-emf. But it’s been consistent for a decade regardless of environment (moved apartments quite a bit, and moved states).

I do see sunrise in the morning, sunset at night, am outside pretty much all day, and minimize lighting / wear blue blockers at night. bedroom is pitch black for sleep

### Anthony Smith — 2024-09-20T00:19:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-etiology-of-or-role-in-brain-fog.29461/post-339740>

> [ajohnson90 said:](/goto/post?id=339734)
>
> I haven’t tested my environment for nn-emf. But it’s been consistent for a decade regardless of environment (moved apartments quite a bit, and moved states).
>
> I do see sunrise in the morning, sunset at night, am outside pretty much all day, and minimize lighting / wear blue blockers at night. bedroom is pitch black for sleep

If you have not tested your environment for nnEMF you have NO IDEA if it has been consistent. RF, electric fields and magnetic fields all vary significantly with location, even room to room.

### sheas — 2024-09-20T00:59:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-etiology-of-or-role-in-brain-fog.29461/post-339744>

I also recommend testing your environment. I started getting terrible brain fog so much so I thought I was getting early onset dementia. And over the last year my hair was falling out in clumps like a chemo patient. I found Dr. Jack and started to reunite with the sun. Never missing a sunrise and blocking all ALAN and getting tanned again. My brain fog lifted and I started remembering my dreams a little bit, and my hair has grown back to its normal thickness filling my hair line back in. But I still knew there was more to be done.

Last week I hired a building biologist to come out and measure everything they measure in my home. Wow! we were being irradiated to death. I have incorporated some of the things like creating a sleeping sanctuary that one was easy...In less than one week, my debilitating low back pain is about 40% better. This morning I actually got out of bed like a normal person and could bend over right away to pick something up. Usually getting out of bed takes a minute with me worming around, and forget trying to bend over until mid day or so. I suspected this might be wifi related after our trip to Romania where it didn't hurt at all until I came home. The other thing that has changed is now I am having super vivid dreams and remembering them. It feels so good to dream again. I also feel sharper, not loosing my words so much when I talk. I feel like I am getting myself back!

I think everyone needs to hire a building biologist. Our tech is slowly poisoning us. If you live in an apartment it might be more of a challenge because you are getting it from outside in. Luckily my outside space is clean as we live under huge oak trees. We changed wifi companies about a year ago right before my back started hurting and of course I didn't connect the two things. We went from one that runs off cable to one that is on fiber optics so our signal would be stronger. Now I realize how dumb that was. Switching back soon. The other thing I changed was using an AUX for my car so I could listen to podcasts when I drive instead of bluetooth. And I turned bluetooth off on everything, starting to hardwire things, etc. I will be buying some of these gadgets to measure these frequencies soon too.

### ajohnson90 — 2024-09-20T11:38:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-etiology-of-or-role-in-brain-fog.29461/post-339754>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=339740)
>
> If you have not tested your environment for nnEMF you have NO IDEA if it has been consistent. RF, electric fields and magnetic fields all vary significantly with location, even room to room.

I’m saying my symptoms have been consistent, despite moving homes (I’ve moved 12 times in the last decade) and moving states.

Presumably EMFs have been different in these locations.

Similar complaints + EHS here.

Fibre optics were installed in my street/throughout town during covid. So was 5G. There already was radar way before. (=RF) However when I switched off all RF and all the breakers in that flat I improved, despite insane RF levels from outside.
I wonder if the indoors tech abuse + covid lockdown lifestyle somehow 'tuned me' into the AC LF 50/60Hz grid, making me more susceptible to RF?

I think I read sth related to this in dr. Kruse's CPC24 blog on his own EHS?
[https://jackkruse.com/cpc-24-the-history-of-my-own-ehs-from-a-chapter-of-my-biohacking-book/](https://jackkruse.com/cpc-24-the-history-of-my-own-ehs-from-a-chapter-of-my-biohacking-book/)

As I improved and went crowded places w/ plenty of LF, ALAN and smartphones and re-used my cell phone (May 2023) health went downhill again.

Electric fields from 60Hz (50Hz) in home AC wiring, for most is the main “load”. Reduce the 60Hz AC (especially at night) and reduce your overall daily load, then the outside RF etc will have less effect.

# Thread: PCOS Guidance
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pcos-guidance.29463/>
**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ddarbari — 2024-09-19T04:48:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pcos-guidance.29463/post-339720>

Hi ,

I am very new to the forum and definitely super late to the party. I am 35 year old software engineer from Seattle originally from India . I have struggled with period issues since I was 14 years old. My very first memory of having missed period is from when I was an Adoloscent when my mother took me to a gynac and she gave me Birth control pills and kept me on it for 7 years without ever stopping. At the time I didnt even know I had PCOS . At 22 I was old enough to understand something is not quite adding up with these pills ,so stopped taking them all of a sudden in an attempt to reverse my condition . I have tried a lot of diet and extreme exercising but I have been unable to reverse my condition so far. Only good thing is that since past 1-2 years my ultrasound show that I have no cysts on my ovaries. However I still do not get regular periods , they go missing 3,4,5,6 or even 8 months at a time and if they do come the flow is very scanty. Every time I go to the docs I have to argue with them that I wont take the BCP . I could not understand root cause of my issues and of course never got any guidance from central docs. I recently stumbled up Dr jack and could not believe the root cause of the issue could be light, water , magnetism.
I have very religiously started to follow the Sun rise watching and blocking blue light for last 2 month. I have been sleeping early , wrapping up dinner early, eating seasonally, taking sun break, doing grounding, eating high quality and high protein diet as much as I can.
I was hoping to get some advise from the forum if they have seen the reversal of PCOS symptoms? I dont know when my periods will show up. I feel very hopeless. Any thing that I can do to reverse my condition is much appreciated.
I got my labs done.
1\. pregnenalone - 47ng/DL
2\. rT3 -24ng/DL
3\. VitaminD 51ng/DL

Testosterone is higher than normal - 68ng/DL

Is there any hope for cases like mine where I took BCP at very early age not really knowing it's side effects.Can I still heal, is there any additional tests I can do to see my progress while I wait for my periods to show up

### Niki Stanton — 2024-09-20T00:28:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pcos-guidance.29463/post-339741>

I also have pcos and began BCp very early. If I eat paleo I don’t have symptoms. Perhaps being in Florida at the time of trying paleo was the reason I had success. Dr. kruse will probably tell you Seattle is not the ideal environment for you to heal.

Adding in Jacks work has been a game changer. He says pcos is a retina issue so ALAN is a big culprit for us. I’m pregnant with my third and a little older now, having a hard time eating paleo and my symptoms are coming back! Hopefully in a couple weeks I can tolerate full paleo again.

Pcos is hard- you’re in the right place though!

### ddarbari — 2024-09-20T04:08:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pcos-guidance.29463/post-339749>

Thank you for the response Niki, really appreciate your positive works and your story helps too
I am planning to go to India for 2 months in Nov and Dec , hoping this time I will get a ton of sunshine. I am being cautious to protect my rentina from artificial light at night but I got to start wearing blue blockers in day as well when using the screen I guess.
In any case I really appreciate your response

\-Divya

# Thread: My Mid-Life Health Journey
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-mid-life-health-journey.29465/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### La-Shawn Hill — 2024-09-20T00:36:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-mid-life-health-journey.29465/post-339742>

Hello. My health issues began six years ago when I entered perimenopause. It was hard adjusting to HBP meds, for starters, but the real atomic bomb has been the toll perimenopause has taken on my mental health. I belong to a group of women referred to as "The Lost Generation." Even though I've always known I had an "attention problem," I wasn't formally diagnosed with ADHD until age 45 when "hor(ror)mones" took away my executive functioning and caused me to develop adrenal fatigue. We were all placed on lockdown during the pandemic. Well, once the lockdown was lifted, I found out just how "not okay" I still was due to my newfound anxiety and high-functioning depression. Perimenopause is harsh, but due to the severity of my mental health issues I wondered if perhaps I was suffering from Long COVID--or maybe even vaccine-injured. ADHD meds weren't helping me. I got tired of centralized medicine doctors and turned to functional medicine doctors. I eventually lost faith in doctors, period, and begin doing some hardcore health research of my own. Eventually I discovered the work of Dr. Kruse, and it made sense. I'd tried "talk therapy" for a time--it never helped. Actually, I'll say the one thing that did help me during my time in counseling was the advice to 'get out and walk' for my depression. Before that, I would literally work from sun-up to sundown inside my home office. Today, since becoming a follower of Dr. Kruse, I work outside on my porch. Can the sun really cure my body of what ails me? I don't know, but I'm here. And, I guess I'm not completely averse to centralized medicine since I just had an appointment with a new gynecologist. She says perhaps the HRT I tried in the past wasn't getting absorbed into the skin. There's a chance that HRT may help me yet. If not, it hurts nothing to improve my circadian biology by working on my light environment. If the sun and/or HRT don't fix me, then I have some other ideas as to what's going on with me. I'm open to any and all suggestions. Thanks in advance.

### India Rose Hopper — 2024-09-20T21:52:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-mid-life-health-journey.29465/post-339764>

Watch the sunrise every morning and get as much sunlight on as much skin as possible. I do believe you will feel a difference but give it time as well
Blessings on your health journey

### La-Shawn Hill — 2024-09-21T04:13:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-mid-life-health-journey.29465/post-339782>

Thank you so much and the same to you—blessings on your health journey.
